korean: 내일 내 대신 근무 좀 해줄 수 있어?
english: Can you do me a solid and cover my shift tomorrow?
alt: Can you help me out and take my shift tomorrow?
note: do me a solid는 do me a favor보다 캐주얼. 친한 사이에서만

korean: 늦어서 미안 금방 갈게
english: Sorry I'm running late. I'll be there in 10.
alt: Sorry for the delay. I'm on my way.
note: I'm on my way = 지금 가는 중

korean: 괜찮아 별거 아니야 신경 쓰지 마
english: No worries at all. It's no big deal.
alt: Don't sweat it. It's totally fine.
note: No biggie도 같은 뜻. Don't sweat it은 더 캐주얼

korean: 야 그거 진짜야? 대박이다!
english: Are you serious? That's insane!
alt: No way! That's amazing!
note: insane은 긍정적 놀라움으로도 많이 씀

korean: 솔직히 말하면 별로인 것 같아
english: To be honest with you I don't think it's that great.
alt: If I'm being real it's not my thing.
note: TBH로 줄여서 문자로도 많이 씀

korean: 어쩔 수 없지 뭐 받아들이는 수밖에
english: It is what it is. We just have to deal with it.
alt: Nothing we can do about it. Let's just move on.
note: 미국인이 입에 달고 사는 표현

korean: 진짜 공감돼 나도 그런 적 있어
english: I can totally relate to that. I've been there.
alt: I know exactly what you mean. Same here.
note: I've been there = 나도 겪어봤어

korean: 나는 아무거나 괜찮아 네가 골라
english: I'm easy. Whatever you want is fine with me.
alt: I'm down for anything. Your call.
note: I'm easy/I'm down for anything 둘 다 자연스러움

korean: 티셔츠에 20만원은 좀 너무 하지 않아?
english: Two hundred bucks for a T-shirt? That's a bit much.
alt: Don't you think $200 for a T-shirt is overkill?
note: overkill = 과한/지나친

korean: 오늘 내가 쏠게 지갑 넣어
english: Today's on me. Put your wallet away.
alt: I got this. Don't even think about paying.
note: It's on me / I got this / My treat 다 같은 뜻

korean: 저기요 계산서 좀 주세요
english: Excuse me can I get the check please?
alt: Could we get the bill when you get a chance?
note: 미국: check / 영국: bill

korean: 각자 내자 오늘은
english: Let's just split the bill today.
alt: Let's go halves on this.
note: Go Dutch는 요즘 잘 안 씀. split이 자연스러움

korean: 양파 빼고 해주실 수 있나요?
english: Can I get this without onions please?
alt: Could you hold the onions on this?
note: hold the onions = 양파 빼주세요 (캐주얼)

korean: 여기 처음인데 뭐가 맛있어요?
english: I've never been here before. What do you recommend?
alt: What's good here? Any personal favorites?
note: What's good here?가 가장 자연스러움

korean: 아이스 아메리카노 큰 거 하나 포장이요
english: Can I get a large iced Americano to go?
alt: I'll have a large iced Americano for takeout.
note: for here or to go? 에 대한 답

korean: 견과류 알레르기가 있어서 확인 좀 해주세요
english: I have a nut allergy, so could you double-check for me?
alt: I'm allergic to nuts. Can you make sure there's none in this?
note: double-check = 다시 확인하다

korean: 두 명인데 자리 있나요?
english: Hi do you have a table for two?
alt: Is there room for two or is there a wait?
note: 예약 있으면 I have a reservation under + 이름

korean: 이거 입어봐도 되나요? 탈의실 어디예요?
english: Can I try this on? Where's the fitting room?
alt: Mind if I try this on? Where can I change?
note: fitting room = dressing room = 탈의실

korean: 이거 미디엄 사이즈 있어요?
english: Do you have this in a medium?
alt: Does this come in a medium?
note: Do you have this in + 사이즈/색상

korean: 그냥 구경하는 거예요 괜찮아요
english: I'm just browsing thanks. I'm good.
alt: Just looking around. I'll let you know if I need help.
note: I'll let you know가 부드러운 마무리

korean: 이거 지금 할인 중인 건가요?
english: Is this on sale right now?
alt: Is there a discount on this?
note: on sale = 할인 중 / for sale = 판매 중 (다른 뜻!)

korean: 영수증 있는데 환불 가능할까요?
english: I have the receipt. Can I return this?
alt: I'd like to return this. Here's my receipt.
note: exchange = 교환 / refund = 환불금

korean: 화장실이 어디에 있는지 알려주실 수 있나요?
english: Could you tell me where the restroom is?
alt: Do you know where the bathroom is?
note: 미국: restroom/bathroom / 영국: toilet/loo

korean: 두 개 사면 좀 깎아주실 수 있어요?
english: If I buy two can you give me a better price?
alt: Any chance of a discount if I get two?
note: Any chance of~? = 혹시 ~될까요?

korean: 오늘 좀 컨디션이 안 좋아서 일찍 갈 수도 있어
english: I'm not feeling great today, so I might leave early.
alt: I'm a bit under the weather. I might head out early.
note: under the weather = 몸이 안 좋은 (관용구)

korean: 두통이 너무 심해서 집중을 못 하겠어
english: I have a splitting headache and I can't focus.
alt: My head is killing me. I can't concentrate.
note: splitting headache / head is killing me 둘 다 강한 표현

korean: 병원 예약 좀 잡고 싶은데요
english: I'd like to make an appointment to see the doctor.
alt: Can I schedule a visit with the doctor?
note: see a doctor = 진료 받다

korean: 여기를 누르면 아파요
english: It hurts right here when I press on it.
alt: I feel a sharp pain right here when I touch it.
note: sharp pain = 날카로운 통증 / dull ache = 둔한 통증

korean: 기침이 심한데 약 좀 처방해주실 수 있나요?
english: Can you prescribe something for my cough?
alt: Is there anything you can give me for this cough?
note: prescription = 처방전 / OTC = 처방전 없이 살 수 있는

korean: 요즘 어떻게 지내? 무슨 일 있었어?
english: What have you been up to lately? Anything new?
alt: How've you been? What's going on in your life?
note: What have you been up to?는 How are you?보다 진심 어린 질문

korean: 별일 없어 그냥 똑같지 뭐
english: Same old same old. Nothing exciting.
alt: Not much really. Just the usual.
note: the usual = 늘 하던 거

korean: 나 먼저 갈게 늦었다
english: I gotta head out. It's getting late.
alt: I should get going. I've got an early morning.
note: I gotta = I've got to (구어체)

korean: 오늘 진짜 재밌었어 다음에 또 보자
english: I had a blast today. Let's do this again soon.
alt: This was so fun. We should hang out again.
note: have a blast = 엄청 즐기다

korean: 연락 끊지 말고 지내자 진짜로
english: Don't be a stranger. Let's keep in touch for real.
alt: Stay in touch okay? Don't disappear on me.
note: Don't be a stranger = 연락하고 지내자는 따뜻한 표현

korean: 그건 네가 알아서 해 나는 상관없어
english: It's totally your call. I'm fine either way.
alt: That's up to you. I'm good with whatever.
note: your call = 네 결정 / either way = 어느 쪽이든

korean: 우리 이 얘기 그만하자 맴돌고 있잖아
english: Let's just drop it. We're going in circles.
alt: Can we move on? We keep saying the same thing.
note: go in circles = 같은 얘기 반복

korean: 뭐라고? 잘 안 들렸어 다시 말해줘
english: Sorry what was that? I didn't catch it.
alt: Come again? I missed what you said.
note: I didn't catch that이 가장 자연스러움

korean: 장난이지? 진짜야?
english: You're kidding me, right? Is that for real?
alt: No way. Are you being serious right now?
note: Are you being serious? = 진심이야?

korean: 나 진짜 진심이야 농담 아니야
english: I'm dead serious. I'm not joking.
alt: I mean it. This isn't a joke.
note: dead serious = 완전 진지한

korean: 큰 결정이니까 하루 생각해볼게
english: That's a big decision. Let me sleep on it.
alt: I need to think it over. Give me a day.
note: sleep on it = 하룻밤 자면서 생각하다

korean: 아 그래서 그랬구나 이제 이해됐어
english: Oh that makes sense now. I was so confused.
alt: Oh I see. Now I get it.
note: Now I get it = 이제 이해됐어

korean: 거의 다 끝나가 5분만 더 줘
english: I'm almost done. Just give me five more minutes.
alt: I'm nearly finished. Just a few more minutes.
note: nearly there도 같은 뜻

korean: 잠깐만 기다려 금방 올게
english: Hold on a sec. I'll be right back.
alt: Hang on. Give me one second.
note: I'll be right back = 금방 올게

korean: 내일 마감 세 개인데 미쳐버리겠어
english: I have three deadlines tomorrow and I'm losing my mind.
alt: Three things due tomorrow. I'm going crazy.
note: losing my mind / going crazy 둘 다 자주 씀

korean: 저 창가 자리 내가 먼저 찜했어!
english: I call dibs on the window seat!
alt: The window seat is mine! I called it first!
note: call dibs = 선점 선언. 미국 캐주얼 표현

korean: 지금 분위기 좀 파악해 타이밍이 아니야
english: Dude read the room. Now's not the time.
alt: Can you not? This isn't the moment.
note: read the room = 분위기/상황을 읽다

korean: 걔 맨날 말만 하고 행동은 안 해
english: He's all talk and no action. Don't count on him.
alt: He never follows through. He just talks big.
situation: 행동 안 하는 사람 얘기할 때
note: all bark and no bite도 같은 뜻

korean: 어제 무슨 일이 있었는지 말하고 싶어 죽겠어
english: I'm dying to tell you what happened yesterday.
alt: You won't believe what happened. I have to tell you.
note: dying to = ~하고 싶어 죽겠는

korean: 뭔가 이상한 느낌이 들어 설명은 못 하겠는데
english: Something feels off. I can't explain it but I just have a gut feeling.
alt: I don't know what it is, but my gut says something's wrong.
note: gut feeling = 직감. trust your gut = 직감을 믿어

korean: 미안 시간 가는 줄 몰랐어 완전 빠져있었어
english: Sorry I totally lost track of time. I was so into it.
alt: I got carried away and didn't realize how late it was.
note: get carried away = 빠져들다/몰입하다

korean: 금도금 폰케이스? 그건 좀 오버 아니야?
english: A gold-plated phone case? Don't you think that's a bit over the top?
alt: That's way too extra. Who needs a gold phone case?
note: extra = 오버하는/과한 (슬랭)

korean: 다음 주 콘서트 진짜 기대된다!
english: I can't wait for the concert next week!
alt: I'm so hyped for the concert next week!
note: hyped = 들뜬/기대되는 (슬랭)

korean: 옆집 개가 밤새 짖어서 미치겠어
english: My neighbor's dog barks all night. It drives me crazy.
alt: The dog next door won't stop barking. It's driving me insane.
note: drive someone crazy/insane = ~을 미치게 하다

korean: 내 생일 잊은 거 좀 서운했어
english: It kind of hurt my feelings that you forgot my birthday.
alt: I was a little bummed you forgot my birthday.
note: bummed = 실망한/서운한 (캐주얼)

korean: 정말 큰 도움이 됐어 진심으로 고마워
english: You've been such a huge help. I really appreciate it.
alt: I can't thank you enough. You really saved me.
note: I can't thank you enough = 감사해도 모자라

korean: 진짜 죄송해요 다시는 안 그럴게요
english: I'm so sorry about that. It won't happen again.
alt: I sincerely apologize. I'll make sure it doesn't happen again.
note: sincerely apologize는 좀 더 격식

korean: 그 말 들으니까 진짜 기분 좋다 고마워
english: That really means a lot to me. Thank you for saying that.
alt: Hearing that made my day. I appreciate you.
note: make my day = 하루를 좋게 만들다

korean: 네가 없었으면 어떻게 했을지 모르겠다
english: I honestly don't know what I'd do without you.
alt: You're a lifesaver. I'd be lost without you.
note: lifesaver = 생명의 은인 (비유적으로 많이 씀)

korean: 핸드폰 배터리 거의 다 됐어 충전기 있어?
english: My phone's about to die. Do you have a charger I can borrow?
alt: I'm at like 2%. Anyone got a charger?
note: about to die = 곧 꺼질 것 같은 (배터리)

korean: 데이터 다 써서 핫스팟 좀 켜줄 수 있어?
english: I'm out of data. Can I use your hotspot for a bit?
alt: I used up all my data. Mind sharing your hotspot?
note: use up / run out of = 다 쓰다

korean: 출발 전에 폰 충전 좀 하고 갈게
english: Let me charge my phone real quick before we leave.
alt: I need to plug in my phone for a sec before we go.
note: plug in = 충전기에 꽂다

korean: 인스타에서 돌아다니는 그 영상 봤어?
english: Did you see that video going around on Instagram?
alt: Have you seen that viral video on Insta?
note: go viral / go around = 퍼지다

korean: 여기 사람 진짜 많다 다른 데 가자
english: It's packed in here. Let's go somewhere else.
alt: This place is way too crowded. Let's bounce.
note: bounce = 떠나다 (슬랭)

korean: 오늘 하루 진짜 길었다 한 잔 하자
english: It's been a long day. Let's grab a drink.
alt: What a day. I could use a drink right now.
note: I could use a ~ = ~이 필요해/하고 싶어

korean: 이거 좀 먹어봐도 돼? 맛있어 보여
english: Can I have a bite? That looks amazing.
alt: Mind if I try some? It looks so good.
note: a bite = 한 입 / a sip = 한 모금

korean: 배 터질 것 같아 더 못 먹겠어
english: I'm so stuffed. I can't eat another bite.
alt: I'm absolutely full. I'm done.
note: stuffed가 full보다 강한 표현

korean: 이거 진짜 맛있다 너도 꼭 먹어봐
english: This is so good. You have to try it.
alt: Oh my god this is amazing. You need to taste this.
note: You have to try it = 꼭 먹어봐야 해

korean: 요즘 식단 조절 중이라 디저트는 패스할게
english: I'm watching what I eat so I'll skip dessert.
alt: I'm trying to eat clean so no dessert for me.
note: watch what I eat / eat clean 둘 다 자연스러움

korean: 그거 진짜 효과 있어? 매일 먹는 거야?
english: Does that actually work? Do you take it every day?
alt: Is it legit? How often do you use it?
note: legit = 진짜인/효과 있는 (슬랭)

korean: 우산 가져가 혹시 모르니까
english: Bring an umbrella just in case.
alt: Better grab an umbrella. You never know.
note: you never know = 뭐가 어떻게 될지 모르니까

korean: 아 말 나온 김에 새로 생긴 식당 들었어?
english: Speaking of which did you hear about the new restaurant?
alt: Oh that reminds me. Have you heard about the new place?
note: speaking of which / that reminds me

korean: 글쎄 그건 경우에 따라 다르지
english: Honestly it depends on the situation.
alt: It's hard to say. It really varies.
note: It depends + on ~ 으로 구체적으로 말할 수 있음

korean: 어디에 뒀는지 하나도 기억이 안 나
english: I have no clue where I put it.
alt: I can't remember where I left it for the life of me.
note: for the life of me = 아무리 해도 (강조)

korean: 그러니까 내 말이 그 말이야
english: Exactly! That's what I'm saying.
alt: Right? That's my whole point.
note: That's what I'm saying! = 내 말이!

korean: 인생 뭐 있어 한 번 해봐
english: You only live once. Just go for it.
alt: Life's too short. Take the leap.
note: YOLO / take the leap = 도전하다

korean: 어디서 많이 본 것 같은데 우리 만난 적 있나요?
english: You look really familiar. Have we met before?
alt: Don't I know you from somewhere?
note: You look familiar가 가장 자연스러운 시작

korean: 오늘 진짜 즐거웠어 번호 교환할래?
english: I had a great time today. Can I get your number?
alt: This was fun. Want to exchange numbers?
note: Can I get your number?가 가장 직접적이고 자연스러움

korean: 거기 새로 생겼다던데 가봤어?
english: There's a new place that opened up. Have you been?
alt: Have you checked out that new spot?
note: check out = 가보다/확인하다

korean: 그럼 이따 봐! 오늘 고마웠어
english: Alright see you later! Thanks for today.
alt: Catch you later! Today was great.
note: Catch you later가 See you later보다 캐주얼

korean: 늦었으니까 조심히 들어가
english: It's late. Get home safe.
alt: Text me when you get home so I know you're safe.
note: Text me when you get home = 도착하면 연락해

korean: 그게 뭐 어이가 없어서 말이 안 나온다
english: I literally can't even. That's unbelievable.
alt: I'm speechless. I don't even know what to say.
note: I can't even = 할 말을 잃었다 (젊은 층)

korean: 나가라고 하는 게 아니라 진짜 놀라서 그래
english: Get out of here! I can't believe it!
alt: Shut up! Are you serious?
note: Shut up!도 놀라움 표현. 진짜 입 다물라는 게 아님

korean: 이 팟캐스트 완전 내 취향이야
english: This podcast is right up my alley.
alt: This is totally my kind of thing.
note: right up my alley = 딱 내 취향인

korean: 그건 니가 꿈에서나 가능한 얘기야
english: In your dreams, buddy. That's never happening.
alt: Yeah right. Keep dreaming.
note: Keep dreaming = 계속 꿈이나 꿔

korean: 시험? 그거 완전 쉬웠어 껌이야
english: The exam? It was a piece of cake.
alt: That test was a breeze. I finished early.
note: a piece of cake / a breeze / a walk in the park

korean: 사람들 앞에서 넘어져서 쪽팔려 죽는 줄 알았어
english: I tripped in front of everyone. I wanted to crawl into a hole.
alt: I fell in public and I was so embarrassed I wanted to disappear.
note: crawl into a hole = 땅굴 파고 숨고 싶다

korean: 그 노래 들을 때마다 소름 돋아
english: That song gives me chills every single time.
alt: I get goosebumps whenever I hear that song.
note: chills = goosebumps = 소름

korean: 통장 잔고 보고 현타 왔어
english: I checked my bank account and reality just hit me.
alt: I looked at my balance and came back down to earth.
note: come back down to earth = 현실로 돌아오다

korean: 계속 바쁘다고 하는데 걔 진짜 눈치가 없어
english: I keep saying I'm busy but he can't take a hint.
alt: I've been dropping hints but he's completely clueless.
note: take a hint = 눈치채다 / clueless = 눈치 없는

korean: 딴 얘기로 넘어가지 마 내 질문에 답해
english: Don't change the subject. Answer my question.
alt: Stop dodging the question. Just answer me.
note: dodge = 피하다

korean: 농담 아니고 그 피자 내 인생 최고였어
english: No joke that was the best pizza I've ever had in my life.
alt: I'm not exaggerating. That was hands down the best pizza ever.
note: hands down = 의심의 여지 없이

korean: 태국 요리 해본 적은 없지만 한 번 도전해볼게
english: I've never cooked Thai food but I'll give it a shot.
alt: I'm no expert but I'll try my hand at Thai cooking.
note: try my hand at = ~을 시도해보다

korean: 포기하지 마 거의 다 왔어 조금만 버텨
english: Don't give up. You're almost there. Just hang in there.
alt: Keep going. You're so close. Don't quit now.
note: hang in there = 버텨/견뎌

korean: 솔직히 별로였어 기대만큼은 아니야
english: Honestly it wasn't great. It didn't live up to the hype.
alt: It was kind of a letdown to be honest.
note: live up to the hype = 기대에 부응하다 / letdown = 실망

korean: 아 맞다 너한테 할 말 있었는데 깜빡했어
english: Oh right! I totally forgot. I had something to tell you.
alt: Oh shoot! I meant to tell you something earlier.
note: shoot = 앗/이런 (가벼운 감탄사)

korean: 날씨가 좀 애매해서 피크닉 하기 찜찜한데
english: The weather looks kind of iffy for the picnic.
alt: I'm not sure about the weather. It could go either way.
note: iffy = 불확실한 / go either way = 어느 쪽이든 될 수 있는

korean: 운동화에 500달러? 너 미쳤어?
english: You spent $500 on sneakers? Are you out of your mind?
alt: $500 for shoes? Have you lost it?
note: Have you lost it? = 미쳤어? (Are you crazy?보다 강함)

korean: 이건 비밀인데 나만 알고 있어
english: This is between us. Keep it to yourself.
alt: Don't tell anyone okay? This stays between you and me.
note: between us / stays between us = 우리끼리만

korean: 뒤에서 네 얘기하더라 조심해
english: She was talking behind your back. Watch out.
alt: Heads up — she's been badmouthing you.
note: badmouth = 헐뜯다 / talk behind one's back = 뒷담화

korean: 하루 종일 전화했는데 왜 안 받아?
english: I've been calling you all day. Why aren't you picking up?
alt: I tried reaching you like ten times. What happened?
note: pick up = 전화 받다 / reach someone = 연락 닿다

korean: 전화 끊지 마 설명 좀 할게
english: Wait don't hang up. Let me explain.
alt: Hold on don't go. Just hear me out.
note: hear me out = 내 말 좀 들어봐

korean: 몇 시간 전에 문자 했는데 왜 답이 없어?
english: I texted you hours ago. Why didn't you text me back?
alt: I sent you a message ages ago. No reply?
note: text back / get back to me = 답장하다

korean: 시험 합격했다며! 내가 한턱 쏠게
english: You passed! Dinner's on me. I'll treat you.
alt: Congrats on passing! Let me take you out to celebrate.
note: take someone out = ~를 데리고 나가서 사주다

korean: 세상에 진짜 오래간만이다! 어떻게 지냈어?
english: Oh my god it's been forever! How have you been?
alt: I haven't seen you in ages! What's new with you?
note: in ages = 아주 오랫동안

korean: 오늘 뭐 먹고 싶어? 피자 아니면 초밥?
english: What are you in the mood for? Pizza or sushi?
alt: What do you feel like eating? Pizza or sushi?
note: be in the mood for = ~이 먹고 싶은/하고 싶은

korean: 요즘 스트레스 받아서 밥맛이 없어
english: I've been so stressed I don't even have an appetite.
alt: I'm so stressed out I can barely eat.
note: stressed out = 스트레스를 많이 받는

korean: 이 우유 유통기한 지난 것 같은데 확인해봐
english: I think this milk is past its expiration date. Check it.
alt: This might be expired. Take a look at the date.
note: expiration date = 유통기한 / expired = 기한 지난

korean: 여기 너무 덥다 에어컨 좀 올려
english: It's boiling in here. Can you crank up the AC?
alt: It's so hot. Turn up the air conditioning.
note: crank up = (세기를) 높이다 / boiling = 끓는 듯이 더운

korean: 진짜 춥다 난방 좀 올려줘
english: I'm freezing. Can you turn up the heat?
alt: It's so cold in here. Can we bump up the thermostat?
note: thermostat = 온도 조절기 / bump up = 올리다

korean: 통화 중이니까 소리 좀 줄여줘
english: I'm on a call. Can you keep it down?
alt: Could you turn it down a bit? I'm on the phone.
note: keep it down = 소리 좀 낮춰 / turn it down = 볼륨 낮춰

korean: 잠깐 화장실 다녀올게 짐 좀 봐줄 수 있어?
english: Can you keep an eye on my stuff? I need to hit the restroom.
alt: Watch my bags for a sec? I'll be right back.
note: keep an eye on = 지켜보다 / hit the restroom = 화장실 가다

korean: 이 가격 세금 포함인 건가요?
english: Does this price include tax?
alt: Is tax included, or is it extra?
note: 미국은 표시가격에 세금 별도가 대부분

korean: 카드 결제 돼요? 현금만 받나요?
english: Do you take card or is it cash only?
alt: Can I pay by card? Or do you only accept cash?
note: Do you take card?가 가장 자연스러움

korean: 영수증 괜찮아요 필요 없어요
english: I'm good without the receipt, thanks.
alt: No receipt needed. Thanks.
note: I'm good = 괜찮아요 (다양한 상황에서 씀)

korean: 택배 시킨 거 아직 안 왔어 일주일 됐는데
english: My package still hasn't arrived. It's been a week.
alt: I ordered something a week ago and it still hasn't shown up.
note: show up = 나타나다/도착하다

korean: 로그인이 안 돼 비밀번호 틀렸다고 나와
english: I can't log in. It keeps saying wrong password.
alt: I'm locked out. It won't accept my password.
note: locked out = 잠겨서 못 들어가는

korean: 와이파이가 안 잡혀 인터넷 끊긴 거야?
english: I can't connect to the Wi-Fi. Is the internet down?
alt: The Wi-Fi's not working. Did the connection drop?
note: the internet is down = 인터넷이 끊겼다

korean: 화면이 자꾸 멈춰서 재부팅해야 할 것 같아
english: My screen keeps freezing. I think I need to restart.
alt: My computer keeps lagging out. I'm gonna reboot.
note: lag = 렉걸리다 / reboot = 재부팅

korean: 잘 모르겠으면 그냥 검색해봐
english: If you're not sure just look it up.
alt: Just Google it. The answer's right there.
note: Google it = 검색해봐 (동사로 씀)

korean: 스크린샷 찍어서 나한테 보내줘
english: Take a screenshot and send it to me.
alt: Grab a screencap and shoot it over.
note: shoot it over = 보내줘 (캐주얼)

korean: 내일 7시에 알람 맞춰놔 절대 늦으면 안 돼
english: Set an alarm for 7 AM. I absolutely cannot oversleep.
alt: Make sure you set your alarm for 7. Don't sleep through it.
note: sleep through = 알람 못 듣고 자다

korean: 어젯밤에 시험 공부하느라 밤새웠어
english: I pulled an all-nighter studying for the exam.
alt: I was up all night cramming for the test.
note: pull an all-nighter = 밤을 새다 / cram = 벼락치기

korean: 졸려 죽겠어 눈을 뜰 수가 없어
english: I can barely keep my eyes open. I'm so exhausted.
alt: I'm running on fumes. I need to crash.
note: run on fumes = 기력이 바닥난 / crash = 쓰러지듯 자다

korean: 아직 커피를 안 마셔서 말 시키지 마
english: Don't talk to me until I've had my coffee.
alt: I haven't had my coffee yet. I'm not functional.
note: not functional = 제 기능을 못 하는 (유머러스)

korean: 시럽 빼고 샷 추가로 해주세요
english: Can I get that with no syrup and an extra shot?
alt: No syrup and add an extra shot please.
note: extra shot = 에스프레소 샷 추가 / decaf = 디카페인

korean: 여기서 마실 거예요
english: For here please.
alt: I'll have it here. Not to go.
note: for here = 매장 / to go = 포장

korean: 대기 시간이 얼마나 되나요? 이름 좀 올려주세요
english: How long is the wait? Can you put us on the list?
alt: What's the wait time? We'd like to get on the waitlist.
note: put us on the list = 대기 명단에 올려주세요

korean: 7시에 박 이름으로 예약했어요
english: I have a reservation for 7 o'clock under Park.
alt: We booked a table for 7. It's under Park.
note: book a table / make a reservation

korean: 물 좀 리필해주실 수 있나요?
english: Could I get some more water when you get a chance?
alt: Can I get a water refill please?
note: when you get a chance = 시간 되실 때

korean: 이거 제가 시킨 게 아닌데요 파스타를 시켰거든요
english: Sorry but I didn't order this. I had the pasta.
alt: This isn't what I ordered. I asked for the pasta, not this.
note: I had = I ordered (과거 주문 의미)

korean: 많이 아파? 둔하게 아픈 거야 찌르듯이 아픈 거야?
english: Does it hurt a lot? Is it a dull ache or a sharp pain?
alt: How bad is it? Is it more of a throbbing or a stabbing pain?
note: throbbing = 욱신거리는 / stabbing = 찌르는

korean: 약 먹었어? 잊지 마
english: Did you take your medicine? Don't forget.
alt: Have you taken your meds yet?
note: meds = medicine의 줄임말 (캐주얼)

korean: 목이 아프고 콧물이 나
english: I have a sore throat and a runny nose.
alt: My throat's scratchy and my nose won't stop running.
note: scratchy throat = 목이 칼칼한

korean: 열이 있는 것 같아 체온계 있어?
english: I think I have a fever. Do we have a thermometer?
alt: I feel feverish. Where's the thermometer?
note: feverish = 열이 나는 듯한

korean: 그거 전염되는 거야? 거리 두는 게 좋을까?
english: Is it contagious? Should I keep my distance?
alt: Can I catch it from you? Should I stay back?
note: catch = (병을) 옮다

korean: 너 많이 안 좋아 보여 집에 가서 푹 쉬어
english: You look terrible. Go home and get some rest.
alt: You don't look good at all. Take it easy and rest up.
note: rest up = 충분히 쉬다

korean: 읽고 왜 답장 안 해? 읽씹이야?
english: I saw the read receipts. Don't leave me on read!
alt: You left me on read! What's up with that?
note: on read = 읽고 답 안 하는. SNS 시대 필수 표현

korean: 그건 좀 TMI야 거기까지는 알고 싶지 않았어
english: That's way too much information. I didn't need to know that.
alt: TMI dude. Keep that to yourself.
note: TMI = Too Much Information. 매우 자주 씀

korean: 다 재밌게 놀고 있었는데 걔가 분위기를 깼어
english: We were all having fun until he totally killed the vibe.
alt: He's such a buzzkill. Ruined the whole mood.
note: buzzkill = 분위기 깨는 사람/것

korean: 그 드라마 완전 꿀잼이야 멈출 수가 없어
english: That show is so addicting. I can't stop watching.
alt: I've been binge-watching that series. It's so good.
note: binge-watch = 몰아보다

korean: 그거 보려고 했는데 재미없어서 잠들었어
english: I tried watching it but it was so boring I fell asleep.
alt: I couldn't get into it. It put me to sleep.
note: put me to sleep = 잠들게 할 만큼 지루한

korean: 이 가격에 이 퀄리티면 진짜 가성비 좋다
english: This quality at this price? That's a great bang for your buck.
alt: This is such a good deal for what you get.
note: bang for your buck = 가격 대비 가치

korean: 살 생각 없었는데 그냥 충동구매 해버렸어
english: I wasn't planning to buy anything but I impulse-bought it.
alt: I couldn't resist. I totally splurged on it.
note: splurge = 돈을 확 쓰다

korean: 혹시 쿠폰이나 할인 코드 같은 거 있어요?
english: Do you have any coupons or promo codes I can use?
alt: Is there any discount code or deal going on?
note: promo code = 프로모션 코드

korean: 가게 문 닫았잖아 괜히 왔다
english: The store was closed. That was a total waste of time.
alt: We came all this way for nothing. What a bummer.
note: bummer = 아쉬운/실망스러운 일

korean: 문 닫기 직전에 겨우 들어갔어 진짜 아슬아슬했어
english: I just barely made it before they closed. That was so close.
alt: I got there by the skin of my teeth. Almost didn't make it.
note: by the skin of my teeth = 아슬아슬하게

korean: 일주일 내내 집에만 있었더니 환기가 필요해
english: I've been home all week. I need a change of scenery.
alt: I'm going stir-crazy. I need to get out of the house.
note: stir-crazy = 갇혀있어서 미칠 것 같은

korean: 할 게 아무것도 없어서 심심해 죽겠어
english: There's literally nothing to do. I'm bored out of my mind.
alt: I'm so bored I could scream.
note: bored out of my mind = 지루해서 미칠 것 같은

korean: 맞다 깜빡했다 완전 까먹고 있었어
english: Oh no I totally forgot. It completely slipped my mind.
alt: Shoot I knew I was forgetting something.
note: slip one's mind = 깜빡하다

korean: 이름이 뭐였더라 입에서 맴도는데 기억이 안 나
english: What's his name again? It's on the tip of my tongue.
alt: I know his name. It's right there but I can't think of it.
note: on the tip of my tongue = 혀끝에서 맴도는

korean: 그건 네 생각이고 난 다르게 봐
english: That's your opinion. I see it differently.
alt: We'll have to agree to disagree on this one.
note: agree to disagree = 서로 다른 의견을 인정하다

korean: 인정 네 말이 맞아
english: Fair enough. You've got a point.
alt: I'll give you that. You make a good argument.
note: I'll give you that = 그건 인정할게

korean: 말 자르지 마 끝까지 좀 들어봐
english: Don't interrupt me. Just let me finish.
alt: Stop cutting me off. Hear me out till the end.
note: hear me out = 끝까지 들어줘

korean: 네 말도 일리가 있어 하지만 좀 더 기다려보자
english: You have a point, but I think we should hold off a bit.
alt: That's a valid point. Still I'd rather wait and see.
note: hold off = 미루다/기다리다 / wait and see = 두고 보다

korean: 취직했다고? 진짜 잘됐다! 축하해
english: You got the job? That's awesome! Congrats!
alt: Good for you! I'm so happy for you!
note: Good for you는 톤에 따라 비꼼이 될 수 있으니 주의

korean: 너 하와이 간다고? 진짜 부럽다
english: You're going to Hawaii? I'm so jealous!
alt: Lucky you! I wish I could go too.
note: Lucky you! = 운 좋은 사람!

korean: 콘서트 취소돼서 진짜 속상해
english: I'm so bummed the concert got canceled.
alt: What a bummer. I was really looking forward to it.
note: look forward to = 기대하다

korean: 이 교통체증 매일 똑같아서 진짜 질려
english: I'm so fed up with this traffic. It's the same every single day.
alt: I'm sick and tired of sitting in traffic every day.
note: fed up / sick and tired = 진절머리가 나는

korean: 내가 제일 좋아하는 꽃 기억하고 있었다니 감동이야
english: You remembered my favorite flower? That's so sweet. I'm touched.
alt: I can't believe you remembered. That really means a lot.
note: That's so sweet = 너무 다정하다

korean: 그때 내가 한 말이 계속 마음에 걸려
english: What I said back then has been weighing on me.
alt: I can't stop thinking about what I said. I feel bad.
note: weigh on someone = 마음에 짐이 되다

korean: 너 완전 안 좋아 보여 제발 좀 쉬어
english: You look exhausted. Please go get some shut-eye.
alt: You need to rest. You look like you haven't slept in days.
note: shut-eye = 수면 (캐주얼) / catch some Z's도 같은 뜻

korean: 알람 못 듣고 늦잠 자서 수업 빠졌어
english: I overslept and missed my first class. I slept right through my alarm.
alt: My alarm went off but I didn't hear it. I missed class.
note: sleep through = ~을 못 듣고 자다

korean: 차가 꽉 막혀서 늦을 것 같아
english: Traffic is bumper to bumper. I'm probably gonna be late.
alt: I'm stuck in traffic. It's not moving at all.
note: bumper to bumper = 꽉 막힌

korean: 저기요! 지갑 떨어뜨리셨어요!
english: Excuse me! You dropped your wallet!
alt: Hey! You left your wallet back there!
note: 가장 자연스러운 시작은 Excuse me!

korean: 제가 먼저 하고 있던 건데요 확인해주세요
english: I was actually in line first. Could you check?
alt: I believe I was here before them.
note: I believe는 공손하지만 단호한 표현

korean: 다 필요 없고 됐고 그냥 자자
english: Forget everything. I'm done. Let's just go to bed.
alt: Screw it. I'm exhausted. I'm going to sleep.
note: screw it = 됐어/집어치워 (캐주얼)

korean: 그거 말이야 완전 개이득이야
english: That's such a steal. You'd be crazy not to get it.
alt: That's a no-brainer. Absolute bargain.
note: steal = 거저나 다름없는 / no-brainer = 고민할 것도 없는

korean: 너 진짜 웃긴다 배 아파 죽겠어
english: You're hilarious. I'm literally dying of laughter.
alt: You crack me up. My stomach hurts from laughing.
note: crack me up = 나를 웃기다 / I'm dying = 죽겠어 (과장)

korean: 그 영화 결말 스포하지 마
english: Don't spoil the ending for me.
alt: No spoilers! I haven't seen it yet.
note: spoiler alert = 스포 주의

korean: 이번 주말에 특별한 계획 있어?
english: Do you have any plans this weekend?
alt: Got anything going on this weekend?
note: Got anything?이 더 캐주얼

korean: 그냥 집에서 넷플릭스나 보면서 쉬려고
english: I'm just gonna stay home and binge Netflix.
alt: I'm planning to veg out and watch some shows.
note: veg out = 아무것도 안 하고 쉬다

korean: 어제 너무 많이 마셔서 숙취가 심해
english: I drank way too much last night. I'm so hungover.
alt: I'm dying from a hangover. Never drinking again.
note: hungover = 숙취인 / hangover = 숙취

korean: 그 사람 첫인상이 진짜 좋았어
english: He made a really great first impression.
alt: I liked him right away. Great first impression.
note: make a good/bad impression = 좋은/나쁜 인상을 주다

korean: 너한테 솔직히 말해야 할 것 같아
english: I need to be upfront with you about something.
alt: I feel like I owe you the truth.
note: be upfront = 솔직하게 말하다

korean: 나 요즘 번아웃 온 것 같아
english: I think I'm burning out. I need a break.
alt: I'm running on empty. I need to recharge.
note: burn out = 지치다 / recharge = 재충전하다

korean: 운동 좀 해야 하는데 귀찮아서 못하겠어
english: I know I should work out but I just can't get motivated.
alt: I need to hit the gym but I keep putting it off.
note: put off = 미루다 / hit the gym = 운동하러 가다

korean: 요즘 잠을 잘 못 자서 만성피로야
english: I haven't been sleeping well lately. I'm constantly tired.
alt: I've been having trouble sleeping and I'm running on fumes.
note: run on fumes = 거의 기력이 없는

korean: 스트레스 받으면 단 거 당기지 않아?
english: Don't you crave sweets when you're stressed?
alt: When I'm stressed all I want is junk food.
note: crave = 갈망하다/당기다

korean: 자세가 안 좋아서 허리가 아프기 시작했어
english: My back is starting to hurt because of my bad posture.
alt: I've been getting back pain from sitting wrong all day.
note: posture = 자세 / slouch = 구부정하게 앉다

korean: 그 소식 듣고 깜짝 놀랐어 전혀 예상 못 했어
english: I was completely caught off guard when I heard that.
alt: That news blindsided me. I had no idea.
note: caught off guard / blindsided = 허를 찔린

korean: 요즘 기분이 좀 울적해 이유는 모르겠는데
english: I've been feeling kind of down lately. I'm not sure why.
alt: I've been in a funk. Can't really explain it.
note: in a funk = 기분이 울적한 / feeling down = 가라앉은

korean: 걔 때문에 열 받아서 한마디 하려다 참았어
english: I was so pissed at him I almost said something but held back.
alt: He made me so mad but I bit my tongue.
note: bite one's tongue = 하고 싶은 말을 참다

korean: 걔 진짜 착하다 이런 사람 흔하지 않아
english: She's genuinely kind. You don't find people like that often.
alt: She's one of a kind. Such a rare soul.
note: one of a kind = 독보적인/유일무이한

korean: 좋은 일에는 항상 나쁜 면도 있는 법이야
english: Every good thing has its downsides. That's just how it goes.
alt: Nothing's perfect. There's always a catch.
note: there's a catch = 함정/조건이 있다

korean: 어차피 된 거 최대한 즐기자
english: Since we're already here might as well make the most of it.
alt: We're in this now. Let's just enjoy the ride.
note: make the most of it = 최대한 활용하다/즐기다

korean: 내가 니 입장이었어도 똑같이 했을 거야
english: If I were in your shoes I would've done the same thing.
alt: I totally would've reacted the same way.
note: in your shoes = 네 입장이라면

korean: 걔한테 한 번 더 기회를 줘봐
english: Give him another chance. People can change.
alt: Maybe cut him some slack and see how it goes.
note: cut someone some slack = 봐주다/너그럽게 대하다

korean: 빌려간 돈 언제 갚을 거야? 좀 부담스러워
english: When are you planning to pay me back? It's getting a bit awkward.
alt: Hey about that money I lent you — any idea when you can return it?
note: pay back = 갚다 / lent = 빌려준 (lend의 과거)

korean: 얼마 안 남았어 조금만 더 힘내자
english: We're almost there. Just a little more, hang in there.
alt: The finish line is right there. Keep pushing.
note: keep pushing = 계속 밀고 나가다

korean: 그거 완전 내 잘못이야 변명 안 할게
english: That's totally on me. No excuses.
alt: I'll own it. That was my bad.
note: on me = 내 잘못 / own it = 책임지다

korean: 너무 무리하지 마 건강이 먼저야
english: Don't push yourself too hard. Your health comes first.
alt: Take it easy. Nothing's worth ruining your health over.
note: push yourself = 무리하다

korean: 마음 편하게 먹어 다 잘 될 거야
english: Don't stress about it. Everything's gonna work out.
alt: Relax. It'll all sort itself out in the end.
note: work out / sort itself out = 잘 풀리다

korean: 내가 뭘 도와줄 수 있는 거 있어?
english: Is there anything I can do to help?
alt: What can I do for you? Just name it.
note: Just name it = 뭐든 말해

korean: 그 얘기 어디서 들었어? 소문 진짜 빠르다
english: Where did you hear that? Word travels fast.
alt: How do you already know about that? News spreads quick.
note: word travels fast = 소문이 빠르다

korean: 아까 무례하게 한 건 아닌데 기분 나빴으면 미안
english: I didn't mean to come off rude earlier. Sorry if it came across that way.
alt: I hope I didn't offend you. That wasn't my intention.
note: come off = ~처럼 보이다 / come across = ~로 받아들여지다

korean: 그거 생각보다 훨씬 쉬웠어 걱정 괜히 했네
english: That was way easier than I expected. I was worried for nothing.
alt: I overthought it. Turns out it was a breeze.
note: worried for nothing = 쓸데없이 걱정한 / breeze = 쉬운 일

korean: 이거 어떻게 하는 건지 좀 알려줄 수 있어?
english: Can you show me how to do this? I'm a bit lost.
alt: Could you walk me through it? I'm not sure where to start.
note: walk me through = 차근차근 알려줘

korean: 내가 잘못 이해한 거면 말해줘
english: Correct me if I'm wrong.
alt: Let me know if I'm off base here.
note: off base = 틀린/빗나간

korean: 그거 듣고 보니까 일리가 있네
english: Now that you mention it that actually makes sense.
alt: Huh I never thought about it that way. Good point.
note: now that you mention it = 네가 말하니까 생각해보니

korean: 아 진짜 몰랐어 알려줘서 고마워
english: I had no idea. Thanks for letting me know.
alt: I didn't know that. Good to know.
note: Good to know = 알아두면 좋은/알려줘서 고마워

korean: 메모해둘게 안 까먹게
english: Let me write that down so I don't forget.
alt: I'll jot it down real quick.
note: jot down = 빠르게 적다

korean: 비유적으로 말한 거야 진짜 그런 건 아니야
english: I was speaking figuratively. I didn't mean it literally.
alt: That was just a figure of speech. Don't take it literally.
note: figure of speech = 비유적 표현

korean: 갑자기 생각이 안 나 아 진짜 답답해
english: My mind just went blank. Ugh this is so frustrating.
alt: I'm drawing a blank. It's right there but I can't remember.
note: go blank / draw a blank = 머리가 하얘지다

korean: 조용히 좀 해줘 집중 좀 하게
english: Can you keep it down? I'm trying to focus.
alt: Could you pipe down? I really need to concentrate.
note: keep it down / pipe down = 조용히 하다

korean: 여기 분위기 진짜 좋다 다음에 또 오자
english: I love the vibe here. We should come back.
alt: This place has such a great atmosphere. Let's make this our spot.
note: vibe = 분위기 / make this our spot = 단골로 삼자

korean: 아 배고파 아무거나 빨리 먹자
english: I'm starving. Let's just grab something quick.
alt: I'm so hungry I could eat anything. Let's go.
note: starving = 매우 배고픈 / grab something = 빨리 먹다

korean: 거스름돈 됐어요 잔돈은 가지세요
english: Keep the change, thanks.
alt: No need for change. That's for you.
note: Keep the change = 거스름돈은 됐어요 (팁 개념)

korean: 이거 리필 되나요?
english: Can I get a refill on this?
alt: Is this free refills or just one?
note: free refills = 무료 리필

korean: 너 요즘 뭔가 달라 보여 좋은 일 있어?
english: You seem different lately. Did something good happen?
alt: There's something about you. What's going on?
note: You seem + 형용사 = ~해 보여

korean: 그건 오해야 내가 그런 뜻으로 말한 게 아니야
english: That's a misunderstanding. That's not what I meant at all.
alt: You're taking it the wrong way. Let me clarify.
note: take it the wrong way = 잘못 받아들이다

korean: 나 원래 아침형 인간이 아니라서 오전에는 좀 힘들어
english: I'm not a morning person so mornings are rough for me.
alt: I don't function well in the morning. I need time to wake up.
note: morning person = 아침형 인간 / night owl = 올빼미형

korean: 쓸데없는 걱정 좀 그만해 아직 일어나지도 않은 일이잖아
english: Stop overthinking. It hasn't even happened yet.
alt: You're worrying about something that might never happen. Chill.
note: overthink = 너무 많이 생각하다

korean: 그 사람이랑 나 케미가 잘 맞아
english: We really click. Our chemistry is amazing.
alt: We hit it off right away. We just vibe.
note: click = 죽이 맞다 / hit it off = 처음부터 잘 통하다

korean: 아 맞다 그거 내가 아까 하려다 말았는데
english: Oh that reminds me. I started doing that earlier but got sidetracked.
alt: Oh right. I meant to finish that but I got distracted.
note: get sidetracked / get distracted = 딴 데로 새다

korean: 이건 딱 봐도 사기 같은데 조심해
english: This screams scam. Be careful.
alt: That looks super sketchy. I wouldn't trust it.
note: scam = 사기 / sketchy = 수상한

korean: 결제 완료됐는지 확인 좀 해줘
english: Can you check if the payment went through?
alt: Did the transaction go through? I want to make sure.
note: go through = 처리되다/완료되다

korean: 자리 좀 옮겨도 될까요? 여기 에어컨 바람이 너무 세서
english: Would you mind if I moved to a different table? The AC is blowing right on me.
alt: Can I switch tables? It's freezing right here.
note: blow right on me = 바람이 바로 나한테 오는

korean: 그 사람 이름은 기억나는데 얼굴이 안 떠올라
english: I remember his name but I can't picture his face.
alt: The name rings a bell but I can't place the face.
note: ring a bell = 어디서 들어본 것 같은 / place = 기억해내다

korean: 그거 한 번 써보고 별로면 반품하면 되지
english: Just try it out and return it if you don't like it.
alt: Give it a test run. You can always bring it back.
note: test run = 시험 사용 / bring it back = 반품하다

korean: 요즘 물가가 미쳤어 장보기도 부담스러워
english: Prices are insane these days. Even grocery shopping is getting expensive.
alt: Everything's so pricey now. The cost of living is no joke.
note: cost of living = 생활비 / no joke = 장난 아닌

korean: 그냥 직감적으로 안 맞는 것 같아 합리적인 이유는 없는데
english: I don't have a logical reason. It just doesn't feel right.
alt: My gut is telling me no. I can't explain why.
note: go with your gut = 직감을 따르다

korean: 이제 슬슬 가봐야 할 것 같아 내일 일찍 일어나야 해서
english: I should probably get going. I have an early morning tomorrow.
alt: I'd love to stay but I gotta head out. Early start tomorrow.
note: I should get going이 가장 자연스러운 퇴장 표현

korean: 걔 진짜 성격 좋아 누구한테든 친절해
english: She's the nicest person ever. She's kind to literally everyone.
alt: She's a sweetheart. Never has a bad word about anyone.
note: sweetheart = 정말 착한 사람

korean: 몸은 좀 어때? 지난번에 아프다고 했잖아
english: How are you feeling? You mentioned you were sick last time.
alt: Are you doing better? I've been worried about you.
note: I've been worried about you = 걱정했어

korean: 주말에 같이 드라이브 갈래? 어디 좋은 데 없나
english: Want to go for a drive this weekend? Know any good spots?
alt: Feel like taking a road trip this weekend? Any places in mind?
note: go for a drive / road trip = 드라이브

korean: 아 그거 원래 그래 처음에는 다 어색해
english: That's totally normal. It's always awkward at first.
alt: Don't worry about it. Everyone feels that way in the beginning.
note: That's totally normal = 그거 완전 정상이야

korean: 뭐 하나 부탁해도 될까? 큰 건 아닌데
english: Can I ask you for a small favor? It's nothing major.
alt: Hey would you mind doing me a quick favor?
note: nothing major = 큰 건 아닌

korean: 앱이 자꾸 튕겨서 삭제하고 다시 깔았어
english: The app keeps crashing so I deleted it and reinstalled.
alt: It kept glitching out so I did a fresh install.
note: crash = 튕기다/멈추다 / glitch = 오류

korean: 우리 언제 한번 제대로 모이자 오래됐다
english: We should all get together properly. It's been too long.
alt: Let's plan a real hangout. We're way overdue.
note: get together = 모이다 / overdue = 한참 지난

korean: 그건 확실히 장단점이 있어 잘 따져봐야 해
english: That definitely has its pros and cons. You should weigh it carefully.
alt: There are upsides and downsides. Think it through before deciding.
note: pros and cons = 장단점 / think it through = 꼼꼼히 생각하다

korean: 나 방금 뭐 하려고 했는지 까먹었어
english: I just totally blanked on what I was about to do.
alt: Wait what was I doing? I completely lost my train of thought.
note: lose one's train of thought = 생각의 흐름을 잃다

korean: 그 식당 예약 힘들어 최소 2주 전에 해야 돼
english: That restaurant is hard to get into. You need to book at least two weeks out.
alt: Good luck getting a table there. It's always booked solid.
note: booked solid = 예약이 꽉 찬

korean: 아 진짜 타이밍 좋다 딱 너 얘기하고 있었어
english: Perfect timing! We were just talking about you.
alt: Speak of the devil! We literally just mentioned you.
note: speak of the devil = 호랑이도 제 말 하면 온다

korean: 이 정도면 충분하지 않아? 더 뭘 바라는 거야
english: Isn't this enough? What more do you want?
alt: I think this is more than sufficient. What else do you need?
note: more than sufficient = 충분하고도 남는

korean: 솔직히 기대 안 했는데 생각보다 괜찮네
english: I honestly wasn't expecting much but it's actually pretty good.
alt: I had low expectations but I'm pleasantly surprised.
note: pleasantly surprised = 좋은 쪽으로 놀란

korean: 알겠어 그러면 그렇게 하자 더 이상 안 따질게
english: Fine let's just go with that. I won't argue anymore.
alt: Alright you win. Let's do it your way.
note: go with that = 그걸로 하자 / you win = 네가 이겼어

korean: 그거 내 버킷리스트에 있어 죽기 전에 꼭 해보고 싶어
english: That's on my bucket list. I want to do it before I die.
alt: I've always dreamed of doing that. It's a must before I go.
note: bucket list = 죽기 전에 하고 싶은 것 목록

korean: 이거 진짜 중독성 있어 한 번 시작하면 멈출 수가 없어
english: This is seriously addictive. Once you start you can't stop.
alt: I'm hooked. I literally can't put it down.
note: hooked = 빠진/중독된 / can't put it down = 손에서 놓을 수 없는

korean: 됐어 이제 그만 얘기하자 우리 둘 다 지쳤잖아
english: Let's call it. We're both exhausted and going nowhere.
alt: I think we've beaten this to death. Let's move on.
note: beat to death = 질리도록 반복하다

korean: 요즘 너무 바빠서 밥 먹을 시간도 없어
english: I've been so swamped lately I barely have time to eat.
alt: I'm running on fumes with how busy things have been.
note: swamped = 일에 파묻힌 / running on fumes = 기력이 다한

korean: 그 사람 말 듣지 마 맨날 뒷담화만 해
english: Don't listen to that person. They're always talking behind people's backs.
alt: Ignore them. All they do is gossip.
note: talk behind someone's back = 뒷담화하다

korean: 너 요즘 뭐 하고 지내? 오랜만이다
english: What have you been up to? It's been forever!
alt: Long time no see! How's life been treating you?
note: How's life been treating you = 잘 지냈어?

korean: 솔직히 나 그때 좀 서운했어
english: Honestly I was kind of hurt when that happened.
alt: Not gonna lie that actually stung a little.
note: stung = 찔렸다/서운했다

korean: 너무 예민하게 반응하지 마 별거 아니잖아
english: Don't be so sensitive about it. It's really not a big deal.
alt: You're reading too much into it. Relax.
note: read too much into = 지나치게 해석하다

korean: 이거 유통기한 지났는데 먹어도 되나?
english: This is past the expiration date. Is it still safe to eat?
alt: Can I still eat this? It expired like two days ago.
note: expiration date = 유통기한 / best before도 같은 의미

korean: 좀 쉬어 번아웃 오기 전에
english: Take a break before you burn out.
alt: You need to slow down before you hit a wall.
note: burn out = 지치다 / hit a wall = 한계에 부딪히다

korean: 어제 밤새서 오늘 좀비 같아
english: I pulled an all-nighter and now I'm a walking zombie.
alt: I didn't sleep at all last night. I'm dead on my feet.
note: pull an all-nighter = 밤새다

korean: 그 영화 봤어? 반전이 미쳤어
english: Have you seen that movie? The plot twist was insane.
alt: Did you watch that film? I did NOT see that ending coming.
note: plot twist = 반전 / didn't see it coming = 예상 못했다

korean: 나 원래 아침형 인간 아니야 일찍 일어나는 게 죽을 맛이야
english: I'm not a morning person at all. Waking up early kills me.
alt: Early mornings are absolute torture for me.
note: morning person ↔ night owl

korean: 우리 진짜 잘 맞는 것 같아 코드가 맞다고 해야 하나
english: I think we really click. We're totally on the same wavelength.
alt: We just vibe so well together. It's like we get each other.
note: click = 잘 맞다 / on the same wavelength = 파장이 같다

korean: 호랑이도 제 말 하면 온다더니
english: Speak of the devil! We were just talking about you.
alt: Well well well look who showed up. We were just mentioning you.
note: speak of the devil = 호랑이도 제 말 하면 온다

korean: 너 요즘 달라진 것 같아 무슨 일 있어?
english: You seem different lately. Is everything okay?
alt: Something's off with you recently. What's going on?
note: something's off = 뭔가 이상하다

korean: 잠깐만 화장실 좀 다녀올게
english: Give me a sec. I need to hit the restroom.
alt: Hold on let me use the bathroom real quick.
note: hit the restroom = 화장실 가다 (캐주얼)

korean: 나 그거 진짜 관심 없어 제발 그만 얘기해
english: I literally could not care less. Please stop bringing it up.
alt: I have zero interest in that. Can we change the subject?
note: could not care less = 진심으로 관심 없다

korean: 이번 주말에 뭐 할 거야? 같이 뭐 하자
english: What are you doing this weekend? Let's hang out.
alt: Got any plans this weekend? We should do something.
note: hang out = 놀다 / do something = 뭐 하다

korean: 오늘 컨디션이 영 아니야 집에서 쉬고 싶어
english: I'm not feeling it today. I just want to stay home and rest.
alt: I'm totally off today. I need a day in.
note: not feeling it = 기분/컨디션 안 좋다

korean: 그 식당 웨이팅이 엄청 길어 예약하고 가야 해
english: That restaurant has a crazy long wait. You need a reservation.
alt: The wait at that place is insane. Book ahead or don't bother.
note: book ahead = 미리 예약하다

korean: 너 진짜 눈치 없다 분위기 좀 읽어
english: You're so oblivious. Read the room.
alt: How can you be so clueless? Pick up on the vibe.
note: read the room = 분위기 파악하다

korean: 이거 한번 써봐 인생템이야 진짜
english: You have to try this. It's a total game changer.
alt: Seriously give this a try. It's life-changing.
note: game changer = 판도를 바꾸는 것

korean: 나 지금 멘탈 나갔어 아무것도 하기 싫어
english: I'm mentally checked out right now. I can't do anything.
alt: My brain is fried. I have absolutely no motivation.
note: checked out = 정신적으로 이탈 / brain is fried = 머리 터짐

korean: 주말에 넷플릭스나 보면서 쉬자
english: Let's just Netflix and chill this weekend.
alt: How about we binge-watch something and take it easy?
note: binge-watch = 몰아보다

korean: 내가 뭐라 그랬어 그럴 줄 알았다니까
english: What did I tell you? I knew this would happen.
alt: Called it! I told you so.
note: I told you so = 내가 뭐랬어

korean: 진짜 답답하다 말이 안 통하네
english: This is so frustrating. I feel like I'm talking to a wall.
alt: I can't get through to them. It's like talking to a brick wall.
note: talking to a wall/brick wall = 벽에 대고 말하는 느낌

korean: 이 카페 분위기 진짜 좋다 인스타 감성이야
english: This cafe has such a vibe. It's totally Instagram-worthy.
alt: The aesthetic here is amazing. Super Instagrammable.
note: Instagrammable/Instagram-worthy = 인스타 감성

korean: 너한테 물어보려고 했는데 마침 잘 됐다
english: I was actually going to ask you about that. Perfect timing.
alt: Great timing! I wanted to talk to you about that.
note: perfect timing = 타이밍 좋다

korean: 한국 음식 매운 거 잘 먹어? 매운 거 추천해줄까?
english: Can you handle spicy food? Want me to recommend something hot?
alt: How's your spice tolerance? I know some fire dishes to try.
note: spice tolerance = 매운맛 내성 / fire = 엄청 매운

korean: 너무 오래 기다렸어 다음에는 좀 일찍 와
english: I've been waiting forever. Try to be on time next time.
alt: You kept me waiting way too long. Please don't be late again.
note: kept me waiting = 기다리게 했다

korean: 이거 환불 가능한가요? 사이즈가 안 맞아서요
english: Can I return this? It doesn't fit right.
alt: I'd like a refund please. The size is wrong.
note: return = 반품 / refund = 환불 / exchange = 교환

korean: 그 사람 첫인상은 좀 차가운데 알고 보면 따뜻해
english: They come off as cold at first but they're actually really warm once you get to know them.
alt: First impressions can be deceiving. They're a total sweetheart.
note: come off as = ~한 인상을 주다

korean: 요즘 물가가 미쳤어 장보러 가면 10만원이 금방이야
english: Prices are insane these days. You drop a hundred bucks at the grocery store without even trying.
alt: Inflation is no joke. A simple grocery run costs a fortune now.
note: drop = (돈을) 쓰다 / cost a fortune = 엄청 비싸다

korean: 와이파이 비번 뭐야? 데이터가 안 터져
english: What's the Wi-Fi password? I can't get any signal.
alt: Can I get the Wi-Fi? My data isn't working here.
note: signal = 신호 / data = 모바일 데이터

korean: 충전기 좀 빌려줄 수 있어? 배터리 1%야
english: Can I borrow your charger? My battery's at one percent.
alt: I'm about to die. Do you have a charger I can use?
note: I'm about to die = 배터리 거의 다 됐어 (구어)

korean: 우리 사이에 비밀 없잖아 솔직하게 말해봐
english: We don't keep secrets from each other. Just be straight with me.
alt: Come on we tell each other everything. Spill it.
note: be straight with me = 솔직하게 말해 / spill it = 다 말해

korean: 이 앱 진짜 유용해 꼭 깔아봐
english: This app is super useful. You should definitely download it.
alt: Seriously get this app. It's a lifesaver.
note: lifesaver = 구세주/엄청 유용한

korean: 너 이거 알아? 나만 몰랐던 거야?
english: Did you know about this? Am I the only one who didn't know?
alt: Wait everyone knew this except me?
note: Am I the only one = 나만 그런 거야?

korean: 월요일만 되면 우울해져 주말이 너무 짧아
english: Mondays always get me down. The weekend goes by way too fast.
alt: I get the Monday blues every single week. Two days off is never enough.
note: Monday blues = 월요일 우울감

korean: 그 드라마 스포하지 마 아직 안 봤어
english: Don't spoil it for me! I haven't watched it yet.
alt: No spoilers please! I'm still catching up.
note: spoil = 스포하다 / catch up = 따라잡다 (안 본 것 보다)

korean: 나 알레르기 때문에 고생 중이야 환절기만 되면 이래
english: I'm suffering from allergies. It happens every time the season changes.
alt: My allergies are acting up again. Seasonal change always gets me.
note: act up = (증상이) 도지다/심해지다

korean: 이거 맛없으면 어쩌려고? 새로운 거 시도하는 게 무서워
english: What if it tastes terrible? I'm scared to try new things.
alt: I'm nervous about trying something different. What if I hate it?
note: scared to try = 시도가 무서운

korean: 좀 급한데 빨리 해줄 수 있어요?
english: I'm in a bit of a rush. Can you speed it up?
alt: This is kind of urgent. Is there any way to get it done faster?
note: in a rush = 급한 / speed it up = 빨리 해주다

korean: 진짜 손발이 오그라들어 너무 민망해
english: That's so cringe. I can't even watch.
alt: I'm dying of secondhand embarrassment right now.
note: cringe = 오글거리는 / secondhand embarrassment = 대리 민망

korean: 에어컨 좀 세게 틀어줄래? 너무 더워서 죽겠어
english: Can you crank up the AC? I'm dying of heat.
alt: Turn up the air conditioning please. I'm melting in here.
note: crank up = 세게 틀다 / melting = 녹는 것 같은

korean: 어제 술을 너무 많이 마셔서 숙취가 심해
english: I drank way too much last night and my hangover is killing me.
alt: I'm so hungover from last night. Never drinking again.
note: hangover = 숙취 / killing me = 죽을 것 같다

korean: 너 진짜 대단하다 나라면 절대 못 했을 거야
english: That's seriously impressive. I could never do that.
alt: You're amazing. There's no way I could have pulled that off.
note: pull that off = 해내다

korean: 우리 언제 한번 모여서 고기 구워 먹자
english: We should all get together and have a barbecue sometime.
alt: Let's plan a cookout with the whole gang soon.
note: get together = 모이다 / the whole gang = 다 같이

korean: 이거 포장해주실 수 있어요? 가져가려고요
english: Can I get this to go, please?
alt: Could you box this up? I'd like to take it with me.
note: to go = 포장 / box up = 포장하다

korean: 미안한데 지금 통화하기 좀 곤란해 나중에 다시 전화할게
english: Sorry but I can't talk right now. I'll call you back later.
alt: Bad timing. Let me hit you back in a bit.
note: call you back = 다시 전화하다 / hit you back = 나중에 연락하다

korean: 그 사람이랑은 사이가 좀 애매해 친구도 아니고 그렇다고 안 친한 것도 아니고
english: We're in this weird gray area. Not exactly friends but not strangers either.
alt: It's complicated with them. We're somewhere between acquaintances and friends.
note: gray area = 애매한 영역

korean: 나 요즘 운동 시작했어 살 좀 빼야 할 것 같아서
english: I started working out recently. I really need to lose some weight.
alt: I've been hitting the gym lately. Trying to shed a few pounds.
note: hit the gym = 헬스장 가다 / shed pounds = 살 빼다

korean: 이거 레시피 좀 알려줘 진짜 맛있다
english: Can you share the recipe? This is absolutely delicious.
alt: This is so good! How do you make it?
note: share the recipe = 레시피 알려주다

korean: 이게 말이 돼? 누가 봐도 이상하잖아
english: How does this even make sense? Anyone can see something's off.
alt: This doesn't add up at all. It's obviously wrong.
note: doesn't add up = 앞뒤가 안 맞다

korean: 그때 그 선택을 안 했으면 어땠을까 가끔 생각나
english: I sometimes wonder what would've happened if I'd made a different choice back then.
alt: I catch myself thinking about the road not taken from time to time.
note: the road not taken = 선택하지 않은 길 (시적 표현)

korean: 좀 깎아주세요 두 개 살 테니까
english: Can you give me a discount if I buy two?
alt: I'll take two. Any chance you can knock the price down a bit?
note: knock down the price = 가격을 깎다

korean: 이거 다른 색상도 있나요? 파란색 있으면 보여주세요
english: Do you have this in other colors? I'd love to see it in blue.
alt: Is this available in blue? Can I take a look?
note: available in = ~로 있는/구할 수 있는

korean: 나 이번 달 카드값 보고 혼절할 뻔했어
english: I nearly passed out when I saw my credit card bill this month.
alt: My credit card statement this month almost gave me a heart attack.
note: passed out = 기절하다 / heart attack = 심장마비 (과장)

korean: 이 근처에 맛집 추천해줄 수 있어요?
english: Can you recommend any good restaurants around here?
alt: Know any good spots to eat nearby?
note: good spots = 맛집/좋은 곳

korean: 나 점심에 뭐 먹을지 매일 고민이야 선택장애야
english: I can never decide what to eat for lunch. I'm so indecisive.
alt: Picking what to eat for lunch is my daily struggle.
note: indecisive = 우유부단한 / daily struggle = 매일의 고민

korean: 거짓말하지 마 눈 보면 다 알어
english: Don't lie to me. I can tell just by looking at your eyes.
alt: You're a terrible liar. Your face gives it away.
note: gives it away = 티가 나다

korean: 내 폰 어디 갔지? 방금까지 여기 있었는데
english: Where did my phone go? It was right here a second ago.
alt: I just had my phone. Where the heck did it go?
note: where the heck = 도대체 어디에

korean: 너 오늘 되게 신나 보인다 무슨 좋은 일 있어?
english: You look really excited today. Something good happen?
alt: You're in a great mood. What's the occasion?
note: what's the occasion = 무슨 좋은 일이야

korean: 이거 세일 언제까지예요? 좀 더 생각해보고 살게요
english: When does this sale end? I want to think about it a little more.
alt: How long is this promotion running? I might come back for it.
note: promotion = 프로모션/세일

korean: 머리가 지끈지끈해 두통약 좀 있어?
english: I have a splitting headache. Do you have any painkillers?
alt: My head is pounding. Got any aspirin or something?
note: splitting headache = 쪼개지는 듯한 두통 / pounding = 쿵쿵거리는

korean: 너 코로나 걸린 거 아니야? 기침을 너무 심하게 하는데
english: Are you sure you don't have COVID? That cough sounds really bad.
alt: That cough is serious. Maybe you should get tested.
note: get tested = 검사받다

korean: 택배 아직 안 왔어? 분명 오늘 도착인데
english: Has the package not arrived yet? It was supposed to come today.
alt: My delivery was supposed to be here today. Still nothing.
note: supposed to = ~하기로 되어 있는

korean: 그 식당 예약 취소해줘 갑자기 일이 생겼어
english: Cancel the restaurant reservation please. Something came up.
alt: Can you call off the dinner reservation? I got pulled into something.
note: something came up = 갑자기 일이 생겼다

korean: 너 피부 진짜 좋다 뭐 발라?
english: Your skin looks amazing. What's your secret?
alt: You're literally glowing. What skincare do you use?
note: glowing = 빛나는 / skincare = 스킨케어

korean: 아침에 커피 안 마시면 사람이 안 돼
english: I can't function without my morning coffee.
alt: I'm not human until I've had my first cup of coffee.
note: can't function = 제 기능을 못 하다

korean: 진짜 꿈만 같다 아직도 실감이 안 나
english: It feels like a dream. I still can't believe it's real.
alt: This is so surreal. I keep pinching myself.
note: surreal = 비현실적인 / pinching myself = 꿈인지 확인

korean: 신발끈 풀렸어 잠깐만 묶을게
english: My shoelace is untied. Hold on let me tie it.
alt: Hang on a sec. My shoe came undone.
note: untied/came undone = 풀린

korean: 그래 그 정도면 됐어 더 이상은 욕심이지
english: That's good enough. Anything more would be greedy.
alt: I'm satisfied with that. No need to push it further.
note: good enough = 충분한 / push it = 무리하다

korean: 그건 네 생각이고 내 생각은 달라
english: That's your opinion and I respectfully disagree.
alt: You're entitled to your view but I see it differently.
note: respectfully disagree = 정중히 반대하다

korean: 선크림 발랐어? 오늘 자외선 지수 높대
english: Did you put on sunscreen? The UV index is really high today.
alt: Don't forget sunscreen. You'll burn in no time today.
note: UV index = 자외선 지수 / burn = 타다 (피부가)

korean: 엘리베이터 고장 났어 계단으로 가야 해
english: The elevator is out of order. We have to take the stairs.
alt: Elevator's broken. Looks like we're walking up.
note: out of order = 고장난

korean: 이거 선물 포장 해주실 수 있나요?
english: Can you gift wrap this for me please?
alt: Could I get this gift wrapped?
note: gift wrap = 선물 포장하다

korean: 이 옷 세탁기 돌려도 돼? 손세탁해야 해?
english: Can I throw this in the washing machine or does it need to be hand washed?
alt: Is this machine washable or hand wash only?
note: machine washable = 세탁기 가능 / hand wash = 손세탁

korean: 요즘 잠을 못 자서 눈 밑에 다크서클 장난 아니야
english: I haven't been sleeping well and the dark circles under my eyes are no joke.
alt: I've got crazy bags under my eyes from lack of sleep.
note: dark circles = 다크서클 / bags under eyes = 눈 밑 부기

korean: 문 잠갔어? 아까 나올 때 확인했어?
english: Did you lock the door? Did you check when we left?
alt: Wait did we lock up? I can't remember.
note: lock up = 문 잠그다

korean: 되게 오랜만에 책 읽었더니 기분이 새롭네
english: I read a book for the first time in ages and it felt so refreshing.
alt: I finally picked up a book after forever and it was such a nice change.
note: for the first time in ages = 정말 오랜만에

korean: 나한테 왜 화를 내? 내가 뭘 잘못했는데?
english: Why are you mad at me? What did I even do?
alt: What are you so upset about? I don't understand what I did wrong.
note: mad at = ~에게 화난

korean: 피곤한데 좀만 더 힘내자 거의 다 왔어
english: I'm tired too but let's push through. We're almost there.
alt: Hang in there. We're so close to the finish line.
note: push through = 힘내서 해내다 / hang in there = 버텨

korean: 이거 사진이랑 실물이 너무 다른데 기대했는데 실망이야
english: This looks nothing like the photos. I'm so disappointed.
alt: The product doesn't match the pictures at all. Total letdown.
note: looks nothing like = 전혀 안 닮은 / letdown = 실망

korean: 오랜만에 부모님한테 전화 좀 해 걱정하실 거야
english: You should call your parents. It's been a while and they're probably worried.
alt: Give your folks a ring. They'd love to hear from you.
note: give a ring = 전화하다 / folks = 부모님 (구어)

korean: 지금 밖에 비 오고 있어 우산 챙겨
english: It's raining outside right now. Don't forget your umbrella.
alt: Heads up it's pouring out there. Grab an umbrella.
note: heads up = 주의해 / pouring = 폭우

korean: 거기 몇 시에 문 닫아? 우리 시간 되겠어?
english: What time do they close? Will we make it in time?
alt: When are they open until? Think we can get there before closing?
note: make it in time = 시간 안에 도착하다

korean: 나 지금 다이어트 중이라 탄수화물 줄이고 있어
english: I'm on a diet right now so I'm cutting back on carbs.
alt: I'm trying to watch my carb intake these days.
note: cut back on = 줄이다 / watch intake = 섭취를 조절하다

korean: 이 사이즈 재고 있나요? 여기 있는 건 다 너무 큰데
english: Do you have this in a smaller size? Everything here is too big.
alt: Is this available in small? These all run large.
note: run large = (옷이) 크게 나오다

korean: 그러게 내 말이 맞다니까 처음부터 내 말 들었으면 됐잖아
english: See I was right all along. You should've listened to me from the start.
alt: If you'd taken my advice from the beginning we wouldn't be in this mess.
note: all along = 처음부터 계속 / in this mess = 이 난리

korean: 이거 어디서 산 거야? 나도 하나 사고 싶어
english: Where did you get this? I want one too.
alt: I love that! Where can I find one?
note: where can I find one = 어디서 살 수 있어?

korean: 다 됐어? 빨리 가야 하는데 시간 없어
english: Are you done yet? We need to hurry. We're running out of time.
alt: You ready? We gotta go like now. We're behind schedule.
note: running out of time = 시간이 없다 / behind schedule = 일정보다 늦는

korean: 저 사람 말투가 좀 그래 은근 무례한 것 같아
english: That person's tone is a bit off. They're kind of rude without realizing it.
alt: Their way of speaking is lowkey disrespectful.
note: lowkey = 은근히 / tone = 말투

korean: 긴장돼 죽겠어 심장이 터질 것 같아
english: I'm so nervous I could die. My heart feels like it's going to burst.
alt: My anxiety is through the roof right now.
note: through the roof = 천장을 뚫을 정도로 / burst = 터지다

korean: 이번 달 카드 한도 초과했어 어떡하지
english: I maxed out my credit card this month. What do I do?
alt: I went over my credit limit. This is bad.
note: max out = 한도 초과 / go over the limit = 한도를 넘다

korean: 이제 그만 미루고 시작하자 더 미루면 진짜 큰일 나
english: Let's stop procrastinating and just start. If we keep putting it off we're screwed.
alt: No more excuses. We need to get on this now or we're in trouble.
note: procrastinate = 미루다 / screwed = 망한 (속어)

korean: 그 영상 봤어? 조회수가 천만이 넘었대
english: Did you see that video? It got over ten million views.
alt: Have you watched that clip? It went totally viral.
note: go viral = 바이럴되다/입소문 타다

korean: 미안한데 난 여기서 빠질게 다음에 또 불러줘
english: Sorry but I'm going to head out. Hit me up next time.
alt: I gotta bounce. Let's do this again though.
note: head out / bounce = 자리를 뜨다 / hit me up = 연락해

korean: 이거 마일리지로 결제 가능한가요?
english: Can I pay for this with my points?
alt: Is it possible to use my reward points for this?
note: reward points = 마일리지/포인트

korean: 저번에 갔던 곳 이름이 뭐였지? 기억이 안 나
english: What was the name of that place we went to last time? I can't remember.
alt: I totally blanked on the name of that spot we visited.
note: blanked on = 기억이 안 나다

korean: 직접 만든 거야? 진짜 대단하다 나는 손재주가 없어서
english: You made this yourself? That's incredible. I have zero crafting skills.
alt: This is handmade? Wow I'm not talented enough to pull something like this off.
note: handmade = 수제/직접 만든 / crafting skills = 손재주

korean: 우리 사이가 왜 이렇게 됐을까 예전에는 안 이랬는데
english: How did things get like this between us? We used to be so close.
alt: What happened to us? Things were never this awkward before.
note: how did things get like this = 어쩌다 이렇게 됐을까

korean: 너무 많이 샀어 지름신이 왔나 봐
english: I bought way too much. I think I went on a shopping spree.
alt: I totally splurged. My wallet is crying right now.
note: shopping spree = 쇼핑 폭주 / splurge = 돈을 펑펑 쓰다

korean: 나 지금 배 아파서 화장실 자주 가는 중이야
english: My stomach is killing me. I keep running to the bathroom.
alt: I've got an upset stomach and I can't stop going to the restroom.
note: upset stomach = 배탈 / stomach is killing me = 배가 죽겠다

korean: 이 브랜드 한국에서 되게 유행이야 너도 들어봤어?
english: This brand is super popular in Korea. Have you heard of it?
alt: Everyone in Korea is obsessed with this brand right now.
note: obsessed with = ~에 빠진

korean: 처음 보는 사람한테 말 걸기가 너무 어려워
english: I find it so hard to approach strangers and start a conversation.
alt: I'm terrible at making small talk with people I don't know.
note: approach = 다가가다 / small talk = 가벼운 대화

korean: 그래서 결국 어떻게 됐어? 결말이 궁금해 죽겠어
english: So what happened in the end? The suspense is killing me.
alt: I need to know how it ended. Don't leave me hanging.
note: suspense is killing me = 궁금해 죽겠어 / leave me hanging = 궁금하게 하다

korean: 나 이제 진짜 끊을 거야 담배도 술도
english: I'm quitting for real this time. No more cigarettes and no more drinking.
alt: I'm going cold turkey on smoking and drinking starting now.
note: go cold turkey = 갑자기 완전히 끊다

korean: 나 오늘 하루 종일 아무것도 안 했어 죄책감 들어
english: I did absolutely nothing all day and I feel so guilty about it.
alt: I wasted the entire day and now the guilt is eating me alive.
note: guilt is eating me alive = 죄책감에 시달리다

korean: 이 앱 업데이트 하고 나서 자꾸 튕겨
english: This app keeps crashing ever since the last update.
alt: After the update this thing is so buggy. It crashes every five minutes.
note: crash = 튕기다/충돌 / buggy = 버그 많은

korean: 오늘 날씨 진짜 좋다 이런 날 실내에 있으면 아까워
english: The weather is gorgeous today. It'd be a shame to stay indoors.
alt: It's too beautiful outside to be stuck inside.
note: it'd be a shame = 아까울 것이다

korean: 나 이 노래 요즘 미친 듯이 듣고 있어 중독성 있어
english: I've had this song on repeat nonstop. It's so catchy.
alt: I've been listening to this song obsessively. I can't get it out of my head.
note: on repeat = 반복 재생 / catchy = 중독성 있는

korean: 이건 말도 안 되는 가격이야 바가지 쓴 것 같아
english: This price is outrageous. I think I got ripped off.
alt: They totally overcharged me. What a scam.
note: ripped off = 바가지 쓴 / scam = 사기

korean: 냉장고에 먹을 게 하나도 없어 장 보러 가야겠다
english: There's literally nothing in the fridge. I need to go grocery shopping.
alt: The fridge is empty. Time for a grocery run.
note: grocery shopping / grocery run = 장보기

korean: 그 사람 진짜 말을 잘해 설득력이 있어
english: That person is such a great speaker. They're really persuasive.
alt: They have a way with words. Super convincing.
note: have a way with words = 말을 잘하다 / persuasive = 설득력 있는

korean: 아 배불러 못 먹겠어 디저트는 포기할게
english: I'm so full I can't eat another bite. I'll pass on dessert.
alt: I'm stuffed. No room for dessert unfortunately.
note: stuffed = 배부른 / can't eat another bite = 한 입도 더 못 먹겠다

korean: 이건 어디서 충전할 수 있어요? 콘센트가 안 보여
english: Where can I charge this? I don't see any outlets.
alt: Is there a power outlet somewhere? I need to plug in.
note: outlet = 콘센트 / plug in = 꽂다

korean: 내 취향은 아닌데 네가 좋다면 나는 괜찮아
english: It's not really my thing but if you like it I'm cool with it.
alt: Not my cup of tea but hey to each their own.
note: not my cup of tea = 내 취향이 아닌 / to each their own = 각자 취향

korean: 와 살 빠졌다 비결이 뭐야?
english: Wow you've lost weight! What's your secret?
alt: You look so fit! What have you been doing?
note: lost weight = 살 빠진 / fit = 건강해 보이는

korean: 비밀번호가 생각이 안 나 어떡하지
english: I can't remember my password. What do I do?
alt: I totally forgot my password. This is so frustrating.
note: forgot my password = 비밀번호를 잊었다

korean: 그때 왜 아무 말 안 했어? 지금 와서 그러면 어떡해
english: Why didn't you say something then? You can't bring this up now.
alt: You should've spoken up at the time. It's too late to raise it now.
note: speak up = 의견을 말하다 / bring up = 꺼내다

korean: 와 진짜 이거 맛있다 레전드야
english: Oh my god this is amazing. This is the best thing I've ever had.
alt: This is absolutely incredible. I'm blown away.
note: blown away = 감탄한/넋이 나간

korean: 그 정보가 맞는지 한번 더 확인해봐 요즘 가짜뉴스가 많아
english: Double-check if that info is accurate. There's so much fake news these days.
alt: Verify that before you share it. Misinformation is everywhere.
note: fake news / misinformation = 가짜뉴스/허위정보

korean: 나 요즘 사람 만나기 싫어 혼자 있고 싶어
english: I don't feel like seeing anyone lately. I just want to be alone.
alt: I've been wanting my own space recently. I need some me time.
note: me time = 나만의 시간

korean: 엄마 생신 선물 뭐가 좋을까? 아이디어가 없어
english: What should I get my mom for her birthday? I'm completely out of ideas.
alt: I have no clue what to buy my mom. Any gift suggestions?
note: out of ideas = 아이디어가 없는 / no clue = 모르겠다

korean: 이거 배송비 포함인가요? 아니면 따로 내야 해요?
english: Does this include shipping? Or is that extra?
alt: Is shipping included in the price or is it charged separately?
note: shipping = 배송 / charged separately = 별도 청구

korean: 여기 엄청 붐비네 사람이 미쳤다
english: This place is packed. There are people everywhere.
alt: It's absolutely mobbed in here. I can barely move.
note: packed / mobbed = 붐비는

korean: 나도 가고 싶었는데 선약이 있어서 못 가
english: I really wanted to go but I already have plans.
alt: I wish I could make it but I'm already committed to something else.
note: have plans = 선약이 있다 / committed to = ~에 약속된

korean: 이번 생일은 조용하게 보내고 싶어 파티 안 해도 돼
english: I want a quiet birthday this year. No party needed.
alt: I'd rather keep it low-key for my birthday. Nothing big.
note: low-key = 조용한/소박한

korean: 차가 너무 막혀서 미칠 것 같아 오늘 왜 이러지
english: Traffic is insane today. I'm about to lose my mind.
alt: I'm stuck in gridlock and I have no idea why it's this bad.
note: gridlock = 극심한 교통 체증 / lose my mind = 미칠 것 같다

korean: 나 갑자기 귀찮아졌어 그냥 배달시키자
english: I suddenly don't feel like going out. Let's just order delivery.
alt: I just lost all motivation to go. Let's get food delivered instead.
note: don't feel like = ~하기 싫다 / order delivery = 배달시키다

korean: 이거 실수한 거 맞지? 일부러 그런 건 아니지?
english: This was an accident right? You didn't do it on purpose?
alt: You didn't mean to do that did you? It was just a mistake?
note: on purpose = 일부러 / by accident = 실수로

korean: 나중에 후회할 거 뻔해 지금 해
english: You're going to regret it if you don't do it now.
alt: You'll kick yourself later if you pass this up.
note: kick yourself = 자책하다 / pass up = 기회를 놓치다

korean: 오늘 일 끝나고 한잔하러 갈래? 스트레스 풀자
english: Want to grab a drink after work? Let's blow off some steam.
alt: How about some drinks tonight? I need to unwind.
note: blow off steam = 스트레스 풀다 / unwind = 긴장 풀다

korean: 아 진짜 지겨워 매일 똑같은 일상이야
english: I'm so sick of this. Every day is exactly the same.
alt: I'm bored out of my mind. My routine is so monotonous.
note: sick of = 지긋지긋한 / monotonous = 단조로운

korean: 고마워 네가 아니었으면 큰일 날 뻔했어
english: Thank you so much. I would've been in big trouble without you.
alt: You saved my life. I don't know what I would've done without you.
note: saved my life = 큰 도움이 됐다 (과장)

korean: 그럴 수도 있지 뭐 사람이니까 실수할 수 있어
english: It happens. We're only human. Everyone makes mistakes.
alt: Nobody's perfect. Don't beat yourself up over it.
note: don't beat yourself up = 자책하지 마

korean: 나 이 동네 10년째 사는데 많이 변했어
english: I've lived in this neighborhood for ten years and it's changed so much.
alt: I've been in this area for a decade. It's totally different from before.
note: decade = 10년

korean: 맛은 괜찮은데 양이 좀 적은 것 같아
english: The taste is fine but the portion is a bit small, don't you think?
alt: It's good but I could use a bigger serving.
note: portion = 양 / serving = 1인분

korean: 새벽배송 시키면 내일 아침에 올 거야
english: If I order with overnight delivery it'll be here by morning.
alt: I'll do the dawn delivery option so we get it first thing tomorrow.
note: overnight delivery = 새벽배송 / first thing = 가장 먼저

korean: 나 원래 계획형이야 즉흥적으로 못 해
english: I'm a total planner. I can't do things spontaneously.
alt: I need everything mapped out in advance. I'm terrible at winging it.
note: spontaneously = 즉흥적으로 / wing it = 즉흥으로 하다

korean: 너 그 짤 봤어? 완전 웃겨서 배 아팠어
english: Did you see that meme? I laughed so hard my stomach hurt.
alt: Have you seen that meme going around? I was dying of laughter.
note: meme = 짤/밈 / dying of laughter = 웃겨 죽는

korean: 편의점에서 간단하게 때우자 시간 없으니까
english: Let's just grab something quick from the convenience store. We don't have time.
alt: Let's do a quick convenience store run. No time for a real meal.
note: grab something quick = 빨리 뭔가 집다

korean: 여기 카드 되나요? 현금이 없어서
english: Do you take cards? I don't have any cash on me.
alt: Can I pay by card? I'm completely cashless right now.
note: take cards = 카드 받다 / cashless = 현금 없는

korean: 내가 실수한 거 인정할게 미안해
english: I'll admit I was wrong. I'm sorry.
alt: My bad. I messed up and I own it.
note: my bad = 내 잘못 / own it = 인정하다

korean: 이 노래 제목이 뭐야? 며칠째 머릿속에서 안 떠나
english: What's this song called? It's been stuck in my head for days.
alt: Do you know the title of this song? I can't get it out of my head.
note: stuck in my head = 머릿속에서 떠나지 않는

korean: 지금 온라인으로 주문하면 할인 더 되는 거야?
english: Is there an extra discount if I order online right now?
alt: Do I get a better deal if I buy it through the website?
note: extra discount = 추가 할인 / better deal = 더 나은 거래

korean: 그래 나 인정 그건 내가 틀렸어
english: Fine I admit it. I was wrong about that.
alt: Okay you were right and I was wrong. There I said it.
note: there I said it = 내가 말했다 (인정하기 어려운 걸 인정할 때)

korean: 오늘 하루 정말 길었다 빨리 집에 가서 쉬고 싶어
english: Today felt like the longest day ever. I just want to go home and rest.
alt: What an exhausting day. I can't wait to crash on my couch.
note: crash = 쓰러지다/기절하듯 쉬다

korean: 나 이거 중고로 샀어 새 거 반값이야
english: I bought this secondhand. It was half the price of a new one.
alt: I got this used for like fifty percent off retail.
note: secondhand / used = 중고 / retail = 정가

korean: 이 자리에 앉아도 되나요? 비어 있는 거 맞죠?
english: Is this seat taken? It's free right?
alt: Mind if I sit here? Nobody's using this seat?
note: is this seat taken = 이 자리 주인 있나요?

korean: 와 벚꽃 진짜 예쁘다 올해는 빨리 피었네
english: The cherry blossoms are absolutely beautiful. They bloomed early this year.
alt: Look at the cherry blossoms! They came out sooner than usual.
note: cherry blossoms = 벚꽃 / bloom = 피다

korean: 남의 일에 참견하지 마 니 할 일이나 해
english: Mind your own business. Focus on your own stuff.
alt: Stay in your lane. Nobody asked for your opinion.
note: mind your own business = 네 일이나 신경 써 / stay in your lane = 네 영역에 있어

korean: 너 진짜 효자다 부모님이 좋아하시겠다
english: You're such a good son. Your parents must be so proud.
alt: That's really sweet of you. Your folks are lucky to have you.
note: proud = 자랑스러운 / lucky to have you = 너를 둬서 행운

korean: 여기 뷔페 가격이 얼마예요? 런치랑 디너 다른가요?
english: How much is the buffet? Is there a different price for lunch and dinner?
alt: What are the buffet rates? Does it cost more for dinner?
note: buffet rates = 뷔페 가격

korean: 이거 조립하는 데 얼마나 걸려? 설명서 봐도 모르겠어
english: How long does it take to assemble this? I can't figure out the instructions.
alt: I've been staring at the manual for an hour and I'm still lost.
note: assemble = 조립하다 / manual = 설명서

korean: 걔네 쌍둥이야? 얼굴이 완전 판박이인데
english: Are they twins? They look exactly alike.
alt: They look identical. Are they actually twins?
note: identical = 완전히 같은 / alike = 닮은

korean: 나 그 소식 듣고 진짜 울 뻔했어
english: I almost cried when I heard the news.
alt: That news nearly brought me to tears.
note: brought me to tears = 눈물 나게 했다

korean: 요즘 뭐 재밌는 거 없나? 취미가 필요해
english: Is there anything fun going on lately? I need a hobby.
alt: I need something new to do. I'm looking for a hobby to pick up.
note: pick up a hobby = 취미를 시작하다

korean: 이 가격이면 차라리 새 거 사는 게 낫지 않아?
english: At this price wouldn't it be better to just buy a new one?
alt: For what they're charging you might as well get it brand new.
note: might as well = 차라리 ~하는 게 낫다 / brand new = 새 것

korean: 한 잔만 더 마시고 가자 마지막이야 진짜로
english: Just one more drink and then we'll go. I promise this is the last one.
alt: One for the road and then we're out. I mean it this time.
note: one for the road = 마지막 한 잔 / I mean it = 진심이야

korean: 오늘 기분 전환 좀 하고 싶어 어디 가자
english: I need a change of scenery today. Let's go somewhere.
alt: I want to shake things up and go out. Let's do something different.
note: change of scenery = 기분 전환 / shake things up = 변화를 주다

korean: 이건 비밀인데 아무한테도 말하면 안 돼
english: This is between you and me. Don't tell anyone.
alt: I'm telling you this in confidence. Keep it to yourself.
note: between you and me = 우리 둘 사이의 / in confidence = 비밀로

korean: 여기 화장실 어디예요? 급해서요
english: Where's the restroom? It's kind of urgent.
alt: Excuse me which way to the bathroom? I really need to go.
note: restroom / bathroom / washroom = 화장실

korean: 유튜브 알고리즘이 나를 너무 잘 알아 소름 끼쳐
english: The YouTube algorithm knows me too well. It's kind of creepy.
alt: My feed is scarily accurate. The algorithm is reading my mind.
note: algorithm = 알고리즘 / creepy = 소름 끼치는

korean: 이건 좀 과하지 않아? 적당히 하자
english: Isn't this a bit much? Let's not go overboard.
alt: We're overdoing it. Let's dial it back a notch.
note: go overboard = 과하게 하다 / dial it back = 줄이다

korean: 그거 먹으면 안 돼 유통기한 한참 지났잖아
english: Don't eat that! It expired ages ago.
alt: Throw that away. It's been expired for way too long.
note: expired = 유통기한 지난 / ages ago = 한참 전에

korean: 눈이 오니까 기분이 좋아진다 겨울 감성이야
english: It's snowing and it just puts me in a good mood. Winter vibes, you know?
alt: Something about snow makes everything feel magical.
note: winter vibes = 겨울 감성 / magical = 마법 같은

korean: 이거 내 사이즈 맞는지 모르겠어 피팅룸 어딨어?
english: I'm not sure this is my size. Where's the fitting room?
alt: I want to make sure this fits. Can I try it on somewhere?
note: fitting room = 피팅룸/탈의실

korean: 너 완전 변했다 좋은 쪽으로 변한 거야
english: You've totally changed. In a good way though.
alt: You're like a completely different person and I mean that as a compliment.
note: in a good way = 좋은 쪽으로 / compliment = 칭찬

korean: 그래 이번엔 네가 이겼어 다음에는 내가 이길 거야
english: Alright you win this time. But I'll get you next time.
alt: Fine I'll give you this one. But watch out next round.
note: I'll get you = 내가 이길 거야 / watch out = 조심해

korean: 이 향수 뭐야? 진짜 좋다 어디 꺼야?
english: What perfume are you wearing? It smells amazing. What brand is it?
alt: You smell incredible. What fragrance is that?
note: fragrance = 향수 / incredible = 놀라운

korean: 올해 목표가 뭐야? 새해 다짐 같은 거 했어?
english: What are your goals for this year? Did you make any New Year's resolutions?
alt: Any resolutions this year? I always make them and break them.
note: New Year's resolutions = 새해 다짐 / break them = 깨뜨리다

korean: 어디서 이런 걸 다 알아? 진짜 박식하다
english: How do you know all this stuff? You're like a walking encyclopedia.
alt: You're so knowledgeable. Where do you get all this information?
note: walking encyclopedia = 걸어 다니는 백과사전

korean: 나 그때 진짜 창피해서 숨고 싶었어
english: I was so embarrassed I wanted to disappear.
alt: I wished the ground would swallow me whole. That was mortifying.
note: mortifying = 창피한 / swallow me whole = 삼켜주다

korean: 배고프기는 한데 뭐가 먹고 싶은지 모르겠어
english: I'm hungry but I have no idea what I'm in the mood for.
alt: I want to eat something but nothing sounds appealing.
note: in the mood for = ~이 먹고 싶은 / appealing = 끌리는

korean: 이거 결제 카드로 할게요 할부 가능한가요?
english: I'll pay by card. Can I do installments?
alt: I'd like to put this on my card. Do you offer payment plans?
note: installments = 할부 / payment plans = 분할 납부

korean: 진짜 웃긴 게 뭔지 알아? 내가 제일 걱정한 게 아무 일도 안 일어났어
english: You know what's funny? The thing I worried about most didn't even happen.
alt: The ironic part is that everything I stressed over turned out to be nothing.
note: ironic = 아이러니한 / turned out to be nothing = 아무것도 아닌 것으로 판명

korean: 너 아직도 그 생각하고 있어? 좀 놔줘 이제
english: Are you still thinking about that? Let it go already.
alt: You need to move on. Stop dwelling on it.
note: let it go = 놓아줘 / dwell on = 곱씹다

korean: 나 커피 마시면 잠이 안 와서 오후에는 디카페인 마셔
english: Coffee keeps me up at night so I only drink decaf in the afternoon.
alt: I switch to decaf after noon otherwise I'm tossing and turning all night.
note: decaf = 디카페인 / tossing and turning = 뒤척이는

korean: 이거 라이브로 보니까 감동이 완전 다르네
english: Seeing this live hits completely different. So much more emotional in person.
alt: Nothing compares to the live experience. It's on another level.
note: hits different = 느낌이 다르다 / on another level = 차원이 다른

korean: 그래 좋은 게 좋은 거지 싸우지 말자
english: Let's just keep the peace. No point in fighting over this.
alt: Agree to disagree and move on. Fighting won't help.
note: keep the peace = 평화 유지 / agree to disagree = 의견 차이를 인정하다

korean: 집에 가는 길에 편의점 좀 들러줘
english: Can you stop by the convenience store on the way home?
alt: Swing by the store on your way back. We need a few things.
note: stop by / swing by = 들르다

korean: 걔 진짜 음식을 잘 만들어 프로 수준이야
english: They're an incredible cook. Seriously restaurant-quality stuff.
alt: Their cooking is on a professional level. No joke.
note: restaurant-quality = 식당 수준 / on a professional level = 프로 수준

korean: 이거 미리 예약 안 하면 자리 없을걸
english: If you don't book in advance there won't be any seats left.
alt: You'll definitely want to make a reservation ahead of time for this place.
note: book in advance = 미리 예약하다 / ahead of time = 사전에

korean: 왜 이렇게 핸드폰만 보고 있어 같이 있는데
english: Why are you on your phone the whole time? We're hanging out.
alt: Put your phone down. You're here with me not on Instagram.
note: the whole time = 내내 / put your phone down = 핸드폰 내려놔

korean: 나 그 얘기 들을 때마다 소름 돋아
english: I get goosebumps every time I hear that story.
alt: That story gives me chills no matter how many times I hear it.
note: goosebumps = 소름 / chills = 오싹함

korean: 이거 리뷰 좋길래 샀는데 기대 이상이야
english: I bought this because of the great reviews and it exceeded my expectations.
alt: The reviews were right. This is even better than I expected.
note: exceeded expectations = 기대를 초과한

korean: 오늘 가스비 청구서 왔는데 생각보다 많이 나왔어
english: The gas bill came today and it was way more than I expected.
alt: I got sticker shock from the utility bill this month.
note: sticker shock = (가격) 충격 / utility bill = 공과금

korean: 나 피아노 배우고 있는데 진도가 너무 안 나가
english: I'm learning piano but I'm making zero progress.
alt: I've been taking piano lessons but I feel like I'm not getting any better.
note: making progress = 진전이 있다 / getting better = 나아지는

korean: 그 넷플릭스 시리즈 정주행 했어? 시즌 3까지 있는데
english: Did you binge-watch that Netflix series? There are three seasons.
alt: Have you caught up on that show? It goes up to season three.
note: binge-watch = 정주행 / caught up = 따라잡은

korean: 운동 후에 단백질 쉐이크 마시는 거 효과 있어?
english: Does drinking a protein shake after working out actually help?
alt: Is there any real benefit to having protein right after a workout?
note: protein shake = 단백질 쉐이크 / workout = 운동
