korean: 나 요즘 명상 시작했는데 확실히 마음이 편해졌어
english: I started meditating recently and it's made a noticeable difference in my stress.
alt: Meditation has seriously helped me chill out. Should've started sooner.
note: meditating = 명상 / chill out = 진정하다

korean: 거기 와이파이 잘 터져? 노트북 가져갈 건데
english: Does that place have good Wi-Fi? I'm planning to bring my laptop.
alt: How's the internet there? I need a stable connection for work.
note: stable connection = 안정적인 연결

korean: 나 그 소식 듣고 입이 안 다물어졌어
english: My jaw literally dropped when I heard that news.
alt: I was absolutely speechless when I found out.
note: jaw dropped = 입이 벌어진 / speechless = 말문이 막힌

korean: 이거 세금 포함된 가격이야? 부가세 별도야?
english: Is this price tax-inclusive? Or is VAT extra?
alt: Does this include tax or will it be added at checkout?
note: tax-inclusive = 세금 포함 / VAT = 부가가치세

korean: 나 어릴 때 여기 자주 왔었는데 추억이 새록새록 떠오르네
english: I used to come here all the time as a kid. So many memories flooding back.
alt: This place brings back so many childhood memories.
note: flooding back = 밀려오는 / brings back memories = 추억을 떠올리게 하다

korean: 매일 같은 시간에 일어나는 게 건강에 좋대
english: They say waking up at the same time every day is good for your health.
alt: Apparently keeping a consistent wake-up time does wonders for your body.
note: consistent = 일관된 / does wonders = 놀라운 효과가 있다

korean: 이 레스토랑 미슐랭이래 가볼 만해?
english: This restaurant has a Michelin star apparently. Think it's worth trying?
alt: It's Michelin-rated. Should we give it a shot?
note: Michelin star = 미슐랭 스타 / give it a shot = 시도해보다

korean: 그 말 취소해 방금 한 말 없던 걸로 해줘
english: Take that back. Pretend I never said it.
alt: Forget what I just said. I didn't mean it.
note: take that back = 취소해 / pretend = ~인 척하다

korean: 나 원래 길게 안 자 6시간이면 충분해
english: I don't sleep much anyway. Six hours is plenty for me.
alt: I'm a short sleeper. Six hours and I'm good to go.
note: plenty = 충분한 / good to go = 준비된

korean: 이사 준비하느라 짐 싸는 중인데 끝이 안 보여
english: I'm packing for the move and there's no end in sight.
alt: Moving prep is endless. I didn't realize I had this much stuff.
note: no end in sight = 끝이 안 보이는 / moving prep = 이사 준비

korean: 뭐 이런 일이 다 있어 말도 안 돼
english: I can't believe this is happening. This is absurd.
alt: What kind of nonsense is this? This makes no sense.
note: absurd = 황당한 / nonsense = 말도 안 되는

korean: 이거 AS 기간 남아 있어? 무상 수리 되나?
english: Is this still under warranty? Can I get it repaired for free?
alt: Is the warranty still valid? I'd like to get this fixed at no cost.
note: warranty = 보증/보증기간 / at no cost = 무료로

korean: 농담이야 진지하게 받아들이지 마
english: I'm just kidding. Don't take it seriously.
alt: I was only joking. Lighten up.
note: lighten up = 가볍게 생각해 / take it seriously = 진지하게 받아들이다

korean: 어제 야식 먹었더니 오늘 속이 안 좋아
english: I ate late last night and my stomach's been off all day.
alt: I shouldn't have had that midnight snack. My stomach is paying for it.
note: stomach is paying for it = 위장이 대가를 치르는

korean: 그 사람 진짜 시간 약속을 안 지켜 매번 늦어
english: They never show up on time. They're late every single time.
alt: That person is chronically late. You can always count on them being behind.
note: chronically late = 만성적으로 늦는 / count on = ~를 확신하다

korean: 이거 인터넷에서 찾아봤는데 정보가 너무 많아서 뭘 봐야 할지 모르겠어
english: I looked it up online but there's so much info I don't know where to start.
alt: I tried researching it but the amount of information is overwhelming.
note: overwhelming = 압도적인 / looked it up = 검색했다

korean: 한번 해보기나 해 안 해보고 어떻게 알아
english: Just try it. You'll never know unless you give it a shot.
alt: How would you know if you don't even try? Just go for it.
note: give it a shot / go for it = 시도해봐

korean: 그거 다음에 하면 안 돼? 지금은 좀 힘들어
english: Can we do that later? I'm not up for it right now.
alt: Rain check on that? I don't have the energy at the moment.
note: rain check = 다음으로 미루다 / not up for it = 할 기분이 아닌

korean: 이 건물 몇 층이야? 헷갈려서 매번 잘못 가
english: What floor is it on? I always get confused and end up on the wrong one.
alt: I can never remember which floor it is. I keep going to the wrong one.
note: get confused = 헷갈리다 / end up = 결국 ~하게 되다

korean: 그래서 결국 걔네 사귀는 거야 아닌 거야?
english: So are they dating or not? I'm confused.
alt: Are they actually together or is it just a rumor?
note: dating = 사귀는 / rumor = 소문

korean: 그냥 느낌적으로 안 좋아 설명은 못 하겠는데
english: I just have a bad feeling about it. I can't explain why.
alt: Something about it doesn't sit right with me. Call it gut instinct.
note: gut instinct = 직감 / doesn't sit right = 찝찝하다

korean: 우리 동창회 언제 하기로 했지? 날짜 까먹었어
english: When did we say the reunion was? I totally forgot the date.
alt: What was the date for our class reunion? It slipped my mind.
note: reunion = 동창회 / slipped my mind = 깜빡했다

korean: 좀 쉬면서 해 건강이 먼저야
english: Take it easy. Your health comes first.
alt: Don't overdo it. Nothing is more important than your well-being.
note: take it easy = 쉬엄쉬엄 해 / well-being = 안녕/건강

korean: 이번에 새로 나온 폰 봤어? 진짜 갖고 싶다
english: Have you seen the new phone that just came out? I want it so bad.
alt: Did you check out the latest phone release? I'm dying to get one.
note: latest release = 최신 출시 / dying to = 너무 ~하고 싶은

korean: 길이 미끄러우니까 조심해서 걸어
english: The road is slippery so watch your step.
alt: Be careful walking. It's icy out there.
note: slippery/icy = 미끄러운 / watch your step = 발 조심

korean: 나 아까 현금 뽑으려고 ATM 갔는데 고장 나 있었어
english: I went to the ATM to withdraw cash but it was out of service.
alt: The ATM was broken when I tried to get money out earlier.
note: withdraw = 인출하다 / out of service = 고장/서비스 중단

korean: 이 영화 자막 없이 볼 수 있어? 원어로 보고 싶은데
english: Can I watch this without subtitles? I want to watch it in the original language.
alt: Is there an option to turn off the subs? I prefer watching in the original.
note: subtitles/subs = 자막 / original language = 원어

korean: 여기 조명이 너무 밝아 좀 어둡게 할 수 있어?
english: The lighting in here is way too bright. Can we dim it a bit?
alt: These lights are blinding. Is there a way to turn them down?
note: dim = 어둡게 하다 / blinding = 눈부신

korean: 점심시간에 산책 좀 하니까 오후에 집중이 잘 돼
english: A quick walk during lunch break really helps me focus in the afternoon.
alt: Taking a midday stroll does wonders for my afternoon productivity.
note: midday stroll = 점심 산책 / productivity = 생산성

korean: 그 브랜드 짝퉁 많으니까 조심해 정품인지 확인해
english: That brand has a lot of counterfeits so be careful. Make sure it's authentic.
alt: Watch out for fakes with that brand. Verify it's the real deal before buying.
note: counterfeit = 짝퉁 / authentic = 정품의 / real deal = 진짜

korean: 와 고기 굽는 냄새 미쳤다 배고파 죽겠어
english: The smell of grilled meat is driving me crazy. I'm starving.
alt: That BBQ smell is making my mouth water. I need food now.
note: mouth water = 군침 돌다 / starving = 배고파 죽겠다

korean: 이거 다른 데서 더 싸게 파는 거 봤는데
english: I saw this cheaper somewhere else.
alt: I found the same thing at a lower price at another store.
note: at a lower price = 더 낮은 가격에

korean: 그 사이트 안전한 거 맞아? 개인정보 유출되면 어쩌려고
english: Are you sure that site is safe? What if your personal info gets leaked?
alt: That website looks sketchy. I'd be worried about a data breach.
note: sketchy = 수상한 / data breach = 정보 유출

korean: 난 이제 진짜 갈게 내일 또 보자
english: I really need to get going now. See you tomorrow.
alt: Alright I'm heading out for real. Catch you tomorrow.
note: heading out = 나가는 / catch you = 또 보자

korean: 와 역시 직접 해보니까 다르다
english: Nothing beats actually doing it yourself. It's a whole different experience.
alt: Hands-on experience really is worth more than just reading about it.
note: hands-on = 직접 해보는 / nothing beats = ~을 이길 것은 없다

korean: 일단 시작하면 반은 한 거야 너무 걱정하지 마
english: Once you start you're already halfway there. Don't worry too much.
alt: The hardest part is just getting started. Everything else flows from there.
note: halfway there = 반은 된 / flows from there = 거기서부터 흘러간다

korean: 이 치약 민트 향이 너무 강해 순한 거 없어?
english: This toothpaste has way too much mint. Do you have a milder one?
alt: The mint flavor on this is intense. I need something gentler.
note: milder/gentler = 순한 / intense = 강한

korean: 이거 다 못 먹겠어 좀 나눠 먹자
english: I can't finish all of this. Let's share.
alt: This is way too much for me. Want to split it?
note: share / split = 나누다

korean: 요즘 관절이 안 좋아 계단 오르내리기가 힘들어
english: My joints have been acting up lately. Going up and down stairs is rough.
alt: My knees are giving me trouble. Stairs are my worst enemy right now.
note: joints acting up = 관절이 안 좋아지다 / giving me trouble = 문제가 되다

korean: 그 사람 첫사랑이었어 잊혀지지가 않아
english: They were my first love. I can't seem to get over them.
alt: They were my first love and somehow they still cross my mind.
note: first love = 첫사랑 / get over = 잊다/극복하다 / cross my mind = 생각나다

korean: 이 동네 맛집 좀 검색해볼까? 리뷰 좋은 데로 가자
english: Shall I look up some good restaurants in this area? Let's pick one with great reviews.
alt: Let me search for top-rated places nearby. We'll go wherever has the best ratings.
note: top-rated = 평점 높은 / ratings = 평점

korean: 나 좀 늦을 수도 있어 먼저 시작하고 있어
english: I might be a little late. Go ahead and start without me.
alt: Don't wait for me. I'll catch up when I get there.
note: go ahead = 먼저 시작해 / catch up = 합류하다

korean: 오늘 아무 데도 안 나가고 집에만 있을 거야
english: I'm not going anywhere today. Just staying home all day.
alt: Today is a full-on pajama day. I'm not leaving the house.
note: pajama day = 잠옷 입고 보내는 날

korean: 나 책상 정리 좀 해야겠다 지저분해서 집중이 안 돼
english: I need to tidy up my desk. It's so messy I can't concentrate.
alt: My workspace is a disaster. I can't think straight with all this clutter.
note: tidy up = 정리하다 / clutter = 어질러진 것

korean: 그 영화 개봉하자마자 볼 거야 진짜 기대돼
english: I'm watching that movie the day it comes out. I'm so hyped.
alt: I'm going opening day for sure. I've been looking forward to it for months.
note: hyped = 기대되는 / opening day = 개봉일

korean: 아 시험 기간이라 스트레스 받아 죽겠어
english: It's exam season and the stress is absolutely killing me.
alt: Finals are coming up and I'm losing my mind.
note: exam season / finals = 시험 기간 / losing my mind = 미칠 것 같다

korean: 에어컨 좀 줄여줘 너무 추워서 얼어 죽겠어
english: Turn down the AC please. I'm freezing to death in here.
alt: Can you lower the air conditioning? It's like a freezer in here.
note: freezing to death = 얼어 죽겠다 / like a freezer = 냉동실 같은

korean: 이거 평소에 이 가격이야 아니면 세일 가격이야?
english: Is this the regular price or the sale price?
alt: Is this what it normally costs or is it discounted?
note: regular price = 정가 / discounted = 할인된

korean: 그 사람 진짜 대화가 안 돼 자기 얘기만 해
english: You can't have a real conversation with them. They only talk about themselves.
alt: They just go on and on about themselves. There's no back and forth.
note: go on and on = 계속 말하다 / back and forth = 주고받는

korean: 이 카페 콘센트 있어? 노트북 충전하면서 일하려고
english: Does this cafe have power outlets? I need to charge my laptop while I work.
alt: Are there any plugs here? I want to work on my laptop.
note: power outlets / plugs = 콘센트

korean: 이거 유기농이에요? 요즘 유기농 제품 찾는 중이라서
english: Is this organic? I've been looking for organic products lately.
alt: I only buy organic these days. Is this one?
note: organic = 유기농

korean: 그 뉴스 봤어? 세상에 이런 일이 다 있다니
english: Did you see the news? I can't believe something like this actually happened.
alt: Have you heard about what happened? It's absolutely unbelievable.
note: unbelievable = 믿을 수 없는

korean: 진짜 가슴이 뭉클하네 눈물 날 것 같아
english: That really tugs at my heartstrings. I'm about to cry.
alt: I'm getting emotional just thinking about it. So touching.
note: tugs at heartstrings = 가슴이 뭉클하다 / touching = 감동적인

korean: 나 이번에 새 노트북 샀어 드디어 바꿨다
english: I finally got a new laptop. It was about time.
alt: I upgraded my laptop at last. My old one was on its last legs.
note: on its last legs = 거의 다 된/수명 다한

korean: 이거 너무 달아 설탕 덜 넣어주세요
english: This is too sweet. Can you put less sugar please?
alt: Could you make this less sweet? I prefer it not so sugary.
note: less sugar = 설탕 적게 / sugary = 달달한

korean: 헬스장 등록하려고 하는데 어디가 좋아?
english: I'm thinking of joining a gym. Any recommendations?
alt: I want to sign up for a gym membership. Know any good ones?
note: join a gym = 헬스장 등록하다 / membership = 회원권

korean: 이거 리콜 대상인지 확인해봐야겠다
english: I should check if this is part of the recall.
alt: Let me look up whether this product has been recalled.
note: recall = 리콜/회수 / look up = 확인하다

korean: 하마터면 큰일 날 뻔했어 진짜 운이 좋았어
english: That was a close call. I got really lucky.
alt: We dodged a bullet there. Things could've gone very wrong.
note: close call = 아슬아슬한 상황 / dodged a bullet = 위기를 모면했다

korean: 나 어제 헌혈했어 좋은 일 하니까 기분이 좋더라
english: I donated blood yesterday. Doing something good felt really nice.
alt: I gave blood yesterday and it actually made my day.
note: donate blood / give blood = 헌혈하다 / made my day = 기분 좋았다

korean: 한국 드라마 추천 좀 해줘 요즘 재밌는 거 없어?
english: Can you recommend a Korean drama? Anything good airing lately?
alt: What K-dramas are worth watching right now?
note: K-drama = 한국 드라마 / airing = 방영 중인

korean: 그 사람 리액션이 너무 세서 진짜인지 모르겠어
english: Their reactions are so over the top I can't tell if they're genuine.
alt: They're so dramatic I never know if they're being real or faking it.
note: over the top = 과한 / genuine = 진짜의/진심의

korean: 날씨가 갑자기 추워졌네 겉옷 가져올걸
english: The weather dropped suddenly. I should've brought a jacket.
alt: It got cold out of nowhere. I wish I had an extra layer.
note: dropped = (기온이) 떨어진 / extra layer = 겉옷 한 겹

korean: 그래 알았어 그건 내가 양보할게
english: Fine. I'll give in on that one.
alt: Okay I'll compromise on that. You win this round.
note: give in = 양보하다 / compromise = 타협하다

korean: 어제 자전거 타다가 넘어졌어 무릎 까졌어
english: I fell off my bike yesterday. Scraped my knee pretty bad.
alt: I wiped out on my bike and skinned my knee.
note: wiped out = 넘어졌다 / skinned my knee = 무릎 까졌다

korean: 이 근처에 세탁소 있나요? 급하게 드라이클리닝 맡겨야 해서
english: Is there a dry cleaner nearby? I need to get something cleaned urgently.
alt: Any laundromat or dry cleaner around here? It's kind of urgent.
note: dry cleaner = 세탁소 / laundromat = 빨래방

korean: 너 진짜 예의 바르다 부모님이 잘 키우셨나 보다
english: You're so well-mannered. Your parents raised you well.
alt: You have such great manners. Your upbringing really shows.
note: well-mannered = 예의 바른 / upbringing = 양육/교육

korean: 뭐 특별히 알레르기 있는 거 있어? 요리할 때 참고하게
english: Do you have any food allergies I should know about? I want to keep that in mind when cooking.
alt: Any allergies or dietary restrictions? I'm cooking tonight.
note: dietary restrictions = 식이 제한

korean: 오늘 점심 뭐 먹을까? 나는 국물 있는 게 먹고 싶어
english: What should we eat for lunch? I'm in the mood for something soupy.
alt: I'm craving soup today. Any good noodle or stew places around here?
note: in the mood for = ~가 먹고 싶은 / craving = 갈망하는

korean: 방금 지진이야? 흔들린 것 같은데
english: Was that an earthquake? I felt something shake.
alt: Did you feel that? I think the ground just moved.
note: earthquake = 지진

korean: 이번 달에 저축 좀 해야겠다 지난달에 너무 많이 써서
english: I need to save more this month. I went way over budget last month.
alt: Time to tighten my belt this month. I overspent like crazy.
note: tighten my belt = 허리띠를 졸라매다 / overspent = 과소비한

korean: 컴퓨터가 자꾸 느려져서 포맷해야 할 것 같아
english: My computer keeps getting slower. I think I need to format it.
alt: My PC is crawling. I might need to do a clean install.
note: crawling = 느린 / clean install = 포맷 후 재설치

korean: 그 게임 아직도 해? 나는 진작에 질렸는데
english: Are you still playing that game? I got bored of it ages ago.
alt: You're still into that game? I dropped it a long time ago.
note: got bored of = ~에 질린 / dropped it = 그만뒀다

korean: 이거 블루투스 연결이 안 돼 뭐가 문제지?
english: The Bluetooth won't connect. What's wrong with it?
alt: I can't get this to pair via Bluetooth. Any ideas?
note: pair = 페어링하다 / Bluetooth = 블루투스

korean: 나 요즘 유튜브 프리미엄 쓰는데 광고 없으니까 진짜 편해
english: I've been using YouTube Premium and not having ads is a game changer.
alt: YouTube without ads is totally worth the subscription fee.
note: game changer = 판도를 바꾸는 / subscription fee = 구독료

korean: 이거 반품 기한 지났나요? 혹시 교환은 되나요?
english: Has the return window passed? Can I at least exchange it?
alt: Am I still within the return period? If not can I swap it for something else?
note: return window = 반품 기간 / swap = 교환하다

korean: 나 어제 너무 웃겨서 배 아팠어 진짜로
english: I laughed so hard yesterday my abs actually hurt.
alt: I was literally crying laughing yesterday. My stomach was sore.
note: crying laughing = 울면서 웃는 / abs = 복근

korean: 그 사람한테는 비밀이야 제발 말하지 마
english: Don't tell them. This is strictly between us.
alt: Keep this quiet. They absolutely cannot find out.
note: strictly between us = 우리 사이에서만 / keep quiet = 비밀 유지

korean: 조금만 볼륨 줄여줘 이웃에 피해 갈 것 같아
english: Turn it down a little. We might get noise complaints from the neighbors.
alt: Lower the volume please. I don't want to disturb the neighbors.
note: noise complaints = 소음 민원 / disturb = 방해하다

korean: 난 항상 플랜B를 준비해 놓는 편이야
english: I always have a backup plan ready just in case.
alt: I like to keep a Plan B in my back pocket. You never know.
note: backup plan = 대안 / in my back pocket = 준비해 둔

korean: 이 약 식후에 먹어야 하나요 식전에 먹어야 하나요?
english: Should I take this medicine before or after meals?
alt: Is this medication meant to be taken on a full or empty stomach?
note: before/after meals = 식전/식후 / on an empty stomach = 공복에

korean: 그래 맞아 내 잘못이야 근데 그렇게 말하면 기분 나쁘잖아
english: You're right it's my fault. But the way you said it really hurt.
alt: I know I was wrong but did you have to say it like that?
note: the way you said it = 네가 말한 방식

korean: 여기 콜키지 되나요? 와인 가져왔는데
english: Do you allow BYOB? I brought my own wine.
alt: Is there a corkage fee? We'd like to bring our own bottle.
note: BYOB = bring your own bottle / corkage fee = 콜키지 비용

korean: 그 사람 말은 반만 들어 과장이 심해
english: Take what they say with a grain of salt. They exaggerate a lot.
alt: Don't believe everything they tell you. They tend to blow things out of proportion.
note: take with a grain of salt = 반만 믿다 / blow out of proportion = 과장하다

korean: 오늘 바람이 너무 불어서 우산이 뒤집어졌어
english: It was so windy today my umbrella flipped inside out.
alt: The wind literally destroyed my umbrella. It turned inside out.
note: flipped inside out = 뒤집어진

korean: 이거 무이자 할부 되나요? 3개월로 하고 싶은데
english: Can I get this on zero-interest installments? I'd like three months.
alt: Do you offer interest-free financing? I'm thinking three monthly payments.
note: zero-interest = 무이자 / installments = 할부

korean: 그 사람 진짜 센스 있어 눈치도 빠르고
english: They have such great social awareness. They pick up on things instantly.
alt: They're really sharp and always know exactly what to say and when.
note: social awareness = 사교적 센스 / pick up on = 알아채다 / sharp = 예리한

korean: 이 링크 피싱 아니야? 클릭하지 마
english: Is this link legit? Don't click on it just in case.
alt: That looks like a phishing link. Whatever you do don't open it.
note: phishing = 피싱 / legit = 합법적인/진짜인

korean: 이번 시즌 옷 다 예쁘다 고르기 힘들어
english: Everything from this season's collection is gorgeous. I can't decide.
alt: This season's lineup is amazing. I want everything.
note: collection = 컬렉션 / lineup = 라인업

korean: 그냥 무시해 반응하면 더 심해져
english: Just ignore it. Engaging will only make it worse.
alt: Don't give them the satisfaction. Walk away.
note: engaging = 반응하는 / give them the satisfaction = 만족감을 주다

korean: 오늘 저녁에 비 온대 빨래 걷어야겠다
english: It's supposed to rain tonight. I should bring in the laundry.
alt: Rain's coming this evening. Better get the laundry inside.
note: bring in = 걷다 / laundry = 빨래

korean: 나 그 일 이후로 트라우마가 생겼어
english: I've been traumatized ever since that happened.
alt: That incident left me with some serious emotional scars.
note: traumatized = 트라우마가 생긴 / emotional scars = 정서적 상처

korean: 이 계단 몇 층이야? 엘리베이터 타자 다리 아파
english: How many flights of stairs is this? Let's take the elevator. My legs hurt.
alt: I'm not walking up all those stairs. Let's find the elevator.
note: flights of stairs = 계단 층수

korean: 그때로 돌아갈 수 있으면 좋겠다
english: I wish I could go back to that time.
alt: If only I could turn back the clock.
note: turn back the clock = 시간을 되돌리다

korean: 이거 진짜 가성비 좋다 이 가격에 이 퀄리티면 대박이지
english: This is incredible value for money. This quality at this price is unbeatable.
alt: You get so much bang for your buck with this. The quality is insane for the price.
note: bang for your buck = 가성비 / unbeatable = 이길 수 없는

korean: 그 사람이랑 눈이 마주쳤어 심장이 쿵 했어
english: I made eye contact with them and my heart skipped a beat.
alt: Our eyes met and I felt butterflies in my stomach.
note: heart skipped a beat = 심장이 한 박자 건너뛴 / butterflies = 설레는 느낌

korean: 약속 시간 변경 가능할까? 좀 당길 수 있어?
english: Can we push the meeting time up? Can we make it a bit earlier?
alt: Any way to move up our appointment? Something came up later.
note: push up / move up = 앞당기다

korean: 나 커피 안 마셔 차 마시는 사람이야
english: I'm not a coffee drinker. I'm more of a tea person.
alt: I don't do coffee. Tea is more my thing.
note: tea person / coffee drinker = 차/커피 마시는 사람

korean: 나 오늘 운이 좋았어 거의 사고 날 뻔했거든
english: I was so lucky today. I almost got into an accident.
alt: I had such a close call today. Nearly got into a crash.
note: close call = 아슬아슬한 / crash = 충돌/사고

korean: 싫은 소리 하는 거 아닌데 솔직히 말할게
english: I don't want to be harsh but I need to be honest with you.
alt: I'm not trying to be mean but I have to be upfront.
note: harsh = 가혹한 / upfront = 솔직한

korean: 열쇠를 집에 놓고 왔어 어떡하지
english: I left my keys at home. What am I going to do?
alt: I locked myself out. My keys are sitting on the kitchen counter.
note: locked myself out = 잠기고 나옴 / sitting on = ~위에 놓인

korean: 생각만 해도 기분 좋아지는 추억이야
english: Just thinking about it puts a smile on my face.
alt: That memory never fails to cheer me up.
note: puts a smile on my face = 미소 짓게 하다 / cheer me up = 기분 좋게 하다

korean: 이거 공짜야? 진짜? 안 받아도 돼?
english: Is this really free? Are you sure I don't have to pay?
alt: Wait this is complimentary? No catch?
note: complimentary = 무료의 / no catch = 조건 없어?

korean: 저 사람 왜 저래? 진짜 이해가 안 돼
english: What is wrong with them? I seriously don't get it.
alt: I cannot for the life of me understand why they're acting like that.
note: for the life of me = 아무리 해도 / acting like that = 그렇게 행동하는

korean: 대기 시간이 얼마나 돼요? 오래 기다려야 하나요?
english: How long is the wait? Do I need to wait a long time?
alt: What's the estimated wait time? Is it going to be a while?
note: estimated wait time = 예상 대기 시간

korean: 와 이거 예쁘다 선물하기 딱이네
english: Oh wow this is beautiful. It would make a perfect gift.
alt: This is gorgeous. I know exactly who I'd give this to.
note: make a perfect gift = 완벽한 선물이 되다

korean: 이번 여름에 수영 배우려고 어른이 되어서도 못 배우면 창피하잖아
english: I'm going to learn to swim this summer. It'd be embarrassing not to know as an adult.
alt: I signed up for swimming lessons. Better late than never right?
note: better late than never = 늦더라도 안 하는 것보다 나은

korean: 이거 정리하는 데 하루 종일 걸리겠다
english: This is going to take all day to organize.
alt: Sorting through all of this is a full day's work easily.
note: sorting through = 정리하는 / full day's work = 하루 종일 걸리는 일

korean: 엄마가 만든 음식이 그립다 아무리 맛집이어도 집밥만 못해
english: I miss my mom's cooking. No restaurant can beat home-cooked meals.
alt: Nothing compares to a home-cooked meal. I miss my mom's food so much.
note: home-cooked meals = 집밥 / nothing compares = 비교할 수 없다

korean: 이건 좀 논쟁의 여지가 있는 주제야 조심해서 말하자
english: This is kind of a sensitive topic. Let's tread carefully.
alt: We should be careful discussing this. It's a bit controversial.
note: tread carefully = 조심히 다루다 / controversial = 논쟁적인

korean: 그래 한번 믿어볼게 실망시키지 마
english: Alright I'll trust you on this. Don't let me down.
alt: I'm putting my faith in you. Don't disappoint me.
note: let me down = 실망시키다 / putting my faith in = 믿음을 두다

korean: 이건 내 평생 먹어본 것 중에 최고야
english: This is hands down the best thing I've ever eaten in my life.
alt: I've never tasted anything this good. This is peak food.
note: hands down = 확실히/의심 없이 / peak = 최고의

korean: 이 사진 보정 좀 해야겠다 실물이 더 예쁜데
english: I need to edit this photo. It looks better in person.
alt: The picture doesn't do it justice. Let me touch it up a bit.
note: touch up = 보정하다 / doesn't do it justice = 실물만큼 안 나오는

korean: 그래서 그 얘기 결론이 뭐야? 듣다가 놓쳤어
english: So what was the conclusion of that story? I zoned out for a sec.
alt: I missed the ending. Can you fill me in?
note: zoned out = 멍 때린 / fill me in = 알려줘

korean: 화분에 물 주는 거 까먹지 마 말라 죽겠어
english: Don't forget to water the plants. They're dying.
alt: Remember to water the flowers. They look like they haven't been watered in days.
note: water the plants = 식물에 물 주다

korean: 퇴근 후에 헬스 가야 하는데 맨날 미루게 돼
english: I need to hit the gym after work but I keep putting it off.
alt: I always plan to work out after work but end up skipping it.
note: putting it off = 미루는 / skipping = 건너뛰는

korean: 이거 세트로 사면 더 싸져? 개별로 사는 것보다?
english: Is it cheaper to buy this as a set? Compared to buying individually?
alt: Do I get a better deal if I buy the bundle instead of separate pieces?
note: bundle = 세트/묶음 / separate = 개별의

korean: 친구가 해외에 있어서 시차 때문에 통화하기 어려워
english: My friend lives abroad so it's hard to call because of the time difference.
alt: The time zone difference makes it nearly impossible to find a good time to talk.
note: time difference/time zone = 시차

korean: 나 이제 스마트폰 없이는 못 살아 의존도가 너무 높아
english: I literally can't live without my smartphone. I'm way too dependent on it.
alt: I'm so attached to my phone it's almost unhealthy.
note: dependent on = 의존하는 / attached to = 집착하는

korean: 그 사람 유머 감각이 진짜 좋아 같이 있으면 늘 웃겨
english: They have an amazing sense of humor. I'm always laughing when I'm with them.
alt: They're hilarious. Being around them is guaranteed entertainment.
note: sense of humor = 유머 감각 / hilarious = 극도로 웃긴

korean: 이번에 아이폰으로 바꿀까 갤럭시로 바꿀까 고민이야
english: I can't decide between iPhone and Galaxy for my next phone.
alt: I'm torn between switching to iPhone or sticking with Galaxy.
note: torn between = 사이에서 갈등하는

korean: 그 사람 진짜 뒤끝이 없어 싸우고 나서도 금방 풀어
english: They never hold grudges. Even after a fight they get over it quickly.
alt: They don't hold things against you. Super easygoing that way.
note: hold a grudge = 앙심을 품다 / easygoing = 쉽게 풀리는

korean: 이거 충전식이야 건전지 넣는 거야?
english: Is this rechargeable or does it take batteries?
alt: Does this use a built-in battery or do I need to buy batteries separately?
note: rechargeable = 충전식 / batteries = 건전지

korean: 왜 이렇게 졸리지 방금 커피 마셨는데
english: Why am I still so sleepy? I just had coffee.
alt: I literally just drank coffee and I'm already nodding off.
note: nodding off = 졸고 있는

korean: 이 드라마 몇 부작이야? 완결났어?
english: How many episodes is this drama? Is it finished?
alt: Is this show completed or still ongoing? How many episodes total?
note: completed = 완결된 / ongoing = 진행 중인

korean: 그 카페 분위기는 좋은데 음료가 별로야
english: That cafe has a great atmosphere but the drinks are mediocre.
alt: The vibe is nice but the coffee itself is nothing special.
note: mediocre = 평범한 / nothing special = 특별할 것 없는

korean: 이게 벌써 2년 전이야? 시간 진짜 빠르다
english: This was already two years ago? Time flies.
alt: I can't believe it's been two years. Where did the time go?
note: time flies = 시간이 빨리 간다

korean: 이 약 부작용 있어요? 졸려지거나 그런 거 없어요?
english: Are there any side effects from this medication? Like drowsiness?
alt: Will this medicine make me sleepy or have any other side effects?
note: side effects = 부작용 / drowsiness = 졸음

korean: 이건 내가 낼게 다음에 네가 내
english: I'll get this one. You can get the next one.
alt: My treat today. You cover it next time.
note: my treat = 내가 쏜다 / cover = 내다

korean: 너 진짜 잘 어울린다 그 색 너한테 딱이야
english: That color really suits you. It looks perfect on you.
alt: That shade is made for you. Seriously it looks incredible.
note: suits you = 어울리다 / shade = 색조

korean: 아직 마음의 결정을 못 내렸어 좀 더 시간이 필요해
english: I haven't made up my mind yet. I need a bit more time.
alt: I'm still on the fence. Give me a little longer to decide.
note: make up my mind = 결정하다 / on the fence = 결정 못 한

korean: 거기 사람 많아? 예약 안 해도 되려나?
english: Is it usually crowded? Do you think we need a reservation?
alt: How busy does it get? Can we just walk in?
note: crowded = 붐비는 / walk in = 예약 없이 가다

korean: 자기 전에 핸드폰 보면 잠이 안 온대 나도 아는데 못 끊겠어
english: They say looking at your phone before bed ruins your sleep. I know but I can't stop.
alt: Screen time before bed is bad for you. I know that but I do it anyway.
note: screen time = 스크린 타임 / ruins sleep = 수면을 망치다

korean: 그 식당 분위기 좋아? 데이트하기 좋은 곳 찾는 중이야
english: Does that restaurant have a nice vibe? I'm looking for a good date spot.
alt: Is it a romantic atmosphere? I want somewhere nice for a date night.
note: date spot = 데이트 장소 / romantic atmosphere = 로맨틱한 분위기

korean: 이 근처 편의점 24시간이야?
english: Is the convenience store nearby open 24 hours?
alt: Do you know if there's a 24-hour store around here?
note: 24 hours / around the clock = 24시간

korean: 차 시동이 안 걸려 배터리 나간 것 같아
english: My car won't start. I think the battery is dead.
alt: The engine won't turn over. Pretty sure it's a dead battery.
note: won't start = 시동이 안 걸리는 / dead battery = 방전된 배터리

korean: 나 인스타 안 해 SNS 안 좋아해서
english: I'm not on Instagram. I'm not really into social media.
alt: I don't use social media. It's not my thing.
note: not into = ~에 관심 없는 / not my thing = 내 취향 아닌

korean: 이거 글루텐프리 메뉴 있나요?
english: Do you have any gluten-free options on the menu?
alt: Is there anything on the menu that's gluten-free?
note: gluten-free = 글루텐 프리 / options = 선택지

korean: 그 노래 나올 때마다 그때 생각나
english: Every time that song plays it takes me back to that moment.
alt: That song is such a throwback. It reminds me of that time.
note: throwback = 추억을 떠올리게 하는 / takes me back = 그때로 돌아가게 하다

korean: 내가 할 말이 있는데 화내지 마
english: I need to tell you something. Please don't get mad.
alt: There's something I have to say. Promise you won't freak out.
note: don't get mad = 화내지 마 / freak out = 발끈하다

korean: 이거 가짜 아니야? 정품 맞아? 너무 싸서 의심이 돼
english: This isn't fake is it? It's the real deal? It's so cheap I'm suspicious.
alt: The price seems too good to be true. Are you sure this is authentic?
note: too good to be true = 말이 안 될 정도로 좋은 / authentic = 정품인

korean: 아 맞다 깜빡했다 오늘 약 먹는 날이었는데
english: Oh right I totally forgot. I was supposed to take my medicine today.
alt: Shoot I just remembered. Today was my pill day.
note: supposed to = ~하기로 된 / pill day = 약 먹는 날

korean: 그 밈 보고 하루종일 웃었어
english: That meme had me laughing all day long.
alt: I couldn't stop chuckling at that meme. It lived in my head rent-free.
note: lived rent-free = 머릿속에 계속 맴돈

korean: 요즘 독서 모임 다니는데 꽤 재밌어
english: I've been going to a book club lately and it's actually really fun.
alt: I joined a reading group recently. It's way more enjoyable than I expected.
note: book club = 독서 모임 / reading group = 독서 모임

korean: 엘리베이터 문 좀 잡아줘 지금 타야 해
english: Hold the elevator door please! I need to get on.
alt: Wait for me! Hold the door!
note: hold the door = 문 잡아줘

korean: 그래도 포기하지 마 여기서 멈추기엔 너무 아까워
english: Don't give up now. You've come too far to stop here.
alt: You can't quit when you're this close. Keep going.
note: come too far = 너무 많이 와서 / keep going = 계속해

korean: 내 계좌로 보내줘 계좌번호 알려줄게
english: Transfer it to my account. I'll give you my account number.
alt: Send it to my bank account. Here's my details.
note: transfer = 이체/송금 / account number = 계좌번호

korean: 이거 안 맞으면 교환 가능해요? 영수증 가져가야 하나요?
english: Can I exchange this if it doesn't fit? Do I need to keep the receipt?
alt: What's the exchange policy? Do I need proof of purchase?
note: exchange policy = 교환 정책 / proof of purchase = 구매 증명

korean: 나 그 영화 세 번이나 봤어 볼 때마다 새로워
english: I've watched that movie three times and I notice something new every time.
alt: I keep rewatching it. It hits different each time.
note: rewatching = 다시 보는 / hits different = 느낌이 다르다

korean: 냉방병 걸린 것 같아 에어컨을 너무 세게 틀었나 봐
english: I think I got air conditioning sickness. I must've had the AC on too high.
alt: I'm feeling under the weather. Probably from sitting under the AC all day.
note: under the weather = 몸이 안 좋은 / AC = 에어컨

korean: 나 선물 받으면 되게 부담스러워해 그냥 작은 거면 좋겠어
english: I feel really awkward receiving gifts. Something small would be best.
alt: I'm not great with gifts. I prefer something simple and thoughtful.
note: awkward = 어색한 / thoughtful = 마음이 담긴

korean: 오늘 정전 때문에 아무것도 못 했어
english: I couldn't do anything today because of the power outage.
alt: The blackout killed my whole day. Total productivity zero.
note: power outage / blackout = 정전 / productivity = 생산성

korean: 나 그거 알고 있었어 굳이 말 안 한 거야
english: I already knew about that. I just chose not to say anything.
alt: I was aware of it the whole time. I kept quiet on purpose.
note: chose not to = 안 하기로 한 / kept quiet = 조용히 있었다

korean: 여기 무슬림 음식점 있나요? 할랄 음식 찾고 있어서
english: Are there any Muslim restaurants around here? I'm looking for halal food.
alt: Is there a halal-certified restaurant nearby?
note: halal = 할랄 / halal-certified = 할랄 인증된

korean: 나 지갑 안 가져왔어 혹시 빌려줄 수 있어? 나중에 갚을게
english: I forgot my wallet. Can you spot me? I'll pay you back.
alt: I left my wallet at home. Mind covering me? I'm good for it.
note: spot me = 돈 빌려줘 / I'm good for it = 꼭 갚을게

korean: 이거 나한테 딱 맞는 사이즈야 안 입어봐도 될 것 같아
english: This is exactly my size. I don't even need to try it on.
alt: It looks like a perfect fit just from holding it up.
note: perfect fit = 딱 맞는 / try it on = 입어보다

korean: 실은 나 요즘 상담 받고 있어 마음이 좀 편해졌어
english: Actually I've been seeing a therapist lately. It's helped a lot.
alt: I started therapy recently and it's made a real difference in how I feel.
note: therapy / seeing a therapist = 상담 받는 / made a difference = 변화를 만든

korean: 그때 네가 그 말 해줘서 진짜 힘이 됐어
english: What you said to me back then really gave me strength.
alt: Your words meant the world to me at that time. I'll never forget it.
note: gave me strength = 힘이 됐다 / meant the world = 세상 전부와 같았다

korean: 주문 취소할 수 있나요? 아직 배송 전이면요
english: Can I cancel my order? It hasn't shipped yet right?
alt: I'd like to cancel if it hasn't been dispatched yet. Is that possible?
note: cancel = 취소 / dispatched/shipped = 배송된

korean: 이번 주말에 등산 갈 사람 있어? 같이 가자
english: Anyone up for hiking this weekend? Let's go together.
alt: Who wants to hit the trails this weekend? The more the merrier.
note: hit the trails = 등산하다 / the more the merrier = 많을수록 좋다

korean: 이거 어떻게 사용하는 건지 설명서 좀 볼 수 있나요?
english: How does this work? Can I see the instructions?
alt: Is there a user manual I can look at? I'm not sure how to operate this.
note: instructions / user manual = 설명서 / operate = 작동하다

korean: 우리 동네에 새로 생긴 카페 가봤어? 괜찮더라
english: Have you tried the new cafe in our neighborhood? It's pretty good.
alt: There's a new coffee shop that just opened near us. Worth checking out.
note: worth checking out = 가볼 만한

korean: 바닥이 미끄러우니 조심하세요 방금 청소했어요
english: Careful the floor is wet. It was just cleaned.
alt: Watch your step. The floor was just mopped and it's slippery.
note: wet floor = 젖은 바닥 / mopped = 걸레질한

korean: 다음에 같이 가자 이번에는 일정이 안 맞아서 못 갔어
english: Let's go together next time. I couldn't make it this time because of scheduling.
alt: I'll definitely join next time. My schedule just didn't line up this round.
note: make it = 참석하다 / line up = 맞다

korean: 그 가게 문 닫았대 얼마 전까지 있었는데 아쉽다
english: That store apparently closed down. It was there not long ago. What a shame.
alt: I just found out that place shut down. So sad I really liked it there.
note: closed down / shut down = 문 닫은 / what a shame = 아쉽다

korean: 이거 국내 배송만 되나요? 해외 배송은 안 되나요?
english: Is this domestic shipping only? No international delivery?
alt: Can this be shipped overseas or is it limited to domestic?
note: domestic = 국내 / international = 해외

korean: 너 그때 왜 갑자기 연락이 안 됐어? 걱정했잖아
english: Why did you suddenly go MIA? I was worried about you.
alt: You disappeared on me. I thought something happened to you.
note: go MIA = 연락 두절되다 / disappeared on me = 갑자기 사라졌다

korean: 여기 와이파이 느리다 영상 로딩이 안 돼
english: The Wi-Fi here is super slow. Videos won't even load.
alt: The internet connection is crawling. I can't stream anything.
note: crawling = 느린 / stream = 스트리밍하다

korean: 아침에 빈속에 커피 마시면 속 쓰려
english: Drinking coffee on an empty stomach gives me heartburn.
alt: I can't do coffee first thing without eating. It wrecks my stomach.
note: on an empty stomach = 공복에 / heartburn = 속쓰림

korean: 그냥 느끼는 건데 이번 건은 잘 될 것 같아
english: I just have a feeling this one is going to work out.
alt: I can't explain it but I'm optimistic about this. Something tells me it'll go well.
note: work out = 잘 되다 / optimistic = 낙관적인

korean: 나 영어 발음이 안 좋아서 말하기가 두려워
english: My English pronunciation is bad so I'm scared to speak.
alt: I'm self-conscious about my accent. It makes me nervous to talk.
note: self-conscious = 의식하는 / accent = 억양/발음

korean: 여기 계산 따로 해주실 수 있나요? 각자 낼게요
english: Can we get separate checks please? We'd each like to pay for our own.
alt: Could you split the bill? We're paying individually.
note: separate checks = 따로 계산 / individually = 각자

korean: 새벽에 갑자기 깼는데 다시 잠들기가 어렵더라
english: I woke up in the middle of the night and couldn't fall back asleep.
alt: I kept waking up at like 3 AM and then just lay there staring at the ceiling.
note: fall back asleep = 다시 잠들다 / staring at the ceiling = 천장 보며

korean: 이거 보험 처리 가능한가요?
english: Is this covered by insurance?
alt: Can I file an insurance claim for this?
note: covered by insurance = 보험 적용되는 / insurance claim = 보험 청구

korean: 솔직히 이 가격에 이 서비스면 가성비 별로야
english: Honestly for this price the service is not great value.
alt: The price-to-quality ratio is off. I expected more for what I paid.
note: value for money = 가성비 / price-to-quality ratio = 가격 대비 품질

korean: 나 어렸을 때 여기 살았어 그때 기억이 아직도 생생해
english: I lived here when I was a kid. The memories are still so vivid.
alt: This is where I grew up. I remember it like it was yesterday.
note: vivid = 생생한 / like it was yesterday = 어제 일처럼

korean: 나 혈압이 좀 높은 편이라 짜게 못 먹어
english: My blood pressure runs a bit high so I can't eat too much salt.
alt: I'm watching my sodium intake because of blood pressure issues.
note: blood pressure = 혈압 / sodium intake = 나트륨 섭취

korean: 그거 나도 관심 있었는데 같이 하자
english: I've been interested in that too. Let's do it together.
alt: Count me in! I was actually thinking about trying that myself.
note: count me in = 나도 끼워줘

korean: 유통기한 확인해봐 이거 색이 좀 이상한데
english: Check the expiration date. The color looks a bit off.
alt: Something about this looks weird. When does it expire?
note: expiration date = 유통기한 / off = 이상한

korean: 일 끝나고 뭐 해? 시간 되면 같이 밥 먹자
english: What are you doing after work? Want to grab dinner if you're free?
alt: Got plans after work? Let's get food together.
note: grab dinner = 저녁 먹다 / got plans = 계획 있어?

korean: 이거 충전 다 하는 데 얼마나 걸려?
english: How long does it take to fully charge?
alt: What's the full charge time on this?
note: full charge = 완충 / charge time = 충전 시간

korean: 나 지금 목이 너무 아파 감기 걸린 것 같아
english: My throat is killing me. I think I'm coming down with a cold.
alt: I've got a sore throat and I feel a cold coming on.
note: coming down with = 걸리려고 하는 / sore throat = 인후통

korean: 이거 몇 인분이에요? 둘이서 먹기에 충분해요?
english: How many servings is this? Is it enough for two people?
alt: Will this feed two people or should we order more?
note: servings = 인분 / feed = 먹이다

korean: 그때 내가 왜 그랬는지 모르겠어 후회돼
english: I don't know why I did that. I really regret it.
alt: Looking back I have no idea what I was thinking. I wish I could take it back.
note: regret = 후회 / take it back = 되돌리다

korean: 이거 양이 좀 많은데 포장해 가도 되나요?
english: This is a bit too much. Can I take the rest to go?
alt: I can't finish all this. Can I get a box for the leftovers?
note: leftovers = 남은 음식 / to go = 포장으로

korean: 네 번호 좀 알 수 있을까? 연락하고 싶어서
english: Could I get your number? I'd like to stay in touch.
alt: Would you mind sharing your contact? I'd love to keep in touch.
note: stay in touch / keep in touch = 연락 유지

korean: 컨디션 안 좋으면 억지로 나오지 마 쉬어
english: If you're not feeling well don't force yourself to come. Rest up.
alt: Stay home and recover if you're sick. Don't push yourself.
note: rest up = 푹 쉬다 / push yourself = 무리하다

korean: 이거 시식 가능한가요? 맛 보고 사고 싶어서
english: Can I try a sample before I buy?
alt: Do you offer tastings? I'd like to try it first.
note: sample = 시식 / tastings = 시음/시식

korean: 나 일요일에는 진짜 아무것도 안 하고 싶어
english: On Sundays I honestly just want to do absolutely nothing.
alt: Sunday is my do-nothing day. I refuse to be productive.
note: do-nothing day = 아무것도 안 하는 날 / productive = 생산적인

korean: 뉴스에서 봤는데 이번 주 미세먼지가 심하대
english: I saw on the news that the fine dust levels are bad this week.
alt: The air quality is supposed to be terrible this week according to the forecast.
note: fine dust = 미세먼지 / air quality = 공기 질

korean: 나 이 동네 맛집 다 알아 뭐 먹고 싶어?
english: I know all the best spots in this neighborhood. What are you craving?
alt: I'm basically the food guide for this area. Name your cuisine.
note: craving = 먹고 싶은 / food guide = 맛집 가이드

korean: 그건 내가 잘못한 거 맞아 다시는 안 그럴게
english: You're right that was my mistake. I won't let it happen again.
alt: I own up to that. I promise it won't happen again.
note: own up to = 잘못을 인정하다 / won't let it happen again = 다시 안 그럴게

korean: 이거 진짜 가볍다 가방에 넣어도 무게 안 느껴져
english: This is incredibly light. You can barely feel it in your bag.
alt: It's so lightweight I forget it's even in my bag.
note: lightweight = 가벼운 / barely feel = 거의 느끼지 못하는

korean: 그 사람 말은 절대 믿으면 안 돼 맨날 거짓말해
english: Never trust what they say. They lie all the time.
alt: They have zero credibility. Everything out of their mouth is BS.
note: credibility = 신뢰도 / BS = 헛소리

korean: 보조배터리 가져왔어? 오늘 하루종일 밖이잖아
english: Did you bring a portable charger? We're out all day.
alt: Pack a power bank. We won't be near an outlet for hours.
note: portable charger / power bank = 보조배터리

korean: 거기 맛있어? 리뷰 보니까 별점이 좀 낮던데
english: Is that place actually good? The ratings seem kind of low.
alt: The reviews are mixed. Is it still worth trying?
note: ratings = 별점 / mixed reviews = 엇갈리는 후기

korean: 나 라식 했어 안경 벗으니까 세상이 달라 보여
english: I got LASIK and the world looks completely different without glasses.
alt: Life after LASIK is amazing. I can't believe I waited so long.
note: LASIK = 라식 / completely different = 완전히 다른

korean: 이거 나중에 중고로 팔 수 있어? 리셀 가치 있어?
english: Can I resell this later? Does it hold its value?
alt: Is there a good resale market for this?
note: resell = 재판매 / hold its value = 가치가 유지되다 / resale = 리셀

korean: 오늘 커플 할인 되나요? 둘이 같이 왔는데
english: Is there a couple's discount today? We came together.
alt: Do you offer any deals for couples?
note: couple's discount = 커플 할인

korean: 이거 USB-C 충전기 맞지? A타입이면 안 맞아서
english: This is a USB-C charger right? It won't work if it's Type-A.
alt: I need to make sure it's USB-C compatible. My phone doesn't take Type-A.
note: USB-C / Type-A = 충전 규격 / compatible = 호환되는

korean: 그 말 듣고 되게 위로가 됐어 고마워
english: Hearing that really comforted me. Thank you.
alt: What you said meant a lot. I really needed to hear that.
note: comforted = 위로된 / meant a lot = 큰 의미가 있었다

korean: 나 요즘 조깅 시작했는데 무릎이 좀 아파
english: I started jogging recently but my knees are bothering me.
alt: I've picked up running but I'm getting some knee pain.
note: bothering me = 신경 쓰이는 / knee pain = 무릎 통증

korean: 이 노래 가사가 진짜 와 닿는다 지금 내 심정이랑 똑같아
english: These lyrics really resonate with me. They're exactly how I feel right now.
alt: This song perfectly captures what I'm going through. The lyrics are so relatable.
note: resonate = 공감되다 / relatable = 공감 가는

korean: 제가 좀 늦을 수 있는데 먼저 주문하고 계세요
english: I might be a bit late, so go ahead and order without me.
alt: Start ordering without me. I'll be there shortly.
note: go ahead = 먼저 하세요 / shortly = 곧

korean: 이 시험 떨어지면 어쩌지 불안해 죽겠어
english: What if I fail this exam? The anxiety is eating me alive.
alt: I'm so worried about failing. I can barely concentrate.
note: anxiety eating me alive = 불안감에 잡아먹히는

korean: 어디 아파? 얼굴이 안 좋아 보여
english: Are you okay? You don't look well.
alt: You look pale. Is something wrong?
note: don't look well = 안 좋아 보이는 / pale = 창백한

korean: 나 원래 새벽형이라 밤에 더 집중이 잘 돼
english: I'm naturally a night owl so I focus better at night.
alt: I do my best work late at night. Always have.
note: night owl = 야행성 / focus better = 집중이 더 잘 되는

korean: 이 앱 알림이 너무 많아 좀 줄여야겠다
english: This app sends way too many notifications. I need to turn some off.
alt: I'm getting notification overload from this app. Time to adjust the settings.
note: notifications = 알림 / overload = 과부하

korean: 그 사람한테 먼저 연락해봐 의외로 반가워할걸
english: Reach out to them first. They'd probably be happy to hear from you.
alt: Why don't you text them first? They might be pleasantly surprised.
note: reach out = 연락하다 / pleasantly surprised = 반갑게 놀란

korean: 지금 앱으로 예약하면 할인 되던데
english: There's a discount if you book through the app right now.
alt: The app has a promotion going on. You can save if you reserve online.
note: book through the app = 앱으로 예약하다 / promotion = 프로모션

korean: 이거 진짜 내 스타일이야 첫눈에 반했어
english: This is totally my style. I fell in love with it at first sight.
alt: Love at first sight! This is exactly what I've been looking for.
note: love at first sight = 첫눈에 반한 / my style = 내 스타일

korean: 피자 한 판 시킬까? 반반으로 하자
english: Should we order a pizza? Let's do half and half.
alt: Let's get a pizza. You pick one half and I'll pick the other.
note: half and half = 반반

korean: 이제 슬슬 집에 갈 시간이야 내일도 일이 있으니까
english: We should probably start heading home. I have work tomorrow.
alt: It's getting late and I've got an early morning. Time to call it a night.
note: heading home = 집으로 가는 / call it a night = 하루 마무리하다

korean: 그 소문 들었어? 사실인지 아닌지 모르겠어
english: Did you hear that rumor? I have no idea if it's true or not.
alt: There's a rumor going around. I can't tell if there's any truth to it.
note: rumor = 소문 / truth to it = 사실 여부

korean: 밖에 나갈 때 마스크 쓰는 게 좋겠어 미세먼지 심하대
english: You should wear a mask when you go out. The air quality is bad today.
alt: Better mask up before heading out. The pollution levels are high.
note: mask up = 마스크 쓰다 / pollution levels = 오염 수치

korean: 이 디자인 진짜 심플하면서 세련됐다
english: This design is really simple yet sophisticated.
alt: Clean and elegant. I love minimalist designs like this.
note: sophisticated = 세련된 / minimalist = 미니멀한 / elegant = 우아한

korean: 이 정도면 충분히 했어 너무 자책하지 마
english: You've done more than enough. Stop being so hard on yourself.
alt: Give yourself some credit. You worked really hard on this.
note: being hard on yourself = 자책하는 / give yourself credit = 자신을 인정해

korean: 레시피대로 했는데 맛이 다르네 뭐가 잘못된 거지?
english: I followed the recipe exactly but it tastes different. What went wrong?
alt: I did everything the recipe said but it doesn't taste right. Am I missing something?
note: followed the recipe = 레시피대로 한 / went wrong = 잘못된

korean: 여기 테라스 자리 있나요? 밖에서 먹고 싶어서
english: Do you have any outdoor seating? We'd like to eat outside.
alt: Is the terrace open? We'd prefer to sit outside.
note: outdoor seating = 야외석 / terrace = 테라스

korean: 다이어트 중인데 자꾸 야식이 땡겨
english: I'm on a diet but I keep craving late-night snacks.
alt: Midnight cravings are killing my diet. I have zero willpower at night.
note: cravings = 갈망 / willpower = 의지력

korean: 와 여기 진짜 핫플이네 사람 엄청 많다
english: This place is a total hotspot. It's packed with people.
alt: No wonder this place went viral. It's absolutely buzzing.
note: hotspot = 핫플 / buzzing = 활기찬

korean: 나 요즘 명품 대신 가성비 좋은 거 찾아
english: I've been looking for affordable quality instead of luxury brands lately.
alt: I'm over designer stuff. I want good quality without the crazy markup.
note: affordable = 합리적인 / markup = 마진/가격 차이

korean: 그래서 결국 누가 이겼어? 결과 알려줘
english: So who won in the end? Tell me the result.
alt: I missed the ending. Who came out on top?
note: came out on top = 이긴 / result = 결과

korean: 이 노래 틀어줘 기분 전환 좀 하게
english: Play this song for me. I need to change my mood.
alt: Put on some music. I need a pick-me-up.
note: pick-me-up = 기분 전환 / put on music = 음악 틀다

korean: 나 주사 맞는 거 너무 무서워 바늘 공포증이야
english: I'm terrified of needles. I have a needle phobia.
alt: Getting shots is my worst nightmare. I can't even look.
note: needle phobia = 바늘 공포증 / shots = 주사

korean: 그 사람이랑은 안 맞아 성격 차이가 너무 커
english: We're just not compatible. Our personalities are too different.
alt: We don't get along at all. We're like oil and water.
note: compatible = 맞는 / oil and water = 물과 기름

korean: 이거 정말 잘 산 것 같아 후회 없어
english: I think this was a really good purchase. No regrets.
alt: This was money well spent. I'm completely satisfied with it.
note: no regrets = 후회 없는 / money well spent = 잘 쓴 돈

korean: 내일 일찍 일어나야 하니까 알람 여러 개 맞춰놔야겠다
english: I need to wake up early tomorrow so I'm setting multiple alarms.
alt: Better set like five alarms. I cannot afford to oversleep tomorrow.
note: multiple alarms = 여러 개 알람 / oversleep = 늦잠 자다

korean: 그래 다음에 보자 오늘 즐거웠어
english: Alright see you next time. Today was fun.
alt: It was great hanging out. Let's do this again soon.
note: hanging out = 함께 시간 보내는 / do this again = 또 하자

korean: 이거 할인 쿠폰 적용 가능한가요?
english: Can I use a discount coupon on this?
alt: Is this eligible for any coupons or promo codes?
note: discount coupon = 할인 쿠폰 / promo codes = 프로모션 코드

korean: 나 원래 선택장애야 메뉴판 보면 30분은 고민해
english: I'm the most indecisive person ever. I stare at the menu for at least thirty minutes.
alt: I have the worst time choosing from menus. It takes me forever to decide.
note: indecisive = 우유부단한 / takes forever = 영원히 걸리다

korean: 그 사람 내 스타일이야 소개팅 주선해줘
english: They're my type. Set me up on a blind date with them.
alt: I'm interested in them. Can you introduce us?
note: blind date = 소개팅 / set me up = 주선해줘

korean: 나 요즘 넷플릭스 다큐만 봐 드라마보다 재밌어
english: I've been watching nothing but Netflix documentaries lately. Better than dramas.
alt: I'm on a documentary kick. They're way more interesting than scripted shows.
note: on a kick = ~에 빠진 / scripted shows = 대본 있는 프로그램

korean: 여기 흡연 구역 어디예요?
english: Where's the smoking area?
alt: Is there a designated smoking spot around here?
note: smoking area = 흡연 구역 / designated = 지정된

korean: 이거 유기농인지 아닌지 어떻게 구별해?
english: How can you tell if this is organic or not?
alt: What should I look for to know if it's genuinely organic?
note: tell = 구별하다 / genuinely = 진짜로

korean: 그냥 솔직하게 말해줘 나 감당할 수 있어
english: Just be straight with me. I can handle it.
alt: Give it to me straight. I'm ready to hear the truth.
note: be straight = 솔직하게 / handle it = 감당하다

korean: 주말에 빨래하고 청소하면 하루가 다 가
english: By the time I do laundry and clean on weekends the entire day is gone.
alt: Weekends disappear between chores. Laundry and cleaning eat up everything.
note: chores = 집안일 / eat up = 다 차지하다

korean: 수고하셨습니다 오늘도 고생하셨어요
english: Great work today. You've had a long day.
alt: Thanks for all your hard work today. Get some rest.
note: hard work = 수고 / get some rest = 푹 쉬세요

korean: 이 브랜드 A/S가 좋은 편이야?
english: Does this brand have good after-sales service?
alt: How's the warranty support for this brand? Are they responsive?
note: after-sales service = A/S / warranty support = 보증 서비스 / responsive = 응답 빠른

korean: 아무리 생각해도 이해가 안 돼 말이 안 되잖아
english: No matter how much I think about it I can't make sense of it.
alt: I've been going over it in my head and it just doesn't add up.
note: make sense of = 이해하다 / doesn't add up = 앞뒤가 안 맞다

korean: 다음에 만나면 커피 한 잔 살게 빚진 게 있으니까
english: I owe you one. Let me buy you coffee next time we meet.
alt: Next time I see you coffee's on me. I need to pay you back for that favor.
note: owe you one = 빚진 게 있다 / on me = 내가 사는

korean: 나 엄청 겁쟁이야 공포영화 못 봐
english: I'm such a scaredy-cat. I can't watch horror movies at all.
alt: I'm the biggest chicken when it comes to scary movies.
note: scaredy-cat / chicken = 겁쟁이 / horror movies = 공포영화

korean: 그냥 집에서 해 먹자 나가기 귀찮아
english: Let's just cook at home. I'm too lazy to go out.
alt: I don't feel like eating out. Let's whip something up at home.
note: whip something up = 후딱 만들다 / eating out = 외식

korean: 몸무게 좀 늘려야 해 너무 말랐어
english: I need to gain some weight. I'm too skinny.
alt: I'm trying to bulk up. I could use a few more pounds.
note: gain weight = 체중 늘리다 / bulk up = 벌크업/체중 늘리다

korean: 나 편식이 심해서 먹을 수 있는 게 별로 없어
english: I'm a really picky eater so there's not much I can eat.
alt: I have a limited palate. I'm pretty selective with food.
note: picky eater = 편식하는 사람 / selective = 까다로운

korean: 이 향초 냄새 진짜 좋다 어디서 산 거야?
english: This candle smells amazing. Where did you get it?
alt: I love this scent. What brand is this candle?
note: scent = 향 / candle = 향초

korean: 나 매운 거 진짜 못 먹어 순한 맛으로 주세요
english: I really can't handle spicy food. Can I get the mild version please?
alt: Make mine as mild as possible. My stomach can't take anything hot.
note: mild = 순한 / can't handle = 못 먹는

korean: 그 사람 월급 얼마 받는지 물어보면 실례야
english: It's rude to ask someone how much they make.
alt: Asking about someone's salary is kind of a taboo topic.
note: rude = 무례한 / taboo = 금기

korean: 피곤하긴 한데 자기 전에 스트레칭은 해야겠다
english: I'm tired but I should stretch before bed.
alt: Even though I'm exhausted I need to do my nighttime stretches.
note: stretch = 스트레칭 / nighttime = 자기 전

korean: 이 주변에 약국이랑 편의점 같이 있는 데 없어?
english: Is there a pharmacy and convenience store near each other around here?
alt: Any place nearby that has both a drugstore and a corner store?
note: pharmacy = 약국 / corner store = 동네 가게

korean: 이거 몇 칼로리야? 다이어트 중이라 신경 쓰여
english: How many calories is this? I'm on a diet so I need to watch it.
alt: What's the calorie count on this? I'm tracking what I eat.
note: calories = 칼로리 / tracking = 기록하는

korean: 이거 직구로 사면 훨씬 싸
english: Buying this through direct overseas purchase is way cheaper.
alt: If you order it directly from abroad the price drops significantly.
note: direct overseas purchase = 해외직구 / drops significantly = 크게 떨어지다

korean: 그래 이 정도면 만족해 더 바라면 욕심이지
english: I'm satisfied with this. Wanting more would be greedy.
alt: This is more than enough. I shouldn't push my luck.
note: push my luck = 행운을 시험하다

korean: 이거 무료 체험 기간이 있어? 써보고 결정하고 싶어
english: Is there a free trial period? I want to try it before I commit.
alt: Can I test it out first? I'd like to see if it's worth subscribing to.
note: free trial = 무료 체험 / commit = 결정하다

korean: 나 옛날 사진 보다가 웃겨 죽는 줄 알았어
english: I was looking at old photos and I almost died laughing.
alt: Going through my old pictures had me cracking up. What was I wearing?
note: cracking up = 배 잡고 웃는

korean: 이거 교환하려면 어디로 가야 해요?
english: Where do I go to exchange this?
alt: Which counter handles exchanges?
note: exchange = 교환 / counter = 카운터

korean: 무리하지 마 천천히 해도 돼 시간은 충분해
english: Don't overdo it. Take your time. There's plenty of time.
alt: No rush. Go at your own pace. We have all the time in the world.
note: go at your own pace = 네 속도로 해 / plenty of time = 시간 충분

korean: 그거 나도 해봤는데 생각보다 어려워
english: I've tried that too and it's harder than you'd think.
alt: It looks easy but trust me it's way more challenging than it seems.
note: harder than you'd think = 생각보다 어려운

korean: 전화 왔는데 모르는 번호라 안 받았어
english: I got a call from an unknown number so I didn't pick up.
alt: Some random number called me. I let it go to voicemail.
note: unknown number = 모르는 번호 / go to voicemail = 음성메시지로 넘기다

korean: 그래 솔직히 말할게 네 요리는 좀 짜
english: I'll be honest with you. Your cooking is a bit salty.
alt: Don't take this the wrong way but you might want to ease up on the salt.
note: don't take this the wrong way = 나쁘게 듣지 마 / ease up on = ~을 줄이다

korean: 코가 막혀서 맛을 못 느끼겠어
english: My nose is so stuffed up I can't taste anything.
alt: I've completely lost my sense of taste because of this congestion.
note: stuffed up = 막힌 / congestion = 코막힘

korean: 나 그 뉴스 보고 충격받았어 말이 안 나왔어
english: I was shocked when I saw that news. I was completely at a loss for words.
alt: That news hit me hard. I literally had no words.
note: at a loss for words = 할 말을 잃은 / hit me hard = 크게 충격을 준

korean: 이 가게 단골이야 매번 올 때마다 잘 해줘
english: I'm a regular here. They always treat me well.
alt: I come here all the time. The staff knows me by now.
note: regular = 단골 / knows me by now = 이제 나를 알아보는

korean: 고맙다는 말 밖에 할 수가 없네 정말 감사해
english: All I can say is thank you. I truly appreciate it.
alt: Words can't express how grateful I am. Thank you from the bottom of my heart.
note: from the bottom of my heart = 진심으로 / grateful = 감사한

korean: 이 구두 좀 깔창 넣으면 편할 것 같은데
english: These shoes would be more comfortable with insoles.
alt: I think adding insoles would make a big difference comfort-wise.
note: insoles = 깔창 / comfort-wise = 편안함 면에서

korean: 요즘 날씨 진짜 변덕스럽다 아침에는 춥고 낮에는 더워
english: The weather has been so unpredictable lately. Cold in the morning and hot by afternoon.
alt: I never know what to wear these days. The temperature swings are crazy.
note: unpredictable = 변덕스러운 / temperature swings = 기온 변화

korean: 이거 포인트 적립 되나요?
english: Can I earn points on this purchase?
alt: Does this qualify for the rewards program?
note: earn points = 포인트 적립 / rewards program = 적립 프로그램

korean: 나 진짜 귀찮은데 해야 하니까 하는 거야
english: I really don't feel like doing this but I have no choice.
alt: I'm only doing this because I have to. Not because I want to.
note: have no choice = 선택의 여지가 없는

korean: 여기 냉난방이 안 돼? 왜 이렇게 추워?
english: Is there no heating in here? Why is it so cold?
alt: Does this place not have climate control? I'm freezing.
note: heating = 난방 / climate control = 냉난방

korean: 이 향이 너무 독해 환기 좀 시키자
english: This smell is too strong. Let's air the room out.
alt: Open a window. The fumes are overwhelming.
note: air out = 환기하다 / fumes = 연기/냄새

korean: 나 요즘 비타민 챙겨 먹고 있어 면역력 높이려고
english: I've been taking vitamins lately to boost my immune system.
alt: I'm on a vitamin regimen now. Trying to stay healthy.
note: boost immune system = 면역력 높이다 / regimen = 관리 계획

korean: 이 색깔 실물이 더 예뻐 화면으로는 색감이 안 나와
english: The color is even prettier in person. The screen doesn't capture it right.
alt: This looks way better in real life. Photos don't show the true color.
note: in person / in real life = 실물로 / true color = 실제 색상

korean: 와 벌써 이 시간이야? 시간 가는 줄 몰랐네
english: Wow is it that time already? I completely lost track of time.
alt: I had no idea it was this late. Time just flew by.
note: lost track of time = 시간 가는 줄 몰랐다 / flew by = 훌쩍 지나간

korean: 일단 먹어봐 생각보다 맛있을걸
english: Just try it first. You might like it more than you think.
alt: Give it a taste. It's better than it looks I promise.
note: give it a taste = 맛봐 / better than it looks = 보기보다 나은

korean: 오늘 운동 빡세게 했더니 온몸이 쑤셔
english: I worked out really hard today and my whole body is sore.
alt: I pushed myself too hard at the gym. Everything aches.
note: sore = 아픈 / aches = 쑤시다

korean: 나 노래방 가고 싶어 스트레스 풀어야 해
english: I want to go to karaoke. I need to let off some steam.
alt: Let's hit the noraebang. I need to sing my stress away.
note: karaoke = 노래방 / let off steam = 스트레스 풀다

korean: 여기 테이블 예약 돼 있는 건가요? 빈 것 같은데
english: Is this table reserved? It looks empty.
alt: Is anyone sitting here or can we take this spot?
note: reserved = 예약된 / take this spot = 이 자리 차지하다

korean: 나 그 얘기 처음 듣는데? 왜 나한테만 안 알려줬어?
english: That's the first I'm hearing about it. Why wasn't I told?
alt: I'm completely out of the loop. How come nobody told me?
note: out of the loop = 소식을 모르는 / how come = 왜

korean: 이거 방수 되나요? 비 올 때도 쓸 수 있어야 하는데
english: Is this waterproof? I need to be able to use it in the rain.
alt: Does it hold up in the rain? Water resistance is a must for me.
note: waterproof = 방수 / water resistance = 방수성

korean: 그래 인생 뭐 있어 하고 싶은 거 하면서 살자
english: Life's too short. Let's just do what makes us happy.
alt: You only live once right? Might as well enjoy it.
note: YOLO = You only live once / might as well = 차라리

korean: 내일 날씨 좋으면 소풍 가자 비 오면 취소야
english: Let's have a picnic tomorrow if the weather's nice. If it rains we cancel.
alt: We'll go for a picnic weather permitting. Rain plan is just staying home.
note: weather permitting = 날씨가 허락하면 / rain plan = 우천 시 계획

korean: 저 사람 왜 저렇게 쳐다봐? 뭐 묻었나?
english: Why is that person staring at me like that? Do I have something on my face?
alt: Am I imagining things or is someone staring? Is there something on me?
note: staring = 쳐다보는 / something on my face = 얼굴에 뭐 묻은

korean: 요즘 배달앱 수수료가 너무 비싸졌어
english: Delivery app fees have gotten ridiculously expensive lately.
alt: The service charges on food delivery apps are insane now.
note: delivery app fees = 배달앱 수수료 / service charges = 서비스 요금

korean: 컴퓨터 새로 사야 하는데 맥으로 갈까 윈도우로 갈까
english: I need a new computer. Should I go Mac or Windows?
alt: I'm torn between getting a Mac or a PC. What do you think?
note: torn between = 사이에서 갈등 / Mac vs Windows = 맥 vs 윈도우

korean: 드디어 금요일이다 이번 주 진짜 길었어
english: Finally it's Friday. This week felt like an eternity.
alt: TGIF! This has been the longest week ever.
note: TGIF = Thank God It's Friday / eternity = 영원

korean: 너 진짜 요리 실력 늘었다 전에는 라면도 못 끓였잖아
english: Your cooking has really improved. You couldn't even make ramen before.
alt: Look at you cooking like a pro now. You've come a long way.
note: improved = 향상된 / come a long way = 많이 성장한

korean: 통화 품질이 안 좋아 다시 전화할게
english: The call quality is terrible. Let me call you back.
alt: I keep breaking up. Hang up and I'll try again.
note: call quality = 통화 품질 / breaking up = 끊기는

korean: 그 사람 보는 눈이 높아 아무나 안 만나
english: They have high standards. They don't just date anyone.
alt: They're super selective when it comes to relationships.
note: high standards = 높은 기준 / selective = 까다로운

korean: 이 운동화 쿠션이 진짜 좋다 오래 걸어도 안 아파
english: The cushioning on these sneakers is amazing. I can walk all day without pain.
alt: These shoes have incredible support. My feet don't get tired at all.
note: cushioning = 쿠션 / support = 지지력

korean: 그 사고 소식 듣고 너무 놀랐어 다행히 크게 안 다쳤대
english: I was so shocked to hear about the accident. Thankfully nobody was seriously hurt.
alt: That accident news scared me. I'm relieved the injuries weren't severe.
note: thankfully = 다행히 / severe = 심각한

korean: 이거 스팸 문자야 무시해 누르면 안 돼
english: That's a spam text. Ignore it. Don't click on anything.
alt: It's a scam message. Whatever you do don't tap any links.
note: spam / scam = 스팸/사기 / don't tap = 누르지 마

korean: 아 벌써 배고파 점심 먹은 지 2시간밖에 안 됐는데
english: I'm already hungry again. It's only been two hours since lunch.
alt: How am I hungry already? I just ate.
note: already hungry = 벌써 배고픈

korean: 나 야채 싫어하는데 건강 때문에 억지로 먹고 있어
english: I hate vegetables but I force myself to eat them for my health.
alt: I'm making myself eat my veggies even though I can't stand them.
note: force myself = 억지로 / can't stand = 싫어하는

korean: 이 노트북 성능이 전에 쓰던 거보다 훨씬 좋아
english: This laptop's performance is way better than my old one.
alt: The upgrade was totally worth it. This thing runs so much smoother.
note: performance = 성능 / runs smoother = 더 부드럽게 돌아가는

korean: 지금 출발해도 늦을 것 같아 서두르자
english: Even if we leave now we'll probably be late. Let's hurry.
alt: We're already cutting it close. We need to move.
note: cutting it close = 빠듯한 / move = 움직이다

korean: 고생 많았어 오늘 하루 수고했어
english: You worked hard today. Great job.
alt: Well done today. Go home and get some well-deserved rest.
note: well-deserved rest = 마땅히 받을 만한 휴식

korean: 나 혼밥 자주 해 혼자 먹는 거 전혀 안 어색해
english: I eat alone all the time. I'm totally comfortable dining solo.
alt: Solo dining is my thing. I actually prefer eating alone sometimes.
note: dining solo = 혼밥 / comfortable = 편한

korean: 피부가 건조해서 갈라지고 있어 보습 크림 필요해
english: My skin is so dry it's cracking. I need a moisturizer.
alt: I need a heavy-duty moisturizer. My skin is peeling from the dryness.
note: moisturizer = 보습 크림 / cracking/peeling = 갈라지는

korean: 이건 선물용이에요 따로 포장 좀 부탁드려요
english: This is a gift. Could you wrap it separately, please?
alt: Can I get this gift-wrapped with a ribbon and everything?
note: gift-wrapped = 선물 포장된 / separately = 따로

korean: 너 시력 괜찮아? 요즘 눈 자주 비비는 것 같은데
english: Is your eyesight okay? I've noticed you rubbing your eyes a lot lately.
alt: You keep squinting. Maybe you should get your eyes checked.
note: eyesight = 시력 / squinting = 눈을 가늘게 뜨는

korean: 우리 엄마가 해준 된장찌개가 세상에서 제일 맛있어
english: My mom's doenjang-jjigae is the best in the entire world.
alt: Nobody makes soybean paste stew like my mother. It's unmatched.
note: unmatched = 비할 바 없는

korean: 결혼식 축의금 얼마나 하는 게 적당해?
english: How much should I give as a wedding gift? What's appropriate?
alt: What's the standard amount for wedding cash gifts these days?
note: wedding gift = 축의금 / appropriate = 적절한

korean: 이거 한정판이래 지금 안 사면 품절될 수도 있어
english: This is a limited edition. If you don't buy it now it might sell out.
alt: It's limited stock. Once it's gone it's gone.
note: limited edition = 한정판 / sell out = 품절되다

korean: 나 옛날에 여기서 첫 데이트 했었는데 추억이다
english: I had my first date here back in the day. Good memories.
alt: This is where we went on our first date. Takes me back.
note: back in the day = 옛날에 / takes me back = 추억에 잠기게 하다

korean: 이 근처에 공원 있어? 좀 걷고 싶어
english: Is there a park nearby? I feel like going for a walk.
alt: I need some fresh air. Any parks around here?
note: fresh air = 신선한 공기 / going for a walk = 산책

korean: 아까 말한 거 취소 다시 생각해보니까 네 말이 맞아
english: Forget what I said earlier. On second thought you were right.
alt: I take back what I said. After thinking it over I agree with you.
note: on second thought = 다시 생각해보니 / take back = 취소하다

korean: 이 주변에 카페 겸 작업 공간 있어? 조용한 데서 일하고 싶어
english: Are there any cafe-style workspaces around here? I need a quiet place to work.
alt: Is there a coworking space or quiet cafe nearby where I can get some work done?
note: coworking space = 코워킹 스페이스

korean: 이거 조립 설명서가 영어밖에 없어 읽기 힘들어
english: The assembly instructions are only in English. It's hard to read.
alt: Is there a Korean version of the manual? I can't follow the English one.
note: assembly instructions = 조립 설명서 / manual = 매뉴얼

korean: 나 올해 안에 토익 900 넘기는 게 목표야
english: My goal is to score above 900 on TOEIC by the end of this year.
alt: I'm aiming for a 900+ TOEIC score before December.
note: score above = ~점 넘기다 / aiming for = ~를 목표로 하다

korean: 그 식당 웨이터가 되게 친절했어 서비스가 좋더라
english: The waiter at that restaurant was really friendly. Great service.
alt: The staff there was super attentive and welcoming.
note: attentive = 세심한 / welcoming = 환영하는

korean: 오늘 하늘 진짜 맑다 구름 한 점 없어
english: The sky is crystal clear today. Not a single cloud.
alt: What a gorgeous day. The sky is completely cloudless.
note: crystal clear = 맑은 / cloudless = 구름 없는

korean: 너 진짜 체력 좋다 나는 벌써 지쳤는데
english: You have incredible stamina. I'm already exhausted.
alt: How are you not tired? I've been done for a while now.
note: stamina = 체력/스태미나 / exhausted = 지친

korean: 이것저것 비교해보다가 결국 제일 처음 본 걸로 샀어
english: After comparing everything I ended up buying the first one I saw.
alt: I went full circle and picked the one I started with.
note: went full circle = 한 바퀴 돌아 원점으로 / comparing = 비교하는

korean: 아 매워 물 좀 줘 입 안에 불났어
english: It's so spicy! Get me some water. My mouth is on fire.
alt: Water! My mouth is literally burning. This is insanely hot.
note: mouth is on fire = 입 안에 불난 / insanely hot = 미치게 매운

korean: 최근에 좋은 팟캐스트 들은 거 있어? 추천 좀 해줘
english: Have you listened to any good podcasts lately? Any recommendations?
alt: I need a new podcast to listen to on my commute. What do you suggest?
note: podcast = 팟캐스트 / commute = 통근

korean: 이 동네가 요즘 뜨는 곳이야 새 가게가 계속 생겨
english: This neighborhood is up and coming. New shops keep popping up.
alt: This area is really trending right now. There's something new every week.
note: up and coming = 뜨는 / popping up = 생겨나는

korean: 그 사람 정이 많아 한번 친해지면 정말 잘 챙겨줘
english: They're really caring. Once you get close to them they look out for you.
alt: They have a big heart. They always make sure the people around them are taken care of.
note: caring = 돌보는 / look out for = 챙기다

korean: 이거 가죽이야 인조가죽이야?
english: Is this real leather or faux leather?
alt: Is this genuine leather or synthetic?
note: real/genuine leather = 진짜 가죽 / faux/synthetic = 인조

korean: 방금 그 말 진심이야? 농담이라고 해줘
english: Did you mean that? Please tell me you were joking.
alt: You're kidding right? Please say you didn't mean that.
note: mean that = 진심이야 / kidding = 농담하는

korean: 나 이제 진짜 정리 정돈의 달인이 됐어
english: I've become a true master of organization.
alt: I'm like a professional organizer now. Everything has its place.
note: master of organization = 정리의 달인

korean: 점심 값 내가 카드로 한꺼번에 긁을 테니까 나중에 보내줘
english: I'll put lunch on my card. Just send me your share later.
alt: Let me pay for everyone and you guys can Venmo me after.
note: put it on my card = 내 카드로 긁다 / share = 몫

korean: 이거 언제 출시되는 거야? 사전 예약 되나?
english: When does this come out? Can I pre-order?
alt: Is this available for pre-order? When's the release date?
note: pre-order = 사전 예약 / release date = 출시일

korean: 내 기억이 맞으면 거기 작년에 갔었는데
english: If I remember correctly we went there last year.
alt: Unless my memory is failing me we visited that place last year.
note: if I remember correctly = 내 기억이 맞으면

korean: 여기 무한리필이에요? 리필 되나요?
english: Is this all-you-can-eat? Can I get refills?
alt: Are refills included or do I have to pay extra?
note: all-you-can-eat = 무한리필 / refills = 리필

korean: 나 사실 고소공포증 있어 높은 데 가면 다리가 떨려
english: I actually have a fear of heights. My legs shake when I'm up high.
alt: I'm acrophobic. Even looking down from a few stories makes me dizzy.
note: fear of heights / acrophobic = 고소공포증 / dizzy = 어지러운

korean: 나 요즘 하루에 물 2리터씩 마시려고 노력 중이야
english: I'm trying to drink two liters of water a day.
alt: I've been making a conscious effort to stay hydrated. Two liters daily.
note: stay hydrated = 수분 보충하다 / conscious effort = 의식적 노력

korean: 오늘 진짜 고생했어 맛있는 거 먹으러 가자
english: You've worked really hard today. Let's go eat something delicious.
alt: What a day. Let's treat ourselves to a nice meal.
note: treat ourselves = 자신에게 보상하다 / what a day = 정말 힘든 하루

korean: 나 이 옷 세 번째 입는 건데 아무도 몰라
english: This is the third time I've worn this outfit and nobody's noticed.
alt: I keep rewearing this and no one has said a thing.
note: rewearing = 다시 입는 / outfit = 옷차림

korean: 배송 추적 번호 좀 알려줘 지금 어디쯤 왔는지 확인할게
english: Can you give me the tracking number? I want to see where it is.
alt: Share the delivery tracking info so I can check the status.
note: tracking number = 추적 번호 / status = 상태

korean: 그래 우리 다음에 또 만나자 연락하고 지내자
english: Alright let's meet up again. Let's keep in touch.
alt: It was great seeing you. Don't be a stranger okay?
note: keep in touch = 연락하고 지내다 / don't be a stranger = 연락해

korean: 이 소스 뭐가 들어가 있어요? 알레르기 때문에 확인해야 해서
english: What's in this sauce? I need to check because of my allergies.
alt: Could you tell me the ingredients in this? I have food allergies.
note: ingredients = 재료 / food allergies = 식품 알레르기

korean: 머리 아프다 오늘 잠을 너무 못 자서 그런가
english: My head hurts. I think it's because I barely slept last night.
alt: I've got a headache that won't go away. Probably from lack of sleep.
note: headache = 두통 / lack of sleep = 수면 부족

korean: 진짜 마지막 한 마디만 할게 그리고 끝내자
english: Just one last thing and then I'll drop it.
alt: Let me say one more thing and we can move on. I promise.
note: drop it = 그만 얘기하다 / move on = 넘어가다

korean: 이건 누가 봐도 네 잘못이야 인정해
english: This is clearly your fault. Just own up to it.
alt: There's no way around it. You need to take responsibility.
note: own up to = 인정하다 / take responsibility = 책임지다

korean: 지금 바로 시작하자 더 늦기 전에
english: Let's start right now before it gets any later.
alt: No more delays. Let's dive in.
note: dive in = 바로 시작하다 / delays = 지체

korean: 이 음식 사진 찍어도 돼요? SNS에 올리려고요
english: Mind if I take a photo of the food? I want to post it on social media.
alt: Can I snap a quick pic of this dish? It looks too good not to share.
note: snap a pic = 사진 찍다 / post = 올리다

korean: 자리가 불편한데 바꿀 수 있을까요?
english: This seat is uncomfortable. Can I switch to a different one?
alt: Would it be possible to move to another seat? This one isn't great.
note: switch = 바꾸다

korean: 나 이제 진짜 정신 차려야 해 놀기만 했어
english: I really need to get my act together. All I've been doing is goofing off.
alt: It's time to buckle down. I've been slacking off way too much.
note: get my act together = 정신 차리다 / slacking off = 게으름 부리다

korean: 그 사람이랑 화해했어? 아직도 어색해?
english: Did you make up with them? Is it still awkward?
alt: Are things still weird between you two or did you patch things up?
note: make up / patch things up = 화해하다 / awkward = 어색한

korean: 나 집순이야 밖에 나가는 거 별로 안 좋아해
english: I'm a total homebody. I'm not big on going out.
alt: I prefer staying in over going out. Home is my happy place.
note: homebody = 집순이/집돌이 / staying in = 집에 있는

korean: 그 드라마 결말 진짜 허무했어 열린 결말이었어
english: That drama's ending was so anticlimactic. It was left open-ended.
alt: The finale was such a letdown. They didn't even wrap up the story properly.
note: anticlimactic = 허무한 / open-ended = 열린 결말

korean: 여기 현금 영수증 발급 되나요?
english: Can I get a cash receipt?
alt: Do you issue cash receipts for tax purposes?
note: cash receipt = 현금 영수증

korean: 나 새벽에 운동하는 게 습관이 됐어 아침이 달라져
english: I've made a habit of working out at dawn. My mornings feel completely different.
alt: Early morning exercise has become my routine and it's transformed my day.
note: made a habit = 습관이 된 / transformed = 변화시킨

korean: 일 끝나면 연락해 같이 저녁 먹자
english: Hit me up when you're off work. Let's grab dinner together.
alt: Text me when you clock out. We'll go eat.
note: hit me up = 연락해 / clock out = 퇴근하다

korean: 이 약 하루에 몇 번 먹어야 해요?
english: How many times a day should I take this medicine?
alt: What's the dosage? How often do I need to take it?
note: dosage = 복용량 / how often = 얼마나 자주

korean: 요즘 전세 사기가 많아서 무서워 계약할 때 조심해야 해
english: Housing scams are everywhere these days. Be extra careful when signing a lease.
alt: Watch out for rental fraud. Do your due diligence before signing anything.
note: housing scams = 전세 사기 / due diligence = 사전 조사

korean: 이 음료 칼로리 낮은 거로 바꿀 수 있어요?
english: Can I get a low-calorie version of this drink?
alt: Is there a lighter option? I'm watching my calorie intake.
note: low-calorie = 저칼로리 / lighter option = 가벼운 선택지

korean: 너 진짜 끈기 있다 나라면 진작에 포기했을 거야
english: You have such great perseverance. I would've given up long ago.
alt: Your persistence is admirable. Most people would've thrown in the towel by now.
note: perseverance / persistence = 끈기 / thrown in the towel = 포기한

korean: 이거 면세 적용 가능한가요? 여행자인데요
english: Can I get tax-free on this? I'm a tourist.
alt: Am I eligible for a tax refund? I'm traveling from abroad.
note: tax-free = 면세 / tax refund = 세금 환급

korean: 왜 이렇게 잠이 안 오지 양 세는 것도 안 통해
english: Why can't I fall asleep? Even counting sheep isn't working.
alt: I've tried everything and I still can't drift off. Insomnia is the worst.
note: counting sheep = 양 세기 / drift off = 잠들다 / insomnia = 불면증

korean: 그 사람 말투가 부드러워서 같이 이야기하면 편해
english: They have such a gentle way of speaking. It's really comfortable talking to them.
alt: Their tone is so calming. Conversations with them always feel easy.
note: gentle = 부드러운 / calming = 차분한

korean: 오늘 몇 보 걸었어? 만보기 켜놨어?
english: How many steps did you take today? Did you have your pedometer on?
alt: Check your step count. I bet we walked more than ten thousand today.
note: pedometer = 만보기 / step count = 걸음 수

korean: 진짜 마지막이다 이번엔 진짜로 간다
english: This is really the last time. I'm actually leaving now for real.
alt: Okay I'm really going this time. For real. No more goodbyes.
note: for real = 진짜로 / no more goodbyes = 작별 그만

korean: 나 요즘 팟캐스트 하나 시작했어 생각보다 재밌어
english: I started a podcast recently and it's actually more fun than I expected.
alt: I launched my own podcast. It's been a surprisingly enjoyable experience.
note: launched = 시작한 / surprisingly = 놀랍게도

korean: 그 사람은 약속을 꼭 지켜 진짜 믿을 수 있는 사람이야
english: They always keep their promises. They're someone you can truly count on.
alt: Their word is their bond. You can rely on them one hundred percent.
note: keep promises = 약속을 지키다 / count on = 의지하다

korean: 나 지금 너무 행복해서 무서워 이게 꿈일까 봐
english: I'm so happy right now it scares me. I'm afraid it might be a dream.
alt: This level of happiness feels almost too good to be true. I'm worried it won't last.
note: too good to be true = 사실이라기엔 너무 좋은

korean: 와 드디어 끝났다 이제 쉬자
english: Finally it's over! Let's rest now.
alt: We made it! Time to kick back and relax.
note: kick back = 편히 쉬다 / we made it = 해냈다

korean: 나 이 커뮤니티에서 정보 많이 얻어 되게 유용해
english: I get a ton of useful information from this online community.
alt: This forum has been such a valuable resource. I learn something new every day.
note: online community / forum = 커뮤니티 / valuable resource = 유용한 자원

korean: 주말에 플리마켓 가볼래? 빈티지 물건 구경하러
english: Want to hit up the flea market this weekend? We can browse vintage stuff.
alt: There's a flea market on Saturday. Let's go treasure hunting.
note: flea market = 플리마켓 / vintage = 빈티지 / treasure hunting = 보물찾기

korean: 오늘 하루도 무사히 끝났다 감사한 하루야
english: Another day safely done. I'm grateful for today.
alt: Made it through another day in one piece. Life is good.
note: in one piece = 무사히 / grateful = 감사한

korean: 다들 수고했어 오늘도 고생 많았어 내일 보자
english: Good work everyone. Thanks for all the effort today. See you tomorrow.
alt: Great job team. Get some rest and we'll hit it again tomorrow.
note: great job team = 팀 수고했어 / hit it again = 다시 시작하다

korean: 넷플릭스 뭐 볼 거 없나? 다 본 것 같아
english: Is there anything good on Netflix? I feel like I've seen everything.
alt: Nothing to watch on Netflix? I think I've watched it all.
note: I feel like = ~한 느낌이다. 주관적 느낌 표현

korean: 이 앱 자꾸 튕겨서 짜증나
english: This app keeps crashing and it's so annoying.
alt: This app keeps freezing on me. So frustrating.
note: crash = 앱이 강제 종료되다 / freeze = 멈추다

korean: 유튜브 알고리즘이 나를 너무 잘 알아
english: The YouTube algorithm knows me too well.
alt: YouTube really has me figured out with its recommendations.
note: have someone figured out = ~를 완전히 파악하다

korean: 와이파이 비번 뭐야?
english: What's the Wi-Fi password?
alt: Can I get the Wi-Fi password?
note: password를 줄여서 비번이라 하듯 영어도 Wi-Fi password로 통용

korean: 핸드폰 배터리 1%인데 충전기 있어?
english: My phone's at 1%. Do you have a charger?
alt: My phone's about to die. Got a charger?
note: about to die = 곧 꺼질 것 같다. 배터리 관련 자주 쓰는 표현

korean: 인스타 스토리 올린 거 봤어?
english: Did you see what I posted on my story?
alt: Did you check out my Instagram story?
note: post on my story = 스토리에 올리다

korean: 이 틱톡 영상 진짜 웃겨 봐봐
english: This TikTok is hilarious. You gotta see this.
alt: Check out this TikTok. It's so funny.
note: You gotta = You have got to의 축약. 강력 추천 시 사용

korean: 스트리밍 구독료가 점점 올라서 부담돼
english: Streaming subscriptions keep getting more expensive, and it's adding up.
alt: All these streaming services are getting pricier. It's too much.
note: add up = 합치면 꽤 되다 / pricier = 더 비싼

korean: 이 게임 렉 걸려서 못 하겠어
english: This game is so laggy I can't even play.
alt: The lag in this game is unbearable.
note: laggy = 렉 걸리는 / unbearable = 견딜 수 없는

korean: 비밀번호를 까먹어서 계정 잠겼어
english: I forgot my password and got locked out of my account.
alt: I got locked out because I couldn't remember my password.
note: get locked out = 접근이 차단되다

korean: 새 업데이트 하고 나니까 더 느려졌어
english: It got even slower after the new update.
alt: The latest update made it worse somehow.
note: somehow = 어째서인지. 의아함 표현

korean: 블루투스 연결이 자꾸 끊겨
english: The Bluetooth connection keeps dropping.
alt: My Bluetooth keeps disconnecting.
note: keep + ~ing = 자꾸 ~하다 / drop = 연결이 끊기다

korean: 팟캐스트 추천해 줘 출퇴근할 때 들을 거
english: Recommend me a podcast for my commute.
alt: Know any good podcasts I can listen to on my way to work?
note: commute = 출퇴근 / on my way to work = 출근길에

korean: 이 필터 쓰니까 피부 좋아 보인다
english: This filter makes my skin look amazing.
alt: I look so much better with this filter on.
note: make + 목적어 + 동사원형 = ~를 ~하게 만들다

korean: 데이터 다 써서 와이파이 잡아야 해
english: I used up all my data so I need to find Wi-Fi.
alt: I'm out of data. I need to connect to Wi-Fi.
note: use up = 다 소진하다 / be out of = ~가 없다

korean: 이 유튜버 영상은 항상 10분 넘겨서 길어
english: This YouTuber's videos are always over 10 minutes. They're so long.
alt: This creator always makes videos longer than 10 minutes.
note: creator는 YouTuber 대신 자주 쓰이는 표현

korean: 게임 한 판만 더 하고 잘게
english: Just one more game and I'll go to bed.
alt: Let me play one more round and then I'll sleep.
note: one more round = 한 판 더. 게임/술 등에 사용

korean: 알림 너무 많아서 다 꺼버렸어
english: I turned off all my notifications because there were too many.
alt: I got so many notifications I just disabled them all.
note: turn off = 끄다 / disable = 비활성화하다

korean: 채팅방 나가고 싶은데 눈치 보여
