english: I want to leave the group chat but I feel awkward about it.
alt: I wanna dip from the group chat but I don't want to be rude.
note: dip = 슬쩍 빠지다 (슬랭) / feel awkward = 눈치 보이다

korean: AI가 대신 해주니까 세상 편해졌어
english: Life's gotten so much easier with AI doing things for us.
alt: AI makes everything so convenient these days.
note: Life's gotten = Life has gotten. 변화 표현

korean: 오늘 날씨 진짜 좋다 나가고 싶어
english: The weather's so nice today. I wanna go outside.
alt: What a beautiful day. I feel like going out.
note: I wanna = I want to의 구어체

korean: 갑자기 소나기 와서 다 젖었어
english: I got caught in a sudden downpour and got soaked.
alt: A random rain shower came out of nowhere and I'm drenched.
note: get soaked / get drenched = 흠뻑 젖다

korean: 바람이 너무 세서 우산이 뒤집혔어
english: The wind was so strong my umbrella flipped inside out.
alt: My umbrella turned inside out from the wind.
note: flip inside out = 뒤집히다

korean: 오늘 체감온도가 영하 15도래
english: The wind chill is supposed to be minus 15 today.
alt: They say the real feel is negative 15 today.
note: wind chill = 체감온도 / real feel도 같은 뜻

korean: 여름이라 습해서 빨래가 안 말라
english: It's so humid in the summer that the laundry won't dry.
alt: The humidity in summer makes it impossible to dry clothes.
note: humid = 습한 / won't dry = 안 마르다

korean: 벚꽃 피면 한강 가자
english: Let's go to the Han River when the cherry blossoms bloom.
alt: When the cherry blossoms are out we should hit up the Han River.
note: bloom = 꽃이 피다 / hit up = ~에 가다 (캐주얼)

korean: 가을 하늘 진짜 높고 예쁘다
english: The autumn sky is so clear and beautiful, isn't it?
alt: The sky in fall is just gorgeous.
note: clear = 맑은 / gorgeous = 매우 아름다운

korean: 눈 오는 날 집에서 따뜻한 코코아 마시면 최고지
english: Nothing beats sipping hot cocoa at home on a snowy day.
alt: Hot cocoa on a snowy day at home is the best thing ever.
note: nothing beats = ~만한 게 없다

korean: 미세먼지 심해서 마스크 꼭 써야 해
english: The fine dust is really bad today, so you definitely need a mask.
alt: The air quality is terrible. Make sure to wear a mask.
note: fine dust = 미세먼지 / air quality = 공기질

korean: 요즘 일교차가 심해서 뭘 입어야 할지 모르겠어
english: The temperature difference between day and night is huge. I don't know what to wear.
alt: The weather's been so unpredictable I can't figure out what to wear.
note: temperature difference = 일교차 / unpredictable = 예측 불가한

korean: 장마 언제 끝나? 비 너무 많이 온다
english: When is the rainy season going to end? It's been raining nonstop.
alt: This monsoon season needs to end. It won't stop raining.
note: rainy season = 장마 / monsoon도 사용 가능

korean: 햇볕이 너무 강해서 선크림 발라야 해
english: The sun is brutal today. I need to put on sunscreen.
alt: It's so sunny I need to slather on some sunscreen.
note: brutal = 강렬한 / slather on = 듬뿍 바르다

korean: 이렇게 더운 날엔 아이스 아메리카노가 최고야
english: On a day this hot nothing beats an iced Americano.
alt: An iced coffee is a must on a scorching day like this.
note: scorching = 매우 더운 / nothing beats = ~만한 게 없다

korean: 첫눈 왔어! 올해도 어김없이
english: The first snow is here! Right on time, like every year.
alt: First snow of the year! It never fails to come.
note: right on time = 어김없이 / it never fails = 반드시 ~하다

korean: 오늘 안개 끼어서 앞이 안 보여
english: It's so foggy today I can barely see ahead.
alt: The fog is so thick I can't see a thing.
note: foggy = 안개 낀 / barely = 거의 ~못하다

korean: 설거지 좀 해줄 수 있어? 내가 요리했잖아
english: Can you do the dishes? I cooked.
alt: It's your turn to wash up. I made dinner.
note: do the dishes = wash up = 설거지하다

korean: 빨래 널어놓는 거 깜빡했어
english: I forgot to hang up the laundry.
alt: I totally spaced on hanging the laundry out to dry.
note: hang up the laundry = 빨래를 널다 / space on = 깜빡하다

korean: 청소기 좀 돌려 먼지 쌓였어
english: Can you vacuum? The dust is piling up.
alt: Run the vacuum. It's getting dusty in here.
note: vacuum = 청소기 돌리다 / pile up = 쌓이다

korean: 화장실 청소는 내가 제일 싫어하는 집안일이야
english: Cleaning the bathroom is my least favorite chore.
alt: I hate cleaning the bathroom more than any other chore.
note: chore = 집안일 / least favorite = 가장 싫어하는

korean: 분리수거 오늘이야 내놓자
english: Recycling goes out today. Let's take it out.
alt: Today's recycling day. Let's put it out.
note: recycling day = 분리수거 날 / put out = 내놓다

korean: 냉장고 정리 좀 해야겠다 뭐가 이렇게 많아
english: I need to clean out the fridge. There's so much stuff in here.
alt: The fridge needs a serious cleanout. It's packed.
note: clean out = 정리하다 / packed = 가득 찬

korean: 이불 빨래 하려면 코인 세탁소 가야 해
english: I need to go to a laundromat to wash my comforter.
alt: The comforter's too big for my machine. I need the laundromat.
note: laundromat = 코인 세탁소 / comforter = 이불

korean: 식기세척기 돌려놓을게
english: I'll run the dishwasher.
alt: Let me start the dishwasher.
note: run the dishwasher = 식기세척기를 돌리다

korean: 걸레질 좀 해야겠다 바닥이 끈적거려
english: I need to mop. The floor's sticky.
alt: The floor feels sticky. Time to mop.
note: mop = 걸레질하다 / sticky = 끈적거리는

korean: 다리미 어디 있어? 셔츠 구겨졌어
english: Where's the iron? My shirt is all wrinkled.
alt: Do we have an iron? My shirt is creased.
note: wrinkled = creased = 구겨진 / iron = 다리미(질하다)

korean: 옷 정리 좀 해야 되는데 옷장이 터질 것 같아
english: I need to organize my closet. It's about to burst.
alt: My closet is overflowing. I really need to sort through my clothes.
note: about to burst = 터질 것 같은 / overflowing = 넘치는

korean: 환기 좀 시키자 공기가 텁텁해
english: Let's air out the room. It feels stuffy.
alt: Open a window. It's so stuffy in here.
note: air out = 환기시키다 / stuffy = 텁텁한

korean: 주차할 데가 없어 한 바퀴 더 돌자
english: There's no parking. Let's go around one more time.
alt: I can't find a spot. Let me circle the block again.
note: circle the block = 한 바퀴 돌다 / spot = 주차 자리

korean: 내비가 자꾸 유턴하래
english: The GPS keeps telling me to make a U-turn.
alt: My navigation keeps rerouting me to U-turn.
note: GPS/navigation 둘 다 사용 / reroute = 경로 재설정하다

korean: 지하철 환승이 너무 복잡해
english: Transferring on the subway is so confusing.
alt: The subway transfers here are a nightmare.
note: transfer = 환승하다 / a nightmare = 악몽 같은 (비유)

korean: 버스 방금 놓쳤어 다음 거 언제 와?
english: I just missed the bus. When's the next one?
alt: The bus just left without me. How long till the next one?
note: miss the bus = 버스를 놓치다

korean: 출퇴근 시간에 지하철 타면 사람에 치여
english: The subway during rush hour is packed like sardines.
alt: You get crushed on the subway during peak hours.
note: packed like sardines = 콩나물시루처럼 / peak hours = 피크 시간

korean: 택시비 너무 올라서 부담돼
english: Taxi fares have gone up so much it's getting expensive.
alt: Cabs are getting ridiculously pricey these days.
note: fare = 요금 / ridiculously = 말도 안 되게

korean: 네비 없으면 길치라 아무 데도 못 가
english: Without GPS I'm so bad with directions I can't go anywhere.
alt: I'm hopeless with directions without my navigation.
note: bad with directions = 길치인 / hopeless = 가망 없는

korean: 기름값이 미쳐서 전기차 사고 싶다
english: Gas prices are insane. I want to get an electric car.
alt: With gas this expensive I'm tempted to go electric.
note: insane = 미친 듯한 / go electric = 전기차로 바꾸다

korean: 고속도로 정체 심해서 늦을 것 같아
english: There's heavy traffic on the highway, so I think I'll be late.
alt: The highway is jammed. I'm probably gonna be late.
note: heavy traffic = 심한 정체 / jammed = 꽉 막힌

korean: 여기서 좌회전하면 되나?
english: Do I turn left here?
alt: Should I make a left here?
note: make a left = turn left의 다른 표현

korean: 앞차가 너무 느려서 답답해
english: The car in front of me is going so slow. It's driving me crazy.
alt: This car ahead is crawling. So frustrating.
note: drive someone crazy = ~를 미치게 하다 / crawl = 느리게 가다

korean: 킥보드 타면 빠른데 위험하긴 해
english: Electric scooters are fast, but they can be dangerous.
alt: E-scooters get you there quick but they're kind of risky.
note: electric scooter = 전동킥보드 / risky = 위험한

korean: 우리 강아지가 산책 가자고 문 앞에서 기다려
english: My dog is waiting by the door wanting to go for a walk.
alt: My pup is sitting by the door begging for a walk.
note: go for a walk = 산책하다 / pup = 강아지 (애칭)

korean: 고양이가 또 키보드 위에 올라왔어
english: My cat climbed on the keyboard again.
alt: The cat's on my keyboard. Again.
note: climb on = 위에 올라오다. 고양이 키보드 올라오는 건 만국 공통

korean: 우리 집 강아지는 분리불안이 좀 있어
english: My dog has a bit of separation anxiety.
alt: Our dog gets anxious when we leave.
note: separation anxiety = 분리불안. 사람에게도 사용

korean: 동물병원 예약해야 하는데 깜빡했어
english: I forgot to make a vet appointment.
alt: I totally blanked on booking the vet.
note: vet = veterinarian의 줄임말 / blank on = 깜빡하다

korean: 우리 고양이 캣닢 주면 완전 미쳐
english: My cat goes absolutely crazy for catnip.
alt: Give my cat catnip and it loses its mind.
note: go crazy for = ~에 열광하다 / lose one's mind = 미치다

korean: 산책 나가니까 강아지가 너무 좋아해서 꼬리를 미친 듯이 흔들어
english: My dog was so happy to go out he was wagging his tail like crazy.
alt: When I grabbed the leash my dog's tail went into overdrive.
note: wag one's tail = 꼬리를 흔들다 / like crazy = 미친 듯이

korean: 유기동물 입양하고 싶은데 어디서 알아봐야 하지?
english: I want to adopt a rescue animal. Where should I look?
alt: Where can I find info about adopting a shelter pet?
note: rescue animal = shelter pet = 유기동물 / adopt = 입양하다

korean: 강아지 발톱 깎아줘야 하는데 가만히 안 있어
english: I need to trim my dog's nails but he won't stay still.
alt: My dog won't sit still for nail clipping.
note: trim nails = 발톱을 깎다 / stay still = 가만히 있다

korean: 고양이 모래 갈아줘야 해
english: I need to change the cat litter.
alt: The litter box needs to be cleaned.
note: cat litter = 고양이 모래 / litter box = 화장실

korean: 우리 강아지 다른 강아지 보면 너무 흥분해
english: My dog gets too excited when he sees other dogs.
alt: My dog goes nuts whenever he spots another dog.
note: go nuts = 흥분하다 / spot = 발견하다

korean: 물고기 키우는 건 생각보다 손이 많이 가
english: Keeping fish is more work than you'd think.
alt: Fish are surprisingly high-maintenance.
note: high-maintenance = 손이 많이 가는 / more than you'd think = 생각보다

korean: 요즘 헬스장 다니기 시작했어
english: I recently started going to the gym.
alt: I just got a gym membership.
note: go to the gym = 헬스장 다니다 / gym membership = 헬스장 회원권

korean: 등산 가고 싶은데 같이 갈 사람 없어
english: I want to go hiking but I don't have anyone to go with.
alt: I'm looking for a hiking buddy.
note: hiking buddy = 등산 친구 / go hiking = 등산하다

korean: 캠핑 가면 밤하늘 별 보는 게 최고야
english: The best part of camping is stargazing at night.
alt: Nothing beats looking at the stars when you're camping.
note: stargazing = 별 보기 / the best part = 최고의 부분

korean: 요즘 뜨개질에 빠졌어
english: I've been really into knitting lately.
alt: I recently got hooked on knitting.
note: get hooked on = ~에 빠지다. knitting과 hooked 언어유희

korean: 주말에 넷플 정주행 했어
english: I binge-watched Netflix all weekend.
alt: I spent the whole weekend on a Netflix marathon.
note: binge-watch = 정주행하다 / marathon = 마라톤 시청

korean: 볼링 치러 갈래? 오랜만에
english: Wanna go bowling? It's been a while.
alt: Feel like hitting the bowling alley? It's been ages.
note: It's been a while = 오랜만이다 / It's been ages = 정말 오래됐다

korean: 요가 하면 확실히 몸이 유연해져
english: Yoga definitely makes you more flexible.
alt: You can really feel the flexibility improvement from yoga.
note: flexible = 유연한 / improvement = 향상

korean: 런닝 하다가 무릎이 아파서 쉬고 있어
english: I hurt my knee while running so I'm taking a break.
alt: My knee's been bothering me from running so I'm resting.
note: take a break = 쉬다 / bother = 아프게 하다

korean: 자전거 타고 한강 가자
english: Let's ride our bikes to the Han River.
alt: How about cycling along the Han River?
note: ride bikes = cycle = 자전거를 타다

korean: 퍼즐 맞추기 생각보다 재밌어
english: Putting together puzzles is more fun than I expected.
alt: Jigsaw puzzles are surprisingly addictive.
note: put together = 맞추다 / addictive = 중독성 있는

korean: 필라테스 하니까 자세가 좋아졌어
english: My posture improved so much since I started Pilates.
alt: Pilates really fixed my posture.
note: posture = 자세 / improve = 개선되다

korean: 주말에 낚시 가는 게 힐링이야
english: Going fishing on weekends is so therapeutic.
alt: Weekend fishing trips are my therapy.
note: therapeutic = 치유되는 / therapy = 치유

korean: 이 라면 국물이 끝내준다
english: The broth of this ramen is incredible.
alt: This ramen broth is out of this world.
note: broth = 국물 / out of this world = 최고의

korean: 이 집 김치찌개가 진짜 칼칼하니 맛있어
english: The kimchi stew here has a nice spicy kick to it.
alt: Their kimchi jjigae is perfectly spicy and flavorful.
note: spicy kick = 칼칼한 맛 / flavorful = 풍미 있는

korean: 간이 좀 심심한데 소금 좀 줄래?
english: It's a bit bland. Can you pass the salt?
alt: This needs a bit more seasoning. Salt please?
note: bland = 심심한 맛 / seasoning = 양념

korean: 이거 불맛 나서 맛있다
english: This has that nice smoky wok flavor.
alt: You can really taste the char on this. So good.
note: smoky = 훈연 향 / wok flavor = 불맛 / char = 그을린 맛

korean: 너무 달아서 좀 느끼해
english: It's too sweet and kind of rich for me.
alt: This is a bit too sugary and heavy.
note: rich = 느끼한 / heavy = 부담스러운 (음식)

korean: 이 빵 겉바속촉이야
english: This bread is crispy on the outside and soft on the inside.
alt: The crust is crunchy but the inside is perfectly fluffy.
note: crispy/crunchy = 바삭한 / soft/fluffy = 촉촉한

korean: 이 떡볶이 존맛이다
english: These tteokbokki are absolutely fire.
alt: This tteokbokki is insanely good.
note: fire = 최고로 맛있는 (슬랭) / insanely good = 미친 듯이 맛있는

korean: 에어프라이어로 하면 간단해
english: Just use the air fryer. It's so easy.
alt: The air fryer makes it super simple.
note: air fryer = 에어프라이어. 전 세계 공통 인기템

korean: 양념치킨이랑 후라이드 반반 시키자
english: Let's get half seasoned and half fried chicken.
alt: How about we order half-and-half chicken?
note: half-and-half = 반반 / seasoned = 양념

korean: 이 카페 디저트가 진짜 예쁘다
english: The desserts at this cafe are so Instagrammable.
alt: This cafe's desserts are almost too pretty to eat.
note: Instagrammable = 인스타 갈만한 / too pretty to eat = 먹기 아까운

korean: 배달 시켜 먹을까? 나가기 귀찮아
english: Should we just order delivery? I don't feel like going out.
alt: Let's get delivery. I'm too lazy to go out.
note: order delivery = 배달 시키다 / feel like = ~하고 싶다

korean: 이거 한 입만 줘봐
english: Let me have just one bite.
alt: Can I try a little bit?
note: one bite = 한 입 / try a bit = 조금 맛보다

korean: 매운 거 잘 먹어? 이거 꽤 매워
english: Can you handle spicy food? This is pretty hot.
alt: Are you good with spice? This one's got some heat.
note: handle spicy food = 매운 거 잘 먹다 / got some heat = 꽤 맵다

korean: 그 옷 너한테 진짜 잘 어울린다
english: That outfit looks amazing on you.
alt: You look great in that.
note: look amazing on someone = ~에게 잘 어울리다

korean: 오늘 뭐 입지? 코디가 안 떠올라
english: What should I wear today? I can't think of an outfit.
alt: I have nothing to wear. I'm out of outfit ideas.
note: outfit = 코디 / out of ideas = 아이디어가 없다

korean: 이 신발 편한데 예쁘기까지 해
english: These shoes are comfortable and cute, too.
alt: These sneakers are both comfy and stylish.
note: comfy = comfortable의 줄임말 / stylish = 세련된

korean: 머리 자르고 싶은데 어디가 잘해?
english: I want to get a haircut. Know any good places?
alt: Where should I go for a haircut? Any recommendations?
note: get a haircut = 머리를 자르다

korean: 요즘 숏컷이 유행이래
english: Short hair is apparently trending right now.
alt: I heard short cuts are all the rage these days.
note: trending = 유행하는 / all the rage = 대유행인

korean: 피부가 건조해서 보습제 발라야 해
english: My skin is so dry I need to put on moisturizer.
alt: I need to moisturize. My skin is flaking.
note: moisturizer = 보습제 / flaking = 각질이 일어나는

korean: 다크서클이 심해서 컨실러 필수야
english: My dark circles are so bad I need concealer.
alt: I can't go without concealer with these dark circles.
note: dark circles = 다크서클 / concealer = 컨실러

korean: 이 향수 무슨 향이야? 좋다
english: What perfume are you wearing? It smells great.
alt: That's a nice scent. What fragrance is that?
note: perfume = fragrance = scent = 향수/향

korean: 옷장에 옷은 많은데 입을 게 없어
english: I have a closet full of clothes but nothing to wear.
alt: My closet is packed but I still feel like I have nothing.
note: closet full of = 옷장 가득 / nothing to wear = 입을 게 없다

korean: 이 바지 좀 큰데 줄일 수 있나?
english: These pants are a bit big. Can they be taken in?
alt: Can I get these altered? They're a little loose.
note: take in = (옷을) 줄이다 / altered = 수선하다 / loose = 헐렁한

korean: 요즘 오버핏이 편해서 좋아
english: I love the oversized fit these days. It's so comfortable.
alt: Oversized clothes are my go-to now. So comfy.
note: oversized = 오버핏 / go-to = 자주 찾는 것

korean: 분리수거 어떻게 하는 거야? 헷갈려
english: How do you sort recycling? I get confused.
alt: I'm confused about how to separate the recycling.
note: sort recycling = separate recycling = 분리수거하다

korean: 텀블러 가져가야지 일회용 컵 줄이려고
english: I should bring my tumbler to cut down on disposable cups.
alt: I'm taking my reusable cup to reduce single-use waste.
note: cut down on = 줄이다 / disposable = 일회용의 / reusable = 재사용

korean: 장바구니 또 안 가져왔어 맨날 깜빡해
english: I forgot my reusable bag again. I always forget.
alt: I keep forgetting to bring my shopping bag.
note: reusable bag = 장바구니 / keep forgetting = 자꾸 잊다

korean: 음식물 쓰레기 냄새 진짜 심하다
english: The food waste smells terrible.
alt: The compost bin reeks.
note: food waste = 음식물 쓰레기 / reek = 악취가 나다

korean: 에코백 쓰는 게 환경에 좋대
english: They say using eco bags is better for the environment.
alt: Apparently tote bags are more eco-friendly.
note: eco-friendly = 친환경적인 / tote bag = 에코백

korean: 플라스틱 줄이려고 노력 중이야
english: I'm trying to reduce my plastic use.
alt: I'm making an effort to cut back on plastic.
note: reduce = cut back on = 줄이다

korean: 이거 재활용이야 일반쓰레기야?
english: Is this recyclable or regular trash?
alt: Does this go in recycling or the garbage?
note: recyclable = 재활용 가능한 / regular trash = garbage = 일반쓰레기

korean: 제로웨이스트 실천하기 쉽지 않아
english: Living zero waste isn't as easy as it sounds.
alt: Going zero waste is harder than people think.
note: zero waste = 제로웨이스트. 영어 그대로 사용

korean: 중고로 사면 훨씬 싸고 환경에도 좋아
english: Buying secondhand is way cheaper and better for the environment, too.
alt: Going with used items saves money and helps the planet.
note: secondhand = used = 중고의 / way cheaper = 훨씬 싼

korean: 전기 아끼려고 안 쓰는 콘센트 다 뽑았어
english: I unplugged everything I'm not using to save electricity.
alt: I pulled all the plugs on unused appliances to save power.
note: unplug = 콘센트를 뽑다 / save electricity = 전기를 아끼다

korean: 식초로 청소하면 세제 안 써도 돼
english: You can clean with vinegar instead of using detergent.
alt: Vinegar works just as well as cleaning products.
note: vinegar = 식초 / detergent = 세제 / instead of = 대신에

korean: 옷 정리할 때 안 입는 건 바로 기부해
english: When organizing clothes donate whatever you don't wear.
alt: If you haven't worn it in a while just donate it.
note: donate = 기부하다 / whatever = ~하는 것은 뭐든

korean: 베이킹소다로 냄새 잡을 수 있어
english: Baking soda can absorb odors.
alt: Baking soda works great for getting rid of smells.
note: absorb odors = 냄새를 흡수하다 / get rid of = 제거하다

korean: 알람 여러 개 맞춰놔야 일어나
english: I need to set multiple alarms to wake up.
alt: I set like five alarms or I'll sleep right through.
note: set alarms = 알람을 맞추다 / sleep through = 알람을 못 듣고 자다

korean: 장볼 때 리스트 적어가면 충동구매 안 해
english: If you make a shopping list you won't impulse buy.
alt: Writing a grocery list helps avoid unnecessary purchases.
note: impulse buy = 충동구매하다 / grocery list = 장보기 목록

korean: 냉동실에 넣어두면 오래 보관할 수 있어
english: You can keep it longer if you freeze it.
alt: Toss it in the freezer and it'll last way longer.
note: freeze = 냉동하다 / last = 보관되다 / toss = 넣다 (캐주얼)

korean: 물 많이 마시는 게 피부에 좋대
english: They say drinking lots of water is good for your skin.
alt: Staying hydrated apparently does wonders for your skin.
note: do wonders = 놀라운 효과가 있다 / hydrated = 수분 보충된

korean: 아침에 스트레칭하면 하루가 달라
english: Morning stretches make such a difference for the rest of the day.
alt: Stretching in the morning sets the tone for the whole day.
note: make a difference = 차이를 만들다 / set the tone = 분위기를 잡다

korean: 짐 쌀 때 지퍼백에 넣으면 정리 잘 돼
english: Packing things in ziplock bags keeps everything organized.
alt: Ziplock bags are a lifesaver for packing.
note: lifesaver = 구세주 같은 것 / organized = 정리된

korean: 자기 전에 핸드폰 보면 잠이 안 와
english: Looking at your phone before bed makes it harder to fall asleep.
alt: Screen time before sleep messes with your ability to doze off.
note: mess with = ~을 방해하다 / doze off = 잠들다

korean: 꿀팁인데 빨래할 때 식초 넣으면 냄새가 사라져
english: Pro tip: add vinegar to your laundry and the smell disappears.
alt: Here's a hack: a bit of vinegar in the wash gets rid of odors.
note: pro tip = 꿀팁 / hack = 생활 팁

korean: 이 어플로 가계부 쓰면 돈 관리 쉬워
english: This app makes budgeting so easy.
alt: Tracking expenses is a breeze with this app.
note: budgeting = 가계부 쓰기 / a breeze = 쉬운 일

korean: 귀찮은데 그냥 배달 시키자
english: I'm too lazy to cook. Let's just get delivery.
alt: Can't be bothered. Let's just order in.
note: can't be bothered = 귀찮다 / order in = 배달 시키다

korean: 오늘 날씨 산책하기 딱 좋다
english: It's perfect weather for a walk today.
alt: Today's weather is just right for a stroll.
note: perfect weather for = ~하기 딱 좋은 날씨

korean: 햇빛 쬐면 기분이 좋아져
english: Getting some sun always lifts my mood.
alt: A little sunshine does wonders for your mood.
note: lift one's mood = 기분을 좋게 하다

korean: 비 오는 날은 집에서 뒹굴거리고 싶어
english: On rainy days I just want to lounge around at home.
alt: I feel like being a couch potato when it rains.
note: lounge around = 뒹굴거리다 / couch potato = 집순이

korean: 추워서 손이 꽁꽁 얼었어
english: It's so cold my hands are frozen solid.
alt: My hands are numb from the cold.
note: frozen solid = 꽁꽁 언 / numb = 감각이 없는

korean: 여름에는 에어컨 없으면 못 살아
english: I can't survive summer without AC.
alt: Air conditioning is a must in the summer.
note: AC = air conditioning = 에어컨 / a must = 필수

korean: 선풍기 틀어줘 더워 죽겠어
english: Turn on the fan. I'm dying of heat.
alt: Can you switch on the fan? It's boiling in here.
note: dying of heat = 더워 죽겠다 / boiling = 매우 더운

korean: 눈이 쌓여서 미끄러워 조심해
english: The snow piled up so it's slippery. Be careful.
alt: Watch your step. It's icy from all the snow.
note: slippery = 미끄러운 / icy = 빙판인 / watch your step = 조심해

korean: 옷이 눅눅해 비 와서 그런가
english: My clothes feel damp. Must be from the rain.
alt: Everything feels moist. Is it because of the rain?
note: damp = moist = 눅눅한

korean: 따뜻한 국물이 땡기는 날씨야
english: It's the kind of weather that makes you crave hot soup.
alt: This weather calls for a warm bowl of soup.
note: crave = 땡기다 / calls for = ~가 필요하다

korean: 찬바람이 살을 에는 것 같아
english: The cold wind is cutting right through me.
alt: The wind is biting today.
note: cut through = 살을 에다 / biting = 살을 에는 듯한

korean: 옷 개는 거 귀찮아서 그냥 걸어둬
english: I hate folding clothes so I just hang them up.
alt: Folding laundry is a pain. I just hang everything.
note: fold clothes = 옷을 개다 / hang up = 걸어두다

korean: 싱크대 배수구가 막혔어
english: The kitchen sink drain is clogged.
alt: The drain in the sink is blocked.
note: clogged = blocked = 막힌 / drain = 배수구

korean: 화분에 물 주는 거 맨날 까먹어
english: I always forget to water my plants.
alt: I keep forgetting to water the houseplants.
note: water the plants = 화분에 물을 주다

korean: 전구 나갔는데 교체할 거 있어?
english: The light bulb burned out. Do we have a replacement?
alt: The bulb's dead. Got a spare one?
note: burn out = 나가다 (전구) / spare = 여분의

korean: 에어컨 필터 청소해야 하는데 귀찮아
english: I need to clean the AC filter but I'm too lazy.
alt: The AC filter needs cleaning but I keep putting it off.
note: put off = 미루다 / filter = 필터

korean: 택배 왔는데 부재중이라 경비실에 맡겼대
english: A package came but I wasn't home so they left it at the security office.
alt: My delivery arrived but I missed it. They left it with the front desk.
note: package = delivery = 택배 / front desk = 경비실/프런트

korean: 이사 준비하려니까 짐이 너무 많아
english: There's so much stuff to pack for the move.
alt: Getting ready to move and I have way too many things.
note: pack for the move = 이사 짐을 싸다

korean: 와이파이 공유기 리셋 좀 해봐
english: Try resetting the Wi-Fi router.
alt: Give the router a restart and see if that helps.
note: reset/restart = 리셋하다 / router = 공유기

korean: 요즘 전기세가 너무 많이 나와
english: The electricity bill has been crazy high lately.
alt: Our power bill is through the roof these days.
note: through the roof = 엄청 높은 / power bill = 전기세

korean: 관리비에 뭐가 이렇게 많이 포함돼 있어?
english: What's all included in the maintenance fee?
alt: Why is the maintenance fee so high? What does it cover?
note: maintenance fee = 관리비 / cover = 포함하다

korean: 주차장에서 차 찾는 데 10분 걸렸어
english: It took me 10 minutes to find my car in the parking lot.
alt: I spent 10 minutes looking for my car in the garage.
note: parking lot = garage = 주차장

korean: 운전면허 따려고 학원 다니고 있어
english: I'm going to driving school to get my license.
alt: I'm taking driving lessons to get my driver's license.
note: driving school = 운전 학원 / driver's license = 운전면허

korean: 자동차 보험료 갱신해야 해
english: I need to renew my car insurance.
alt: My auto insurance is up for renewal.
note: renew = 갱신하다 / up for renewal = 갱신 시기

korean: 세차 좀 해야겠다 차가 너무 더러워
english: I need to get a car wash. My car is filthy.
alt: My car is so dirty. Time for a wash.
note: car wash = 세차 / filthy = 매우 더러운

korean: 주유소 어디 있어? 기름이 거의 없어
english: Where's a gas station? I'm almost on empty.
alt: I need to find a gas station. My tank's nearly empty.
note: on empty = 기름이 없는 / tank = 연료 탱크

korean: 신호 무시하는 차 진짜 위험해
english: Cars that run red lights are so dangerous.
alt: Drivers who blow through red lights are a menace.
note: run a red light = blow through a red light = 신호 무시하다

korean: 강아지 간식 사러 펫샵 가자
english: Let's go to the pet store to buy some dog treats.
alt: I need to pick up some treats for the pup at the pet shop.
note: dog treats = 강아지 간식 / pet store = 펫샵

korean: 고양이가 새벽에 운동회를 해
english: My cat has the zoomies at like 3 AM.
alt: My cat decides to go wild in the middle of the night.
note: zoomies = 갑자기 미친 듯이 뛰어다니는 것. 반려동물 자주 쓰는 표현

korean: 산책할 때 배변 봉투 꼭 챙겨
english: Always bring poop bags when you walk your dog.
alt: Don't forget waste bags for the walk.
note: poop bags = waste bags = 배변 봉투

korean: 우리 고양이 그루밍을 너무 많이 해서 걱정돼
english: My cat grooms itself so much that I'm starting to worry.
alt: I'm concerned about how much my cat's been grooming.
note: groom = 그루밍하다 / be concerned = 걱정되다

korean: 반려동물 보험 들어야 하나?
english: Should I get pet insurance?
alt: Is pet insurance worth it?
note: pet insurance = 반려동물 보험 / worth it = 가치 있는

korean: 배드민턴 치러 갈래? 코트 예약했어
english: Wanna play badminton? I booked a court.
alt: I reserved a badminton court. You in?
note: book/reserve a court = 코트를 예약하다 / You in? = 할 거야?

korean: 수영 배우고 싶은데 혼자 가기 좀 그래
english: I want to learn to swim but I feel weird going alone.
alt: I'd love to take swimming lessons but going solo feels awkward.
note: feel weird = 좀 그렇다 / solo = 혼자

korean: 독서 모임 들었는데 생각보다 재밌어
english: I joined a book club, and it's more fun than I expected.
alt: The book club I joined turned out to be really enjoyable.
note: book club = 독서 모임 / turn out = 알고 보니 ~이다

korean: 보드게임 하면 시간 가는 줄 몰라
english: When we play board games I lose track of time.
alt: Board games make time fly.
note: lose track of time = 시간 가는 줄 모르다 / time flies = 시간이 빨리 가다

korean: 헬스장 가기 싫은데 가면 기분은 좋아
english: I never feel like going to the gym, but I always feel good after.
alt: I dread the gym but it always puts me in a good mood afterward.
note: dread = 가기 싫다 / put in a good mood = 기분 좋게 하다

korean: 스케이트보드 배우다가 넘어졌어
english: I fell while learning to skateboard.
alt: I wiped out trying to learn skateboarding.
note: wipe out = 넘어지다 / fall = 쓰러지다

korean: 등산 끝나고 막걸리 한 잔이 최고야
english: A glass of makgeolli after a hike is the best.
alt: Nothing tops makgeolli after a good hike.
note: nothing tops = ~만한 게 없다

korean: 오늘 만보 걸었어 다리 아파
english: I walked 10000 steps today. My legs are killing me.
alt: I hit 10K steps today and my legs are so sore.
note: my legs are killing me = 다리가 너무 아프다 / sore = 아픈

korean: 이거 겉은 바삭한데 속은 촉촉해
english: The outside is crispy, but the inside is moist.
alt: It's crunchy on the outside and juicy on the inside.
note: crispy/crunchy = 바삭한 / moist/juicy = 촉촉한

korean: 이 집 분위기 좋다 데이트하기 딱이야
english: This place has a great vibe. Perfect for a date.
alt: The ambiance here is amazing. Ideal for a date night.
note: vibe = ambiance = 분위기

korean: 포장해 갈게요
english: I'll take it to go please.
alt: Can I get this to go?
note: to go = 포장 (take out보다 자연스러운 표현)

korean: 맛집 줄이 너무 길어 기다릴 만해?
english: The line at this restaurant is so long. Is it worth the wait?
alt: There's a huge line. Think it's worth waiting for?
note: worth the wait = 기다릴 만한 / huge line = 긴 줄

korean: 이 음식 식었는데 데워줄 수 있어?
english: This food got cold. Can you heat it up?
alt: Can you warm this up for me? It went cold.
note: heat up = warm up = 데우다

korean: 이거 맵기 조절 돼요?
english: Can you adjust the spice level?
alt: Do you have different heat levels for this?
note: spice level = heat level = 맵기 단계 / adjust = 조절하다

korean: 디저트 배는 따로 있지
english: There's always room for dessert.
alt: I always have a separate stomach for dessert.
note: There's always room = 자리는 항상 있다 (여유가 있다)

korean: 리필 되나요?
english: Can I get a refill?
alt: Is there free refills?
note: refill = 리필. 한국어와 영어 동일 표현

korean: 이 음식 사진 찍기 좋게 예쁘다
english: This food is so photogenic.
alt: This dish looks picture-perfect.
note: photogenic = 사진 잘 받는 / picture-perfect = 완벽하게 예쁜

korean: 여기 맛있는데 가격이 좀 있어
english: This place is good, but it's a bit pricey.
alt: The food's great here, but it'll cost you.
note: pricey = 비싼 / it'll cost you = 돈 좀 들 거야

korean: 남기지 말고 다 먹자
english: Let's not waste food. Finish it all.
alt: Let's clean our plates. No leftovers.
note: clean one's plate = 다 먹다 / leftovers = 남은 음식

korean: 국물 없는 거로 주세요
english: Can I get the dry version without broth?
alt: I'd like it without the soup please.
note: dry version = 국물 없는 버전 / without broth = 국물 없이

korean: 이 원피스 세일하는데 사도 될까?
english: This dress is on sale. Should I get it?
alt: This dress is marked down. Think I should buy it?
note: on sale = marked down = 세일 중인

korean: 겨울에는 패딩이 최고야
english: A puffer jacket is the way to go in winter.
alt: Nothing beats a puffer when it's cold.
note: puffer jacket = 패딩 / the way to go = 최고의 선택

korean: 운동화 빨아야 하는데 귀찮아
english: I need to wash my sneakers but I'm too lazy.
alt: My sneakers need a wash but I keep putting it off.
note: sneakers = 운동화 / put off = 미루다

korean: 오늘 메이크업 잘 먹었다
english: My makeup turned out great today.
alt: My makeup game is on point today.
note: on point = 완벽한 / turn out great = 잘 됐다

korean: 자외선 차단제 안 바르면 주근깨 생겨
english: If you don't wear sunscreen you'll get freckles.
alt: Skipping sunscreen leads to freckles.
note: sunscreen = 자외선 차단제 / freckles = 주근깨

korean: 이 색깔 나한테 안 어울리는 것 같아
english: I don't think this color suits me.
alt: This color doesn't look right on me.
note: suit someone = look right on someone = 어울리다

korean: 스타일 바꾸고 싶은데 용기가 안 나
english: I want to change my style but I don't have the guts.
alt: I'd love a new look but I'm too chicken to try.
note: have the guts = 용기가 있다 / too chicken = 겁이 나서

korean: 이 가방 가벼운데 많이 들어가
english: This bag is light but fits a lot.
alt: This bag is surprisingly spacious for how light it is.
note: spacious = 넓은 / light = 가벼운

korean: 립스틱 색 예쁘다 뭐야 그거?
english: That lipstick color is gorgeous. What shade is it?
alt: I love your lip color. Which one is that?
note: shade = 색상 (화장품) / gorgeous = 매우 예쁜

korean: 옷에 얼룩 졌는데 안 빠져
english: I got a stain on my shirt and it won't come out.
alt: There's a stubborn stain on my clothes that won't wash out.
note: stain = 얼룩 / come out = wash out = 빠지다

korean: 음식물 쓰레기 줄이려면 계획적으로 장봐야 해
english: To reduce food waste you need to plan your grocery shopping.
alt: Meal planning helps cut down on food waste.
note: food waste = 음식물 쓰레기 / meal planning = 식단 계획

korean: 종이 빨대 너무 불편해 눅눅해져
english: Paper straws are so annoying. They get soggy.
alt: I can't stand paper straws. They fall apart.
note: soggy = 눅눅한 / fall apart = 무너지다

korean: 다회용 컵 쓰면 할인해 주는 곳 많아
english: A lot of places give you a discount for using a reusable cup.
alt: Many cafes offer a discount if you bring your own cup.
note: reusable cup = 다회용 컵 / bring your own = 직접 가져오다

korean: 대중교통 이용이 탄소 배출 줄이는 방법이야
english: Taking public transportation is a way to reduce carbon emissions.
alt: Public transit helps cut carbon emissions.
note: carbon emissions = 탄소 배출 / public transit = 대중교통

korean: 이 앱으로 탄소발자국 계산할 수 있어
english: You can calculate your carbon footprint with this app.
alt: This app lets you track your carbon footprint.
note: carbon footprint = 탄소발자국. 영어 그대로 사용되는 표현

korean: 채식 메뉴 있는 식당 찾기 힘들어
english: It's hard to find restaurants with vegetarian options.
alt: Finding places with veggie menu items is a struggle.
note: vegetarian options = 채식 메뉴 / a struggle = 힘든 일

korean: 못 쓰는 건전지 어디에 버려야 해?
english: Where should I throw away used batteries?
alt: Where do dead batteries go for disposal?
note: used batteries = dead batteries = 다 쓴 건전지 / disposal = 폐기

korean: 리모컨 배터리 없나봐 안 돼
english: I think the remote's out of batteries. It's not working.
alt: The remote must be dead. It won't respond.
note: out of batteries = 배터리가 없는 / dead = 방전된

korean: 선풍기 겨울 되니까 치워야지
english: Winter's coming, so I should put the fan away.
alt: Time to store the fan now that it's getting cold.
note: put away = store = 치우다/보관하다

korean: 자취하면 요리 안 하게 돼
english: When you live alone you end up not cooking.
alt: Living by yourself makes you too lazy to cook.
note: live alone = live by yourself = 자취하다

korean: 주말에 집 대청소 해야겠다
english: I need to do a deep clean of the house this weekend.
alt: This weekend calls for a major house cleaning.
note: deep clean = 대청소 / calls for = ~가 필요하다

korean: 세탁기 돌리는 거 깜빡했어
english: I forgot to run the washing machine.
alt: I totally blanked on starting the laundry.
note: run the washing machine = 세탁기를 돌리다

korean: 옷 건조기 쓰면 옷이 줄어들까 봐 걱정돼
english: I'm worried the dryer might shrink my clothes.
alt: I'm afraid of using the dryer in case my clothes shrink.
note: shrink = 줄어들다 / in case = ~할까 봐

korean: 환풍기 소리가 너무 커서 시끄러워
english: The exhaust fan is so loud it's noisy.
alt: The ventilation fan makes too much noise.
note: exhaust fan = ventilation fan = 환풍기

korean: 음식 해먹으면 설거지가 문제야
english: Cooking is fine, but the dishes after are the problem.
alt: I don't mind cooking. It's the cleanup that gets me.
note: cleanup = 뒷정리 / get someone = ~를 힘들게 하다

korean: 행거에 옷 걸어두면 구김이 덜 가
english: Hanging clothes on a rack prevents wrinkles.
alt: If you use a clothes rack you get fewer wrinkles.
note: wrinkles = 구김 / prevent = 방지하다 / rack = 행거

korean: 전동 킥보드 주차 아무 데나 하면 안 돼
english: You can't just park electric scooters anywhere.
alt: E-scooters need to be parked in designated areas.
note: designated area = 지정된 구역

korean: 내비 말고 지도 앱 쓰면 실시간 교통정보도 나와
english: Map apps show real-time traffic info unlike regular GPS.
alt: Using a map app gives you live traffic updates.
note: real-time = live = 실시간 / traffic info = 교통정보

korean: 차 시동이 안 걸려
english: My car won't start.
alt: The engine won't turn over.
note: won't start = 시동이 안 걸리다 / turn over = 엔진이 걸리다

korean: 카풀하면 기름값 반으로 줄어
english: Carpooling cuts gas costs in half.
alt: If we carpool we split the gas money.
note: carpool = 카풀하다 / cut in half = 반으로 줄이다

korean: 전기차 충전소 찾기가 아직 좀 불편해
english: Finding EV charging stations is still a bit inconvenient.
alt: EV chargers are still not easy to come by.
note: EV = electric vehicle / come by = 찾다/구하다

korean: 버스 앱으로 보니까 5분 뒤에 와
english: According to the bus app it'll be here in 5 minutes.
alt: The app says the bus is 5 minutes away.
note: according to = ~에 따르면

korean: 지하철에서 자리 잡기 힘들어
english: It's hard to get a seat on the subway.
alt: Snagging a seat on the subway is nearly impossible.
note: snag a seat = 자리를 잡다

korean: 강아지 미용 시켰더니 딴 강아지 같아
english: My dog looks like a completely different dog after grooming.
alt: I barely recognized my dog after the groomer.
note: grooming = 미용 / barely recognize = 거의 못 알아보다

korean: 고양이가 택배 상자에 들어가서 안 나와
english: My cat got in the delivery box and won't come out.
alt: The cat claimed the cardboard box and refuses to leave.
note: claim = 차지하다 / refuse to = ~를 거부하다

korean: 강아지가 신발을 물어뜯었어
english: My dog chewed up my shoes.
alt: The puppy destroyed my shoes.
note: chew up = 물어뜯다 / destroy = 망가뜨리다

korean: 산책 갔다 오니까 강아지가 지쳐서 바로 잠들었어
english: My dog passed out right after the walk.
alt: The pup was so tired from the walk he fell asleep instantly.
note: pass out = 곯아떨어지다 / fall asleep instantly = 바로 잠들다

korean: 고양이가 밤에만 활발해져서 잠을 못 자
english: My cat gets active only at night so I can't sleep.
alt: The cat turns into a night owl and keeps me up.
note: night owl = 야행성 / keep someone up = 잠 못 자게 하다

korean: 강아지 훈련시키는 거 생각보다 어려워
english: Training a dog is harder than I thought.
alt: Dog training is way more challenging than I expected.
note: training = 훈련 / challenging = 어려운

korean: 플랭크 1분도 못 버티겠어
english: I can't even hold a plank for one minute.
alt: A one-minute plank feels like an eternity.
note: hold a plank = 플랭크를 버티다 / eternity = 영원

korean: 오늘 점심 뭐 먹을까? 메뉴 고르기 너무 힘들어
english: What should we eat for lunch? I can never decide.
alt: What are we having for lunch? Choosing is the hardest part of my day.
note: I can never decide = 항상 결정을 못 함. the hardest part = 제일 어려운 부분

korean: 아 배고프다 아침 안 먹었더니 힘도 없어
english: I'm starving. I skipped breakfast and I have zero energy.
alt: I'm so hungry. Not eating breakfast was a mistake.
note: starving = 매우 배고픈 / skip breakfast = 아침을 거르다

korean: 이 집 맛있다고 해서 왔는데 웨이팅이 장난 아니야
english: This place is supposed to be good but the wait is ridiculous.
alt: I heard this restaurant was amazing but the line is insane.
note: the wait is ridiculous = 대기가 말도 안 되게 길다

korean: 나 요즘 다이어트 중이라 탄수화물 줄이고 있어
english: I'm on a diet so I'm cutting back on carbs.
alt: I've been watching my carb intake lately.
note: cut back on = 줄이다 / carb intake = 탄수화물 섭취량

korean: 여기 디저트가 진짜 맛있대 꼭 먹어봐
english: I heard the dessert here is to die for. You have to try it.
alt: The desserts here are supposedly amazing. Don't skip them.
note: to die for = 죽을 만큼 맛있는 (과장 표현)

korean: 커피 한 잔 하면서 쉬자
english: Let's grab a coffee and take a break.
alt: Let's chill over some coffee.
note: grab a coffee = 커피 한 잔 하다 / chill = 쉬다

korean: 아이스 아메리카노 한 잔이요 샷 추가해 주세요
english: One iced Americano, please. With an extra shot.
alt: Can I get an iced Americano with an extra shot, please?
note: extra shot = 샷 추가 / Can I get = 주문 시 자연스러운 표현

korean: 이 카페 분위기 진짜 좋다 여기 단골 해야겠다
english: This café has such a nice vibe. I should make this my regular spot.
alt: The ambiance here is amazing. I'm definitely coming back.
note: vibe = 분위기 / regular spot = 단골집

korean: 와이파이 비밀번호가 뭐예요?
english: What's the Wi-Fi password?
alt: Could I get the Wi-Fi password?
note: 자연스러운 질문 형태. Could I get은 좀 더 공손한 표현

korean: 여기 콘센트 있는 자리 있나요?
english: Are there any seats near an outlet?
alt: Do you have any spots with a power outlet?
note: outlet = 콘센트 (미국). power point는 호주식

korean: 이거 포장해 주세요
english: Can I get this to go?
alt: I'd like this for takeout please.
note: to go = 포장 / for takeout도 같은 뜻

korean: 여기 주차 되나요?
english: Is there parking available here?
alt: Can I park here?
note: parking available = 주차 가능한

korean: 네비가 자꾸 이상한 데로 안내해
english: My GPS keeps taking me to the wrong place.
alt: The navigation keeps sending me on weird routes.
note: GPS = 네비게이션 / weird routes = 이상한 경로

korean: 막히는 거 보니까 30분은 더 걸리겠다
english: Judging by the traffic it's gonna take at least 30 more minutes.
alt: With this traffic we're looking at another half hour minimum.
note: judging by = ~로 판단하건대 / at least = 최소

korean: 지하철 놓쳤어 다음 거 언제 와?
english: I missed the subway. When's the next one?
alt: I just missed my train. How long till the next one?
note: How long till = 얼마나 걸려? / missed the train = 열차를 놓치다

korean: 택시 잡기가 하늘의 별 따기야
english: Getting a cab right now is next to impossible.
alt: It's impossible to hail a taxi at this hour.
note: next to impossible = 거의 불가능한 / hail a taxi = 택시를 잡다

korean: 버스가 왜 이렇게 안 와? 벌써 20분째야
english: Where is the bus? I've been waiting for 20 minutes.
alt: The bus is taking forever. It's been 20 minutes already.
note: taking forever = 너무 오래 걸리다

korean: 요즘 기름값이 너무 올라서 차 끌기가 부담돼
english: Gas prices are so high these days. Driving is getting expensive.
alt: With gas prices this high I can barely afford to drive.
note: gas prices = 기름값 / can barely afford = 감당하기 어려운

korean: 주말에 뭐 할 거야? 특별한 계획 있어?
english: What are you up to this weekend? Any plans?
alt: Got anything going on this weekend?
note: what are you up to = 뭐 할 거야? / any plans = 계획 있어?

korean: 아 이번 주말은 그냥 집에서 뒹굴 거야
english: I'm just gonna be a couch potato this weekend.
alt: I'm planning on doing absolutely nothing this weekend.
note: couch potato = 소파에서 뒹구는 사람 / doing nothing = 아무것도 안 하기

korean: 토요일에 영화 보러 갈래? 새로 나온 거 있던데
english: Wanna catch a movie on Saturday? There's a new one out.
alt: Want to go see a movie Saturday? Something new just came out.
note: catch a movie = 영화 보다 / came out = 개봉하다

korean: 그 영화 재밌었어? 볼까 말까 고민 중이야
english: Was that movie any good? I'm on the fence about seeing it.
alt: How was the movie? I'm debating whether to watch it or not.
note: on the fence = 결정을 못 하는 / debating = 고민 중인

korean: 넷플릭스에서 뭐 볼 거 추천해 줘
english: Recommend something on Netflix for me.
alt: Know any good shows on Netflix?
note: recommend = 추천하다. Know any good ~는 자연스러운 추천 요청

korean: 그 드라마 정주행 중이야 잠을 못 자겠어
english: I'm binge-watching that show. I can't stop.
alt: I've been glued to that drama. Sleep is not happening.
note: binge-watch = 정주행하다 / glued to = ~에 빠진

korean: 스포일러 하지 마! 나 아직 안 봤어
english: No spoilers! I haven't seen it yet.
alt: Don't spoil it for me! I haven't watched it yet.
note: No spoilers = 스포하지 마. spoil it = 결말을 말해버리다

korean: 헬스장 등록할까 생각 중이야
english: I'm thinking about signing up for a gym membership.
alt: I've been considering joining a gym.
note: sign up for = 등록하다 / gym membership = 헬스장 회원권

korean: 운동 안 한 지 너무 오래돼서 몸이 찌뿌둥해
english: I haven't worked out in so long my body feels all stiff.
alt: It's been ages since I exercised and my body feels so sluggish.
note: stiff = 뻣뻣한 / sluggish = 나른하고 무거운

korean: 러닝 시작했는데 5분 만에 숨이 차더라
english: I started running but I was out of breath after just 5 minutes.
alt: I tried jogging and was gasping for air within 5 minutes.
note: out of breath = 숨이 찬 / gasping for air = 헐떡이는

korean: 오늘 하체 운동했더니 다리가 후들후들해
english: I did leg day today and my legs are shaking.
alt: My legs are jelly after leg day.
note: leg day = 하체 운동하는 날 / jelly = 힘없이 흔들리는

korean: 내일 근육통 장난 아니겠다
english: I'm gonna be so sore tomorrow.
alt: Tomorrow's gonna be rough. DOMS is coming.
note: sore = 근육통이 있는 / DOMS = 지연성 근육통

korean: 스트레칭 안 하면 다치니까 꼭 해
english: You need to stretch or you'll get hurt.
alt: Don't skip stretching. That's how you pull a muscle.
note: pull a muscle = 근육을 삐다 / skip = 건너뛰다

korean: 요가 시작했는데 유연성이 너무 안 좋아서 힘들어
english: I started yoga but I'm so inflexible it's a struggle.
alt: Yoga is tough when you have zero flexibility.
note: inflexible = 유연성이 없는 / struggle = 고전하다

korean: 수영 배우고 싶은데 물이 무서워
english: I want to learn to swim but I'm scared of the water.
alt: I'd love to take swimming lessons but I have a fear of water.
note: scared of = ~이 무서운 / fear of water = 물에 대한 공포

korean: 오늘 날씨 진짜 좋다 나들이 가기 딱이야
english: The weather is gorgeous today. Perfect for an outing.
alt: What a beautiful day. It's ideal for going out.
note: gorgeous = 매우 아름다운 / outing = 나들이

korean: 갑자기 비 와서 우산도 없이 흠뻑 젖었어
english: It started raining out of nowhere and I got soaked.
alt: I got caught in the rain without an umbrella and got drenched.
note: got soaked/drenched = 흠뻑 젖은 / out of nowhere = 갑자기

korean: 오늘 미세먼지 심하대 마스크 꼭 써
english: The fine dust is really bad today. Make sure you wear a mask.
alt: Air quality is terrible today. Don't forget your mask.
note: fine dust = 미세먼지 / air quality = 공기질

korean: 여름인데 왜 이렇게 추워? 이상하다
english: It's summer but why is it so cold? That's weird.
alt: How is it this cold in summer? Something's off.
note: something's off = 뭔가 이상한 / weird = 이상한

korean: 습도가 높아서 빨래가 안 마른다
english: The humidity is so high my laundry won't dry.
alt: It's so humid that nothing is drying.
note: humidity = 습도 / laundry = 빨래

korean: 태풍 온다고 해서 일찍 퇴근했어
english: I left work early because there's a typhoon warning.
alt: They let us go early because of the typhoon.
note: typhoon warning = 태풍 경보 / let us go = 보내주다

korean: 꽃샘추위가 아직 안 끝났나 봐 진짜 춥다
english: I guess the late cold snap isn't over yet. It's freezing.
alt: The lingering winter cold is still hanging around. So chilly.
note: cold snap = 한파 / lingering = 남아있는

korean: 일기예보 확인했어? 내일 눈 온대
english: Did you check the forecast? It's supposed to snow tomorrow.
alt: Have you seen the weather forecast? Snow is expected tomorrow.
note: forecast = 일기예보 / supposed to = ~할 예정인

korean: 자외선 지수가 높으니까 선크림 꼭 발라
english: The UV index is high so make sure you put on sunscreen.
alt: Don't forget sunscreen. The UV rays are brutal today.
note: UV index = 자외선 지수 / brutal = 가혹한

korean: 에어컨 좀 틀어도 돼? 너무 더워
english: Can I turn on the AC? It's boiling in here.
alt: Mind if I crank up the air conditioning? I'm dying of heat.
note: boiling = 매우 더운 / crank up = 세게 틀다

korean: 히터 좀 올려줘 발이 시려
english: Can you turn up the heater? My feet are freezing.
alt: Bump up the heat a bit. My toes are numb.
note: turn up = 높이다 / numb = 감각이 없는

korean: 머리 자르러 가야 되는데 귀찮아
english: I need to get a haircut but I'm too lazy.
alt: I should go get my hair done but I can't be bothered.
note: get a haircut = 머리를 자르다 / can't be bothered = 귀찮다

korean: 앞머리 좀만 다듬어 주세요
english: Just a trim on the bangs please.
alt: Can you clean up my bangs a little?
note: trim = 다듬기 / bangs = 앞머리

korean: 염색하고 싶은데 어떤 색이 어울릴까?
english: I want to dye my hair but what color would look good on me?
alt: I'm thinking about coloring my hair. What shade suits me?
note: dye = 염색하다 / shade = 색조 / suit = 어울리다

korean: 파마했는데 너무 곱슬거려 이거 맞아?
english: I got a perm but it's way too curly. Is this right?
alt: My perm turned out curlier than I expected. Is this normal?
note: perm = 파마 / curly = 곱슬거리는 / turn out = 결과가 나오다

korean: 이 헤어스타일 사진 보여주면서 이렇게 해주세요
english: Can you do it like this? (showing a picture)
alt: I'd like this style please. (showing reference photo)
note: reference photo = 참고 사진. 사진 보여주며 주문하는 게 자연스러움

korean: 병원 예약 잡아야 하는데 전화할 시간이 없어
english: I need to book a doctor's appointment but I don't have time to call.
alt: I've been meaning to schedule a doctor's visit but haven't had a chance.
note: book/schedule an appointment = 예약을 잡다

korean: 감기 기운이 있어서 약 좀 먹어야겠다
english: I think I'm coming down with a cold. I need to take some medicine.
alt: I feel a cold coming on. Time to pop some cold medicine.
note: come down with = ~에 걸리다 / pop medicine = 약을 먹다 (구어)

korean: 약 먹었는데도 열이 안 떨어져
english: I took medicine but my fever won't go down.
alt: The medicine isn't bringing my fever down.
note: fever won't go down = 열이 안 내려간다

korean: 알레르기 때문에 눈이 가렵고 콧물이 줄줄 나와
english: My allergies are killing me. Itchy eyes and runny nose.
alt: My allergies are acting up. My eyes are itchy and my nose won't stop running.
note: allergies are killing me = 알레르기가 심하다 / runny nose = 콧물

korean: 허리가 아파서 오래 못 앉아 있겠어
english: My back hurts so bad I can't sit for long.
alt: I can't sit for extended periods because of my back pain.
note: back pain = 허리/등 통증 / extended periods = 장시간

korean: 두통이 심해서 일을 못 하겠어
english: I have such a bad headache I can't focus on work.
alt: This headache is so bad I can't get anything done.
note: headache = 두통 / can't get anything done = 아무것도 못 하다

korean: 건강검진 결과 나왔는데 콜레스테롤이 높대
english: I got my checkup results and my cholesterol is high.
alt: My health screening came back and my cholesterol levels are elevated.
note: checkup = 건강검진 / cholesterol = 콜레스테롤 / elevated = 높은

korean: 잠을 못 자서 눈이 충혈됐어
english: I didn't sleep well and my eyes are all bloodshot.
alt: My eyes are so red from lack of sleep.
note: bloodshot = 충혈된 / lack of sleep = 수면 부족

korean: 비타민 좀 챙겨 먹어 요즘 면역력 떨어졌잖아
english: Take your vitamins. Your immune system's been weak lately.
alt: You should be taking supplements. Your immunity has been low.
note: immune system = 면역 체계 / supplements = 영양제

korean: 은행 가서 통장 만들어야 하는데 뭐 가져가야 돼?
english: I need to open a bank account. What do I need to bring?
alt: What documents do I need to open a bank account?
note: open a bank account = 통장/계좌를 개설하다

korean: 이체 수수료가 왜 이렇게 비싸?
english: Why are the transfer fees so high?
alt: How come the wire transfer charges are this expensive?
note: transfer fee = 이체 수수료 / wire transfer = 송금

korean: ATM이 고장 났어 현금을 못 뽑아
english: The ATM is out of order. I can't withdraw any cash.
alt: The ATM is broken so I can't get cash out.
note: out of order = 고장난 / withdraw cash = 현금을 인출하다

korean: 대출 이자가 올라서 부담이 커졌어
english: The loan interest rate went up so the burden got heavier.
alt: My mortgage payments are higher now because of the rate hike.
note: loan interest rate = 대출 이자율 / rate hike = 금리 인상

korean: 카드 분실 신고해야 하는데 전화번호가 몇 번이지?
english: I need to report my card as lost. What's the number to call?
alt: I lost my card and need to report it. Do you know the hotline?
note: report as lost = 분실 신고하다 / hotline = 긴급 전화번호

korean: 이거 카드로 결제할게요
english: I'll pay by card.
alt: I'd like to put this on my card.
note: pay by card = 카드로 결제하다 / put on my card = 카드에 넣다

korean: 현금영수증 해주세요
english: Can I get a cash receipt please?
alt: I'd like a cash receipt for this.
note: cash receipt = 현금영수증. 한국 특유의 세금 관련 제도

korean: 이거 환불 가능한가요? 영수증 있어요
english: Can I return this? I have the receipt.
alt: Is this refundable? I've got the receipt.
note: return = 환불하다 / refundable = 환불 가능한

korean: 사이즈가 안 맞아서 교환하고 싶어요
english: I'd like to exchange this because the size doesn't fit.
alt: This doesn't fit. Can I swap it for a different size?
note: exchange = 교환하다 / swap = 바꾸다

korean: 세일 언제 해요? 기다렸다가 살까
english: When's the next sale? Should I wait?
alt: When do you usually have sales? Maybe I should hold off.
note: hold off = 미루다 / sale = 할인 행사

korean: 이거 온라인이 더 싸더라 가격 비교해 봐
english: This is cheaper online. You should price check.
alt: I found this for less online. Compare prices first.
note: price check = 가격 비교하다 / for less = 더 싸게

korean: 장바구니에 넣어놨는데 결제를 할까 말까 고민이야
english: I added it to my cart but I'm debating whether to buy it.
alt: It's been sitting in my cart for days. I can't decide.
note: cart = 장바구니 / debating = 고민하는 / sitting in my cart = 장바구니에 넣어둔

korean: 쿠폰 있는데 이거 적용 되나요?
english: I have a coupon. Can I use it on this?
alt: Is this coupon valid for this item?
note: coupon = 쿠폰 / valid = 유효한

korean: 포인트 적립해 주세요
english: Can you add points to my account?
alt: I'd like to earn points on this purchase.
note: earn points = 포인트를 적립하다 / add points = 포인트를 추가하다

korean: 배송 언제 오는지 확인해 볼게
english: Let me check when the delivery is coming.
alt: I'll track the shipment and see when it arrives.
note: track the shipment = 배송을 추적하다

korean: 배달 시켜 먹자 나가기 귀찮아
english: Let's just order delivery. I don't feel like going out.
alt: Let's get something delivered. I'm too lazy to go out.
note: order delivery = 배달 시키다 / don't feel like = ~하기 싫다

korean: 배달비가 너무 비싸서 최소주문금액 맞추느라 더 시켰어
english: The delivery fee was so high I ordered extra to meet the minimum.
alt: I had to add more items just to hit the minimum order for free delivery.
note: delivery fee = 배달비 / minimum order = 최소 주문 금액

korean: 배달 예상 시간이 1시간이래 너무 오래 걸린다
english: The estimated delivery time is an hour. That's way too long.
alt: An hour for delivery? That's ridiculous.
note: estimated delivery time = 예상 배달 시간 / ridiculous = 말도 안 되는

korean: 리뷰 보고 시켰는데 사진이랑 다르잖아
english: I ordered based on the reviews but it looks nothing like the picture.
alt: The food looks completely different from what was advertised.
note: looks nothing like = 전혀 닮지 않은 / advertised = 광고된

korean: 오늘 기분 너무 좋아 아무 이유 없이
english: I'm in such a good mood today for no reason.
alt: I just feel great today. No particular reason.
note: in a good mood = 기분이 좋은 / for no reason = 이유 없이

korean: 갑자기 우울해졌어 왜 이러지
english: I suddenly feel so down. What's wrong with me?
alt: I just got hit with a wave of sadness out of nowhere.
note: feel down = 우울하다 / hit with a wave of = ~이 밀려오다

korean: 스트레스 받아서 미칠 것 같아
english: I'm so stressed I'm about to lose it.
alt: This stress is driving me insane.
note: lose it = 미치다 / driving me insane = 미치게 하는

korean: 아 짜증 나 왜 맨날 나한테만 이래
english: This is so annoying. Why does this always happen to me?
alt: I'm so irritated. Why is it always me?
note: annoying = 짜증나는 / irritated = 짜증난

korean: 너무 설레서 잠이 안 와 내일이 기대돼
english: I'm so excited I can't sleep. I can't wait for tomorrow.
alt: I'm too hyped up to fall asleep. Tomorrow can't come soon enough.
note: excited/hyped up = 설레는 / can't come soon enough = 빨리 왔으면

korean: 속상해서 울 뻔했어 참느라 힘들었어
english: I almost cried. It was so hard to hold back tears.
alt: I was on the verge of tears. Holding it in was tough.
note: hold back tears = 눈물을 참다 / on the verge of tears = 울 뻔한

korean: 완전 감동받았어 눈물 날 뻔했어
english: I was so moved I almost teared up.
alt: That was so touching I nearly cried.
note: moved/touched = 감동받은 / tear up = 눈물이 나다

korean: 화났는데 참고 있어 말하면 싸움 될 것 같아서
english: I'm angry but I'm holding it in. If I say something we'll fight.
alt: I'm biting my tongue because I know it'll start a fight.
note: hold it in = 참다 / bite my tongue = 말을 참다

korean: 뭔가 허무하다 이게 다인가 싶어
english: I feel so empty. Is this all there is?
alt: There's this hollowness. Like is this really it?
note: empty/hollow = 공허한 / is this all there is = 이게 다인가

korean: 요즘 의욕이 없어 뭘 해도 재미가 없어
english: I have no motivation lately. Nothing feels fun.
alt: I've lost all drive. Everything feels pointless.
note: motivation/drive = 의욕 / pointless = 무의미한

korean: 진짜 고마워 네가 아니었으면 어쩔 뻔했어
english: Thank you so much. I don't know what I'd do without you.
alt: I really appreciate it. You're a lifesaver.
note: lifesaver = 생명의 은인 / what I'd do without you = 너 없었으면 어쩔 뻔

korean: 도와줘서 정말 감사합니다 큰 도움이 됐어요
english: Thank you so much for your help. It really made a difference.
alt: I really appreciate your assistance. It meant a lot.
note: made a difference = 큰 도움이 됐다 / meant a lot = 많은 의미가 있었다

korean: 밥 사줘서 고마워 다음엔 내가 쏠게
english: Thanks for buying me food. Next time it's on me.
alt: Thanks for the meal. I'll get the next one.
note: it's on me = 내가 살게 / get the next one = 다음에 내가 내다

korean: 선물 너무 마음에 들어 센스 있다 진짜
english: I love the gift! You have great taste.
alt: This present is perfect. You really know me.
note: great taste = 센스 있는 / you really know me = 나를 잘 아는

korean: 챙겨줘서 고마워 네가 최고야
english: Thanks for looking out for me. You're the best.
alt: I appreciate you taking care of me. You're amazing.
note: look out for = 챙기다 / take care of = 돌보다

korean: 미안 내가 실수했어 다시는 안 그럴게
english: I'm sorry. I messed up. It won't happen again.
alt: My bad. I dropped the ball. I promise it won't happen again.
note: mess up = 실수하다 / drop the ball = 실수하다 (비유)

korean: 늦어서 진심으로 사과할게 정말 미안해
english: I sincerely apologize for being late. I'm really sorry.
alt: I'm truly sorry for the delay. I feel terrible.
note: sincerely apologize = 진심으로 사과하다 / feel terrible = 마음이 안 좋다

korean: 오해해서 미안해 내가 잘못 생각했어
english: Sorry for the misunderstanding. I was wrong.
alt: I apologize for jumping to conclusions. That was my mistake.
note: misunderstanding = 오해 / jump to conclusions = 성급하게 판단하다

korean: 기분 나쁘게 했으면 미안해 그런 의도가 아니었어
english: Sorry if I hurt your feelings. That wasn't my intention.
alt: I didn't mean to offend you. I'm sorry if it came across that way.
note: hurt your feelings = 기분을 상하게 하다 / intention = 의도

korean: 깜빡해서 미안 다음부터 꼭 기억할게
english: Sorry I totally forgot. I'll make sure to remember next time.
alt: My bad for spacing out. I'll put it on my calendar.
note: space out = 깜빡하다 / put it on my calendar = 일정에 기록하다

korean: 내 생각에는 그거 별로인 것 같아
english: In my opinion that's not a great idea.
alt: Honestly I don't think that's the best move.
note: in my opinion = 내 생각에는 / not the best move = 좋은 선택이 아닌

korean: 나는 좀 다르게 생각해 이유를 말해줄게
english: I see it differently. Let me explain why.
alt: I have a different take on this. Here's my reasoning.
note: see it differently = 다르게 보다 / take = 관점

korean: 그건 좀 아닌 것 같은데 다시 생각해 봐
english: I don't think that's quite right. Maybe reconsider?
alt: That doesn't sit well with me. Maybe think it over?
note: doesn't sit well with me = 마음에 안 드는 / think it over = 다시 생각하다

korean: 좋은 의견이네 한번 해보자
english: That's a good point. Let's give it a shot.
alt: Great idea. Let's go for it.
note: give it a shot = 시도해 보다 / go for it = 해보자

korean: 맞아 나도 그렇게 생각해
english: Exactly. I feel the same way.
alt: I totally agree. That's what I was thinking too.
note: feel the same way = 같은 생각이다 / totally agree = 완전 동의

korean: 괜찮을 것 같긴 한데 좀 더 알아보자
english: It sounds okay but let's look into it more.
alt: It seems alright but I'd like to do more research first.
note: look into = 조사하다 / do more research = 더 알아보다

korean: 제안 하나 해도 될까? 이렇게 하면 어때?
english: Can I make a suggestion? How about we do it this way?
alt: Mind if I throw out an idea? What if we tried this instead?
note: make a suggestion = 제안하다 / throw out an idea = 아이디어를 던지다

korean: 이번에는 좀 쉬는 게 어때? 너무 무리하지 마
english: Why don't you take it easy this time? Don't overdo it.
alt: Maybe you should rest this time around. Don't push yourself.
note: take it easy = 쉬다 / overdo it = 무리하다 / push yourself = 자신을 몰아붙이다

korean: 미안한데 이번에는 안 될 것 같아
english: Sorry but I don't think I can make it this time.
alt: I hate to say no but I can't do it this time.
note: can't make it = 참석할 수 없다 / hate to say no = 거절하기 싫지만

korean: 그건 좀 부담스러운데 다른 방법은 없을까?
english: That's a bit much for me. Is there another way?
alt: That feels like a lot. Are there any alternatives?
note: a bit much = 좀 부담스러운 / alternatives = 대안

korean: 생각해 볼게 바로 대답하기가 어려워
english: Let me think about it. I can't give you an answer right away.
alt: I'll need some time to think it over. Can't commit just yet.
note: think it over = 곰곰이 생각하다 / commit = 약속하다

korean: 불만 접수하고 싶은데요 서비스가 너무 안 좋았어요
english: I'd like to file a complaint. The service was terrible.
alt: I need to make a complaint. The service was unacceptable.
note: file/make a complaint = 불만을 제기하다 / unacceptable = 받아들일 수 없는

korean: 이게 말이 돼? 너무 불합리해
english: Does this even make sense? This is so unfair.
alt: How is this reasonable? This is completely unjust.
note: make sense = 말이 되다 / unfair/unjust = 불공정한

korean: 대기 시간이 너무 길어요 언제까지 기다려야 해요?
english: The wait time is way too long. How much longer do I have to wait?
alt: I've been waiting forever. When will it be my turn?
note: wait time = 대기 시간 / How much longer = 얼마나 더

korean: 진짜 잘했다! 대단해!
english: You did amazing! That's incredible!
alt: Great job! I'm so proud of you!
note: amazing/incredible = 놀라운 / great job = 잘했어

korean: 역시 너밖에 없어 최고다
english: Nobody does it like you. You're the best.
alt: You never disappoint. You're one of a kind.
note: nobody does it like you = 너처럼 하는 사람 없다 / one of a kind = 유일무이한

korean: 센스 있다 어떻게 이런 걸 생각했어?
english: That's so thoughtful. How did you think of that?
alt: What a creative idea. Where did that come from?
note: thoughtful = 사려깊은 / creative = 창의적인

korean: 옷 잘 입었다 오늘 좀 멋진데?
english: You look sharp today. Nice outfit.
alt: Looking good today! Love the fit.
note: look sharp = 날카로워 보이다 / fit = 옷차림 (슬랭)

korean: 힘내 다 잘 될 거야
english: Cheer up. Everything's gonna work out.
alt: Hang in there. Things will get better.
note: cheer up = 힘내 / work out = 잘 되다 / hang in there = 버텨

korean: 너무 자책하지 마 누구나 실수할 수 있어
english: Don't be too hard on yourself. Everyone makes mistakes.
alt: Don't beat yourself up. We all mess up sometimes.
note: be hard on yourself = 자신에게 엄격한 / beat yourself up = 자책하다

korean: 내가 옆에 있을게 필요한 거 있으면 말해
english: I'm here for you. Let me know if you need anything.
alt: I've got your back. Just say the word.
note: I'm here for you = 내가 여기 있어 / I've got your back = 내가 뒤를 봐줄게

korean: 시간이 약이야 조금만 참아
english: Time heals all wounds. Just hang in there a little longer.
alt: Give it time. It'll get easier.
note: time heals all wounds = 시간이 약이다 / hang in there = 견디다

korean: 인스타 스토리 봤어? 완전 웃기더라
english: Did you see their Insta story? It was hilarious.
alt: Have you checked their Instagram story? I was dying laughing.
note: Insta = Instagram의 줄임말 / hilarious = 매우 웃긴

korean: 좋아요 눌러줘 팔로우도 해주고
english: Like my post and give me a follow.
alt: Hit that like button and drop a follow.
note: hit the like button = 좋아요를 누르다 / drop a follow = 팔로우 하다

korean: 프로필 사진 바꿨는데 어때?
english: I changed my profile pic. How does it look?
alt: I updated my profile photo. What do you think?
note: profile pic = 프로필 사진 / What do you think = 어떻게 생각해?

korean: DM으로 보내줄게 잠깐만
english: I'll send it to you via DM. Hold on.
alt: Let me DM you real quick. One sec.
note: DM = Direct Message / hold on = 잠깐만 / one sec = 잠깐

korean: 그 밈 봤어? 완전 공감이야
english: Did you see that meme? So relatable.
alt: Have you seen that meme going around? I felt that.
note: meme = 밈 / relatable = 공감되는 / felt that = 공감된다 (슬랭)

korean: 사진 보정 좀 해야겠다 그냥 올리기엔 좀 그래
english: I need to edit this photo. I can't just post it raw.
alt: Let me touch up this pic before posting. It needs some work.
note: edit/touch up = 보정하다 / post it raw = 보정 없이 올리다

korean: 릴스 찍자 이거 바이럴 될 듯
english: Let's film a Reel. This could totally go viral.
alt: We should make a Reel out of this. It has viral potential.
note: Reel = 릴스 / go viral = 바이럴 되다

korean: 댓글 달아줘 아무도 안 달아서 슬퍼
english: Leave a comment. Nobody commented and I'm sad.
alt: Drop a comment on my post please. It's so dead.
note: leave/drop a comment = 댓글을 달다 / dead = 반응 없는

korean: 구독자 몇 명이야? 요즘 많이 늘었어?
english: How many followers do you have? Have you been gaining a lot lately?
alt: What's your follower count at? Has it been growing?
note: followers = 팔로워/구독자 / gaining = 늘어나는

korean: 해시태그 뭐 달지? 노출 잘 되는 거 알아?
english: What hashtags should I use? Know any that get good reach?
alt: What tags should I put? Which ones boost visibility?
note: hashtags = 해시태그 / reach/visibility = 노출도

korean: 전화 받아 왜 안 받아
english: Pick up the phone. Why aren't you answering?
alt: Answer your phone. Why aren't you picking up?
note: pick up = 전화를 받다 / answer the phone = 전화를 받다

korean: 통화 중이야 나중에 다시 전화할게
english: I'm on the phone right now. I'll call you back later.
alt: I'm in the middle of a call. I'll hit you up later.
note: on the phone = 통화 중 / hit you up = 연락하다 (슬랭)

korean: 잠깐 통화 가능해? 할 말이 있어
english: Can you talk for a sec? I have something to tell you.
alt: Got a minute? There's something I need to discuss.
note: for a sec = 잠깐 / got a minute = 시간 있어?

korean: 문자로 보내줘 지금 전화 못 받아
english: Text me instead. I can't take calls right now.
alt: Send me a text. I'm not able to talk on the phone right now.
note: text me = 문자 해 / take calls = 전화를 받다

korean: 보이스톡 할래? 타이핑 치기 귀찮아
english: Wanna voice call? I'm too lazy to type.
alt: Let's just hop on a call. Typing is such a hassle.
note: voice call = 음성 통화 / hassle = 귀찮은 일

korean: 안녕하세요 처음 뵙겠습니다
english: Hello. It's nice to meet you.
alt: How do you do? Pleased to make your acquaintance.
note: How do you do는 격식. Pleased to make your acquaintance는 매우 격식

korean: 오랜만이다! 잘 지냈어?
english: Long time no see! How've you been?
alt: It's been ages! What have you been up to?
note: long time no see = 오랜만이야 / what have you been up to = 뭐 하며 지냈어?

korean: 먼저 갈게 안녕!
english: I'm heading out. See ya!
alt: I gotta go. Catch you later!
note: heading out = 나가다 / catch you later = 나중에 봐

korean: 내일 봐 잘 자
english: See you tomorrow. Good night.
alt: See ya tomorrow. Sleep tight.
note: sleep tight = 잘 자 / see ya = see you의 줄임

korean: 수고하셨습니다 먼저 퇴근할게요
english: Good work today. I'm heading out first.
alt: Great job today. I'll take off now.
note: heading out = 나가다 / take off = 출발하다. 한국식 '수고하셨습니다'의 자연스러운 영어 표현

korean: 새해 복 많이 받으세요
english: Happy New Year!
alt: Wishing you a wonderful new year!
note: Happy New Year는 가장 보편적. Wishing you는 좀 더 격식

korean: 생일 축하해! 원하는 거 다 이루어져라
english: Happy birthday! May all your wishes come true.
alt: Happy birthday! Hope this year is your best one yet.
note: May all your wishes come true = 모든 소원이 이루어지길

korean: 결혼 축하해 행복하게 잘 살아
english: Congratulations on your wedding. Wishing you a lifetime of happiness.
alt: Congrats on tying the knot! Wishing you both all the best.
note: tying the knot = 결혼하다 (관용 표현)

korean: 새 집 축하해! 집들이는 언제야?
english: Congrats on the new place! When's the housewarming?
alt: Nice new digs! When are you having people over?
note: housewarming = 집들이 / new digs = 새 집 (슬랭)

korean: 추석 잘 보내! 가족들이랑 좋은 시간 보내
english: Have a great Chuseok! Enjoy the time with your family.
alt: Happy Chuseok! Hope you have a wonderful holiday with your family.
note: Chuseok은 영어로 보통 그대로 사용하거나 Korean Thanksgiving이라 설명

korean: 거기서 만날까 아니면 내가 데리러 갈까?
english: Should we meet there or should I pick you up?
alt: Want to meet at the spot or should I swing by and get you?
note: pick you up = 데리러 가다 / swing by = 들르다

korean: 몇 시에 만날까? 나는 아무 때나 괜찮아
english: What time should we meet? I'm flexible.
alt: When works for you? I'm free whenever.
note: flexible = 유연한 / whenever = 아무 때나

korean: 미안 오늘 약속 취소해야 할 것 같아
english: Sorry I think I have to cancel our plans for today.
alt: I'm sorry but I need to take a rain check on today.
note: take a rain check = 다음에 하자 (취소 시). cancel plans = 약속을 취소하다

korean: 장소 바꾸자 거기보다 여기가 더 가깝잖아
english: Let's change the location. This place is closer for both of us.
alt: How about we switch venues? This one is more convenient.
note: switch venues = 장소를 바꾸다 / convenient = 편리한

korean: 30분 정도 늦을 것 같아 먼저 시작해
english: I'm gonna be about 30 minutes late. Go ahead and start without me.
alt: Running 30 minutes behind. Don't wait up for me.
note: running behind = 늦어지고 있는 / don't wait up = 기다리지 마

korean: 거의 다 왔어 5분이면 도착해
english: I'm almost there. Five minutes away.
alt: I'm right around the corner. Be there in five.
note: almost there = 거의 다 온 / right around the corner = 바로 근처에

korean: 어제 야근했더니 오늘 너무 피곤해
english: I worked overtime yesterday and I'm exhausted today.
alt: I stayed late at work last night and I'm running on fumes.
note: work overtime = 야근하다 / running on fumes = 체력이 바닥난

korean: 월요일이 왜 이렇게 빨리 와? 주말이 너무 짧아
english: Why does Monday come so fast? Weekends are way too short.
alt: Monday already? The weekend flew by.
note: flew by = 빨리 지나갔다 / way too short = 너무 짧은

korean: 퇴근하고 싶다 시계만 보고 있어
english: I just wanna go home. I keep looking at the clock.
alt: I'm counting down the minutes until I can leave.
note: counting down = 카운트다운 하는 / keep looking at = 계속 보고 있는

korean: 점심시간이 왜 이렇게 안 오지
english: Why is lunch break taking so long to come?
alt: Lunch can't come soon enough.
note: can't come soon enough = 빨리 왔으면. taking so long = 오래 걸리는

korean: 회의가 너무 길어서 집중이 안 돼
english: The meeting is dragging on and I can't focus.
alt: This meeting is going on forever and I've lost all focus.
note: drag on = 질질 끌다 / lost all focus = 집중력을 잃은

korean: 상사한테 깨졌어 진짜 억울해
english: I got chewed out by my boss. It's so unfair.
alt: My boss ripped into me and I feel so wronged.
note: chew out / rip into = 심하게 혼내다 / wronged = 억울한

korean: 이직할까 고민 중이야 여기 너무 답답해
english: I'm thinking about switching jobs. This place is suffocating.
alt: I'm considering a career change. I feel stuck here.
note: switch jobs = 이직하다 / suffocating = 숨 막히는 / stuck = 갇힌

korean: 연봉 협상 어떻게 하지? 떨려
english: How do I negotiate my salary? I'm so nervous.
alt: Salary negotiation is stressing me out. Any tips?
note: negotiate salary = 연봉을 협상하다 / stressing me out = 스트레스 주는

korean: 재택근무가 편하긴 한데 집중이 안 돼
english: Working from home is convenient but I can't focus.
alt: Remote work is nice but it's hard to stay productive.
note: work from home / remote work = 재택근무 / productive = 생산적인

korean: 오늘 면접 봤는데 망한 것 같아
english: I had an interview today and I think I blew it.
alt: My interview today was a disaster. I don't think I got it.
note: blew it = 망치다 / disaster = 재앙

korean: 내일 발표 준비 아직 안 했어 큰일이다
english: I haven't prepared for tomorrow's presentation yet. I'm screwed.
alt: My presentation is tomorrow and I'm not ready at all. I'm in trouble.
note: screwed = 큰일 난 / in trouble = 곤란한

korean: 동료가 자기 일을 나한테 떠넘겨
english: My coworker keeps dumping their work on me.
alt: My colleague is constantly pawning off their tasks on me.
note: dump on / pawn off = 떠넘기다

korean: 야 그 사이트 해킹당했대 비밀번호 바꿔
english: Hey that site got hacked. Change your password.
alt: That website had a data breach. Update your password ASAP.
note: data breach = 데이터 유출 / ASAP = 최대한 빨리

korean: 충전기 좀 빌려줄 수 있어? 배터리 1%야
english: Can I borrow your charger? My battery is at 1%.
alt: My phone is about to die. Got a charger I can use?
note: about to die = 곧 꺼질 / charger = 충전기

korean: 알람을 꺼놨었나 봐 완전 늦잠 잤어
english: I must've turned off my alarm. I totally overslept.
alt: Somehow my alarm didn't go off and I slept way past my time.
note: overslept = 늦잠 잔 / alarm didn't go off = 알람이 안 울린

korean: 이불 밖은 위험해 나가기 싫어
english: Leaving the blanket is dangerous. I don't wanna get out.
alt: The outside world beyond my blanket is a no-go zone.
note: 이불 밖은 위험해는 한국 유행어. no-go zone = 접근 금지 구역

korean: 커피 없으면 하루를 못 시작해
english: I can't start my day without coffee.
alt: Coffee is the only thing that gets me going in the morning.
note: get me going = 활력을 주다 / can't start my day = 하루를 시작 못 한다

korean: 잠이 부족해서 하루 종일 졸려
english: I didn't get enough sleep so I'm drowsy all day.
alt: I'm running on no sleep and I've been nodding off all day.
note: drowsy = 졸린 / nodding off = 졸고 있는 / running on no sleep = 잠을 못 잔 채로

korean: 오늘 하루 진짜 길다 빨리 끝나라
english: Today feels so long. I just want it to be over.
alt: This day is dragging. Can it just end already?
note: dragging = 질질 끄는 / be over = 끝나다

korean: 퇴근하고 치킨 시켜야지 오늘 자격 있어
english: I'm ordering chicken after work. I deserve it today.
alt: Treating myself to chicken tonight. I've earned it.
note: deserve it = 자격이 있다 / treat myself = 나한테 보상하다 / earned it = 자격 있다

korean: 집에 오니까 살 것 같다 오늘 진짜 힘들었어
english: Being home feels like heaven. Today was brutal.
alt: Finally home. What a rough day.
note: feels like heaven = 천국 같다 / brutal/rough = 힘든

korean: 샤워하고 나니까 개운하다
english: I feel so refreshed after that shower.
alt: That shower hit different. I feel like a new person.
note: refreshed = 개운한 / hit different = 특별히 좋다 (슬랭)

korean: 자기 전에 폰 좀 그만 봐야 하는데 못 끊겠어
english: I should stop scrolling before bed but I can't help it.
alt: I need to put my phone down before sleep but I'm addicted.
note: scrolling = 스크롤하다 / can't help it = 어쩔 수 없다 / addicted = 중독된

korean: 내일 뭐 입지? 옷이 없어
english: What am I gonna wear tomorrow? I have nothing.
alt: I need to pick an outfit for tomorrow but my closet is empty.
note: outfit = 옷차림 / closet = 옷장. I have nothing to wear는 과장 표현

korean: 우유가 떨어졌네 마트 가야 되나
english: We're out of milk. Should I go to the store?
alt: We ran out of milk. Guess I need to make a grocery run.
note: out of / ran out of = 떨어진 / grocery run = 장 보러 가기

korean: 장 보러 가는데 뭐 필요한 거 있어?
english: I'm going grocery shopping. Need anything?
alt: I'm heading to the store. Want me to pick anything up?
note: grocery shopping = 장보기 / pick up = 사오다

korean: 냉장고에 먹을 게 하나도 없어
english: There's nothing to eat in the fridge.
alt: The fridge is completely empty.
note: nothing to eat = 먹을 것이 없는 / completely empty = 완전히 빈

korean: 이거 유통기한 지났다 버려야겠다
english: This is past its expiration date. I should throw it out.
alt: This expired. Time to toss it.
note: expiration date = 유통기한 / throw out / toss = 버리다

korean: 설거지 좀 해라 싱크대에 그릇이 쌓여 있잖아
english: Do the dishes. The sink is overflowing.
alt: Wash the dishes already. They're piling up in the sink.
note: do the dishes = 설거지하다 / overflowing/piling up = 쌓여있는

korean: 빨래 돌려야 하는데 맨날 까먹어
english: I need to do the laundry but I keep forgetting.
alt: I always forget to start the laundry.
note: do the laundry = 빨래하다 / keep forgetting = 계속 까먹다

korean: 방 좀 치워 너무 지저분해
english: Clean your room. It's a mess.
alt: Tidy up your room. It looks like a tornado hit it.
note: a mess = 엉망인 / looks like a tornado hit it = 매우 지저분한 (과장)

korean: 화장실 청소 누가 할 거야? 맨날 나만 해
english: Who's gonna clean the bathroom? It's always me.
alt: I'm always the one scrubbing the bathroom. Someone else take a turn.
note: scrubbing = 문질러 닦는 / take a turn = 번갈아 하다

korean: 분리수거 해야 되는데 귀찮다
english: I need to sort the recycling but I'm too lazy.
alt: I should separate the recyclables but I can't be bothered.
note: sort/separate the recycling = 분리수거하다 / can't be bothered = 귀찮다

korean: 전기세가 이번 달 왜 이렇게 많이 나왔지?
english: Why is the electricity bill so high this month?
alt: How come our power bill skyrocketed this month?
note: electricity/power bill = 전기세 / skyrocketed = 급등한

korean: 수도꼭지에서 물이 새고 있어 고쳐야 해
english: The faucet is leaking. We need to fix it.
alt: There's a drip from the faucet. We should call a plumber.
note: faucet = 수도꼭지 / leaking = 새는 / plumber = 배관공

korean: 에어컨 필터 청소해야 하는데 어떻게 하는 거야?
english: I need to clean the AC filter. How do I do that?
alt: The AC filter needs cleaning. Do you know how?
note: AC filter = 에어컨 필터. 정기적으로 청소해야 하는 부분

korean: 택배 왔는데 내가 주문한 거 아닌 것 같아
english: A package came but I don't think it's what I ordered.
alt: I got a delivery but it doesn't look like my order.
note: package = 택배 / doesn't look like = ~같지 않은

korean: 이사 준비해야 하는데 짐이 너무 많아
english: I need to start packing for the move but I have so much stuff.
alt: Moving prep is overwhelming with all this stuff I've accumulated.
note: packing = 짐 싸기 / accumulated = 쌓아온

korean: 옆집이 너무 시끄러워 잠을 못 자겠어
english: The neighbors are so loud I can't sleep.
alt: The people next door are making so much noise I can't get any sleep.
note: neighbors = 이웃 / next door = 옆집 / loud = 시끄러운

korean: 코딩 하다가 막혀서 한 시간째 삽질 중이야
english: I've been stuck on this code and I've been going in circles for an hour.
alt: I hit a wall with my code and I've been spinning my wheels for an hour.
note: going in circles / spinning my wheels = 삽질하다 / hit a wall = 벽에 부딪히다

korean: 노래방 가자 스트레스 풀자!
english: Let's go to karaoke! I need to blow off some steam.
alt: Let's hit up noraebang. I need to destress.
note: blow off steam = 스트레스를 풀다 / hit up = 가다 (슬랭)

korean: 이 노래 귀에서 안 떠나 계속 흥얼거려
english: This song is stuck in my head. I keep humming it.
alt: I've had this song on repeat in my brain all day.
note: stuck in my head = 머릿속에서 안 떠나는 / humming = 흥얼거리는

korean: 새로 나온 앨범 들어봤어? 완전 명반이야
english: Have you listened to the new album? It's a masterpiece.
alt: Did you check out the new album? It's an absolute banger.
note: masterpiece = 명작 / banger = 대박 곡/앨범 (슬랭)

korean: 콘서트 티켓팅 실패했어 1분 만에 매진됐어
english: I failed to get concert tickets. They sold out in a minute.
alt: Ticketing was a disaster. Everything was gone within seconds.
note: sold out = 매진된 / ticketing = 티켓 구매 경쟁 (콩글리시를 설명)

korean: 플레이리스트 공유해 줘 요즘 들을 노래가 없어
english: Share your playlist with me. I have nothing to listen to.
alt: Send me your playlist. I need new music.
note: playlist = 재생목록 / I need new music = 새 노래가 필요해

korean: 이어폰 한쪽이 안 들려 또 사야 하나
english: One side of my earbuds isn't working. Do I need new ones?
alt: My left earbud died. Guess I need to buy another pair.
note: earbud = 이어폰 / died = 작동 안 되는 (기기에 대해)

korean: 화면 깨져서 수리 맡겨야 해
english: My screen is cracked. I need to get it repaired.
alt: I cracked my screen and need to take it in for repairs.
note: cracked = 금이 간 / take it in for repairs = 수리 맡기다

korean: 새 폰 나왔는데 바꿀까 말까
english: The new phone is out. Should I upgrade or not?
alt: A new phone just dropped. I'm debating whether to upgrade.
note: dropped = 출시된 (슬랭) / upgrade = 기기를 바꾸다

korean: 데이터가 다 떨어졌어 와이파이 찾아야 해
english: I used up all my data. I need to find Wi-Fi.
alt: I'm out of mobile data. Gotta find a hotspot.
note: used up / out of = 다 쓴 / hotspot = 와이파이 지점

korean: 클라우드에 백업해 놔야 폰 바꿔도 안 날아가
english: You should back it up to the cloud so you don't lose it when switching phones.
alt: Cloud backup is a must if you don't wanna lose everything.
note: back up = 백업하다 / cloud = 클라우드

korean: 앱이 자꾸 튕겨 업데이트해도 안 고쳐져
english: The app keeps crashing. Updating didn't fix it.
alt: This app is buggy. Even after updating it still crashes.
note: crashing = 튕기는 / buggy = 버그가 있는

korean: 사진 저장 공간이 부족해 뭘 지워야 할지 모르겠어
english: I'm running out of storage because of photos. I don't know what to delete.
alt: My phone storage is full and I can't figure out what to get rid of.
note: running out of storage = 저장 공간 부족 / get rid of = 없애다

korean: 요즘 챗GPT 많이 쓰더라 나도 써볼까?
english: Everyone's using ChatGPT these days. Should I try it too?
alt: ChatGPT is everywhere lately. Maybe I should give it a shot.
note: give it a shot = 시도해 보다 / everywhere = 어디에나 있는

korean: 갤럭시가 나아 아이폰이 나아? 맨날 이 논쟁이야
english: Galaxy or iPhone? Which is better? This debate never ends.
alt: Samsung vs Apple — this argument is literally endless.
note: debate/argument = 논쟁 / never ends / endless = 끝나지 않는

korean: 시험 망했어 공부를 더 할 걸 그랬어
english: I bombed the exam. I should've studied more.
alt: I totally failed the test. I wish I had studied harder.
note: bombed = 망치다 / should've = ~했어야 했는데

korean: 레포트 마감이 내일인데 아직 시작도 안 했어
english: My report is due tomorrow and I haven't even started.
alt: The paper's deadline is tomorrow and I literally haven't touched it.
note: due = 마감인 / haven't even started = 시작도 안 한

korean: 과제 같이 할 사람? 혼자 하기 싫어
english: Anyone wanna work on the assignment together? I don't want to do it alone.
alt: Looking for a study buddy for this assignment.
note: study buddy = 함께 공부하는 친구 / assignment = 과제

korean: 도서관 자리 없어서 카페 왔어
english: The library was full so I came to a café.
alt: Couldn't find a seat at the library so I ended up at a café.
note: full = 꽉 찬 / ended up = 결국 ~하게 됐다

korean: 강의 녹화본 다시 들어야 하는데 어디서 봐?
english: I need to rewatch the lecture recording. Where can I find it?
alt: Where do I access the recorded lecture? I need to review it.
note: lecture recording = 강의 녹화본 / review = 복습하다

korean: 학점이 이번에 잘 나왔으면 좋겠다
english: I hope I get good grades this semester.
alt: Fingers crossed my GPA goes up this semester.
note: fingers crossed = 행운을 빌며 / GPA = 학점 평균

korean: 졸업하려면 아직 멀었어 학점이 부족해
english: I still have a long way to go before graduation. I need more credits.
alt: Graduation is still far off. I'm short on credits.
note: a long way to go = 갈 길이 먼 / short on credits = 학점이 부족한

korean: 등록금이 너무 비싸서 대출 받았어
english: Tuition is so expensive I had to take out a loan.
alt: I had to get a student loan because the tuition fees are ridiculous.
note: tuition = 등록금 / take out a loan = 대출을 받다

korean: 술 한 잔 하러 갈까? 오늘 기분 좋으니까
english: Wanna grab a drink? I'm in a good mood today.
alt: Let's go for a drink. I feel like celebrating.
note: grab a drink = 한 잔 하다 / celebrating = 축하하는

korean: 어제 너무 마셨더니 숙취가 장난 아니야
english: I drank too much last night and my hangover is killing me.
alt: I'm so hungover from last night. Never drinking again.
note: hangover = 숙취 / killing me = 죽을 것 같은 / never drinking again = 또 안 마신다 (항상 하는 말)

korean: 나 술 안 마셔 오늘 운전해야 해서
english: I'm not drinking tonight. I have to drive.
alt: I'll pass on the drinks. I'm the designated driver tonight.
note: designated driver = 대리운전자/음주 안 하는 운전자

korean: 안주 뭐 시킬까? 치킨 어때?
english: What should we order for snacks? How about chicken?
alt: What sides should we get? Fried chicken sound good?
note: snacks/sides = 안주 / sound good = 괜찮아?

korean: 소주 한 잔 더 할래? 아직 괜찮아?
english: Want another shot of soju? You still good?
alt: One more round? Can you handle it?
note: another shot = 한 잔 더 / can you handle it = 괜찮아?

korean: 대리운전 불러야겠다 이만큼 마셨으면
english: I should call a designated driver. I've had too much.
alt: I need to get a ride service. I'm way too drunk to drive.
note: designated driver service = 대리운전 / way too drunk = 너무 취한

korean: 여행 가고 싶다 어디 좋을까?
english: I want to travel somewhere. Any suggestions?
alt: I'm itching to go on a trip. Where should I go?
note: itching to = ~하고 싶어 근질거리는 / any suggestions = 추천 있어?

korean: 비행기 표 언제 사는 게 제일 싸?
english: When's the cheapest time to buy a plane ticket?
alt: When should I book my flight to get the best deal?
note: cheapest time = 가장 싼 시기 / get the best deal = 최고의 가격을 얻다

korean: 숙소 예약했어? 이 시기에는 빨리 해야 돼
english: Did you book accommodation? You need to do it quick during this season.
alt: Have you reserved a place to stay? It fills up fast this time of year.
note: accommodation = 숙소 / fills up fast = 빨리 차는

korean: 여권 만료됐어 갱신해야 하는데
english: My passport expired. I need to renew it.
alt: My passport's out of date. I gotta get it renewed.
note: expired / out of date = 만료된 / renew = 갱신하다

korean: 짐 싸는 거 맨날 까먹어서 체크리스트 만들었어
english: I always forget stuff when packing so I made a checklist.
alt: I created a packing list because I always leave something behind.
note: packing = 짐 싸기 / leave something behind = 뭔가를 두고 오다

korean: 환율이 좋을 때 환전해야지
