english: I should exchange money when the rate is good.
alt: I'll convert currency when the exchange rate is favorable.
note: exchange rate = 환율 / convert currency = 환전하다 / favorable = 좋은

korean: 제주도 가는데 렌트카 빌려야 해?
english: Are you renting a car for Jeju?
alt: Should we get a rental car for the Jeju trip?
note: rental car = 렌트카 / rent a car = 차를 빌리다

korean: 면세점에서 뭐 사올 거 있어?
english: Do you want anything from duty-free?
alt: Need me to pick up anything from the duty-free shop?
note: duty-free = 면세 / pick up = 사오다

korean: 시차 적응이 안 돼서 새벽에 깨
english: I can't adjust to the time difference and I keep waking up at dawn.
alt: Jet lag is hitting me hard. I've been waking up at 4 AM.
note: jet lag = 시차 부적응 / adjust to = 적응하다

korean: 여행 사진 정리해야 하는데 너무 많아
english: I need to organize my travel photos but there are so many.
alt: I have thousands of travel photos to sort through.
note: organize/sort through = 정리하다

korean: 아 국내 여행도 좋더라 근처에 좋은 데 많아
english: Domestic travel is nice too. There are great spots nearby.
alt: You don't have to go abroad. There are amazing places close by.
note: domestic travel = 국내 여행 / go abroad = 해외에 가다

korean: 캠핑 가고 싶다 자연 속에서 쉬고 싶어
english: I want to go camping. I want to relax in nature.
alt: I'm craving a camping trip. I need some time in nature.
note: craving = 간절히 원하는 / in nature = 자연 속에서

korean: 텐트 치는 거 생각보다 어렵더라
english: Setting up a tent is harder than I thought.
alt: Pitching a tent turned out to be way more difficult than expected.
note: set up / pitch a tent = 텐트를 치다

korean: 바베큐 준비물 뭐 필요해?
english: What do we need for the barbecue?
alt: What supplies should we bring for the BBQ?
note: supplies = 준비물 / BBQ = 바베큐

korean: 별 보러 가자 오늘 하늘 맑대
english: Let's go stargazing. I heard it's gonna be clear tonight.
alt: The sky is supposed to be clear tonight. Let's go look at the stars.
note: stargazing = 별 보기 / clear = 맑은

korean: 드라이브 가자 바람 쐬러
english: Let's go for a drive. I need some fresh air.
alt: Wanna take a road trip? I need to clear my head.
note: go for a drive = 드라이브하다 / clear my head = 머리를 식히다

korean: 독서 모임 시작했는데 생각보다 재밌어
english: I joined a book club and it's more fun than I expected.
alt: I started going to a reading group. It's surprisingly enjoyable.
note: book club / reading group = 독서 모임 / surprisingly = 놀랍게도

korean: 퍼즐 맞추다가 시간 가는 줄 몰랐어
english: I was doing a puzzle and lost track of time.
alt: I got so absorbed in the puzzle I didn't realize how late it got.
note: lost track of time = 시간 가는 줄 모르다 / absorbed = 몰두한

korean: 그림 그리기 시작했는데 힐링 된다
english: I started painting and it's so therapeutic.
alt: I picked up drawing and it's really healing.
note: therapeutic = 치유적인 / pick up = (취미를) 시작하다

korean: 뜨개질 배우고 싶은데 어려울까?
english: I want to learn how to knit. Is it difficult?
alt: I'm thinking about picking up knitting. Is it hard?
note: knit = 뜨개질하다 / pick up = 배우기 시작하다

korean: 요리 유튜브 보면서 따라 했는데 실패했어
english: I tried following a cooking video on YouTube and failed.
alt: I attempted a recipe from YouTube and it turned out terrible.
note: following a recipe = 레시피를 따라하다 / turned out terrible = 결과가 안 좋은

korean: 밀키트 사서 해먹으니까 편하더라
english: Meal kits are so convenient. I just buy and cook.
alt: Using meal kits has made cooking so much easier.
note: meal kit = 밀키트 / convenient = 편리한

korean: 이 레시피 쉬운데 맛있어 해봐
english: This recipe is easy and delicious. You should try it.
alt: Try this recipe. It's super simple and tastes amazing.
note: recipe = 레시피 / simple = 간단한

korean: 라면 끓이는 것도 사람마다 다르더라
english: Everyone has their own way of making ramen.
alt: Apparently everyone makes instant noodles differently.
note: instant noodles = 인스턴트 라면. ramen은 미국에서도 통용

korean: 에어프라이어 없으면 어떻게 살아? 진짜 편해
english: How do people live without an air fryer? It's a game changer.
alt: An air fryer is literally essential. Life before it was rough.
note: game changer = 판도를 바꾸는 / essential = 필수적인

korean: 이 맛집 예약 잡기가 하늘의 별 따기야
english: Getting a reservation at this restaurant is nearly impossible.
alt: Booking a table here is like winning the lottery.
note: reservation = 예약 / nearly impossible = 거의 불가능한

korean: 오마카세 가볼까? 요즘 핫하던데
english: Should we try an omakase? It's trending lately.
alt: Want to try an omakase spot? They're all the rage right now.
note: omakase = 오마카세 / all the rage = 대유행인

korean: 맵찔이라 매운 거 못 먹어
english: I can't handle spicy food. I'm a total lightweight.
alt: I have zero spice tolerance.
note: can't handle = 감당 못 하는 / spice tolerance = 매운맛 내성

korean: 여기 양이 너무 적다 가성비가 안 좋아
english: The portions here are tiny. Not worth the money.
alt: The serving size is so small for what you pay. Bad value.
note: portions = 양 / not worth the money = 돈 값을 못 하는

korean: 디저트는 별도 위장이야
english: Dessert goes in a separate stomach.
alt: There's always room for dessert.
note: 별도 위장 = separate stomach. There's always room for dessert = 디저트 공간은 항상 있다 (영어 관용구)

korean: 맛있다고 소문난 집은 다 가봤어
english: I've been to every restaurant that's supposed to be good.
alt: I've tried all the hyped-up places.
note: hyped-up = 소문난 / supposed to be = ~라고 하는

korean: 리뷰 별점 높은 데로 가자
english: Let's go to the one with the highest ratings.
alt: Let's pick the place with the best reviews.
note: ratings = 평점 / reviews = 리뷰

korean: 저 밀가루 끊으려고 하는데 너무 힘들어
english: I'm trying to cut out flour but it's so hard.
alt: Going gluten-free is way harder than I imagined.
note: cut out = 끊다 / gluten-free = 글루텐 프리

korean: 간헐적 단식 중인데 배가 너무 고파
english: I'm doing intermittent fasting and I'm starving.
alt: I'm on IF and my stomach won't stop growling.
note: intermittent fasting (IF) = 간헐적 단식 / growling = 꼬르륵 거리는

korean: 물 많이 마셔야 하는데 맨날 까먹어
english: I need to drink more water but I always forget.
alt: Staying hydrated is something I constantly forget to do.
note: hydrated = 수분 보충된 / constantly = 항상

korean: 체중계 올라가기 싫다 무서워
english: I'm scared to step on the scale.
alt: I'm afraid to weigh myself.
note: step on the scale = 체중계에 올라가다 / weigh myself = 체중을 재다

korean: 간식 끊으려고 했는데 또 먹었어
english: I was trying to quit snacking but I caved again.
alt: I told myself no more snacks but I gave in.
note: caved / gave in = 굴복하다 / quit snacking = 간식을 끊다

korean: 이번 달 식비가 너무 많이 나왔어
english: I spent way too much on food this month.
alt: My food expenses this month are through the roof.
note: food expenses = 식비 / through the roof = 매우 높은

korean: 가계부 써야 하는데 귀찮아서 못 쓰겠어
english: I should keep a budget but I'm too lazy.
alt: Tracking my expenses feels like a chore so I keep putting it off.
note: keep a budget = 가계부를 쓰다 / putting it off = 미루다

korean: 월급이 통장을 스쳐 지나갔어
english: My paycheck just passed through my account.
alt: My salary barely touched my account before it was gone.
note: passed through = 스쳐 지나간. 한국에서 흔히 쓰는 유머 표현

korean: 돈 모으기가 왜 이렇게 힘들까
english: Why is saving money so hard?
alt: How come it's so difficult to build up savings?
note: saving money = 돈을 모으다 / build up savings = 저축을 쌓다

korean: 카드값 나올 때마다 심장이 쫄깃해
english: My heart skips a beat every time I see my credit card bill.
alt: Getting my card statement gives me anxiety every month.
note: heart skips a beat = 심장이 철렁한 / card statement = 카드 명세서

korean: 충동구매 했는데 후회 중이야
english: I impulse-bought something and now I'm regretting it.
alt: I made a spur-of-the-moment purchase and I'm kicking myself.
note: impulse buy = 충동구매 / spur-of-the-moment = 즉흥적인 / kicking myself = 후회하는

korean: 택배 기다리는 게 제일 설레
english: Waiting for a delivery is the most exciting thing.
alt: The anticipation of a package arriving is the best feeling.
note: anticipation = 기대감 / the best feeling = 최고의 기분

korean: 중고로 팔까 버리기는 아까워
english: Should I sell it secondhand? It'd be a waste to throw it away.
alt: I might list it on a resale app. Tossing it seems wasteful.
note: secondhand = 중고 / resale app = 중고거래 앱 / wasteful = 낭비인

korean: 이거 직구로 사면 훨씬 싸
english: It's way cheaper if you buy it directly from overseas.
alt: Ordering this through international shipping saves a ton.
note: buy directly from overseas = 해외 직구 / saves a ton = 많이 절약하는

korean: 관세 맞으면 싸게 산 의미 없잖아
english: If customs hits you it defeats the purpose of buying cheap.
alt: Getting slapped with duties makes the savings pointless.
note: customs / duties = 관세 / defeats the purpose = 의미가 없어지다

korean: 이번 블프 때 뭐 살 거야?
english: What are you getting during Black Friday?
alt: Any Black Friday deals you're eyeing?
note: Black Friday = 블프 / eyeing = 눈여겨보는

korean: 장마 시작됐어 우산 꼭 챙겨
english: The rainy season started. Don't forget your umbrella.
alt: Monsoon season is here. Make sure you carry an umbrella.
note: rainy season / monsoon season = 장마철 / carry an umbrella = 우산을 챙기다

korean: 눈 오니까 기분 좋다 겨울 분위기 나는데?
english: It's snowing and I love it. Feels so cozy and wintery.
alt: The snow puts me in such a good mood. Winter vibes for real.
note: cozy = 아늑한 / wintery = 겨울 같은 / vibes = 분위기

korean: 봄이 왔나 봐 벚꽃 피기 시작했어
english: Spring must be here. The cherry blossoms are starting to bloom.
alt: Looks like spring has arrived. The cherry trees are blossoming.
note: cherry blossoms = 벚꽃 / bloom / blossom = 피다

korean: 단풍 구경 가자 이번 주가 절정이래
english: Let's go see the fall foliage. This week is supposed to be peak.
alt: We should check out the autumn leaves. They're at their peak this week.
note: fall foliage = 단풍 / peak = 절정

korean: 겨울인데 패딩 안 입으면 못 나가
english: You can't go outside without a puffy jacket in this weather.
alt: It's impossible to leave the house without a heavy winter coat.
note: puffy jacket = 패딩 / winter coat = 겨울 코트

korean: 바람이 너무 세서 우산이 뒤집어졌어
english: The wind was so strong my umbrella turned inside out.
alt: My umbrella flipped inside out because of the crazy wind.
note: turned inside out = 뒤집어진 / crazy wind = 미친 바람

korean: 아 덥다 아이스 아메리카노 시급하다
english: It's so hot. I desperately need an iced Americano.
alt: I'm dying from the heat. I need iced coffee stat.
note: desperately = 간절히 / stat = 즉시 (의학 용어에서 유래한 슬랭)

korean: 한파 주의보 떴어 내일 영하 15도래
english: There's a cold wave warning. It's supposed to drop to minus 15 tomorrow.
alt: Cold weather advisory is up. Tomorrow's low is negative 15.
note: cold wave warning = 한파 주의보 / drop to = ~까지 떨어지다

korean: 황사 때문에 목이 칼칼해
english: The yellow dust is making my throat scratchy.
alt: My throat feels raw because of the dust storm.
note: yellow dust = 황사 / scratchy / raw = 칼칼한

korean: 운전면허 시험 떨어졌어 다시 봐야 해
english: I failed my driving test. I have to retake it.
alt: I didn't pass the driving exam. Gotta go again.
note: failed / didn't pass = 떨어졌다 / retake = 다시 보다

korean: 주차할 데가 없어서 세 바퀴나 돌았어
english: There was nowhere to park so I circled the block three times.
alt: I drove around for ages looking for parking.
note: circled the block = 블록을 돌다 / drove around = 돌아다니다

korean: 과속 카메라에 찍혔어 벌금 나오겠다
english: I got caught by a speed camera. I'm gonna get a ticket.
alt: I was speeding and got flashed by a camera. Expecting a fine.
note: speed camera = 과속 카메라 / ticket / fine = 벌금

korean: 출퇴근 시간에 지하철 타면 지옥이야
english: Taking the subway during rush hour is absolute hell.
alt: The subway at commute time is a nightmare.
note: rush hour = 출퇴근 시간 / absolute hell = 완전 지옥

korean: 킥보드 타고 다니는 사람 요즘 진짜 많다
english: So many people ride electric scooters these days.
alt: Electric scooters are everywhere now.
note: electric scooter = 전동 킥보드 / everywhere = 어디에나

korean: 자전거 출퇴근 시작했는데 운동도 되고 좋아
english: I started biking to work and it's great. I get exercise too.
alt: Cycling to work kills two birds with one stone — exercise and commute.
note: biking/cycling to work = 자전거 출퇴근 / kill two birds with one stone = 일석이조

korean: 하이패스가 안 인식돼서 그냥 지나갔어
english: My toll pass didn't register so I just drove through.
alt: The automatic toll reader didn't pick up my card.
note: toll pass = 하이패스/통행료 패스 / register = 인식하다

korean: 네비 없으면 길치라 아무 데도 못 가
english: Without GPS I'd be lost. I have a terrible sense of direction.
alt: I'm completely directionally challenged. GPS is my lifeline.
note: terrible sense of direction = 방향 감각이 나쁜 / lifeline = 생명줄

korean: 우버 불러 여기서 걸어가기엔 너무 멀어
english: Let's get an Uber. It's too far to walk from here.
alt: Call a ride. Walking from here would take forever.
note: get an Uber / call a ride = 차를 부르다

korean: 기차 예매했어? 명절에는 매진 빨리 돼
english: Did you book the train? It sells out fast during holidays.
alt: Have you reserved train tickets? They go quick during the holiday season.
note: sell out / go quick = 빨리 매진되다

korean: 맛집 투어 하자 이 동네 먹을 거 많대
english: Let's do a food crawl. I heard this neighborhood has tons of good eats.
alt: Let's go on a food tour. This area is known for great restaurants.
note: food crawl / food tour = 맛집 투어 / good eats = 맛있는 음식

korean: 혼밥해야 되는데 어디 가지? 혼자 가기 편한 데 없나
english: I need to eat alone. Where's a good solo-friendly place?
alt: Eating by myself tonight. Know any spots that are comfortable for one?
note: solo-friendly = 혼자 가기 편한. 혼밥 문화는 한국에서 흔함

korean: 여기 웨이팅 몇 팀이에요?
english: How many groups are ahead of me?
alt: How long is the wait?
note: groups ahead = 앞에 있는 팀 수 / how long is the wait = 얼마나 기다려야 해

korean: 여기 리필 되나요?
english: Are refills free here?
alt: Can I get a refill on this?
note: refill = 리필 / free refills = 무료 리필

korean: 계산서 주세요
english: Can I get the check please?
alt: Check please.
note: check / bill = 계산서. 미국에서는 check이 일반적

korean: 더치페이 하자 각자 내자
english: Let's split the bill.
alt: Let's go Dutch. Everyone pays for their own.
note: split the bill = 나눠 내다 / go Dutch = 더치페이하다

korean: 오늘 내가 살게 네가 지난번에 쐈잖아
english: It's on me today. You got it last time.
alt: My treat today. You paid last time.
note: on me / my treat = 내가 사는 / got it = 냈다

korean: 카드 안 되나요? 현금밖에 안 받아요?
english: Do you not take cards? Cash only?
alt: Is card payment not available? Do you only accept cash?
note: take cards = 카드를 받다 / cash only = 현금만

korean: 포장 가능한가요?
english: Can I get this to go?
alt: Is takeout available?
note: to go = 포장 / takeout = 테이크아웃

korean: 알바하기 싫다 오늘 하루만 쉬고 싶어
english: I don't wanna go to my part-time job. I just want one day off.
alt: I'm dreading my shift today. I wish I could call in.
note: part-time job = 알바 / dreading = 두려운 / call in = 결근하다

korean: 월급날 아직 멀었나 벌써 돈이 없어
english: Is payday still far off? I'm already broke.
alt: Payday can't come soon enough. I'm flat broke.
note: broke / flat broke = 돈이 없는 / payday = 월급날

korean: 보너스 나온다고 했는데 안 나오네
english: They said we'd get a bonus but it never came.
alt: The bonus they promised never materialized.
note: bonus = 보너스 / materialized = 실현된

korean: 직장 상사가 오늘도 야근시켰어
english: My boss made me work overtime again today.
alt: My supervisor had me stay late again.
note: work overtime / stay late = 야근하다 / supervisor = 상사

korean: 팀 회식인데 가기 싫다
english: There's a team dinner and I don't want to go.
alt: We have a company dinner and I'm not feeling it.
note: team/company dinner = 회식 / not feeling it = 내키지 않는

korean: 워라밸이 중요하지 일만 하면 뭐해
english: Work-life balance matters. What's the point of just working?
alt: WLB is everything. Life isn't just about work.
note: work-life balance (WLB) = 워라밸 / what's the point = 무슨 의미가 있어

korean: 이번 프로젝트 진짜 힘들다 언제 끝나지
english: This project is killing me. When will it be over?
alt: This project is draining. I can't see the end.
note: killing me = 힘들게 하는 / draining = 지치게 하는

korean: 후임이 일을 안 배우려고 해서 답답해
english: The new guy won't learn the job and it's frustrating.
alt: Training the newbie is driving me crazy. They just won't pick things up.
note: newbie = 신입 / pick things up = 배우다 / driving me crazy = 미치게 하는

korean: 오늘 칼퇴하자 더 이상 못 버텨
english: Let's leave on time today. I can't take it anymore.
alt: I'm clocking out on the dot today. I'm done.
note: leave on time / clock out on the dot = 칼퇴하다 / I'm done = 더 못 하겠다

korean: 메일 보냈는데 답장이 안 와 확인 좀 해줘
english: I sent you an email. Can you check it? I haven't gotten a reply.
alt: I emailed you but haven't heard back. Could you take a look?
note: haven't heard back = 답장을 못 받은 / take a look = 확인하다

korean: 회의 시간 바뀐 거 알고 있어? 2시로 변경됐어
english: Did you know the meeting time changed? It got moved to 2.
alt: Heads up — the meeting got rescheduled to 2 o'clock.
note: heads up = 알려주는 (사전 경고) / rescheduled = 일정이 변경된

korean: 보고서 마감 오늘까지야 다 끝냈어?
english: The report is due today. Did you finish?
alt: Today's the deadline for the report. All done?
note: due = 마감인 / deadline = 마감일 / all done = 다 했어?

korean: 프린터 종이 없어 누가 좀 넣어줘
english: The printer is out of paper. Can someone refill it?
alt: We're out of paper in the printer. Someone needs to load it.
note: out of paper = 종이가 없는 / load = 넣다

korean: 점심 같이 먹을 사람? 혼자 먹기 외로워
english: Anyone free for lunch? I don't wanna eat alone.
alt: Who wants to grab lunch? I need some company.
note: grab lunch = 점심 먹다 / company = 함께하는 사람

korean: 엘리베이터가 너무 느려서 계단으로 올라갔어
english: The elevator was so slow I took the stairs.
alt: I got tired of waiting for the lift so I walked up.
note: took the stairs = 계단으로 올라간 / lift = elevator의 영국식 표현

korean: 에어컨 누가 끄지 마세요 진짜 더워요
english: Please don't turn off the AC. It's really hot.
alt: Whoever keeps turning off the AC — please stop. I'm melting.
note: melting = 녹고 있는 (더워서). whoever keeps = 누가 자꾸

korean: 복사기 고장 났어 수리 요청해야 해
english: The copy machine is broken. We need to call for repairs.
alt: The copier is down. Someone needs to submit a maintenance request.
note: broken / down = 고장난 / maintenance request = 수리 요청

korean: 입사한 지 벌써 1년이야 시간 진짜 빠르다
english: It's already been a year since I joined the company. Time flies.
alt: I can't believe it's been a year since I started here.
note: time flies = 시간이 빨리 간다 / since I joined = 입사한 이후

korean: 신입사원 들어왔는데 열정이 넘쳐
english: The new hire started and they're so enthusiastic.
alt: We got a new employee and they're full of energy.
note: new hire = 신입 / enthusiastic = 열정적인

korean: 주말에 부업 하고 있어 돈이 필요해서
english: I have a side hustle on weekends because I need extra cash.
alt: I'm doing a side gig on weekends to make some extra money.
note: side hustle / side gig = 부업 / extra cash = 추가 수입

korean: 프리랜서로 전환할까 고민 중이야
english: I'm thinking about going freelance.
alt: I'm considering transitioning to freelance work.
note: going freelance = 프리랜서로 전환하다 / transitioning = 전환하는

korean: 재택이라 집에서 잠옷 입고 일해
english: I work from home so I'm in my pajamas all day.
alt: Remote work perk — working in PJs without anyone knowing.
note: pajamas / PJs = 잠옷 / perk = 장점

korean: 줌 회의 중에 마이크 끄는 거 까먹었어
english: I forgot to mute myself during the Zoom meeting.
alt: I accidentally left my mic on during the video call.
note: mute = 음소거하다 / left my mic on = 마이크를 켜둔

korean: VPN 연결이 안 돼서 업무를 못 하겠어
english: The VPN won't connect so I can't work.
alt: VPN issues are preventing me from getting any work done.
note: VPN = 가상사설망 / preventing = 방해하는

korean: 슬랙 메시지 읽었는데 답장 까먹었어
english: I read the Slack message but forgot to reply.
alt: I saw the Slack notification but totally forgot to respond.
note: Slack = 업무 메신저 / forgot to reply = 답장을 까먹은

korean: 노트북 배터리가 다 돼서 충전해야 해
english: My laptop battery is dying. I need to charge it.
alt: My laptop's about to run out of juice. Where can I plug in?
note: dying = 다 되어가는 / run out of juice = 배터리가 떨어진 (슬랭)

korean: 모니터 두 개 쓰니까 업무 효율이 올라갔어
english: Using dual monitors boosted my productivity.
alt: Having two screens has been a game changer for my workflow.
note: dual monitors = 듀얼 모니터 / productivity = 생산성

korean: 키보드 소리가 시끄러워서 민원 들어왔어
english: Someone complained about my keyboard being too loud.
alt: I got a noise complaint about my mechanical keyboard.
note: noise complaint = 소음 민원 / mechanical keyboard = 기계식 키보드

korean: 오늘 점심 도시락 싸왔어 절약 중이야
english: I brought a packed lunch today. I'm trying to save money.
alt: I packed my own lunch to cut down on spending.
note: packed lunch = 도시락 / cut down on spending = 지출을 줄이다

korean: 자판기 커피가 이 가격이면 차라리 카페 가지
english: Vending machine coffee at this price? I might as well go to a café.
alt: If the machine coffee costs this much I'd rather just buy real coffee.
note: might as well = 차라리 ~하겠다 / I'd rather = ~하는 게 낫다

korean: 요즘 너무 바빠서 친구들을 못 만나
english: I've been so busy I haven't been able to see my friends.
alt: I barely have time to hang out with my friends lately.
note: barely have time = 시간이 거의 없는 / hang out = 만나 놀다

korean: 주말부부라 평일에 혼자 있어서 외로워
english: I'm in a weekend marriage, so I'm alone on weekdays and it's lonely.
alt: We live separately during the week so I get lonely on weekdays.
note: weekend marriage = 주말부부 / live separately = 따로 살다

korean: 친구가 약속을 또 펑크 냈어 짜증 나
english: My friend flaked on me again. I'm so annoyed.
alt: My friend bailed on our plans again. It's getting old.
note: flake on / bail on = 약속을 어기다 / getting old = 지겨워지는

korean: 걔 연락 안 되는데 무슨 일 있나?
english: I can't reach them. I wonder if something happened.
alt: They're not responding. Should I be worried?
note: can't reach = 연락이 안 되는 / not responding = 응답이 없는

korean: 우리 언제 한번 모이자 오랜만에
english: We should all get together sometime. It's been a while.
alt: Let's plan a reunion soon. It's been too long.
note: get together = 모이다 / reunion = 재회 / it's been a while = 오래됐다

korean: 같이 스터디 할 사람 구해
english: I'm looking for people to form a study group.
alt: Anyone interested in joining a study group?
note: study group = 스터디 그룹 / form = 구성하다

korean: 결혼식 축의금 얼마 내야 할까?
english: How much should I give as a wedding gift?
alt: What's the appropriate amount for wedding money?
note: wedding gift/money = 축의금. 한국 문화에서 현금 봉투가 일반적

korean: 장례식 가야 하는데 뭐라고 말해야 할지 모르겠어
english: I have to go to a funeral and I don't know what to say.
alt: I'm attending a funeral and I'm struggling with what to say.
note: funeral = 장례식 / struggling with = ~에 고민하는

korean: 돌잔치 초대받았는데 선물 뭐가 좋을까?
english: I was invited to a first birthday party. What should I get as a gift?
alt: What's a good gift for a baby's first birthday celebration?
note: first birthday party = 돌잔치. 한국 전통 행사 / celebration = 축하 행사

korean: 소개팅 나가는데 너무 떨려
english: I'm going on a blind date and I'm so nervous.
alt: I have a setup tonight and I'm freaking out.
note: blind date / setup = 소개팅 / freaking out = 매우 긴장한

korean: 첫인상이 중요하니까 옷 좀 신경 써
english: First impressions matter so put some effort into your outfit.
alt: Make sure you dress nice. First impressions count.
note: first impression = 첫인상 / put effort into = 신경 쓰다

korean: 연애하고 싶다 솔로 너무 오래됐어
english: I want to be in a relationship. I've been single for too long.
alt: I'm ready to date again. Being single is getting old.
note: be in a relationship = 연애하다 / getting old = 지겨워지는

korean: 썸 타는 중인데 고백해야 할까?
english: We've been vibing but should I confess my feelings?
alt: We're in the talking stage. Should I make it official?
note: vibing = 감정이 통하는 / talking stage = 썸 타는 단계 / make it official = 공식화하다

korean: 우리 사귀는 거야 아닌 거야? 확실히 해줘
english: Are we together or not? I need clarity.
alt: Are we a thing or not? Just give me a straight answer.
note: a thing = 사귀는 사이 (슬랭) / clarity = 명확함 / straight answer = 분명한 답

korean: 기념일 깜빡했어 큰일났다
english: I forgot our anniversary. I'm in big trouble.
alt: I completely blanked on our anniversary. I'm dead.
note: anniversary = 기념일 / blanked on = 깜빡한 / I'm dead = 큰일 났다 (슬랭)

korean: 장거리 연애 힘들다 빨리 보고 싶어
english: Long-distance relationships are hard. I miss you so much.
alt: LDR is tough. I can't wait to see you.
note: long-distance relationship (LDR) = 장거리 연애

korean: 이별하고 나니까 허전하다
english: I feel so empty after the breakup.
alt: There's a void since we broke up.
note: breakup = 이별 / void = 공허함 / empty = 텅 빈

korean: 전 여자친구를 우연히 만났어 어색했어
english: I ran into my ex and it was so awkward.
alt: I bumped into my ex-girlfriend randomly. It was uncomfortable.
note: ran into / bumped into = 우연히 만나다 / ex = 전 연인 / awkward = 어색한

korean: 육아가 이렇게 힘든 줄 몰랐어
english: I had no idea parenting was this hard.
alt: Raising kids is way harder than I ever imagined.
note: parenting / raising kids = 육아 / had no idea = 전혀 몰랐다

korean: 아기가 밤에 자꾸 깨서 잠을 못 자
english: The baby keeps waking up at night so I can't sleep.
alt: I'm not getting any sleep because the baby wakes up constantly.
note: keeps waking up = 계속 깨는 / constantly = 계속해서

korean: 어린이집 자리가 없어서 대기 중이야
english: There are no spots at the daycare so we're on a waiting list.
alt: We're waitlisted for daycare. No openings available.
note: daycare = 어린이집 / waiting list = 대기 명단 / openings = 자리

korean: 부모님이 건강하셔서 다행이야
english: I'm grateful my parents are healthy.
alt: Thank goodness my parents are doing well health-wise.
note: grateful = 감사한 / thank goodness = 다행히

korean: 부모님한테 효도해야 하는데 자주 못 뵈서 미안해
english: I should be a better child to my parents but I feel bad that I can't visit often.
alt: I wish I could see my parents more. I feel guilty for not visiting enough.
note: feel guilty = 죄책감을 느끼는 / visit = 방문하다

korean: 형제끼리 싸우면 안 되지 화해해
english: Siblings shouldn't fight. Make up with each other.
alt: Don't fight with your sibling. Patch things up.
note: siblings = 형제자매 / make up / patch things up = 화해하다

korean: 시댁 가기 싫다 명절만 되면 스트레스야
english: I don't want to go to my in-laws. Holidays stress me out.
alt: Visiting the in-laws during holidays is so stressful.
note: in-laws = 시댁/처가 / stress me out = 스트레스를 주다

korean: 조카가 너무 귀여워 삼촌 바보 됐어
english: My nephew is so cute. I'm a total pushover uncle.
alt: I've become the world's biggest softie for my niece.
note: pushover = 쉽게 넘어가는 사람 / softie = 마음이 약한 사람

korean: 강아지 산책 시간이야 가자!
english: It's time for the dog's walk. Let's go!
alt: Walkies! Time to take the pup out.
note: walkies = 산책 (강아지에게 하는 귀여운 표현)

korean: 고양이가 키보드 위에 올라와서 일을 못 해
english: My cat is on the keyboard and I can't work.
alt: The cat parked itself on my laptop and won't move.
note: parked itself = 떡하니 앉은 / won't move = 안 움직이는

korean: 동물 병원 갈 때마다 지갑이 텅텅 비어
english: Every vet visit empties my wallet.
alt: Going to the vet is highway robbery every single time.
note: vet = 동물 병원 / highway robbery = 바가지 요금 (비유)

korean: 반려동물 보험 들어야 하나? 병원비가 너무 비싸
english: Should I get pet insurance? The vet bills are insane.
alt: Pet insurance might be worth it considering how expensive vet bills are.
note: pet insurance = 반려동물 보험 / worth it = 가치 있는

korean: 아파트 관리비가 또 올랐어
english: The apartment maintenance fee went up again.
alt: Our building fees increased yet again.
note: maintenance fee = 관리비 / increased = 올라간

korean: 월세 내는 날이 또 왔다
english: Rent is due again.
alt: That time of the month — rent day.
note: rent is due = 월세 내야 할 때 / that time of the month = 그 시기가 왔다

korean: 전세 계약 갱신해야 하는데 주인이 올리래
english: I need to renew my lease but the landlord wants to raise the deposit.
alt: My lease renewal is coming up and the landlord wants to increase the jeonse.
note: lease renewal = 계약 갱신 / landlord = 집주인 / jeonse = 전세 (한국 특유 임대 제도)

korean: 이사 비용이 생각보다 많이 들어
english: Moving costs more than I expected.
alt: The moving expenses are way higher than I budgeted for.
note: moving costs/expenses = 이사 비용 / budgeted for = 예산을 잡은

korean: 인테리어 바꾸고 싶은데 돈이 없어
english: I want to redecorate but I don't have the money.
alt: I'd love to redo the interior but my budget says no.
note: redecorate = 인테리어를 바꾸다 / budget says no = 예산이 안 된다 (유머)

korean: 가구 조립하다가 나사가 남았어 이게 맞나?
english: I assembled the furniture and had leftover screws. Is that normal?
alt: There are spare screws after putting the furniture together. Should I be worried?
note: assembled = 조립한 / leftover/spare screws = 남은 나사

korean: 방음이 안 돼서 옆집 소리가 다 들려
english: The soundproofing is terrible. I can hear everything from next door.
alt: The walls are paper-thin. I hear every noise from the neighbors.
note: soundproofing = 방음 / paper-thin = 종이처럼 얇은

korean: 벌레가 나왔어! 누가 좀 잡아줘
english: A bug came out! Someone kill it!
alt: There's a bug! Can someone please get rid of it?
note: bug = 벌레 / get rid of = 제거하다

korean: 곰팡이가 생겼어 습기 때문인가 봐
english: There's mold growing. It must be because of the moisture.
alt: Mold appeared. The humidity must be the culprit.
note: mold = 곰팡이 / moisture/humidity = 습기 / culprit = 원인

korean: 보일러가 고장 났어 추워서 죽겠어
english: The boiler broke down and I'm freezing to death.
alt: The heating system is busted. I'm so cold I can barely function.
note: boiler = 보일러 / broke down / busted = 고장난

korean: 로봇 청소기 돌려놓으면 알아서 청소해줘서 편해
english: The robot vacuum cleans on its own. So convenient.
alt: Setting the robot vacuum to auto-clean is a game changer.
note: robot vacuum = 로봇 청소기 / on its own = 스스로 / auto-clean = 자동 청소

korean: 정수기 필터 교체 시기가 지났어
english: The water filter is overdue for a replacement.
alt: I'm past due on changing the water purifier filter.
note: overdue = 기한이 지난 / water purifier = 정수기 / filter = 필터

korean: 음쓰 버리러 가기 싫다 냄새 나
english: I don't want to take out the food waste. It smells.
alt: Taking out the food scraps is the worst. The smell is unbearable.
note: food waste / food scraps = 음식물 쓰레기 / unbearable = 참을 수 없는

korean: 택배 경비실에 맡겨달라고 해야겠다
english: I should ask them to leave the package at the security desk.
alt: I'll request the delivery to be left at the front desk.
note: security desk / front desk = 경비실 / leave the package = 택배를 맡기다

korean: 아이 학원비가 부담이야 너무 비싸
english: The tuition for my kid's academy is a financial burden.
alt: Hagwon fees are draining my wallet.
note: academy / hagwon = 학원 / financial burden = 재정적 부담

korean: 학교 폭력이 걱정돼 우리 아이 괜찮을까
english: I'm worried about school bullying. I hope my child is okay.
alt: Bullying at school scares me. Is my kid safe?
note: school bullying = 학교 폭력 / scares me = 무섭다

korean: 아이가 게임만 해서 걱정이야
english: My kid only plays games and I'm worried.
alt: All my child does is play video games. It's concerning.
note: concerning = 걱정되는 / all they do is = ~만 하다

korean: 참교육이 필요해 요즘 아이들은 버릇이 없어
english: Kids these days need some discipline. They have no manners.
alt: Today's kids lack basic respect. They need proper upbringing.
note: discipline = 훈육 / manners = 예의 / upbringing = 양육

korean: 시험 기간이라 아이가 예민해
english: It's exam season so my kid is on edge.
alt: My child is stressed out because of exams.
note: on edge = 예민한 / stressed out = 스트레스 받은

korean: 입시 준비 때문에 온 가족이 힘들어
english: The college entrance exam prep is exhausting for the whole family.
alt: The entire family is under stress because of university admissions.
note: college entrance exam = 대입 시험 / admissions = 입학

korean: 학부모 모임 가야 하는데 어색해
english: I have a parent meeting and it's always awkward.
alt: Parent-teacher conferences are so uncomfortable.
note: parent meeting / parent-teacher conference = 학부모 모임

korean: 오늘 수업이 너무 재밌었어 선생님이 최고야
english: Today's class was so fun. The teacher is the best.
alt: Class was awesome today. That teacher is amazing.
note: class = 수업 / awesome = 최고의

korean: 등하교길이 위험해서 걱정이야
english: The route to school is dangerous and I'm worried.
alt: I'm concerned about the safety of the walk to school.
note: route to school = 통학로 / concerned about safety = 안전이 걱정인

korean: 급식이 맛없대 도시락 싸줘야 하나?
english: My kid says the school lunch is bad. Should I pack a lunchbox?
alt: The cafeteria food is terrible according to my kid. Maybe I should make lunch.
note: school lunch = 급식 / cafeteria = 구내식당 / pack a lunchbox = 도시락을 싸다

korean: 방학이라 아이 돌볼 사람이 없어
english: It's school break and there's no one to watch the kids.
alt: The kids are on vacation and I have no childcare options.
note: school break = 방학 / watch the kids = 아이를 돌보다 / childcare = 보육

korean: 피부가 건조해서 각질이 일어나
english: My skin is so dry it's flaking.
alt: I've got dry patches all over. My skin is peeling.
note: flaking / peeling = 각질이 일어나는 / dry patches = 건조한 부분

korean: 여드름이 나서 신경 쓰여 어떻게 하지?
english: I'm breaking out and it's bothering me. What should I do?
alt: I've got acne and it's driving me crazy. Any tips?
note: breaking out = 여드름이 나는 / acne = 여드름

korean: 선크림 안 바르고 나갔다가 얼굴이 탔어
english: I went out without sunscreen and got a sunburn on my face.
alt: I forgot sunscreen and now my face is burnt to a crisp.
note: sunburn = 햇볕 화상 / burnt to a crisp = 심하게 탄

korean: 네일아트 받으러 가야지 손톱이 엉망이야
english: I need to get my nails done. They're a mess.
alt: Time for a manicure. My nails are in terrible shape.
note: get my nails done = 네일 받다 / manicure = 매니큐어

korean: 다크서클이 심해서 아무리 컨실러를 발라도 안 가려져
english: My dark circles are so bad that concealer can't even cover them.
alt: No amount of concealer can hide these dark circles.
note: dark circles = 다크서클 / concealer = 컨실러 / cover/hide = 가리다

korean: 향수 추천해 줘 새로운 거 쓰고 싶어
english: Recommend me a perfume. I want to try something new.
alt: Know any good fragrances? I'm looking for something different.
note: perfume / fragrance = 향수 / something different = 다른 것

korean: 메이크업 유튜브 보고 따라 했는데 완전 다르잖아
english: I followed a makeup tutorial on YouTube but it looks nothing like the original.
alt: I tried recreating a makeup look from YouTube and failed miserably.
note: tutorial = 튜토리얼 / recreating = 재현하는 / miserably = 비참하게

korean: 다이어트 중인데 야식의 유혹을 못 참겠어
english: I'm on a diet but I can't resist the temptation of late-night snacks.
alt: Late-night cravings are destroying my diet.
note: temptation = 유혹 / late-night cravings = 야식 욕구 / resist = 저항하다

korean: 계단 오르는데 무릎이 아파 나이 드는 게 느껴져
english: My knees hurt going up stairs. I can feel myself aging.
alt: Climbing stairs hurts my knees now. I'm really feeling my age.
note: feel myself aging / feeling my age = 나이를 느끼다

korean: 치과 가기 싫다 치과만 생각하면 무서워
english: I hate going to the dentist. Just thinking about it scares me.
alt: The dentist is my worst nightmare. I dread every visit.
note: dread = 두려워하다 / worst nightmare = 최악의 악몽

korean: 안경 도수가 안 맞아서 바꿔야 해
english: My glasses prescription is wrong. I need new ones.
alt: My prescription is off. I need to update my lenses.
note: prescription = 처방/도수 / lenses = 렌즈

korean: 렌즈 끼면 눈이 건조해져서 인공눈물 달고 살아
english: My eyes get dry with contacts so I'm always using eye drops.
alt: I can't survive wearing contacts without artificial tears.
note: contacts = 콘택트 렌즈 / eye drops / artificial tears = 인공눈물

korean: 건강보험 적용 안 되는 거라 비쌌어
english: Insurance didn't cover it, so it was expensive.
alt: It wasn't covered by health insurance so the bill was steep.
note: insurance / health insurance = 건강보험 / steep = 비싼

korean: 물리치료 받으러 가야 하는데 시간이 안 나
english: I need to go to physical therapy but I can't find the time.
alt: I've been putting off my PT sessions because of my schedule.
note: physical therapy (PT) = 물리치료 / putting off = 미루는

korean: MRI 찍어봐야 한대 무서워
english: They said I need an MRI and I'm scared.
alt: The doctor recommended an MRI. I'm freaking out a little.
note: MRI = 자기공명영상 / recommended = 권유한 / freaking out = 겁먹는

korean: 하루에 만 보 걷기 하고 있어
english: I'm walking 10000 steps a day.
alt: I've been doing the 10K steps challenge daily.
note: 10000 steps = 만 보 / daily = 매일

korean: 홈트 하는데 효과가 있는지 모르겠어
english: I'm working out at home but I'm not sure if it's working.
alt: I've been doing home workouts but I don't see any results.
note: working out at home / home workouts = 홈트 / results = 결과

korean: PT 받으면 자세 교정이 되더라
english: Personal training really helps with posture correction.
alt: A PT session does wonders for fixing your form.
note: personal training = 퍼스널 트레이닝 / posture correction = 자세 교정

korean: 등산 가고 싶은데 같이 갈 사람 없어
english: I want to go hiking but I have no one to go with.
alt: Looking for a hiking buddy. Anyone down?
note: hiking = 등산 / anyone down = 누구 할래? (슬랭)

korean: 계단 대신 엘리베이터 타는 것도 죄책감이야
english: I feel guilty for taking the elevator instead of the stairs.
alt: Even choosing the lift over the stairs makes me feel bad.
note: feel guilty = 죄책감을 느끼다 / instead of = 대신

korean: 기말고사 끝나서 해방이다!
english: Finals are over! I'm free!
alt: Done with finals! Sweet freedom!
note: finals = 기말고사 / freedom = 자유 / I'm free = 해방이다

korean: 오늘 집에서 넷플릭스 정주행 할 거야
english: I'm gonna binge Netflix all day at home.
alt: Today's plan: couch + Netflix + zero responsibilities.
note: binge = 정주행하다 / zero responsibilities = 할 일 없음

korean: 게임하다 보면 시간이 어떻게 가는지 모르겠어
english: When I'm gaming I completely lose track of time.
alt: I sit down to play and suddenly it's 3 AM.
note: lose track of time = 시간 가는 줄 모르다 / suddenly = 갑자기

korean: 보드게임 할 사람? 4명 필요해
english: Who wants to play a board game? We need four people.
alt: Anyone up for a board game? We need one more player.
note: board game = 보드게임 / anyone up for = 누구 할래?

korean: 새로운 취미 찾고 싶은데 뭐가 좋을까?
english: I want to pick up a new hobby. Any recommendations?
alt: I need a new hobby. What do you suggest?
note: pick up a hobby = 취미를 시작하다 / recommendations = 추천

korean: 사진 찍는 게 취미인데 카메라가 비싸
english: Photography is my hobby but cameras are expensive.
alt: I love taking photos as a hobby but the gear costs a fortune.
note: photography = 사진 촬영 / gear = 장비 / costs a fortune = 매우 비싼

korean: 낚시 갔는데 한 마리도 못 잡았어
english: I went fishing and didn't catch a single fish.
alt: Went fishing and came back empty-handed.
note: catch a fish = 물고기를 잡다 / empty-handed = 빈손으로

korean: 볼링 치러 가자 오랜만에
english: Let's go bowling. It's been a while.
alt: How about a round of bowling? Haven't gone in ages.
note: bowling = 볼링 / in ages = 오랫동안

korean: 탁구 좋아해? 한 판 칠래?
english: Do you like ping pong? Wanna play a game?
alt: Are you into table tennis? Up for a match?
note: ping pong / table tennis = 탁구 / up for = ~할 의향이 있는

korean: 배드민턴 치자 운동도 되고 재밌어
english: Let's play badminton. It's exercise and it's fun.
alt: Badminton is a blast and you burn calories too.
note: badminton = 배드민턴 / a blast = 매우 재밌는 / burn calories = 칼로리를 태우다

korean: 골프 시작했는데 돈이 너무 많이 들어
english: I started playing golf but it's so expensive.
alt: Golf is draining my bank account.
note: draining my bank account = 통장을 비우는 / expensive = 비싼

korean: 클라이밍 해봤어? 생각보다 재밌더라
english: Have you tried rock climbing? It's more fun than you'd think.
alt: You should try bouldering. It's surprisingly addictive.
note: rock climbing = 클라이밍 / bouldering = 볼더링 / addictive = 중독성 있는

korean: 필라테스 시작했는데 코어가 약해서 힘들어
english: I started Pilates but my core is weak so it's tough.
alt: Pilates is no joke when your core strength is basically zero.
note: Pilates = 필라테스 / core = 코어(복부 중심) / no joke = 만만치 않은

korean: 크로스핏 하고 나면 일주일은 아파
english: After CrossFit I'm sore for a whole week.
alt: CrossFit wrecks my body for days afterward.
note: CrossFit = 크로스핏 / sore = 아픈 / wrecks = 망가뜨리다

korean: 축구 보러 가자 이번 경기 대박이래
english: Let's go watch soccer. This game is supposed to be epic.
alt: Wanna catch the match? I heard it's gonna be a banger.
note: soccer / football = 축구 / epic / banger = 대박인

korean: 야구장 가서 치맥 하자
english: Let's go to the baseball stadium and have chicken and beer.
alt: Nothing beats fried chicken and beer at the ballpark.
note: ballpark = 야구장 / chicken and beer = 치맥

korean: 올림픽 보느라 밤새웠어
english: I stayed up all night watching the Olympics.
alt: I pulled an all-nighter for the Olympic games.
note: stayed up all night / pulled an all-nighter = 밤새운

korean: 응원하는 팀이 졌어 너무 아쉬워
english: My team lost. I'm so disappointed.
alt: The team I root for lost. What a bummer.
note: root for = 응원하다 / bummer = 실망스러운 일

korean: MVP가 부상당해서 시즌 끝이래
english: The MVP got injured and is out for the season.
alt: Their star player is sidelined for the rest of the season with an injury.
note: MVP = 최우수선수 / out for the season = 시즌 아웃 / sidelined = 빠진

korean: 동네 한 바퀴 돌고 올게
english: I'm gonna take a walk around the neighborhood.
alt: I'll go for a quick stroll around the block.
note: take a walk = 산책하다 / stroll = 산책 / around the block = 동네 한 바퀴

korean: 공원에서 피크닉 하자 날씨도 좋은데
english: Let's have a picnic in the park. The weather's perfect.
alt: It's perfect weather for a park picnic.
note: picnic = 피크닉 / perfect weather = 완벽한 날씨

korean: 한강 가서 치킨 먹자
english: Let's go to the Han River and eat chicken.
alt: Let's hit up the Han River for some riverside chicken.
note: hit up = 가다 (슬랭). 한강에서 치킨 먹기는 한국 문화

korean: 놀이공원 가고 싶다 롤러코스터 타자!
english: I wanna go to an amusement park! Let's ride the roller coaster!
alt: Let's hit up a theme park and go on all the roller coasters!
note: amusement/theme park = 놀이공원 / roller coaster = 롤러코스터

korean: 워터파크 갈까? 더위에 물놀이 최고지
english: Should we go to a water park? Swimming is the best way to beat the heat.
alt: Wanna hit the water park? Best way to cool off in this heat.
note: water park = 워터파크 / beat the heat / cool off = 더위를 식히다

korean: 찜질방 가자 땀 좀 빼자
english: Let's go to a jjimjilbang. Let's sweat it out.
alt: How about a Korean sauna? I need to detox.
note: jjimjilbang = 찜질방 (한국식 대중목욕탕) / sweat it out = 땀을 빼다 / detox = 해독

korean: 노래방 몇 시간 할까? 2시간은 기본이지
english: How many hours for karaoke? Two hours is the minimum.
alt: Karaoke for at least two hours. One hour is never enough.
note: karaoke = 노래방 / minimum = 최소

korean: 방탈출 해봤어? 진짜 재밌더라
english: Have you tried an escape room? It's so much fun.
alt: You should do an escape room. It's a total adrenaline rush.
note: escape room = 방탈출 / adrenaline rush = 아드레날린이 솟는

korean: PC방 갈래? 한 판만 하자
english: Wanna hit the PC room? Just one game.
alt: Let's go to the gaming café. One round, I promise.
note: PC room / gaming café = PC방. 한 판만 = just one game

korean: VR 체험 해봤어? 어지럽긴 한데 재밌어
english: Have you tried VR? It makes you dizzy but it's fun.
alt: VR experiences are cool but they can make you nauseous.
note: VR = 가상현실 / dizzy / nauseous = 어지러운

korean: 페스티벌 가고 싶다 올해는 꼭!
english: I want to go to a festival this year. For sure!
alt: I'm going to a music festival this year no matter what.
note: festival = 페스티벌 / no matter what = 무슨 일이 있어도

korean: 전시회 보러 갈래? 이번 주까지야
english: Want to go to the exhibition? It ends this week.
alt: There's an exhibition closing this week. Wanna check it out?
note: exhibition = 전시회 / closing = 끝나는

korean: 뮤지컬 봤는데 감동적이었어
english: I saw a musical and it was so moving.
alt: The musical I watched was absolutely breathtaking.
note: musical = 뮤지컬 / moving / breathtaking = 감동적인

korean: 독서실 가서 공부해야 하는데 집중이 안 돼
english: I need to study at the reading room but I can't concentrate.
alt: I should go to the study hall but my focus is nonexistent.
note: reading room / study hall = 독서실 / concentrate / focus = 집중하다

korean: 자격증 따려고 공부 중이야
english: I'm studying to get a certification.
alt: I'm working toward getting my license/certification.
note: certification / license = 자격증 / working toward = ~을 위해 노력하는

korean: 코딩 독학 중인데 어렵다
english: I'm self-teaching coding and it's hard.
alt: I'm learning to code on my own and it's a struggle.
note: self-teaching = 독학하는 / on my own = 혼자서 / struggle = 고전

korean: 영어 공부 꾸준히 하는 게 제일 중요해
english: Consistency is key when studying English.
alt: The most important thing with English is being consistent.
note: consistency = 꾸준함 / key = 핵심 / consistent = 꾸준한

korean: 토익 점수 올려야 취업이 돼
english: I need a higher TOEIC score to get a job.
alt: Improving my TOEIC score is essential for employment.

note: dry cleaner = 세탁소 / ridiculous = 터무니없는

korean: 우산 또 잃어버렸어 올해만 세 번째야
english: I lost my umbrella again. That's the third time this year.
alt: Another umbrella gone. I've lost three this year alone.
note: lost = 잃어버린 / that's the third time = 세 번째다

korean: 지갑에 현금이 하나도 없어 카드만 있어
english: I have zero cash in my wallet. Only cards.
alt: My wallet is card-only. Not a single bill inside.
note: zero cash = 현금 제로 / card-only = 카드만

korean: 교통카드 잔액이 부족해서 충전해야 해
english: My transit card balance is low. I need to top it up.
alt: I need to reload my bus card. It's almost empty.
note: transit card = 교통카드 / top it up / reload = 충전하다 / balance = 잔액

korean: 일회용품 줄여야 하는데 습관이 안 바뀌어
english: I should reduce single-use products but I can't change my habits.
alt: I know I should cut back on disposables but old habits die hard.
note: single-use / disposables = 일회용품 / old habits die hard = 오래된 습관은 바꾸기 어렵다

korean: 텀블러 가져오면 할인해 주는 데 많아
english: A lot of places give you a discount if you bring your own tumbler.
alt: Many cafés offer a discount for bringing a reusable cup.
note: tumbler / reusable cup = 텀블러 / discount = 할인

korean: 에코백 쓰려고 하는데 맨날 까먹고 안 가져가
english: I'm trying to use an eco bag but I always forget to bring it.
alt: I keep meaning to bring my tote bag but I forget every time.
note: eco bag / tote bag = 에코백 / keep meaning to = 하려고 하는데 계속

korean: 분리배출 제대로 해야 해 환경을 위해서
english: We need to sort our recycling properly for the environment.
alt: Proper waste separation is important for the planet.
note: sort recycling = 분리배출하다 / waste separation = 쓰레기 분류

korean: 미세 플라스틱이 심각하대 무서워
english: They say microplastics are a serious problem. It's scary.
alt: The microplastic issue is alarming. It's everywhere in our food.
note: microplastics = 미세 플라스틱 / alarming = 심각한

korean: 채식 시작해 볼까 건강에도 좋고 환경에도 좋잖아
english: Should I go vegetarian? It's good for health and the environment.
alt: I'm considering going plant-based. It's better for my health and the planet.
note: vegetarian / plant-based = 채식 / considering = 고려하는

korean: 제로 웨이스트 실천하기 진짜 어렵다
english: Practicing zero waste is really hard.
alt: Going zero waste is way more difficult than influencers make it look.
note: zero waste = 제로 웨이스트 / practicing = 실천하는

korean: 전기차 충전소가 부족해서 불편해
english: There aren't enough EV charging stations. It's inconvenient.
alt: The lack of charging infrastructure for electric vehicles is frustrating.
note: EV = 전기차 / charging stations = 충전소 / infrastructure = 인프라

korean: 요즘 날씨가 이상해 지구온난화 때문인가
english: The weather has been weird lately. Is it because of global warming?
alt: Climate change is making the weather so unpredictable.
note: global warming / climate change = 지구온난화 / unpredictable = 예측 불가능한

korean: 재활용이 안 되는 게 너무 많아
english: So many things can't actually be recycled.
alt: It's shocking how much of what we think is recyclable actually isn't.
note: recycled / recyclable = 재활용되는 / shocking = 충격적인

korean: 봉사활동 하면 보람 있더라
english: Volunteering feels so rewarding.
alt: Doing community service gives you a real sense of purpose.
note: volunteering / community service = 봉사활동 / rewarding = 보람 있는

korean: 기부하고 싶은데 어디에 해야 좋을까?
english: I want to donate but where should I give?
alt: I'd like to make a donation. Any reputable organizations you'd recommend?
note: donate / make a donation = 기부하다 / reputable = 평판 좋은

korean: 아나바다 운동 알아? 절약하자는 뜻이야
english: Do you know the 아나바다 movement? It means to save and reuse.
alt: There's a Korean concept called 아나바다 — it promotes saving, reusing, and sharing.
note: 아나바다 = 아껴쓰고 나눠쓰고 바꿔쓰고 다시쓰자. movement = 운동

korean: 플리마켓에서 싸게 샀어 보물 같은 거 발견했어
english: I got it cheap at a flea market. Found a real gem.
alt: I scored a treasure at the flea market.
note: flea market = 플리마켓 / gem / treasure = 보물 같은 것

korean: 빈티지 좋아해? 요즘 레트로가 유행이야
english: Do you like vintage? Retro is trending these days.
alt: Are you into vintage stuff? The retro aesthetic is all the rage.
note: vintage = 빈티지 / retro = 레트로 / all the rage = 대유행

korean: 다이소 가면 뭐든 다 있어 진짜 신기해
english: Daiso has literally everything. It's amazing.
alt: You can find anything at Daiso. That place is a goldmine.
note: Daiso = 다이소 / literally everything = 정말 다 / goldmine = 보물 창고

korean: 마트에서 1+1 하길래 충동구매 했어
english: The grocery store had a buy-one-get-one deal so I impulse bought it.
alt: I couldn't resist the BOGO offer at the supermarket.
note: buy-one-get-one (BOGO) = 1+1 / impulse buy = 충동구매

korean: 블랙프라이데이 때 미리 사놔야지
english: I'm gonna stock up during Black Friday.
alt: I'll do all my shopping on Black Friday to save big.
note: stock up = 미리 사놓다 / save big = 크게 절약하다

korean: 택배 상자가 너무 많아서 집이 창고 같아
english: I have so many delivery boxes my house looks like a warehouse.
alt: There are so many packages my place is turning into a storage unit.
note: warehouse / storage unit = 창고 / delivery boxes = 택배 상자

korean: 코스트코 가면 대용량만 있어서 고민돼
english: Everything at Costco is bulk-sized and I'm never sure if I need that much.
alt: Costco's bulk quantities always make me debate whether it's worth it.
note: bulk-sized / bulk quantities = 대용량 / worth it = 가치 있는

korean: 장 볼 때 장바구니 가져가야 비닐봉지 안 써
english: Bring a shopping bag so you don't have to use plastic bags.
alt: If you bring your own bag you won't need to buy a plastic one.
note: shopping bag = 장바구니 / plastic bags = 비닐봉지

korean: 영양제 뭐 먹어? 추천 좀 해줘
english: What supplements do you take? Can you recommend some?
alt: What vitamins and supplements are you on? Any recommendations?
note: supplements = 영양제 / vitamins = 비타민 / recommendations = 추천

korean: 프로바이오틱스가 장 건강에 좋대
english: I heard probiotics are good for gut health.
alt: Probiotics apparently do wonders for digestive health.
note: probiotics = 프로바이오틱스 / gut health / digestive health = 장 건강

korean: 오메가3 매일 먹어야 한대
english: They say you should take omega-3 daily.
alt: Daily omega-3 supplementation is recommended.
note: omega-3 = 오메가3 / supplementation = 보충 / daily = 매일

korean: 콜라겐 먹으면 진짜 피부 좋아져?
english: Does collagen actually improve your skin?
alt: Do collagen supplements really make a difference for skin?
note: collagen = 콜라겐 / make a difference = 차이를 만들다

korean: 운동 후에 단백질 보충 해야 돼
english: You need to refuel with protein after working out.
alt: Post-workout protein intake is essential for muscle recovery.
note: protein = 단백질 / refuel = 보충하다 / muscle recovery = 근육 회복

korean: 수면의 질이 중요하지 시간만 많다고 되는 건 아니야
english: Sleep quality matters more than quantity.
alt: It's not about how long you sleep but how well you sleep.
note: sleep quality = 수면의 질 / quantity = 양

korean: 카페인 줄여야 하는데 못 줄이겠어
english: I should cut down on caffeine but I just can't.
alt: Reducing my caffeine intake is something I know I should do but can't commit to.
note: cut down on = 줄이다 / caffeine intake = 카페인 섭취 / commit to = 실천하다

korean: 명상 해봤어? 진짜 도움 된대
english: Have you tried meditation? It's supposed to really help.
alt: Meditation is supposed to be incredibly beneficial. Have you given it a shot?
note: meditation = 명상 / beneficial = 유익한 / give it a shot = 시도하다

korean: 마인드풀니스가 뭐야? 요즘 많이 들리는데
english: What is mindfulness? I keep hearing about it.
alt: What exactly is mindfulness? Everyone talks about it but I'm not sure what it means.
note: mindfulness = 마인드풀니스 (현재에 집중하는 것) / keep hearing about = 자주 듣는

korean: 정신 건강도 중요해 상담 받는 거 부끄러운 게 아니야
english: Mental health matters too. There's no shame in getting counseling.
alt: Seeking therapy isn't something to be embarrassed about. Mental health is just as important.
note: mental health = 정신 건강 / counseling / therapy = 상담 / no shame = 부끄럽지 않은

korean: 그 사람 성격이 좋아서 같이 있으면 편해
english: Their personality is so great that I feel comfortable around them.
alt: They're so easygoing that hanging out with them is always relaxing.
note: personality = 성격 / comfortable = 편안한 / easygoing = 편한

korean: 사회생활 힘들다 눈치 보는 게 일이야
english: Navigating social dynamics at work is exhausting. Reading the room is a full-time job.
alt: Workplace politics are draining. Having to watch what you say all the time is tiring.
note: social dynamics = 사회적 역학 / reading the room = 눈치 보기

korean: 세대 차이가 느껴져 요즘 유행어를 모르겠어
english: I can feel the generation gap. I don't know the latest slang.
alt: The generational divide is real. I have no idea what young people are saying.
note: generation gap = 세대 차이 / slang = 유행어

korean: 갑분싸 됐어 분위기가 갑자기 싸해졌어
english: The mood suddenly got awkward. Total buzzkill.
alt: Things got really quiet all of a sudden. Super uncomfortable.
note: 갑분싸 = 갑자기 분위기 싸해짐. buzzkill = 분위기를 깨는 사람/것

korean: TMI야 그건 말 안 해도 돼
english: That's TMI. You didn't need to share that.
alt: Too much information. I did not need to know that.
note: TMI = Too Much Information = 과도한 정보. 한국에서도 그대로 씀

korean: 리액션 좀 해줘 내가 말하는데 반응이 없어
english: Give me some kind of reaction. You're not responding to anything I say.
alt: At least react when I'm talking. You're giving me nothing.
note: reaction = 반응 / respond = 응답하다

korean: 공감 능력이 좋은 사람이 좋아
english: I like people with high empathy.
alt: I appreciate people who are emotionally intelligent.
note: empathy = 공감 / emotionally intelligent = 감정 지능이 높은

korean: 뒷담화 하지 마 안 좋아
english: Don't talk behind people's backs. It's not cool.
alt: Gossiping about others is a bad look.
note: talk behind someone's back = 뒷담화하다 / gossiping = 험담하는 / bad look = 안 좋아 보이는

korean: 솔직히 말해줘 돌려 말하지 말고
english: Just be straight with me. Don't sugarcoat it.
alt: Give it to me straight. No beating around the bush.
note: be straight = 솔직하게 / sugarcoat = 감싸서 말하다 / beating around the bush = 돌려 말하다

korean: 비밀이야 아무한테도 말하지 마
english: This is a secret. Don't tell anyone.
alt: Keep this between us. It's confidential.
note: secret / confidential = 비밀의 / keep between us = 우리끼리만

korean: 거짓말은 안 통해 나 다 알아
english: Your lies won't work on me. I know everything.
alt: Don't even try lying. I can see right through you.
note: lies won't work = 거짓말이 안 통하다 / see through = 꿰뚫어 보다

korean: 약속은 지켜야지 신뢰가 중요해
english: You have to keep your promises. Trust is important.
alt: Keeping your word is essential. It's a matter of trust.
note: keep promises / keep your word = 약속을 지키다 / trust = 신뢰

korean: 경쟁이 너무 심해서 힘들어
english: The competition is so fierce it's exhausting.
alt: The competitive pressure is overwhelming.
note: fierce competition = 치열한 경쟁 / overwhelming = 압도적인

korean: 인맥이 넓으면 도움이 많이 돼
english: Having a wide network really helps.
alt: A broad social network opens a lot of doors.
note: network = 인맥 / opens doors = 기회를 열어주다

korean: 첫 월급 받았을 때 기분이 어땠어?
english: How did you feel when you got your first paycheck?
alt: What was it like receiving your first salary?
note: first paycheck / salary = 첫 월급

korean: 부모님한테 첫 월급으로 선물 사드렸어
english: I bought my parents a gift with my first paycheck.
alt: I treated my parents with my first salary.
note: treated = 대접하다 / first paycheck = 첫 월급

korean: 명절에 세뱃돈 얼마 받았어?
english: How much money did you get for the holiday?
alt: How much did you receive in New Year's money?
note: New Year's money = 세뱃돈 / receive = 받다

korean: 로또 사볼까? 이번 주 당첨금이 크대
english: Should I buy a lottery ticket? The jackpot is big this week.
alt: The lotto jackpot is huge this week. Should I take a shot?
note: lottery ticket = 로또 / jackpot = 당첨금 / take a shot = 시도하다

korean: 편의점에서 삼각김밥이랑 우유 사 먹자
english: Let's get a triangle kimbap and milk from the convenience store.
alt: Let's grab onigiri and milk at the konbini.
note: convenience store = 편의점 / 삼각김밥 = triangle kimbap/onigiri

korean: 야식 먹으면 살찌는 거 아는데 참을 수가 없어
english: I know late-night snacking makes you gain weight but I can't resist.
alt: I'm fully aware that midnight munchies are bad for me but willpower is nonexistent.
note: late-night snacking = 야식 / resist = 참다 / midnight munchies = 한밤중 간식 욕구

korean: 떡볶이 먹고 싶다 매콤한 거
english: I'm craving some spicy tteokbokki.
alt: I could really go for some spicy rice cakes right now.
note: craving = 간절히 원하는 / tteokbokki = 떡볶이 / go for = 먹고 싶다

korean: 치킨은 역시 양념이지 후라이드 파?
english: Seasoned chicken is the way to go. Or are you team fried?
alt: You're either a seasoned chicken person or a fried chicken person. Which one?
note: seasoned = 양념 / fried = 후라이드 / the way to go = 최고의 선택

korean: 삼겹살 먹으러 갈래? 고기 땡겨
english: Wanna go eat pork belly? I'm craving meat.
alt: Let's go for samgyeopsal. I'm in the mood for some grilled meat.
note: pork belly = 삼겹살 / craving = 당기는 / grilled meat = 구운 고기

korean: 소주 한 잔 걸치자 기분 전환 겸
english: Let's have a shot of soju to change the mood.
alt: A glass of soju would really hit the spot right now.
note: hit the spot = 딱 맞다 / change the mood = 기분 전환

korean: 커피 중독인 것 같아 하루에 세 잔은 마셔
english: I think I'm addicted to coffee. I drink at least three cups a day.
alt: I'm definitely a coffee addict. Three cups minimum daily.
note: addicted = 중독된 / minimum = 최소

korean: 차 한 잔 하면서 이야기하자
english: Let's chat over a cup of tea.
alt: Let's have a heart-to-heart over some tea.
note: chat over = ~하면서 이야기하다 / heart-to-heart = 진심어린 대화

korean: 이 빵집 크루아상이 유명하대
english: This bakery is famous for its croissants.
alt: The croissants at this bakery are supposed to be legendary.
note: bakery = 빵집 / legendary = 전설적인 / famous for = ~로 유명한

korean: 디카페인으로 주세요 카페인에 민감해서
english: Decaf please. I'm sensitive to caffeine.
alt: Can I get that in decaf? Caffeine hits me hard.
note: decaf = 디카페인 / sensitive to = ~에 민감한 / hits me hard = 크게 영향을 주는

korean: 텀블러에 넣어주세요 일회용 컵 안 쓸래요
english: Put it in my tumbler please. I don't want a disposable cup.
alt: Can you fill my reusable cup? I'm trying to cut down on waste.
note: tumbler / reusable cup = 텀블러 / disposable cup = 일회용 컵

korean: 여기 케이크 맛있어? 하나 먹어볼까
english: Is the cake here good? Should I try one?
alt: Are the cakes any good here? I'm tempted.
note: tempted = 유혹받는 / any good = 맛있는

korean: 카공족이 많아서 자리가 없네
english: There are so many people studying here that there are no seats.
alt: This café is packed with students studying. Can't find a spot.
note: 카공족 = 카페에서 공부하는 사람. packed with = ~로 가득 찬

korean: 이 카페 사장님이 친절해서 자주 와
english: The owner here is so friendly that I come often.
alt: I'm a regular because the café owner is super nice.
note: owner = 주인 / regular = 단골 / friendly = 친절한

korean: SNS 끊어야 하는데 자꾸 보게 돼
english: I should quit social media but I keep checking it.
alt: I know I need a social media detox but I can't stop scrolling.
note: social media detox = SNS 디톡스 / scrolling = 스크롤하는

korean: 팔로워 수가 중요한 게 아니야 콘텐츠가 중요하지
english: It's not about follower count. Content is what matters.
alt: Follower numbers don't mean much. Quality content is king.
note: follower count = 팔로워 수 / content is king = 콘텐츠가 왕이다

korean: 알림 끄니까 훨씬 마음이 편해
english: Turning off notifications made me so much more at peace.
alt: Disabling push notifications was the best decision for my sanity.
note: notifications = 알림 / at peace = 마음이 편한 / sanity = 정신 건강

korean: 디지털 디톡스 해봤어? 효과 있대
english: Have you tried a digital detox? They say it's effective.
alt: Digital detox is supposed to do wonders for your well-being.
note: digital detox = 디지털 디톡스 / well-being = 웰빙/건강

korean: 화면 시간 줄여야 하는데 어떻게 줄이지
english: I need to reduce my screen time but how?
alt: My screen time is embarrassingly high. I need to cut back.
note: screen time = 화면 시간 / cut back = 줄이다 / embarrassingly = 부끄러울 정도로

korean: 블루라이트 차단 안경 쓰면 눈이 편해져?
english: Do blue light blocking glasses actually help your eyes?
alt: Are blue light glasses worth it for eye strain?
note: blue light blocking glasses = 블루라이트 차단 안경 / eye strain = 눈의 피로

korean: 이어폰 오래 끼면 귀 아파 어떡하지
english: My ears hurt from wearing earbuds too long. What should I do?
alt: Extended earbud use is killing my ears. Any solutions?
note: earbuds = 이어폰 / extended use = 장시간 사용 / killing my ears = 귀가 아프다

korean: 거북목이 심해졌어 자세 교정해야 해
english: My tech neck is getting worse. I need to fix my posture.
alt: My forward head posture has gotten really bad. I need to work on it.
note: tech neck / forward head posture = 거북목 / fix posture = 자세를 교정하다

korean: 손목이 아파 마우스 너무 오래 쓰나 봐
english: My wrist hurts. I must be using the mouse too much.
alt: I think I'm developing carpal tunnel from too much mouse usage.
note: carpal tunnel = 손목터널 증후군 / wrist = 손목

korean: 일어나자마자 폰부터 보는 습관 고쳐야 해
english: I need to break the habit of checking my phone first thing in the morning.
alt: Reaching for my phone the moment I wake up is a habit I need to quit.
note: first thing in the morning = 아침에 제일 먼저 / break the habit = 습관을 고치다

korean: 매일 일기 쓰면 마음이 정리된대
english: Writing a journal every day apparently helps organize your thoughts.
alt: Keeping a daily diary is great for mental clarity.
note: journal / diary = 일기 / mental clarity = 정신적 명료함

korean: 감사 일기 써봤어? 하루에 세 가지씩
english: Have you tried a gratitude journal? Three things a day.
alt: Writing down three things you're grateful for daily changes your perspective.
note: gratitude journal = 감사 일기 / perspective = 관점

korean: 모닝루틴 만들어야 아침이 편해
english: Having a morning routine makes your mornings smoother.
alt: Establishing a morning routine sets the tone for the whole day.
note: morning routine = 아침 루틴 / sets the tone = 분위기를 잡다

korean: 목표를 너무 크게 잡으면 포기하기 쉬워
english: If you set your goals too high you'll give up easily.
alt: Unrealistic goals lead to quick burnout.
note: set goals = 목표를 세우다 / unrealistic = 비현실적인 / burnout = 번아웃

korean: 작은 습관부터 시작해야 해 한 번에 다 바꾸려고 하지 마
english: Start with small habits. Don't try to change everything at once.
alt: Small incremental changes stick better than drastic overhauls.
note: small habits = 작은 습관 / incremental = 점진적인 / drastic = 급격한

korean: 루틴이 깨지면 다시 시작하기가 힘들어
english: Once your routine breaks it's hard to start again.
alt: Getting back on track after falling off your routine is the hardest part.
note: routine = 루틴 / get back on track = 다시 궤도에 오르다

korean: 피드백 받으면 기분이 안 좋아도 받아들여야 해
english: Even if feedback hurts you have to accept it.
alt: Constructive criticism stings but it's necessary for growth.
note: feedback / constructive criticism = 건설적 비판 / necessary for growth = 성장에 필요한

korean: 실패해도 괜찮아 그게 경험이 되는 거야
english: It's okay to fail. That becomes experience.
alt: Failure isn't the end. It's a learning opportunity.
note: failure = 실패 / learning opportunity = 배움의 기회

korean: 완벽하지 않아도 돼 최선을 다하면 되는 거야
english: You don't have to be perfect. Just do your best.
alt: Perfection isn't the goal. Giving it your all is what counts.
note: perfection = 완벽 / giving it your all = 최선을 다하는

korean: 남들이랑 비교하지 마 네 속도로 가면 돼
english: Don't compare yourself to others. Go at your own pace.
alt: Stop comparing yourself to everyone else. Your journey is your own.
note: compare = 비교하다 / at your own pace = 너만의 속도로 / journey = 여정

korean: 오늘 하루도 수고했어 잘 자
english: You worked hard today. Good night.
alt: Great job today. Rest well.
note: worked hard = 수고한 / rest well = 잘 쉬어

korean: 내일은 더 좋은 하루가 될 거야
english: Tomorrow will be a better day.
alt: Things will look up tomorrow.
note: look up = 나아지다 / better day = 더 좋은 날

korean: 회사에서 커피 머신 고장 났어 하루가 안 돌아가
english: The office coffee machine broke down. The day can't function.
alt: The coffee maker at work is busted. Everyone's in crisis mode.
note: coffee machine = 커피 머신 / busted = 고장난 / crisis mode = 위기 상태

korean: 프레젠테이션 잘됐어 떨렸는데 다행이야
english: The presentation went well. I was nervous but it turned out fine.
alt: My presentation went better than expected. Relief is an understatement.
note: presentation = 발표 / turned out fine = 잘 됐다 / understatement = 절제된 표현

korean: 야 점심시간에 좀 자자 너무 졸려
english: Let's take a nap during lunch break. I'm so sleepy.
alt: I need a power nap at lunch. I can barely keep my eyes open.
note: power nap = 짧은 낮잠 / barely keep my eyes open = 눈을 뜨기 힘든

korean: 미팅 취소됐대 아싸!
english: The meeting got canceled! Yes!
alt: The meeting's been called off. What a relief!
note: canceled / called off = 취소된 / what a relief = 정말 다행이다

korean: 내일 면접인데 무슨 옷 입지
english: I have an interview tomorrow and I don't know what to wear.
alt: Job interview tomorrow. What should I wear?
note: interview = 면접 / what to wear = 뭘 입을지

korean: 이력서 좀 봐줄 수 있어? 피드백 해줘
english: Can you look at my resume and give me feedback?
alt: Would you mind reviewing my CV? I'd love some input.
note: resume / CV = 이력서 / feedback / input = 피드백

korean: 야근 수당이라도 주면 좋겠다
english: I wish they'd at least give us overtime pay.
alt: If only we got compensated for overtime.
note: overtime pay = 야근 수당 / compensated = 보상받는

korean: 출장 가야 하는데 귀찮다
english: I have a business trip coming up and I'm not looking forward to it.
alt: Business travel sounds glamorous but it's exhausting in reality.
note: business trip = 출장 / looking forward to = 기대하는

korean: 팀장님이 오늘 기분 좋으신 것 같아 지금이 기회야
english: The team lead seems to be in a good mood today. Now's our chance.
alt: The boss is in a good mood. Strike while the iron is hot.
note: strike while the iron is hot = 기회를 놓치지 마라 (속담)

korean: 재택하다가 회사 가니까 적응이 안 돼
english: Going back to the office after working from home is an adjustment.
alt: Readjusting to office life after remote work is rough.
note: adjustment = 적응 / readjusting = 재적응하는

korean: 동기가 먼저 승진했어 좀 부러워
english: My colleague who started the same time got promoted first. I'm a bit jealous.
alt: Someone from my cohort got promoted before me. Can't help feeling envious.
note: promoted = 승진한 / cohort = 동기 / envious = 부러운

korean: 명함 다 떨어졌어 새로 주문해야 해
english: I ran out of business cards. I need to order more.
alt: My business cards are all gone. Time to reorder.
note: business cards = 명함 / ran out of = 다 떨어진

korean: 올해 연차 다 썼어 쉴 날이 없어
english: I used up all my annual leave this year. No days off left.
alt: I've already burned through all my vacation days this year.
note: annual leave / vacation days = 연차 / burned through = 다 써버린

korean: 반차 쓰고 싶은데 눈치 보여
english: I want to take a half day off but I feel self-conscious about it.
alt: I'd love to use a half day but everyone will judge me.
note: half day off = 반차 / self-conscious = 눈치 보이는 / judge = 판단하다

korean: 워크숍 간다고? 어디서 해?
english: You're going on a workshop? Where is it?
alt: There's a company workshop? Where are they holding it?
note: workshop = 워크숍 / holding = 개최하는

korean: 연수 가면 공부도 하고 네트워킹도 할 수 있어
english: Going to training lets you study and network at the same time.
alt: Training programs are great for learning and making connections.
note: training = 연수 / networking = 네트워킹 / connections = 인맥

korean: 세미나 들었는데 유익했어
english: I attended a seminar and it was really informative.
alt: The seminar I went to was packed with useful insights.
note: seminar = 세미나 / informative = 유익한 / insights = 통찰

korean: 회사 복지가 좋으면 이직 안 하지
english: If the company benefits are good nobody would quit.
alt: Good employee benefits are the best retention strategy.
note: company benefits = 회사 복지 / retention = 유지 / quit = 그만두다

korean: 식대가 올랐으면 좋겠다 점심값이 부족해
english: I wish the meal allowance would go up. It's not enough for lunch.
alt: The lunch stipend doesn't even cover a proper meal anymore.
note: meal allowance / lunch stipend = 식대 / doesn't cover = 충당하지 못하는

korean: 사내 동호회 있어? 가입하고 싶어
english: Does the company have any clubs? I want to join one.
alt: Are there any employee clubs or groups I can sign up for?
note: employee clubs = 사내 동호회 / sign up for = 가입하다

korean: 팀 빌딩 활동 뭐 할 거야?
english: What are we doing for team building?
alt: Any ideas for our team bonding activity?
note: team building / team bonding = 팀 빌딩 / activity = 활동

korean: 슬랙 알림이 너무 많아서 집중이 안 돼
english: I get so many Slack notifications I can't concentrate.
alt: The constant Slack pings are destroying my focus.
note: notifications = 알림 / pings = 알림음 / destroying my focus = 집중력을 파괴하는

korean: 화상 회의할 때 배경 뭐로 해?
english: What background do you use for video calls?
alt: What virtual background do you set for Zoom meetings?
note: virtual background = 가상 배경 / video calls = 화상 통화

korean: 공유 오피스 써봤어? 분위기 어때?
english: Have you tried a coworking space? How's the vibe?
alt: What are coworking spaces like? Are they worth it?
note: coworking space = 공유 오피스 / worth it = 가치 있는

korean: 자격증이 있으면 연봉 협상에 유리해
english: Having certifications gives you an edge in salary negotiations.
alt: Certifications are leverage when negotiating your pay.
note: certifications = 자격증 / edge / leverage = 유리한 점

korean: 인턴 끝나고 정규직 전환 됐으면 좋겠다
english: I hope I get converted to a full-time position after my internship.
alt: Fingers crossed they offer me a permanent role after the internship ends.
note: full-time position / permanent role = 정규직 / internship = 인턴십

korean: 신입 연수가 다음 주부터야 떨려
english: New employee orientation starts next week. I'm nervous.
alt: Onboarding begins next week and I'm already anxious.
note: orientation / onboarding = 신입 연수 / anxious = 불안한

korean: 업무 인수인계 제대로 해줘
english: Make sure you do a proper handover of your work.
alt: Please ensure a thorough transition of responsibilities.
note: handover / transition = 인수인계 / thorough = 철저한

korean: 퇴사 결심했어 더 이상 못 참겠어
english: I've decided to resign. I can't take it anymore.
alt: I'm putting in my notice. I'm done with this place.
note: resign = 사직하다 / putting in my notice = 사직서를 내다

korean: 이직 준비하면서 다니는 거 진짜 힘들다
english: Job hunting while still employed is exhausting.
alt: Searching for a new job while working full-time is incredibly draining.
note: job hunting = 구직 / employed = 재직 중인 / draining = 지치게 하는

korean: 면접 결과 언제 나올까 기다리기 힘들어
english: When will the interview results come out? The wait is killing me.
alt: I'm anxiously waiting for the interview outcome. The suspense is unbearable.
note: interview results / outcome = 면접 결과 / suspense = 긴장감

korean: 합격 통보 받았어! 드디어!
english: I got accepted! Finally!
alt: I received an offer! At last!
note: got accepted / received an offer = 합격한 / at last = 드디어

korean: 축하한다! 네가 해냈어!
english: Congratulations! You did it!
alt: Way to go! You pulled it off!
note: congratulations = 축하 / pulled it off = 해냈다

korean: 안 되면 다른 기회가 올 거야 너무 낙담하지 마
english: If it doesn't work out another opportunity will come. Don't be too discouraged.
alt: Don't let one rejection get you down. There are plenty of other chances.
note: discouraged = 낙담한 / rejection = 거절 / plenty of = 많은

korean: 경력 쌓으려면 시간이 필요해 조급해하지 마
english: Building a career takes time. Don't be impatient.
alt: Rome wasn't built in a day. Career growth requires patience.
note: career = 경력 / impatient = 조급한 / patience = 인내

korean: 일이 손에 안 잡혀 딴생각이 자꾸 나
english: I can't focus on work. My mind keeps wandering.
alt: I can't get into the zone today. I'm completely distracted.
note: mind keeps wandering = 생각이 자꾸 다른 데로 / get into the zone = 집중 상태에 들어가다

korean: 주 4일제 되면 좋겠다
english: I wish we had a four-day work week.
alt: A four-day work week would be a dream come true.
note: four-day work week = 주 4일 근무 / dream come true = 꿈이 실현된

korean: 점심 메뉴 추천 앱 있어?
english: Is there an app that recommends lunch menus?
alt: Know any good apps for finding lunch spots?
note: recommend = 추천하다 / lunch spots = 점심 식당

korean: 회식 때 2차 안 갈 거야
english: I'm not going to the after-party at the company dinner.
alt: I'm skipping the second round. One round is enough.
note: after-party / second round = 2차 / skipping = 건너뛰는

korean: 평일에 쉬면 어디 가도 한산해서 좋아
english: Taking a weekday off is great because everywhere is empty.
alt: The beauty of a weekday day off is that nothing is crowded.
note: weekday off = 평일 휴가 / empty / not crowded = 한산한

korean: 원데이 클래스 해봤어? 재밌더라
english: Have you tried a one-day class? It was fun.
alt: I did a one-day workshop and really enjoyed it.
note: one-day class / workshop = 원데이 클래스

korean: 꽃꽂이 배우고 싶어 힐링 될 것 같아
english: I want to learn flower arranging. It seems therapeutic.
alt: Flower arrangement classes look so relaxing.
note: flower arranging / arrangement = 꽃꽂이 / therapeutic = 치유적인

korean: 도자기 만들기 해봤는데 생각보다 어려워
english: I tried pottery and it's harder than it looks.
alt: Making ceramics is way more difficult than I anticipated.
note: pottery / ceramics = 도자기 / anticipated = 예상한

korean: 향초 만들기 클래스 재밌었어
english: The candle-making class was so much fun.
alt: I went to a candle workshop and had a blast.
note: candle-making = 향초 만들기 / had a blast = 매우 즐거웠다

korean: 가죽 공예 배우고 있어 지갑 만들 거야
english: I'm learning leather crafting. I'm going to make a wallet.
alt: I've been taking leatherworking classes. My first project is a wallet.
note: leather crafting / leatherworking = 가죽 공예 / project = 프로젝트

korean: 서핑 배우러 양양 갈까?
english: Should we go to Yangyang to learn surfing?
alt: Wanna take a surf trip to Yangyang?
note: surfing = 서핑 / surf trip = 서핑 여행. 양양은 한국 서핑 명소

korean: 스쿠버 다이빙 자격증 따고 싶어
english: I want to get a scuba diving certification.
alt: I'm thinking about getting my PADI certification.
note: scuba diving = 스쿠버 다이빙 / PADI = 국제 다이빙 자격증

korean: 패러글라이딩 해봤어? 무섭지 않아?
english: Have you tried paragliding? Isn't it scary?
alt: Have you gone paragliding? Were you terrified?
note: paragliding = 패러글라이딩 / terrified = 매우 무서워하는

korean: 번지점프 할 용기가 없어
english: I don't have the guts to bungee jump.
alt: Bungee jumping? No way. I could never.
note: guts = 용기 / bungee jump = 번지점프 / no way = 절대 안 해

korean: 짚라인 타봤는데 스릴 있더라
english: I rode a zipline and it was such a thrill.
alt: Ziplining was an adrenaline rush. Totally recommend it.
note: zipline = 짚라인 / thrill / adrenaline rush = 스릴

korean: 스키 시즌이야 보드 타러 가자
english: It's ski season. Let's go snowboarding.
alt: Ski season is here! Wanna hit the slopes?
note: ski season = 스키 시즌 / snowboarding = 스노보드 / hit the slopes = 스키장에 가다

korean: 리프트 줄이 너무 길어 한 시간은 기다려야겠다
english: The lift line is so long. We'll have to wait at least an hour.
alt: The chairlift queue is insane. We're looking at a one-hour wait.
note: lift / chairlift = 리프트 / queue = 줄

korean: 렌탈 장비 빌려야 해 나 장비 없어
english: I need to rent equipment. I don't have my own.
alt: I'll have to get rental gear since I don't own any.
note: rent / rental = 대여 / equipment / gear = 장비

korean: 초보자 코스부터 시작하자 무리하면 다쳐
english: Let's start with the beginner course. You'll get hurt if you push it.
alt: Stick to the bunny slope first. Overconfidence leads to injuries.
note: beginner course / bunny slope = 초보자 코스 / overconfidence = 과신

korean: 넘어지면서 배우는 거야 괜찮아
english: You learn by falling. It's okay.
alt: Falling is part of the learning process. Don't worry about it.
note: learn by falling = 넘어지면서 배우다 / part of the process = 과정의 일부

korean: 장마철에는 제습기가 필수야
english: A dehumidifier is essential during the rainy season.
alt: You absolutely need a dehumidifier during monsoon season.
note: dehumidifier = 제습기 / essential = 필수적인

korean: 가습기 틀어야 겠다 공기가 너무 건조해
english: I should turn on the humidifier. The air is so dry.
alt: The air is bone dry. Time to fire up the humidifier.
note: humidifier = 가습기 / bone dry = 매우 건조한

korean: 공기청정기 필터 바꿔야 하는데 비싸
english: I need to replace the air purifier filter but it's pricey.
alt: Air purifier replacement filters cost an arm and a leg.
note: air purifier = 공기청정기 / cost an arm and a leg = 매우 비싼

korean: 건조기 사니까 빨래 너는 게 필요 없어서 편해
english: Getting a dryer means I don't have to hang laundry anymore. So convenient.
alt: Having a clothes dryer eliminated the need to hang dry. Total game changer.
note: dryer = 건조기 / hang laundry = 빨래를 널다 / eliminated = 없앤

korean: 식기세척기 꼭 사 설거지 스트레스에서 해방이야
english: Get a dishwasher for sure. You'll be free from dish-washing stress.
alt: A dishwasher is a must-have. It'll change your life.
note: dishwasher = 식기세척기 / must-have = 필수품

korean: 스마트홈으로 바꾸니까 집이 편해졌어
english: Turning my home into a smart home made everything so convenient.
alt: Going smart home was the best upgrade. Everything is automated now.
note: smart home = 스마트홈 / automated = 자동화된

korean: 음성 인식으로 불 끄고 켜니까 편해
english: Turning lights on and off with voice commands is so handy.
alt: Voice-activated lighting is amazing. I feel like I'm living in the future.
note: voice commands = 음성 명령 / voice-activated = 음성 활성화된

korean: 로봇 청소기 이름 지어줬어 우리 집 일꾼이야
english: I named my robot vacuum. It's the hardest worker in the house.
alt: My robot vacuum has a name. It's basically a family member now.
note: named = 이름을 지어준 / hardest worker = 가장 열심히 일하는

korean: 무선 충전기 사니까 케이블이 필요 없어서 깔끔해
english: Wireless charging is so clean. No more messy cables.
alt: Getting a wireless charger decluttered my desk completely.
note: wireless charging = 무선 충전 / decluttered = 정리된

korean: 스마트워치 쓰면 운동량 체크가 편해
english: A smartwatch makes tracking exercise so much easier.
alt: Fitness tracking with a smartwatch is incredibly convenient.
note: smartwatch = 스마트워치 / tracking exercise = 운동량 추적

korean: 블루투스 스피커 하나 사야겠다 음질 좋은 거
english: I should get a Bluetooth speaker. A good quality one.
alt: I'm in the market for a decent Bluetooth speaker.
note: Bluetooth speaker = 블루투스 스피커 / in the market for = 구매를 고려하는

korean: 노이즈 캔슬링 이어폰 짱이야 세상이 조용해져
english: Noise-canceling earbuds are the best. The world goes silent.
alt: ANC earphones are a revelation. Everything just goes quiet.
note: noise-canceling / ANC = 노이즈 캔슬링 / revelation = 혁신적인 발견

korean: 이 앱 유료 버전 살까? 광고 없으면 좋을 텐데
english: Should I buy the premium version of this app? It'd be nice without ads.
alt: I'm debating whether the ad-free version is worth paying for.
note: premium version = 유료 버전 / ad-free = 광고 없는

korean: 클라우드 저장소 용량이 다 찼어
english: My cloud storage is full.
alt: I've maxed out my cloud storage capacity.
note: cloud storage = 클라우드 저장소 / maxed out = 한도에 다다른

korean: 비밀번호 관리 앱 쓰면 편해 추천해
english: A password manager makes life so much easier. I recommend getting one.
alt: Use a password manager. You won't regret it.
note: password manager = 비밀번호 관리 앱 / won't regret = 후회 안 할

korean: 이중 인증 설정해 놔 보안이 중요해
english: Set up two-factor authentication. Security is important.
alt: Enable 2FA on all your accounts. Cyber security is no joke.
note: two-factor authentication (2FA) = 이중 인증 / cyber security = 사이버 보안

korean: 와이파이가 느려서 영상이 자꾸 버퍼링 돼
english: The Wi-Fi is slow so videos keep buffering.
alt: My internet is so laggy that streaming keeps freezing.
note: buffering = 버퍼링 / laggy = 느린 / freezing = 멈추는

korean: 인터넷 속도 측정해 봐 뭔가 이상해
english: Check your internet speed. Something seems off.
alt: Run a speed test. I think something's wrong with the connection.
note: speed test = 속도 측정 / something seems off = 뭔가 이상한

korean: 유선이 무선보다 안정적이긴 해
english: Wired connection is more stable than wireless.
alt: Ethernet is definitely more reliable than Wi-Fi.
note: wired / Ethernet = 유선 / stable / reliable = 안정적인

korean: 앱 삭제했다가 다시 설치하면 될 때도 있어
english: Sometimes deleting and reinstalling the app fixes it.
alt: A simple uninstall and reinstall often solves the issue.
note: reinstall = 재설치 / solves the issue = 문제를 해결하다

korean: 컴퓨터 껐다 켜봐 그러면 되는 경우가 많아
english: Try turning your computer off and back on. That often fixes things.
alt: The classic reboot. It solves more problems than you'd think.
note: reboot = 재시작 / turn off and back on = 껐다 켜다

korean: 업데이트 뜨면 바로 해 보안 패치가 중요해
english: Update right away when prompted. Security patches are important.
alt: Don't ignore software updates. They often contain critical security fixes.
note: software update = 소프트웨어 업데이트 / security patches = 보안 패치

korean: 한국어 줄임말이 너무 많아서 못 알아듣겠어
english: There are too many Korean abbreviations. I can't keep up.
alt: Korean internet slang changes so fast I can't follow anymore.
note: abbreviations = 줄임말 / keep up / follow = 따라가다

korean: 그 짤 봤어? 완전 레전드야
english: Did you see that meme pic? It's legendary.
alt: That image is an absolute classic. Peak internet humor.
note: meme pic = 짤 / legendary / classic = 전설적인

korean: 갑자기 옛날 노래 듣고 싶다 추억이 새록새록해
english: I suddenly want to listen to old songs. So many memories coming back.
alt: I'm feeling nostalgic. Time for a throwback playlist.
note: nostalgic = 향수를 느끼는 / throwback = 옛날 것 / memories = 추억

korean: 초등학교 때가 제일 좋았어 걱정이 없었잖아
english: Elementary school was the best. No worries at all.
alt: I miss being a kid. Life was so carefree back then.
note: carefree = 걱정 없는 / no worries = 걱정이 없는

korean: 시간이 너무 빨리 간다 벌써 올해도 반이 지났어
english: Time goes by so fast. Half the year is already gone.
alt: Where did the time go? We're already halfway through the year.
note: time goes by / where did the time go = 시간이 빠르다 / halfway through = 반이 지난

korean: 나이 들수록 시간이 빨리 가는 것 같아
english: The older I get the faster time seems to pass.
alt: Time accelerates as you age. It's a universal truth.
note: accelerates = 가속하는 / as you age = 나이가 들면서

korean: 새해 목표 세웠어? 나는 벌써 포기했어
english: Did you set any New Year's resolutions? I already gave up on mine.
alt: Any New Year's goals? I abandoned mine in February.
note: New Year's resolutions = 새해 목표 / gave up / abandoned = 포기한

korean: 버킷리스트에 뭐 있어? 나는 오로라 보고 싶어
english: What's on your bucket list? I want to see the Northern Lights.
alt: What's one thing on your bucket list? Mine is seeing the aurora borealis.
note: bucket list = 버킷리스트 / Northern Lights / aurora borealis = 오로라

korean: 10년 뒤에 뭐 하고 있을까 상상이 안 돼
english: I can't imagine what I'll be doing in 10 years.
alt: Where do you see yourself in a decade? I honestly have no clue.
note: in 10 years / in a decade = 10년 후 / no clue = 모르겠다

korean: 요즘 하루하루가 다 비슷해서 지루해
english: Every day feels the same lately and it's boring.
alt: My days are all blending together. I need something to shake things up.
note: all the same / blending together = 다 비슷한 / shake things up = 변화를 주다

korean: 뭔가 새로운 거 시작하고 싶어 도전해 보고 싶어
english: I want to start something new. I want to challenge myself.
alt: I'm craving a fresh challenge. Something completely new.
note: challenge = 도전 / craving = 갈망하는 / fresh = 새로운

korean: 오늘 하루 진짜 알차게 보냈다
english: Today was really productive and fulfilling.
alt: I had such a full and rewarding day today.
note: productive = 생산적인 / fulfilling / rewarding = 보람 있는

korean: 아무것도 안 하는 날이 필요해
english: I need a day where I do absolutely nothing.
