alt: Sometimes you just need a do-nothing day.
note: do absolutely nothing = 아무것도 안 하다 / do-nothing day = 아무것도 안 하는 날

korean: 나한테 투자하는 게 제일 중요해
english: Investing in yourself is the most important thing.
alt: Self-investment yields the best returns.
note: investing in yourself / self-investment = 자기 투자 / yields = 수익을 내다

korean: 성장하는 느낌이 들어서 기분 좋아
english: I feel like I'm growing and it makes me happy.
alt: The feeling of personal growth is incredibly satisfying.
note: personal growth = 개인적 성장 / satisfying = 만족스러운

korean: 삶의 균형이 중요해 한쪽으로만 치우치면 안 돼
english: Balance in life is important. You can't lean too much in one direction.
alt: Maintaining equilibrium in life is key. Don't put all your eggs in one basket.
note: balance / equilibrium = 균형 / don't put all your eggs in one basket = 한 곳에 올인하지 마

korean: 오늘도 감사한 하루야
english: I'm grateful for another day.
alt: Today is another day to be thankful for.
note: grateful / thankful = 감사하는

korean: 작은 것에서 행복을 찾아야 해
english: You need to find happiness in the little things.
alt: Happiness is in the small moments.
note: happiness = 행복 / little things / small moments = 작은 것들

korean: 결국 건강이 최고야 돈보다 건강이 중요해
english: At the end of the day health is the most important thing.
alt: Nothing matters more than your health. Money can't buy it.
note: at the end of the day = 결국 / money can't buy it = 돈으로 살 수 없다

korean: 스트레스 푸는 나만의 방법이 있어?
english: Do you have your own way of relieving stress?
alt: What's your personal go-to for stress relief?
note: relieving stress / stress relief = 스트레스 해소 / go-to = 자주 하는 방법

korean: 가끔은 멍 때리는 것도 좋아
english: Sometimes just zoning out is nice.
alt: Staring into space and thinking about nothing is underrated.
note: zoning out / staring into space = 멍 때리기 / underrated = 과소평가된

korean: 맛있는 거 먹으면 기분이 좋아지더라
english: Eating something delicious always lifts my mood.
alt: Good food has this magical ability to make everything better.
note: lifts my mood = 기분을 좋게 하다 / magical ability = 마법 같은 능력

korean: 산에 가면 머리가 맑아져
english: Going to the mountains clears my head.
alt: Mountain air just hits different. My mind feels so clear.
note: clears my head = 머리가 맑아지다 / hits different = 특별하게 느껴지다

korean: 바다 보면 마음이 편해져
english: Looking at the ocean puts my mind at ease.
alt: There's something about the sea that's incredibly calming.
note: puts my mind at ease = 마음을 편하게 하다 / calming = 차분하게 하는

korean: 반려 식물 키우기 시작했는데 보람 있어
english: I started growing houseplants and it's rewarding.
alt: Indoor gardening is surprisingly fulfilling.
note: houseplants = 실내 식물 / indoor gardening = 실내 정원 가꾸기 / fulfilling = 보람 있는

korean: 선인장도 죽이는 사람인데 괜찮을까?
english: I've killed cacti before. Will I be okay?
alt: I'm notorious for killing even the hardiest plants. Should I still try?
note: cacti = 선인장 (복수) / notorious = 악명 높은 / hardiest = 가장 강인한

korean: 산책하면서 음악 듣는 게 내 힐링이야
english: Walking while listening to music is my form of healing.
alt: My idea of self-care is a nice walk with good music.
note: healing / self-care = 힐링 / form of = ~의 형태

korean: 혼자 여행 가본 적 있어? 생각보다 좋더라
english: Have you ever traveled alone? It's better than I expected.
alt: Solo travel is surprisingly liberating. Have you tried it?
note: solo travel = 혼자 여행 / liberating = 자유로운

korean: 워케이션 해봤어? 일하면서 여행하는 거
english: Have you tried a workation? Working while traveling.
alt: Have you done a work-travel combo? Workations are becoming a thing.
note: workation = 워케이션 (work + vacation) / becoming a thing = 유행이 되고 있는

korean: 글램핑 가자 캠핑은 불편하니까
english: Let's go glamping. Regular camping is too uncomfortable.
alt: Glamping is perfect for people who want nature without the roughing it.
note: glamping = 글램핑 (glamorous + camping) / roughing it = 힘든 환경을 견디다

korean: 차박해 봤어? 차에서 자는 거 은근 편하더라
english: Have you tried car camping? Sleeping in the car is surprisingly comfortable.
alt: Car camping is actually quite cozy once you set it up right.
note: car camping = 차박 / cozy = 아늑한

korean: 요즘 한옥 스테이가 인기야
english: Hanok stays are trending these days.
alt: Traditional Korean house stays are really popular right now.
note: Hanok stay = 한옥 스테이 / trending / popular = 인기 있는

korean: 게스트하우스가 호텔보다 저렴하고 분위기 좋아
english: Guesthouses are cheaper than hotels and have a better vibe.
alt: Hostels offer better value and a more social atmosphere than hotels.
note: guesthouse / hostel = 게스트하우스 / better value = 더 좋은 가성비

korean: 에어비앤비로 예약했어 호텔보다 넓어서 좋아
english: I booked through Airbnb. It's more spacious than a hotel.
alt: I went with an Airbnb. More room for less money.
note: Airbnb = 에어비앤비 / spacious = 넓은

korean: 여행 계획 세우는 것도 여행의 일부야
english: Planning the trip is part of the fun.
alt: Half the excitement of traveling is in the planning phase.
note: planning = 계획 / part of the fun = 재미의 일부 / excitement = 흥분

korean: 즉흥 여행 좋아해? 나는 계획 없이 떠나는 게 좋아
english: Do you like spontaneous trips? I love traveling without a plan.
alt: I prefer unplanned adventures. Going with the flow is the best.
note: spontaneous = 즉흥적인 / unplanned = 계획 없는 / going with the flow = 흐름에 맡기다

korean: 현지 음식 먹어봐야지 관광지 음식 말고
english: You have to try the local food. Not the tourist trap stuff.
alt: Skip the touristy restaurants and eat where the locals eat.
note: local food = 현지 음식 / tourist trap = 관광객 바가지 / locals = 현지인

korean: 기념품 뭐 사갈까? 뭐가 좋아?
english: What should I get as a souvenir? Any ideas?
alt: What souvenirs should I bring back? What do people usually want?
note: souvenir = 기념품 / bring back = 사 가다

korean: 공항 면세점에서 화장품 사자
english: Let's buy cosmetics at the airport duty-free.
alt: The duty-free cosmetics at the airport are a steal.
note: duty-free = 면세 / cosmetics = 화장품 / a steal = 매우 저렴한

korean: 탑승구 어디야? 게이트 번호가 바뀌었어
english: Where's the boarding gate? The gate number changed.
alt: Our gate changed. We need to find the new one quick.
note: boarding gate = 탑승구 / gate number = 게이트 번호

korean: 기내식 뭐 나와? 치킨이야 비프야?
english: What's the in-flight meal? Chicken or beef?
alt: What are the options for the plane food?
note: in-flight meal = 기내식 / options = 선택지

korean: 비행기에서 못 자서 도착하니까 좀비 같아
english: I couldn't sleep on the plane so I arrived looking like a zombie.
alt: No sleep on the flight. I'm functioning like a zombie.
note: zombie = 좀비 / functioning = 기능하는

korean: 입국 심사 줄이 너무 길어
english: The immigration line is so long.
alt: The queue for passport control is insane.
note: immigration = 입국 심사 / passport control = 여권 심사

korean: 로밍 안 했는데 와이파이 어디서 잡지?
english: I didn't set up roaming. Where can I find Wi-Fi?
alt: I don't have international data. Any free Wi-Fi spots around?
note: roaming = 로밍 / international data = 해외 데이터

korean: 환전소 어디 있어요?
english: Where's the currency exchange?
alt: Where can I exchange money around here?
note: currency exchange = 환전소

korean: 택시비가 바가지야 미터기 안 켰잖아
english: The taxi ripped me off. They didn't turn on the meter.
alt: The cab driver overcharged me. The meter was off the whole time.
note: ripped off = 바가지를 씌운 / meter = 미터기 / overcharged = 과다 청구한

korean: 길을 잃었는데 영어가 안 통해
english: I'm lost and nobody speaks English.
alt: I can't find my way and there's a language barrier.
note: lost = 길을 잃은 / language barrier = 언어 장벽

korean: 구글맵이 안 되는 나라야 어떡하지
english: Google Maps doesn't work in this country. What do I do?
alt: This country blocks Google Maps. I need an alternative.
note: alternative = 대안 / blocks = 차단하는

korean: 해외에서 카드가 안 먹혀 현금밖에 안 돼
english: My card doesn't work abroad. They only take cash.
alt: My credit card isn't accepted here. Cash only apparently.
note: doesn't work abroad = 해외에서 안 되는 / cash only = 현금만

korean: 짐 분실됐어 항공사에 신고해야 해
english: My luggage was lost. I need to report it to the airline.
alt: My bags didn't come through. I have to file a claim with the airline.
note: luggage = 수하물 / lost = 분실된 / file a claim = 신고하다

korean: 비행기 연착돼서 연결편 놓쳤어
english: My flight was delayed so I missed my connecting flight.
alt: The delay caused me to miss my connection.
note: delayed = 연착된 / connecting flight / connection = 연결편

korean: 여행자 보험 들어? 안 들면 위험해
english: Do you have travel insurance? It's risky without it.
alt: You should definitely get travel insurance. Better safe than sorry.
note: travel insurance = 여행자 보험 / better safe than sorry = 미리 대비하는 게 나은

korean: 이 동네 치안이 안 좋대 조심해
english: I heard this area isn't safe. Be careful.
alt: The safety of this neighborhood is questionable. Stay alert.
note: safety = 안전 / stay alert = 경계하다 / questionable = 의심스러운

korean: 나 장 보러 가는 길인데 뭐 사다 줄까?
english: I'm on my way to the store. Want me to get you anything?
alt: I'm heading to the grocery store. Need anything?
note: on my way to = ~가는 길 / get you anything = 뭐 사다 줄까

korean: 이건 세트로 사면 더 싸
english: This is cheaper if you buy it as a set.
alt: Buying the bundle saves you money.
note: set / bundle = 세트 / saves money = 돈을 절약하다

korean: 시식해 봐도 돼요?
english: Can I try a sample?
alt: May I taste a sample?
note: sample = 시식 / taste = 맛보다

korean: 이 브랜드 괜찮아? 써봤어?
english: Is this brand any good? Have you tried it?
alt: Have you used this brand before? Worth buying?
note: brand = 브랜드 / worth buying = 살 가치가 있는

korean: 이거 신상이야 아직 안 나온 거
english: This is a new release. It's not even out yet.
alt: This just dropped. It's brand new.
note: new release = 신상 / just dropped = 방금 출시된 / brand new = 완전 새 것

korean: 한정판이래 빨리 안 사면 품절돼
english: It's limited edition. If you don't buy it now it'll be sold out.
alt: This is a limited run. It'll sell out fast.
note: limited edition / limited run = 한정판 / sold out = 품절

korean: 오프라인 매장이 온라인보다 비싸
english: The physical store is more expensive than online.
alt: Brick-and-mortar stores charge more than online shops.
note: physical store / brick-and-mortar = 오프라인 매장

korean: AS 받으려면 영수증 필요해
english: You need the receipt for after-sales service.
alt: Keep your receipt in case you need warranty service.
note: after-sales service / warranty service = AS / receipt = 영수증

korean: 보증 기간이 얼마나 돼요?
english: How long is the warranty?
alt: What's the warranty period on this?
note: warranty = 보증 / warranty period = 보증 기간

korean: 체험판 써봤는데 정품 살 가치가 있어?
english: I tried the trial version. Is the full version worth buying?
alt: I used the free trial. Is it worth upgrading to premium?
note: trial version = 체험판 / full version / premium = 정품 / upgrading = 업그레이드

korean: 이거 가짜 아니야? 진짜 맞아?
english: This isn't fake right? Is it authentic?
alt: Are you sure this is genuine? It looks suspicious.
note: fake = 가짜 / authentic / genuine = 진품 / suspicious = 의심스러운

korean: 인생은 한 번뿐이야 하고 싶은 거 해
english: You only live once. Do what you want.
alt: Life's too short to hold back. Go for it.
note: you only live once (YOLO) = 인생은 한 번뿐 / go for it = 해봐

korean: 걱정해도 안 되는 건 안 돼 너무 걱정하지 마
english: Worrying won't change the outcome. Don't worry too much.
alt: Stressing about things beyond your control is pointless.
note: outcome = 결과 / beyond your control = 통제할 수 없는

korean: 모든 게 다 경험이야 나중에 웃을 거야
english: Everything is an experience. You'll laugh about it later.
alt: Someday you'll look back on this and smile.
note: experience = 경험 / look back on = 되돌아보다

korean: 지금이 제일 젊을 때야 후회 없이 살자
english: You're the youngest you'll ever be right now. Live without regrets.
alt: This is as young as we'll ever be. No regrets.
note: youngest you'll ever be = 가장 젊을 때 / no regrets = 후회 없이

korean: 행복은 가까이에 있어 멀리서 찾지 마
english: Happiness is closer than you think. Don't look too far.
alt: You don't have to go far to find happiness. It's right in front of you.
note: happiness = 행복 / closer than you think = 생각보다 가까운

korean: 소확행이 중요해 작은 행복들을 즐겨
english: Small but certain happiness matters. Enjoy the little joys.
alt: Find pleasure in the small things. That's what 소확행 is all about.
note: 소확행 = 소소하지만 확실한 행복. small but certain happiness

korean: 사람 스트레스가 제일 힘들어
english: People stress is the worst.
alt: Interpersonal conflicts are the most draining kind of stress.
note: people stress = 사람 스트레스 / interpersonal conflicts = 대인 갈등 / draining = 지치게 하는

korean: 혼자 있는 시간도 소중해
english: Time spent alone is valuable too.
alt: Solitude has its own kind of beauty.
note: time alone / solitude = 혼자만의 시간 / valuable = 소중한

korean: 친구가 많은 것보다 좋은 친구 한 명이 나아
english: One good friend is better than many friends.
alt: Quality over quantity when it comes to friendships.
note: quality over quantity = 양보다 질 / friendships = 우정

korean: 고민 있으면 말해 내가 들어줄게
english: If you have worries tell me. I'll listen.
alt: If something's bothering you I'm all ears.
note: worries = 고민 / I'll listen / I'm all ears = 귀 기울이고 있어

korean: 너는 충분히 잘하고 있어
english: You're doing great. More than enough.
alt: You're doing better than you think. Give yourself some credit.
note: doing great = 잘하고 있는 / give yourself some credit = 자신을 인정해

korean: 실패가 두려워서 시작을 못 하겠어
english: I'm so afraid of failure I can't even start.
alt: Fear of failing is keeping me from taking the first step.
note: afraid of failure = 실패가 두려운 / taking the first step = 첫걸음을 떼다

korean: 용기를 내야 하는 거 알아 근데 쉽지 않아
english: I know I need to be brave but it's not easy.
alt: I know courage is needed but actually summoning it is another story.
note: brave / courage = 용기 / summoning = 모아내는

korean: 잘 될 거야 나를 믿어
english: It'll work out. Trust me.
alt: Things are going to be fine. Have faith in me.
note: work out = 잘 되다 / trust me / have faith = 나를 믿어

korean: 우리 같이 하면 못 할 게 없어
english: There's nothing we can't do together.
alt: Together we're unstoppable.
note: nothing we can't do = 못 할 게 없다 / unstoppable = 막을 수 없는

korean: 도움이 필요하면 언제든 말해
english: If you need help just let me know anytime.
alt: Don't hesitate to reach out if you ever need anything.
note: let me know = 알려줘 / don't hesitate = 주저하지 마 / reach out = 연락하다

korean: 진심으로 응원할게 넌 할 수 있어
english: I'm rooting for you from the bottom of my heart. You've got this.
alt: I genuinely believe in you. You can do it.
note: rooting for = 응원하는 / from the bottom of my heart = 진심으로 / you've got this = 넌 할 수 있어

korean: 한 걸음씩 천천히 가면 돼
english: One step at a time. Take it slow.
alt: No need to rush. Progress at your own pace.
note: one step at a time = 한 걸음씩 / take it slow = 천천히 하다 / progress = 나아가다

korean: 포기하지 마 끝까지 해보자
english: Don't give up. Let's see it through to the end.
alt: Don't quit now. We're so close to the finish line.
note: give up / quit = 포기하다 / see it through = 끝까지 하다 / finish line = 결승선

korean: 넌 특별한 사람이야 잊지 마
english: You're a special person. Don't forget that.
alt: Never forget how special and unique you are.
note: special = 특별한 / unique = 유일무이한

korean: 이 노래 가사가 공감돼 너무 좋아
english: The lyrics of this song are so relatable. I love it.
alt: This song's lyrics hit me right in the feels.
note: lyrics = 가사 / relatable = 공감되는 / hit me in the feels = 감정적으로 와닿는 (슬랭)

korean: 라이브로 들으니까 완전 다르더라
english: Hearing it live was a completely different experience.
alt: The live version hits totally different from the studio recording.
note: live = 라이브 / different experience = 다른 경험 / studio recording = 스튜디오 녹음

korean: 기타 배우고 싶은데 손가락이 아플까?
english: I want to learn guitar but will my fingers hurt?
alt: Is learning guitar painful for your fingertips?
note: guitar = 기타 / fingertips = 손가락 끝

korean: 피아노 칠 줄 알아? 부러워
english: Can you play the piano? I'm jealous.
alt: You play piano? That's so impressive. I wish I could.
note: play the piano = 피아노를 치다 / impressive = 인상적인

korean: 노래 잘 부르는 사람 진짜 부럽다
english: I'm so jealous of people who can sing well.
alt: Being a good singer is such a gift. I wish I had that talent.
note: sing well = 노래를 잘 부르다 / gift = 재능 / talent = 재능

korean: 이 노래 멜로디가 귀에서 안 떠나
english: This melody is stuck in my head.
alt: I can't get this tune out of my head.
note: melody / tune = 멜로디 / stuck in my head = 머릿속에서 안 떠나는

korean: 이번 시즌 드라마 뭐가 재밌어?
english: What drama is good this season?
alt: Any must-watch shows this season?
note: must-watch = 꼭 봐야 하는 / this season = 이번 시즌

korean: 결말이 허무했어 기대한 만큼 아니야
english: The ending was anticlimactic. Not what I expected.
alt: The finale was underwhelming. All that buildup for nothing.
note: anticlimactic / underwhelming = 허무한 / buildup = 축적

korean: OST가 너무 좋아서 계속 듣고 있어
english: The OST is so good I keep listening to it on repeat.
alt: I've had the soundtrack on loop all day.
note: OST = Original Soundtrack / on repeat / on loop = 반복 재생

korean: 감독판이 더 낫대 봐야겠다
english: I heard the director's cut is better. I should watch it.
alt: The director's cut is supposedly superior. Adding it to my list.
note: director's cut = 감독판 / supposedly = ~라고 하는

korean: 자막 없이 볼 수 있으면 좋겠다
english: I wish I could watch without subtitles.
alt: My goal is to understand movies without subs.
note: subtitles / subs = 자막

korean: 더빙보다 원어가 좋아 감정 전달이 다르잖아
english: The original audio is better than dubbing. The emotions come through differently.
alt: I prefer the original language over dubs. The acting hits different.
note: dubbing / dubs = 더빙 / original audio = 원어

korean: 이 배우 연기 진짜 잘한다 몰입이 돼
english: This actor is incredible. I'm completely immersed.
alt: Their acting is phenomenal. I'm totally drawn in.
note: incredible / phenomenal = 놀라운 / immersed / drawn in = 몰입한

korean: 극장에서 보는 게 넷플릭스보다 나아
english: Watching in the theater is better than Netflix.
alt: Nothing beats the big screen experience.
note: theater = 극장 / big screen experience = 대형 스크린 경험

korean: 팝콘 없이 영화를 어떻게 봐
english: How can you watch a movie without popcorn?
alt: Movies without popcorn just aren't the same.
note: popcorn = 팝콘 / aren't the same = 같지 않은

korean: 3D로 볼까 IMAX로 볼까?
english: Should we watch it in 3D or IMAX?
alt: 3D or IMAX? Which format do you prefer?
note: 3D = 3D / IMAX = 아이맥스 / format = 형식

korean: 예매 했어? 좌석 좋은 데로 잡아
english: Did you book the tickets? Get good seats.
alt: Book the tickets and try to snag the best spots.
note: book tickets = 예매하다 / snag = 차지하다

korean: 매진이야 다른 시간에 보자
english: It's sold out. Let's watch a different showtime.
alt: All screenings are full. We'll have to go at another time.
note: sold out = 매진 / showtime / screening = 상영 시간

korean: 웹툰 추천해 줘 요즘 볼 게 없어
english: Recommend me a webtoon. I have nothing to read lately.
alt: Know any good webtoons? I've run out of things to read.
note: webtoon = 웹툰 / run out of = 다 떨어진

korean: 이 웹툰 작가 그림체가 독특해
english: The art style of this webtoon artist is unique.
alt: This webtoon creator has such a distinctive drawing style.
note: art style = 그림체 / distinctive / unique = 독특한

korean: 만화책 다시 읽으니까 추억이야
english: Rereading manga brings back so many memories.
alt: Going back to old comics is such a nostalgia trip.
note: manga / comics = 만화 / nostalgia trip = 추억 여행

korean: 이 게임 그래픽이 미쳤어 진짜 현실 같아
english: The graphics in this game are insane. It looks so realistic.
alt: The visuals in this game are next level. Almost photorealistic.
note: graphics / visuals = 그래픽 / realistic / photorealistic = 현실적인

korean: 팀 게임하는데 트롤이 있어서 짜증 나
english: There's a troll on my team in ranked. It's so frustrating.
alt: Playing ranked with a griefer on the team is infuriating.
note: troll / griefer = 트롤 (방해하는 사람) / ranked = 경쟁전 / infuriating = 화나게 하는

korean: 인디 게임 해봤어? 숨겨진 명작 많아
english: Have you tried indie games? There are so many hidden gems.
alt: Indie games have some real hidden masterpieces. You should explore them.
note: indie games = 인디 게임 / hidden gems = 숨겨진 명작

korean: 이 퀘스트 어떻게 깨? 공략 봐야겠다
english: How do I complete this quest? I need to look up a guide.
alt: I'm stuck on this mission. Time to check a walkthrough.
note: quest / mission = 퀘스트 / guide / walkthrough = 공략

korean: 업데이트 내용이 별로야 유저들이 화났어
english: The update content is disappointing. Players are furious.
alt: The latest patch is terrible. The community is outraged.
note: update / patch = 업데이트 / furious / outraged = 분노한

korean: 게임 과금 너무 많이 해서 후회 중이야
english: I spent too much on in-game purchases and I regret it.
alt: I went overboard with microtransactions. Major regret.
note: in-game purchases / microtransactions = 게임 과금 / went overboard = 과하게 한

korean: 전자책이 편하긴 한데 종이책 느낌이 좋아
english: E-books are convenient but I prefer the feel of a physical book.
alt: Digital reading is handy but nothing beats the tactile sensation of a real book.
note: e-book = 전자책 / physical book = 종이책 / tactile sensation = 촉감

korean: 이 책 완전 베스트셀러야 읽어봐
english: This book is a total bestseller. You should read it.
alt: This is a number one bestseller. Highly recommend.
note: bestseller = 베스트셀러 / highly recommend = 강력 추천

korean: 독서가 제일 좋은 자기 계발이야
english: Reading is the best form of self-improvement.
alt: Books are the most affordable way to invest in yourself.
note: self-improvement = 자기 계발 / invest in yourself = 자기 투자

korean: 이거 실화야? 믿기 어렵다
english: Is this based on a true story? It's hard to believe.
alt: This is nonfiction? That's unbelievable.
note: true story = 실화 / nonfiction = 논픽션 / unbelievable = 믿기 어려운

korean: 도장 모으는 거 좋아해? 나는 스탬프 다 모아야 직성이 풀려
english: Do you like collecting stamps? I can't rest until I fill the card.
alt: I'm a sucker for stamp cards. I have to complete them all.
note: stamp card = 스탬프 카드 / sucker for = ~에 약한

korean: 이 근처에 맛있는 집 알아?
english: Do you know any good restaurants around here?
alt: Know any good eats in this area?
note: good restaurants / good eats = 맛있는 집 / around here = 이 근처에

korean: 맛이 기대 이하야 별로다
english: The taste is below expectations. Not great.
alt: This doesn't taste as good as I hoped. Disappointing.
note: below expectations = 기대 이하 / disappointing = 실망스러운

korean: 양이 많아서 나눠 먹자
english: The portion is huge. Let's share.
alt: This is a lot of food. Wanna split it?
note: portion = 양 / share / split = 나눠 먹다

korean: 매장에서 먹을까 포장할까?
english: Should we eat here or get it to go?
alt: Dine in or takeout?
note: dine in = 매장에서 먹다 / takeout / to go = 포장

korean: 추가 주문할게요 이거 하나 더요
english: I'd like to order one more of these, please.
alt: Can we get one more of these?
note: order one more = 하나 더 주문하다

korean: 물 좀 더 주세요
english: Can I get some more water please?
alt: Could we have a refill on the water?
note: more water = 물 더 / refill = 리필

korean: 여기 음식 나오는 거 왜 이렇게 느려?
english: Why is the food taking so long here?
alt: The service is really slow. Our food hasn't come yet.
note: taking so long = 오래 걸리는 / service = 서비스

korean: 이 가게 뷔페식이야 원하는 만큼 먹어
english: This place is buffet style. Eat as much as you want.
alt: It's all-you-can-eat here. Go crazy.
note: buffet style = 뷔페식 / all-you-can-eat = 무한리필 / go crazy = 마음껏 해

korean: 음식이 짜다 물 좀 줘
english: The food is salty. Can I get some water?
alt: This is way too salty. I need water.
note: salty = 짠 / way too = 너무

korean: 여기 화장실 어디예요?
english: Where's the restroom?
alt: Could you point me to the bathroom?
note: restroom / bathroom = 화장실 / point me to = 가르쳐 주다

korean: 주차 요금이 얼마예요?
english: How much is the parking fee?
alt: What are the parking charges?
note: parking fee / charges = 주차 요금

korean: 발렛 파킹 되나요?
english: Do you offer valet parking?
alt: Is valet parking available here?
note: valet parking = 발렛 파킹 / available = 가능한

korean: 예약 이름으로 해놨어요
english: I made a reservation under my name.
alt: The booking should be under my name.
note: reservation / booking = 예약 / under my name = 내 이름으로

korean: 창가 자리로 부탁해요
english: Can we get a window seat please?
alt: Could we have a table by the window?
note: window seat = 창가 자리 / by the window = 창가 쪽

korean: 알레르기가 있어서 이 재료 빼주세요
english: I have an allergy, so please leave this ingredient out.
alt: Could you remove this ingredient? I have a food allergy.
note: food allergy = 식품 알레르기 / ingredient = 재료 / remove = 빼다

korean: 이거 맵나요? 안 매운 걸로 주세요
english: Is this spicy? Can I get something not spicy?
alt: How spicy is this? I'd prefer something mild.
note: spicy = 매운 / mild = 순한

korean: 비건 메뉴 있어요?
english: Do you have any vegan options?
alt: Are there plant-based items on the menu?
note: vegan / plant-based = 비건 / options / items = 선택지

korean: 글루텐 프리 메뉴 있어요?
english: Do you have a gluten-free menu?
alt: Are there any gluten-free options available?
note: gluten-free = 글루텐 프리 / available = 이용 가능한

korean: 여기 와이파이 되나요? 비밀번호 뭐예요?
english: Do you have Wi-Fi here? What's the password?
alt: Is there free Wi-Fi? What's the code?
note: Wi-Fi = 와이파이 / password / code = 비밀번호

korean: 에스컬레이터가 고장 났어 계단으로 가야 해
english: The escalator is broken. We have to take the stairs.
alt: The escalator is out of service. Stairs it is.
note: escalator = 에스컬레이터 / out of service = 고장난 / stairs it is = 계단으로 가야지

korean: 여기 몇 층이에요? 길을 잃었어요
english: What floor is this? I'm lost.
alt: Which floor am I on? I got turned around.
note: floor = 층 / got turned around = 방향을 잃은

korean: 유모차 들고 다니기 불편해
english: It's inconvenient getting around with a stroller.
alt: Navigating with a stroller is such a hassle.
note: stroller = 유모차 / hassle = 번거로운 일

korean: 점자 블록 위에 주차하지 마세요
english: Don't park on the tactile paving blocks.
alt: Please don't block the braille walkway with your vehicle.
note: tactile paving / braille walkway = 점자 블록 / block = 막다

korean: 엘리베이터에서 담배 냄새 나 최악이야
english: The elevator smells like cigarettes. The worst.
alt: Someone smoked in the elevator and the stench is awful.
note: smells like cigarettes = 담배 냄새가 나는 / stench = 악취

korean: 층간 소음 때문에 민원 넣었어
english: I filed a noise complaint about the noise between floors.
alt: I submitted a complaint about the interfloor noise.
note: noise complaint = 소음 민원 / interfloor noise = 층간 소음

korean: 재건축 한다는데 이사 가야 하나
english: They're doing a reconstruction. Should I move?
alt: The building is being redeveloped. Might have to relocate.
note: reconstruction / redeveloped = 재건축 / relocate = 이사하다

korean: 부동산에서 좋은 매물 있다고 연락 왔어
english: The real estate agent called about a good listing.
alt: My realtor reached out about a nice property.
note: real estate agent / realtor = 부동산 / listing / property = 매물

korean: 전세 사기 조심해야 해 요즘 너무 많대
english: Be careful of lease fraud. It's rampant these days.
alt: Jeonse scams are everywhere. You need to do your due diligence.
note: lease fraud = 전세 사기 / rampant = 만연한 / due diligence = 충분한 조사

korean: 등기부등본 확인했어?
english: Did you check the property registry?
alt: Have you verified the ownership records?
note: property registry / ownership records = 등기부등본 / verify = 확인하다

korean: 복비가 얼마예요?
english: How much is the brokerage fee?
alt: What's the commission for the real estate agent?
note: brokerage fee / commission = 중개 수수료

korean: 잔금 치르고 나면 이사야
english: Once I pay the remaining balance I can move in.
alt: After settling the final payment the move can happen.
note: remaining balance / final payment = 잔금 / settle = 치르다

korean: 전입신고 했어? 빨리 해야 돼
english: Did you file the move-in registration? You need to do it quickly.
alt: Have you done your address change registration? Don't delay.
note: move-in registration / address change = 전입신고

korean: 관리사무소에 연락해 봐
english: Contact the building management office.
alt: Try reaching out to the property management.
note: building management / property management = 관리사무소

korean: 여기 주민센터 어디예요?
english: Where's the community center?
alt: Where is the local district office?
note: community center / district office = 주민센터

korean: 인감증명서 떼러 왔어요
english: I'm here to get a certified seal certificate.
alt: I need to pick up my official seal document.
note: certified seal certificate = 인감증명서 (한국 행정 서류)

korean: 주민등록등본 발급해 주세요
english: Can I get a copy of my resident registration?
alt: I'd like to request my resident registration certificate.
note: resident registration = 주민등록등본 / request = 요청하다

korean: 민원 처리가 너무 느려
english: The civil service processing is so slow.
alt: Government paperwork takes forever to process.
note: civil service / government paperwork = 민원 / processing = 처리

korean: 세무서 가야 하는데 뭐 준비해야 해?
english: I need to go to the tax office. What should I prepare?
alt: What documents do I need for the tax office?
note: tax office = 세무서 / documents = 서류

korean: 공증 받아야 하는 서류가 있어
english: I have documents that need to be notarized.
alt: There are papers that require notarization.
note: notarized / notarization = 공증 / documents / papers = 서류

korean: 우체국에서 등기 보내야 해
english: I need to send registered mail at the post office.
alt: I have to mail a registered letter.
note: registered mail = 등기 우편 / post office = 우체국

korean: 택배 보내려면 포장 어떻게 해야 해?
english: How should I package this to ship it?
alt: What's the best way to pack this for delivery?
note: package / pack = 포장하다 / ship / delivery = 발송

korean: 국제 택배 보내는 데 얼마나 걸려?
english: How long does it take to send an international package?
alt: What's the delivery time for international shipping?
note: international package / shipping = 국제 택배 / delivery time = 배송 시간

korean: 편지 쓰는 사람이 요즘 없지?
english: Nobody writes letters these days right?
alt: Letter writing is a lost art in the digital age.
note: letter writing = 편지 쓰기 / lost art = 사라져가는 기술

korean: 이메일 보냈는데 스팸으로 갔나 봐
english: I sent an email but it might have gone to spam.
alt: My email probably ended up in your spam folder.
note: spam = 스팸 / ended up in = ~에 들어간

korean: 카톡 읽씹당했어 섭섭하다
english: They left me on read on KakaoTalk. I'm a bit hurt.
alt: I got read-receipted on KaTalk and they never replied. Ouch.
note: left on read = 읽고 답장 안 한 / read-receipted = 읽음 표시만 되고 / ouch = 아야

korean: 답장이 너무 늦어 무슨 일 있나?
english: They're taking forever to reply. Is something wrong?
alt: The late response is making me worried. Everything okay?
note: taking forever to reply = 답장이 너무 늦는 / worried = 걱정되는

korean: 그룹 채팅에서 나만 빠졌어 왜?
english: I was left out of the group chat. Why?
alt: They made a group chat without me. That stings.
note: left out = 빠진 / group chat = 그룹 채팅 / stings = 아프다

korean: 이모티콘 새로 나왔는데 귀여워서 샀어
english: New emoticons came out and they were so cute I bought them.
alt: There are new stickers and I couldn't resist buying them. Too adorable.
note: emoticons / stickers = 이모티콘 / adorable = 귀여운 / couldn't resist = 참을 수 없었다

korean: 프사 바꿀까? 좋은 사진이 없어
english: Should I change my profile pic? I don't have any good photos.
alt: I want to update my profile picture but I have no decent shots.
note: profile pic = 프사 / decent shots = 괜찮은 사진

korean: 상메 뭐라고 쓰지?
english: What should I write as my status message?
alt: What should my bio say?
note: status message / bio = 상태 메시지

korean: 단톡방이 너무 시끄러워 알림 끌래
english: The group chat is too noisy. I'm muting it.
alt: I'm putting the group chat on silent. Too many messages.
note: group chat = 단톡방 / muting / on silent = 알림 끄기

korean: 보이스 메시지로 보내줘 타이핑 귀찮아
english: Send me a voice message. I'm too lazy to type.
alt: Just send a voice note. Typing takes too long.
note: voice message / voice note = 보이스 메시지

korean: 화상 통화할래? 얼굴 보면서 얘기하자
english: Wanna video call? Let's talk face to face.
alt: Let's FaceTime. I want to see your face while we talk.
note: video call / FaceTime = 화상 통화 / face to face = 얼굴을 보며

korean: 전화보다 문자가 편해
english: I prefer texting over calling.
alt: Texting is so much easier than making a phone call.
note: texting = 문자하기 / prefer = 선호하다

korean: 전화 올 때마다 심장 떨려 혹시 무슨 일인가 해서
english: My heart races every time the phone rings. I always think something's wrong.
alt: Phone calls give me anxiety. I always assume the worst.
note: heart races = 심장이 빨리 뛰는 / anxiety = 불안 / assume the worst = 최악을 상상하다

korean: 모르는 번호는 안 받아
english: I don't answer unknown numbers.
alt: I never pick up calls from numbers I don't recognize.
note: unknown numbers = 모르는 번호 / don't recognize = 인식 못 하는

korean: 스팸 전화가 너무 많아 차단해야지
english: I get way too many spam calls. I need to block them.
alt: The spam calls are out of control. Time to install a call blocker.
note: spam calls = 스팸 전화 / block = 차단하다 / call blocker = 전화 차단 앱

korean: 통화 녹음 되는 앱 있어?
english: Is there an app that can record calls?
alt: Do you know a good call recording app?
note: record calls = 통화를 녹음하다 / call recording = 통화 녹음

korean: 국제전화 요금이 비싸니까 카톡으로 해
english: International calls are expensive so let's use KakaoTalk.
alt: Use a messenger app for overseas calls. Way cheaper than regular phone calls.
note: international calls = 국제전화 / messenger app = 메신저 앱

korean: 와 진짜 오랜만에 듣는 목소리다 반갑다
english: Wow it's been so long since I heard your voice! So nice!
alt: It's been ages since we talked! So great to hear from you!
note: so long / ages = 오랜만 / great to hear from you = 연락 받아서 반갑다

korean: 끊기 전에 한 가지만 더 얘기할게
english: Before you hang up let me tell you one more thing.
alt: One more thing before we end the call.
note: hang up = 전화를 끊다 / end the call = 통화를 끝내다

korean: 배터리 없어서 끊어야 돼 나중에 다시 전화할게
english: I'm running out of battery. I'll call you back later.
alt: My phone is dying so I gotta go. I'll ring you back.
note: running out of battery = 배터리가 없는 / ring you back = 다시 전화하다

korean: 통화 품질이 안 좋아 끊겼다 이어졌다 해
english: The call quality is bad. It keeps cutting in and out.
alt: The connection is terrible. You keep breaking up.
note: call quality = 통화 품질 / cutting in and out / breaking up = 끊겼다 이어지는

korean: 잘못 걸었어요 죄송합니다
english: I'm sorry. I dialed the wrong number.
alt: Sorry about that. Wrong number.
note: wrong number = 잘못 건 전화 / dialed = 전화를 건

korean: 여보세요? 잘 안 들려요 좀 크게 말해주세요
english: Hello? I can't hear you well. Could you speak up?
alt: Hello? The connection is faint. Can you speak louder?
note: can't hear well = 잘 안 들리는 / speak up / speak louder = 크게 말하다

korean: 음식물 알레르기 있는 사람 있어? 주문 전에 확인하자
english: Does anyone have food allergies? Let's check before we order.
alt: Any food allergies at the table? We should double-check before ordering.
note: food allergies = 식품 알레르기 / double-check = 재확인하다

korean: 다이어트 식단 추천해 줘 뭐 먹어야 할지 모르겠어
english: Recommend a diet meal plan. I don't know what to eat.
alt: What should I eat on a diet? I need a proper meal plan.
note: diet meal plan = 다이어트 식단 / proper = 제대로 된

korean: 단백질 쉐이크 맛있는 거 있어?
english: Is there a protein shake that actually tastes good?
alt: Know any protein shakes that don't taste terrible?
note: protein shake = 단백질 쉐이크

korean: 이 음식 칼로리 얼마나 될까?
english: How many calories do you think this has?
alt: What's the calorie count on this?
note: calories = 칼로리 / calorie count = 칼로리 수치

korean: 건강 검진 결과 기다리는 중인데 긴장돼
english: I'm waiting for my health checkup results and I'm nervous.
alt: The anticipation of my health screening results is nerve-wracking.
note: health checkup / screening = 건강 검진 / nerve-wracking = 초조하게 만드는

korean: 헌혈하고 왔어 기분이 좋아
english: I just donated blood and I feel good about it.
alt: I went to give blood today. Feels great to help out.
note: donated blood / give blood = 헌혈하다

korean: 응급실 가야 할 것 같아 너무 아파
english: I think I need to go to the ER. It hurts so bad.
alt: The pain is unbearable. I need to get to the emergency room.
note: ER / emergency room = 응급실 / unbearable = 참을 수 없는

korean: 예방 접종 맞으러 가야 돼
english: I need to go get my vaccination.
alt: It's time for my shot. I need to schedule my vaccine appointment.
note: vaccination / vaccine = 예방 접종 / shot = 주사

korean: 진료 예약 변경하고 싶은데요
english: I'd like to reschedule my appointment.
alt: Can I change my appointment to a different date?
note: reschedule = 일정을 변경하다 / appointment = 예약

korean: 처방전 받아서 약국 가야 해
english: I got a prescription and need to go to the pharmacy.
alt: I need to fill my prescription at the drugstore.
note: prescription = 처방전 / pharmacy / drugstore = 약국 / fill a prescription = 처방전대로 약을 받다

korean: 약 먹는 시간을 자꾸 잊어버려
english: I keep forgetting to take my medicine on time.
alt: I always miss my medication schedule.
note: take medicine = 약을 먹다 / medication schedule = 약 복용 일정

korean: 부작용이 있나요? 미리 알고 싶어요
english: Are there any side effects? I'd like to know in advance.
alt: What are the potential side effects? I want to be informed.
note: side effects = 부작용 / in advance = 미리

korean: 마취가 풀리니까 아프기 시작해
english: The anesthesia is wearing off and it's starting to hurt.
alt: Now that the numbing is gone the pain is kicking in.
note: anesthesia = 마취 / wearing off = 효과가 사라지는 / kicking in = 시작되는

korean: 입원해야 한대 며칠 걸릴까?
english: They said I need to be hospitalized. How long will it take?
alt: I need to be admitted. How many days will I be staying?
note: hospitalized / admitted = 입원한

korean: 퇴원했어 집에서 회복 중이야
english: I got discharged. I'm recovering at home.
alt: I'm out of the hospital and resting at home now.
note: discharged = 퇴원한 / recovering = 회복 중인

korean: 보험 청구 어떻게 해? 서류가 복잡해
english: How do I file an insurance claim? The paperwork is complicated.
alt: The insurance claim process is confusing. Too many forms.
note: insurance claim = 보험 청구 / paperwork / forms = 서류

korean: 간병인 구해야 하는데 어디서 찾지?
english: I need to find a caregiver but where do I look?
alt: Where can I find a reliable caretaker?
note: caregiver / caretaker = 간병인 / reliable = 신뢰할 수 있는

korean: 요양원에 계신 할머니 면회 가야 해
english: I need to visit my grandmother at the nursing home.
alt: I should go see my grandma at the senior care facility.
note: nursing home / senior care facility = 요양원 / visit = 면회하다

korean: 재활 운동 열심히 해야 빨리 나아
english: You need to do your rehab exercises diligently to recover quickly.
alt: Consistent rehab is the key to a speedy recovery.
note: rehab / rehabilitation = 재활 / diligently = 부지런히 / speedy recovery = 빠른 회복

korean: 건강할 때 건강을 지켜야 해
english: You should protect your health while you're healthy.
alt: Prevention is better than cure. Take care of yourself now.
note: protect your health = 건강을 지키다 / prevention is better than cure = 예방이 치료보다 낫다

korean: 나 오늘 컨디션이 안 좋아 쉬어야 할 것 같아
english: I'm not feeling well today. I think I need to rest.
alt: I'm under the weather today. I should take it easy.
note: under the weather = 몸이 안 좋은 / take it easy = 쉬다

korean: 어제 무리했더니 오늘 몸살 기운이 있어
english: I pushed myself too hard yesterday and I feel sick today.
alt: I overdid it yesterday and now I'm paying for it.
note: pushed too hard / overdid it = 무리한 / paying for it = 대가를 치르는

korean: 그 소식 들었어? 깜짝 놀랐어
english: Did you hear the news? I was totally shocked.
alt: Have you heard? I was completely blindsided.
note: shocked / blindsided = 충격받은

korean: 말도 안 돼 진짜? 거짓말이지?
english: No way. For real? You're lying right?
alt: Seriously? That can't be true. You're messing with me.
note: no way = 말도 안 돼 / messing with me = 나 놀리는 거지

korean: 간만에 좋은 소식이다 기분 좋아
english: Finally some good news. I'm happy.
alt: That's the best news I've heard in a while. Made my day.
note: good news = 좋은 소식 / made my day = 하루를 기분 좋게 했다

korean: 안타깝다 그 사람한테 무슨 일이 있었던 거야
english: That's so sad. What happened to them?
alt: I feel terrible for them. What went wrong?
note: feel terrible = 마음이 안 좋은 / what went wrong = 무슨 일이 있었는지

korean: 나쁜 소식은 듣기 싫다 좋은 것만 말해줘
english: I don't want to hear bad news. Only tell me good things.
alt: Spare me the bad news. I only want to hear positives today.
note: spare me = 면해줘 / positives = 긍정적인 것들

korean: 풍경이 너무 예뻐서 사진 찍어야겠다
english: The scenery is so beautiful I have to take a photo.
alt: This view is stunning. I need to capture this.
note: scenery / view = 풍경 / stunning = 놀랍게 아름다운 / capture = 담다

korean: 일출 보러 새벽에 일어났는데 완전 감동이야
english: I woke up at dawn to see the sunrise and it was absolutely breathtaking.
alt: Getting up early for the sunrise was so worth it. Truly moving.
note: sunrise = 일출 / breathtaking = 숨 막히게 아름다운 / worth it = 가치 있는

korean: 별이 너무 많다 도시에서는 볼 수 없는 광경이야
english: There are so many stars. This is a sight you can't see in the city.
alt: The starry sky out here is incredible. You'd never see this in the city.
note: sight = 광경 / starry sky = 별이 가득한 하늘

korean: 여기 공기가 달라 상쾌하다
english: The air here is different. So refreshing.
alt: The fresh air out here is on another level.
note: refreshing = 상쾌한 / on another level = 차원이 다른

korean: 이 동네 분위기가 좋아서 살고 싶어
english: I love the vibe of this neighborhood. I want to live here.
alt: This area has such a great atmosphere. I could see myself living here.
note: vibe / atmosphere = 분위기 / could see myself = 나 자신을 상상할 수 있는

korean: 시골 생활도 나쁘지 않겠다
english: Country life might not be so bad.
alt: Rural living is starting to appeal to me.
note: country life / rural living = 시골 생활 / appeal to = 끌리다

korean: 도시 생활이 피곤해 자연으로 돌아가고 싶어
english: City life is tiring. I want to return to nature.
alt: Urban living is exhausting. I'm craving a slower pace of life.
note: city/urban life = 도시 생활 / slower pace = 느린 속도

korean: 정원 가꾸기 시작했는데 보람 있어
english: I started gardening and it's so rewarding.
alt: Taking up gardening has been incredibly fulfilling.
note: gardening = 정원 가꾸기 / rewarding / fulfilling = 보람 있는

korean: 텃밭에서 토마토 키우고 있어
english: I'm growing tomatoes in my garden plot.
alt: I've been cultivating tomatoes in my little vegetable patch.
note: garden plot / vegetable patch = 텃밭 / cultivating = 재배하는

korean: 직접 키운 채소로 요리하면 맛이 다르더라
english: Cooking with vegetables I grew myself tastes completely different.
alt: Home-grown produce hits different. The flavor is incomparable.
note: home-grown produce = 직접 키운 농산물 / incomparable = 비교할 수 없는

korean: 아침에 새소리 듣고 일어나면 기분이 좋아
english: Waking up to the sound of birds singing puts me in a great mood.
alt: Nothing beats waking up to birdsong in the morning.
note: birdsong / sound of birds = 새소리 / puts me in a great mood = 기분이 좋아지게 한다

korean: 비 오는 소리 들으면서 자면 꿀잠이야
english: Falling asleep to the sound of rain is the best sleep ever.
alt: Rain sounds are the ultimate lullaby. I sleep like a baby.
note: sound of rain = 빗소리 / lullaby = 자장가 / sleep like a baby = 깊이 잠든

korean: 캠프파이어 앞에서 마시멜로 구워 먹자
english: Let's roast marshmallows by the campfire.
alt: Nothing beats toasting marshmallows around a fire.
note: campfire = 캠프파이어 / roast / toast marshmallows = 마시멜로를 굽다

korean: 여름 밤에 치맥은 국룰이지
english: Chicken and beer on a summer night is an absolute must.
alt: Chimaek on a hot summer evening is the ultimate Korean tradition.
note: chimaek = 치맥 / ultimate = 궁극의 / must = 필수

korean: 한강 라면 먹어봤어? 진짜 맛있어
english: Have you tried eating ramen by the Han River? It's amazing.
alt: Han River ramen hits different. It's a must-try experience.
note: Han River ramen = 한강 라면. 한국 문화에서 특별한 경험

korean: 불꽃놀이 보러 가자 올해는 꼭!
english: Let's go see fireworks this year for sure!
alt: We have to catch the fireworks display this year. No excuses!
note: fireworks / fireworks display = 불꽃놀이 / no excuses = 변명 없이

korean: 벚꽃 필 때 여의도 가야지
english: We should go to Yeouido when the cherry blossoms bloom.
alt: Cherry blossom season at Yeouido is a must-see.
note: cherry blossoms = 벚꽃 / bloom = 피다 / must-see = 꼭 봐야 할

korean: 단풍 시즌에 내장산 가볼까?
english: Should we visit Naejangsan during autumn foliage season?
alt: Naejangsan in fall is supposed to be spectacular.
note: autumn foliage = 단풍 / spectacular = 장관인

korean: 제주도는 사계절 다 좋아
english: Jeju Island is beautiful in all four seasons.
alt: Jeju is gorgeous no matter what time of year you visit.
note: all four seasons = 사계절 / gorgeous = 아름다운

korean: 여행 가서 현지인처럼 생활해보고 싶어
english: I want to travel and live like a local.
alt: I'd love to immerse myself in the local culture when I travel.
note: live like a local = 현지인처럼 살다 / immerse myself = 몰입하다

korean: 사진만 찍지 말고 눈으로도 즐겨
english: Don't just take photos. Enjoy it with your own eyes.
alt: Put the camera down sometimes and just take in the moment.
note: take in the moment = 순간을 만끽하다 / enjoy with your own eyes = 눈으로 즐기다

korean: 추억은 사진보다 경험으로 남는 거야
english: Memories are made from experiences not just photos.
alt: The best souvenirs are the experiences and stories you bring home.
note: memories = 추억 / experiences = 경험 / souvenirs = 기념품

korean: 인생 사진 건졌어 이거 프사로 해야지
english: I got an amazing photo. This is going to be my new profile pic.
alt: This shot is fire. Definitely making it my new pfp.
note: amazing photo = 인생 사진 / fire = 최고의 (슬랭) / pfp = profile picture

korean: 셀카봉 가져왔어? 단체 사진 찍자
english: Did you bring a selfie stick? Let's take a group photo.
alt: Anyone got a selfie stick? Time for a group shot.
note: selfie stick = 셀카봉 / group photo / shot = 단체 사진

korean: 여행 브이로그 찍어볼까? 유튜브에 올리자
english: Should we film a travel vlog? Let's post it on YouTube.
alt: Let's vlog this trip and upload it to YouTube.
note: vlog = 브이로그 / upload = 올리다

korean: 여기 SNS에서 핫한 곳이야 사진 찍기 좋아
english: This place is trending on social media. Great for photos.
alt: This is an Insta-famous spot. Perfect for photos.
note: trending = 인기인 / Insta-famous = 인스타에서 유명한

korean: 플레이팅이 예뻐서 사진 찍고 먹어야 해
english: The plating is so pretty, I have to take a photo before eating.
alt: This dish is so photogenic I need to snap a pic first.
note: plating = 플레이팅 / photogenic = 사진이 잘 나오는 / snap a pic = 사진을 찍다

korean: 여기 포토존이 있어 가서 찍자
english: There's a photo zone here. Let's go take pictures.
alt: They set up a photo spot. Let's go get some shots.
note: photo zone / photo spot = 포토존 / get some shots = 사진을 찍다

korean: 사진 보정 어떤 앱 써? 자연스러운 거 추천해줘
english: What photo editing app do you use? Recommend something natural-looking.
alt: What filter app gives the most natural results?
note: photo editing app = 사진 보정 앱 / natural-looking = 자연스러운

korean: 사진 태그하지 마 나 안 나왔어
english: Don't tag me in that photo. I look terrible.
alt: Please don't tag me. I didn't come out well in that shot.
note: tag = 태그하다 / I look terrible = 나 못 나왔어

korean: 라이브 방송 켤까? 같이 소통하자
english: Should I go live? Let's interact with everyone.
alt: I'm thinking of doing a live stream. Let's chat with viewers.
note: go live / live stream = 라이브 방송 / interact = 소통하다 / viewers = 시청자

korean: 요즘 뭐에 빠져 있어? 관심사가 뭐야?
english: What are you into these days? What are your interests?
alt: What's been keeping you busy lately? Any new hobbies?
note: into = 빠져 있는 / interests = 관심사 / hobbies = 취미

korean: 취미가 돈이 많이 들어서 문제야
english: The problem is my hobbies cost a lot of money.
alt: My hobbies are expensive. It's becoming a financial issue.
note: hobbies = 취미 / cost a lot = 돈이 많이 드는 / financial issue = 재정 문제

korean: 덕질하는 게 행복이야 최애가 있으니까
english: Fangirling makes me happy. Having a bias gives me joy.
alt: Being a fan of someone gives my life meaning. My fave is everything.
note: fangirling = 덕질 / bias / fave = 최애 / gives my life meaning = 삶에 의미를 준다

korean: 굿즈 사느라 돈을 다 썼어
english: I spent all my money buying merch.
alt: I blew my entire budget on fan merchandise.
note: merch / merchandise = 굿즈 / blew my budget = 예산을 다 쓴

korean: 팬미팅 갔다 왔는데 실물이 더 멋있어
english: I went to a fan meeting and they're even better looking in person.
alt: I just came back from a fan meet. They're stunning in real life.
note: fan meeting = 팬미팅 / in person / in real life = 실물

korean: 콘서트 떼창이 소름 돋아
english: The whole crowd singing together at the concert gave me goosebumps.
alt: The fan chant at the concert was spine-tingling.
note: crowd singing = 떼창 / fan chant = 팬 떼창 / goosebumps / spine-tingling = 소름 돋는

korean: 앨범 여러 버전 다 사야 하나? 지갑이 텅텅
english: Do I have to buy every version of the album? My wallet is crying.
alt: Multiple album versions are bleeding me dry. But I can't not buy them.
note: multiple versions = 여러 버전 / bleeding me dry = 돈을 다 빼가는

korean: 포카 뽑기 운이 안 좋아 원하는 게 안 나와
english: My luck with photocard pulls is terrible. I never get the one I want.
alt: I keep getting duplicates instead of the photocard I actually want.
note: photocard = 포카 / pulls = 뽑기 / duplicates = 중복

korean: 오늘 잠이 안 온다 내일 피곤하겠지
english: I can't fall asleep tonight. Tomorrow's gonna be rough.
alt: Insomnia is hitting me hard tonight. I'll be a wreck tomorrow.
note: insomnia = 불면증 / rough = 힘든 / wreck = 엉망인 사람

korean: 잠이 보약이야 충분히 자야 해
english: Sleep is the best medicine. You need to get enough of it.
alt: Nothing heals the body like a good night's sleep.
note: sleep is the best medicine = 잠이 보약이다 / good night's sleep = 숙면

korean: 자기 전에 따뜻한 우유 마시면 잠이 잘 온대
english: Drinking warm milk before bed helps you sleep better.
alt: A glass of warm milk before bedtime supposedly helps with sleep.
note: warm milk = 따뜻한 우유 / bedtime = 취침 시간 / supposedly = ~라고 하는

korean: 알람 시계 5개 맞춰놨는데도 못 일어나
english: I set five alarms and still can't wake up.
alt: Even with five alarms going off I somehow sleep through them all.
note: set alarms = 알람을 맞추다 / sleep through = 알람을 못 듣고 자다

korean: 주말에는 정오까지 자고 싶어
english: I want to sleep until noon on weekends.
alt: My ideal weekend involves sleeping in until at least midday.
note: sleep until noon = 정오까지 자다 / sleeping in = 늦잠 자기

korean: 낮잠 자면 밤에 잠이 안 와 악순환이야
english: If I nap during the day I can't sleep at night. It's a vicious cycle.
alt: Daytime naps mess up my nighttime sleep. It's a never-ending cycle.
note: nap = 낮잠 / vicious cycle / never-ending cycle = 악순환

korean: 수면 패턴이 완전 망가졌어 새벽 3시에 자
english: My sleep pattern is completely messed up. I go to bed at 3 AM.
alt: My sleep schedule is wrecked. I've been staying up until 3 AM.
note: sleep pattern / schedule = 수면 패턴 / wrecked / messed up = 망가진

korean: 잠자리가 바뀌면 잠이 안 와
english: I can't sleep when I'm not in my own bed.
alt: Sleeping in an unfamiliar bed always messes with my sleep.
note: unfamiliar bed = 익숙하지 않은 침대 / messes with = 방해하다

korean: 꿈을 너무 생생하게 꿔서 피곤해
english: I dream so vividly that I wake up tired.
alt: My dreams are so vivid they're exhausting. I wake up feeling like I haven't slept.
note: vividly / vivid = 생생한 / exhausting = 지치게 하는

korean: 오늘 꿈이 이상했어 무슨 의미일까?
english: I had a weird dream today. I wonder what it means.
alt: My dream last night was bizarre. Does it mean something?
note: weird / bizarre = 이상한 / what it means = 무슨 의미인지

korean: 코골이가 심해서 같이 못 자겠어
english: The snoring is so loud I can't sleep next to them.
alt: I can't share a room because of the snoring. It's unbearable.
note: snoring = 코골이 / unbearable = 참을 수 없는

korean: 수면 무호흡증 검사 받아봐야 할 것 같아
english: I think I should get tested for sleep apnea.
alt: It might be time to get checked for sleep apnea.
note: sleep apnea = 수면 무호흡증 / get tested / checked = 검사를 받다

korean: 멜라토닌 먹어봤어? 효과 있어?
english: Have you tried melatonin? Does it work?
alt: Is melatonin effective for sleep? Have you used it?
note: melatonin = 멜라토닌 / effective = 효과적인

korean: 이 매트리스 좋다 허리가 안 아파
english: This mattress is great. My back doesn't hurt.
alt: This mattress is a game changer for my back pain.
note: mattress = 매트리스 / game changer = 판도를 바꾸는

korean: 베개가 높으면 목이 아프더라
english: A high pillow gives me neck pain.
alt: Using a tall pillow strains my neck.
note: pillow = 베개 / neck pain = 목 통증 / strains = 무리를 주다

korean: 자면서 이갈이를 한대 치과 가봐야 하나?
english: Apparently I grind my teeth in my sleep. Should I see a dentist?
alt: Someone told me I grind my teeth at night. Time for a dental visit?
note: grind teeth = 이를 갈다 / dental visit = 치과 방문

korean: 잠꼬대가 심해서 가족이 놀랐대
english: My sleep talking was so bad it startled my family.
alt: Apparently I talk in my sleep and my family found it alarming.
note: sleep talking = 잠꼬대 / startled = 놀라게 한 / alarming = 놀라운

korean: 아침형 인간이 되고 싶은데 저녁형이라 힘들어
english: I want to be a morning person but I'm a night owl. It's hard.
alt: Transitioning from a night owl to an early bird is a real struggle.
note: morning person / early bird = 아침형 / night owl = 저녁형

korean: 오늘 할 일 다 했어 뿌듯하다
english: I finished everything I needed to do today. So satisfying.
alt: I checked off everything on my to-do list. Feeling accomplished.
note: finished everything = 다 끝낸 / to-do list = 할 일 목록 / accomplished = 성취감을 느끼는

korean: 주말이 빨리 왔으면 좋겠다 일주일이 너무 길어
english: I wish the weekend would come faster. The week is too long.
alt: Friday can't come soon enough. This week is dragging.
note: come faster = 빨리 오다 / dragging = 질질 끄는
